gmw_00000823.name: NGC 2273
gmw_00000823.overview: NGC 2273 is a double-barred Seyfert 2 spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0308022, arXiv:2210.15376] that hosts a heavily obscured, Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9806055, arXiv:2210.15376]. Its complex central structure includes a warped circumnuclear disk [arXiv:1304.4254], a star-forming nuclear ring [arXiv:0905.3349, arXiv:astro-ph/0606667], and a compact Keplerian water megamaser disk [arXiv:2101.07736].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2273 as a barred Seyfert with a star-forming nuclear ring, radio and [O III] structures, gas-velocity differences, and S-shaped [O III] kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 (Mrk 620) NGC 2273 is a barred galaxy vanDriel91 , with a star-forming nuclear ring FerrHST . Deviations from axial symmetry were confirmed by GMOS IFU observation of the stellar kinematics in the central regions Barbosa . Radio emission extends along PA 20 at the 20 cm wavelength, and two components along PA 90 are evident at the 6 cm wavelength in the central 1 Ulve84,Ho01 . HST observations resolved a jet-like [ O iii ] structure extending 2 east of the nucleus, at PA 90 , aligned with the double radio source FerrHST .

The velocity field in [ O iii ] lines shows S-shaped isocontours (Fig. NGC2273maps ), which might indicate deviations of the gravitational potential from axisymmetry. The data quality in H is insufficient to confirm or exclude a similar velocity pattern in hydrogen. However, one-dimensional cuts of the velocity fields show important systematic differences between LOS velocities in [ O iii ] and H . The stellar velocity field is well measured for this galaxy and shows rotation in a direction similar to gas, with the kinematic axis at the outer parts of the FOV approximately parallel to the H photometric major axis.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC2273 as a nearby spiral with prominent rings, a central disk, tightly wound arms, an apparent bar, and a nuclear ring that must be modeled separately.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 NGC2273 (Figures fig:NGC2273-1 and fig:NGC2273-2 ) is a nearby ( 26 ) spiral galaxy with several prominent rings and a central disk. Due to the proximity of the galaxy, the inner structure is well-resolved and the galaxy extends 100 \ on the sky. At the largest radii ( SMA 60'' / 7.5 ), the light distribution is flattened ( q 0.6 ) with a major-axis PA of about 140 E of N. The ends of the spiral arms are also visible.

They are tightly wound, forming a broad ring at 10-20'' ( 1.3-2.5 ) along the SMA. The ellipticity of the ring is lower than that of the outer disk, and the PA is misaligned by about +40 from the outer disk. The spiral arms emerge from opposite sides of an apparent bar, which thickens to become lens-like towards the center. Located well inside this bar/bulge region, at SMA =2'' ( 250 ), is a bright and slightly asymmetric nuclear ring and disk Mulchaey+97,Erwin+Sparke03,Gu+03 with similar PA and axis ratio as the outer disk see also Petitpas+Wilson02,Barbosa+06,Falcon-Barroso+06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2273 as a Seyfert 2 and Compton-thick candidate with a hidden nucleus; its spectral fitting favors a disk-like reflector and Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273* NGC 2273* is a Seyfert 2 galaxy 1999risaliti considered a Compton-thick due to the detection of a strong Fe K line with an equivalent width >1 keV and a low ratio of L _ X /L [ OIII ] 2005guazaniti . Observations with Suzaku suggested that the nucleus of NGC 2273 is obscured by a Compton thick column of 1.5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 2009awaki . A hidden nucleus is also suggested by the detection of polarized broad lines 2000moran .

We perform a simultaneous fit of the , and /BAT data with PEXMON reflection model and the result is consistent with R _ f >9.02. We modeled the reflection using borus02 instead of PEXMON finding a Compton thin torus ( (N_ H,refl )=23.75_ 23.56 ^ 24.19 ) with a covering factor lower than 59 of the sky. Modeling the reflection with XILLVER instead produced moderately ionized disk with =2.15_ 1.74 ^ 2.31 and R _ f >8.80.

The disk like reflector modelled with XILLVER is the best representation of the data. The fit with this model requires Compton thick absorption in the line of sight to the coronal emission with (N_ H, los )> 24.55 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2273 as morphologically complex, with a large-scale bar driving gas-flow signatures and residual velocities that the authors interpret as inflow motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 ( Mrk 620 ) Van Driel Buta ( ) have described a complex morphology of this galaxy: a large-scale bar of 50'' in diameter and two systems of spiral arms ending at distances 1 1 and 1 6 from the center. The influence of this large-scale bar appears clearly in the IFP velocity field in the emission line (Fig. 470ifp ): the dynamical axis deviates by more than 20^ from the line of nodes PA_0 at radii =3-45'' . The turn of occurs in the opposite direction with respect to the PA of the isophotes at these distances (Fig. 2273m ), in accordance with a common picture of gas flows in barred galaxies.

The residual line-of-sight velocities along the minor axis have amplitudes of more than 50-80 (after pure circular model subtraction) at radial distances of 10-13'' . The sign of the residual velocities corresponds to the gas inflow motions We assume that spiral arms are trailing. Thus the NW-side of the galaxy is the nearest to an observer. due to the large-scale bar.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2273 as a relatively isolated SBa Seyfert galaxy with a bar, pseudo-ring, gas-rich disk, central bulge dominance, and complex gas and stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273. This is a relatively isolated galaxy of moderate luminosity with a Seyfert nucleus and an unusually clear and symmetric structure in the central region, with a radius of 25'' (a bright bar and a pseudo-ring) and faint fuzzy spirals beyond it. The photometric profile of the galaxy cannot be described by a simple exponential law: it steepens at > 80'' 100''   [ 10, 15 ] . Like NGC 1167, NGC 2273 contains a fairly large amount of gas  [ 6, 16 ] . At <40'' , the brightness profile is dominated by the bulge. The total hydrogen mass is M_ HI = 2.42 10^9   M_ , but the average gas surface density is roughly as low as it is in NGC 1167 ( HI = 2.2   M_ / pc ^2 )  [ 6 ] .

Moiseev et al.  [ 9 ] and the SAURON team  [ 17 ] analyzed the optical spectra of the galaxy in order to study the two-dimensional velocity distribution of gas and stars. In the bar region ( <30'' ), gas (but not the stars) exhibits appreciable circular motions  [ 9 ] .

sources.summary: The paper reports H2 behavior unlike most galaxies and a centrally peaked [ FeII ] profile in NGC 2273, co-rotating disk gas, and a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper uses curvature to identify NGC 2273 as a barred spiral with small bulge, bar or ring structures, disk, multiple rings, and possible nuclear structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 is classified as SBa nilson1973 and SB()a RC3_1991 . In fig:ngc2273rkur the curvature indicates a small bulge contained inside R/R_p 0.1 (region 1). Considering the valley in region 2, it retain a gradient in , considerable lower compared to the first and second local peaks, suggesting a bar component. However this behaviour is not well pronounced here, but still indicates correctly the existence of different structural components.

The next valley in region 3 is formed by two tightly wound spiral arms laurikainen2017 , which behaves like a ring. The fourth peak at R/R_p 0.5 makes the transition of this component with the outermost region of the galaxy. worked on the details considering the aforementioned structures, adding a lens component and assuming that the outer region is a disk.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 2273 stellar kinematics, finding non-axisymmetric rotation and a low-dispersion ring aligned with a nuclear partial ring, interpreted as a recent star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 The radial velocity map (central right panel of Fig. painel-2273 ) shows a rotation pattern which deviates from the classical spider diagram, evidenced by the fact that the observed kinematic minor and major axes are not perpendicular to each other, indicating deviations from axial symmetry. The residuals map show indeed deviations from the model above the noise level, which seem to be co-spatial with the star-forming ring (see discussion below). The rotation curve (upper right panel of Fig. painel-2273 ) along the kinematic major axis seems to have reached the turnover at only 250 pc from the nucleus.

The velocity dispersion map (bottom left panel of Fig. painel-2273 ) presents a ring-like structure with values of about 60-80 km s ^ 1 while in the centre the velocity dispersion reaches values of up to 100 km s ^ 1 . In Fig. corte-2273 we present one-dimensional cuts of the velocity dispersion map along the major and minor axis of the galaxy which helps the visualization of the ring-like structure. This structure is co-spatial with the nuclear partial ring observed in the HST line emission images and color maps of FWM00 . The lower velocity dispersion can be interpreted as a signature of recently formed stars, which still keep -- at least partially -- the kinematics of the gas from which they have been formed woz03 and thus map the location of a starburst ring with semi-major axis of 300 pc.

sources.summary: The paper resolves compact central dense-gas and continuum emission in NGC 2273, finding similar line sizes and spectral widths for its nuclear molecular tracers.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 The 3 mm continuum appears to be partially resolved, having a FWHM of 1.86 1.13 at a PA =87^ (from a Gaussian fit to the data in the left panel of Fig. fg:2273 ). The 3 mm flux density of 2.46 0.16 mJy from a Gaussian fit to the continuum map is consistent with the mean spectral value of 2.34 0.09 mJy from the integrated spectrum (Fig. fg:sp2273 ). The \ line (Fig. fg:2273 , middle panel) has an integrated flux of 1.76 0.07 Jy km/s in a 3 aperture. Its FWHM is 1.87 1.17 at a position angle of 18^ .
The \ and \ lines are also similar spectrally. Indeed a single Gaussian fit to the spectra in Fig. fg:sp2273 gives FWHMs of 181 km/s and 175 km/s for \ (top panel) and \ (bottom panel) respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2273 as a clean maser-disk galaxy, compares new and previous compact radio-core maps, and finds its VLBA continuum misaligned with several disk axes.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 This galaxy poses a clean maser disk kuo2011 . NGC 2273 was previously observed with the VLBA at C-band in 2004 by lal2004 and showed a compact radio core. anderson2005 observed this galaxy at 8.4 GHz with the VLBA and detected radio emission extended along the northwest to southeast direction with a beam width of approximately 2 mas 1 mas. The structure is similar to our map shown in the lower right panel of Fig. fig:images1 . The distribution of the masers shows a hint of a warp in the disk kuo2011 .

For NGC 2273, the VLBA radio continuum is not aligned with any of the rotation axes: 32 from the maser disk's normal, 36 from the angular momentum orientation of the nuclear 150 pc scale disk and 58 from the normal vector to the galaxy's large scale disk.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 2273 outside the Hubble flow, solving for peculiar redshift, fixing the black-hole vertical offset, and obtaining a reduced chi-square with a scatter angle.

quotes.quote: In this work we perform a Bayesian statistical fit to estimate the mass-to-distance ratio and the recessional redshift of 10 different black holes hosted at the centre of active galactic nuclei, namely the galaxies NGC 5765b, NGC 6323, UGC 3789, CGCG 074-064, ESO 558-G009, NGC 2960, NGC 6264, NGC 4388, J0437+2456 and NGC 2273. Our general relativistic method makes use of the positions in the sky and frequency shift observations of water megamasers circularly orbiting the central black hole on their accretion disks.

NGC 2273 Similarly as NGC 4388, this galaxy is not into the Hubble flow since it is 25 Mpc away MCPIII . We therefore fit for the peculiar redshift Z_ pec as explained above. We fixed the BH y offset at y_0 = 0.050 mas and, varying y_0 along the disk we found differences in M/D up to 0.6 , which is less than the percentage error of the posterior M/D . The obtained reduced ^2 is of 1.627 for this astrophysical system with a scattering angle of = 15 °.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2273 best explained by a Compton-thick cold-reflection model, supported by iron-line strength, flat transmission fits, and high line-of-sight column density.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 The Compton thick, cold reflection model fits the high energy data better than the warm scattering model at a high confidence level: above 3 keV, ^2 = 6 with the same number of degrees of freedom. The Compton thin, transmission model indicates N _H = 2.6 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and is worse than the cold reflection model only at the 70 confidence level (same ^2 with only one degree of freedom less). However, the Compton thick nature of this spectrum is supported by the large EW of the iron line(s). Also, if in the transmission model the photon index is thawed (see Appendix) the best fitting value is = 0.7 , much flatter than expected for the intrinsic spectrum, thus further supporting the Compton thick, cold reflection model.

Therefore, if the Compton thick model is valid the column density along our line of sight must be larger than 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 . The fit of the iron line improves significantly ( > 99 confidence level) by splitting the line in two components at 6.4 and 6.96 keV.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 2273's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273: We performed a bulge plus disk decomposition on an R- band image from Erwin2003 using the Imfit software of Erwin2015c . A Sersic bulge (function Sersic of imfit ), a bar (function Sersic GenEllipse of Imfit ), an exponential disk (function Exponential of Imfit ) and an outer ring (function GaussianRing2Side of Imfit ) were fit to the image; see Erwin2015c for a definition of the function parameters. We set _e=-2.5 I_e and _0=-2.5 I_0 . The parameter values of the fit are given in Table tab:2273decomp . Model images of the bar, disk and inner ring components (see Figs. fig_ngc2273 and fig_imfitiso ) were subtracted from the original image and the bulge photometry was derived on the residual image.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2273 as a Seyfert 2 sample target, notes an instrumental three-spot pattern in its CanariCam image, and says it was not detected in the image subtraction.

quotes.quote: The chosen sources were the Low Ionisation Nuclear Emission-Line Region (LINER) NGC 2146, the Seyfert 1 NGC 931 and the Seyfert 2's NGC 1194 and NGC 2273, the former being classified as a Compton Thick source based on X-Ray data Guainazzi2005b . The selected sources also followed a technical limit of detectability for a reasonable integration time of roughly 0.2 Jy in the 12 filter of IRAS. This is also the lower limit in the Extended 12 Galaxy Sample Rush1993 , although NGC 2273 violates the |b| > 25^ restriction of this catalogue.

Artefacts in CanariCam imaging In some of the images, particularly in the case of NGC 931 and NGC 2273, one can clearly see a pattern of three bright spots circling the central object. Since the same pattern is also visible in the standard star images they are almost certainly not real.
Among these seven galaxies, four show unresolved emission, NGC 253 has clearly identifiable star formation nodes, and ESO 005-G004 and NGC 2273 have not been detected in the image subtraction.

sources.summary: The paper finds a circumnuclear disk in NGC 2273, with an inner ring and dust lanes, and cites kinematic evidence that the disk is warped and misaligned.

quotes.quote: In three galaxies (NGC 2273, NGC 2960 or Mrk 1419, and UGC 3789) we see evidence for a circumnuclear disk. In the case of NGC 2273 and UGC 3789, there is an inner ring which manifests as a spike in the ellipticity profile, and then dust-lanes interior to the ring most readily identified with spiral arms. In the case of NGC 2273, kinematic data confirm this interpretation barbosaetal2006,falconbarrosoetal2006 .
Finally, the kinematic major and minor axes of the disk in NGC 2273 are not aligned, and the disk is likely warped e.g., barbosaetal2006 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2273 has a small warped Keplerian maser disk and heavily absorbed X-ray spectral modeling broadly consistent with prior work.

quotes.quote: From this figure, one can see that, for GROD05 (top left panel), 3*XTER98 (bottom left panel), and the average (bottom right panel), all values are consistent with the ratio of 1 within a factor of 3, and a good agreement is found also with GXD03 (top right panel) with two sources (IC 2560 and NGC 2273) that have slightly larger values.
NGC 2273 also shows indications of a warped but much smaller disk (with inner and outer disk radii of 0.028 and 0.084 pc) with Keplerian rotation kuo11 .

NGC 2273: The starting spectral model for this source is again provided by the work of mas16 , who fitted the \ spectrum with the \ model that favored a heavily absorbed scenario with N_ H > 7 10^ 24 cm^ 2 . In our fitting, we used our baseline model, which yielded a best fit broadly consistent with their results.

sources.summary: NGC 2273 is used as the barred Sa test galaxy for adaptive IFS binning, with March 2001 panoramic spectra covering a 49 by 44 arcsec field and showing strong contrast and spiral-arm structure.

quotes.quote: In what follows we consider different methods and we apply each one to observations of the barred Sa galaxy NGC 2273 ( fig:n2273-SNmap ) taken with the panoramic IFS sauron-paper1,sauron-paper2 at the 4.2-m William Herschel telescope on La Palma. These observations, carried on in March 2001, are based on two pointings of 4 1800 s exposures. They cover a field-of-view of 49'' 44'' with an effective spatial sampling of 0 8 0 8 , over the 4810--5350 spectral range. They have been selected for having high contrast between the inner and the outer parts (in the range 1 - 150 ) and a complex distribution, caused by the presence of spiral arms and the merging of multiple exposures (leading to jumps and irregular outer boundaries, see fig:n2273-SNmap ).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 2273 as an unequal radio double with arcsecond-scale amorphous structure, a radio peak offset from the optical nucleus, and weak mas-scale emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 (0645+609) Y : Our map has similar features to those seen previously by at 8.4 GHz and at 5 GHz. The source consists of an unequal double with a separation of nearly 1 arcsec in P.A. 80 ^ . The WSRT image of this source at 5 GHz reported by shows amorphous structure on larger scales ( > 2.5 arcsec). Our central-component flux density and total flux density are slightly higher than those in the 5 GHz image of with a similar resolution.

sources.summary: NGC 2273 is described as a double-barred galaxy with stellar kinematics misaligned from the bar, a twisted central zero-velocity curve, and a likely nuclear stellar disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 is a well-studied double barred galaxy fwm00 . As expected in a low-inclined barred system, the \ stellar velocity field is misaligned with respect to the bar major axis. The zero-velocity curve displays a twist in the central 3 . This velocity feature is also accompanied by a central dip in the velocity dispersion maps and anti-correlates with the central h_3 values, suggesting the presence of a nuclear stellar disc.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 2273 a double-barred Sy 2 galaxy, notes studied emission-line structure, and finds dusty inner regions occupied by young stellar populations in a central disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 is a double-barred active (Sy 2) galaxy. Its emission line distribution has been studied by Ferruit et al. (2000). In the inner regions the galaxy has a very dusty zone occupied by young stellar populations in a central disc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2273 as a well-studied barred Seyfert 2 galaxy with nuclear spiral arms, ringlike dust, aligned radio structure, and extended [O III] emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 2273 (S2) This galaxy, classified as Seyfert 2 by Ho97 , has been extensively studied in the literature, from radio to X-ray wavelengths Anderson05,Terashima02,Falcon-Barroso06 . It is a well-known barred galaxy Ferruit00 , with nuclear spiral arms inside a ringlike dust feature Laurikainen05 . VLBA imaging shows an elongated multiple-component structure aligned east-west on the sky Anderson05 .

sources.summary: UGC03546 is used as a more massive SPARC comparison galaxy with a monotonic RAR track and properties resembling simulated massive galaxies with cuspy central dark-matter profiles.

quotes.quote: Namely, we focus on DDO154 (crimson circles), NGC0055 (orange upside-down triangles), and UGC03546 (magenta plus signs).

On the other hand, UGC03546 is a more massive galaxy with a monotonic track in RAR space. Its properties are similar to those of simulated massive galaxies with cuspy central dark matter density profiles.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 2273 as an early-type double-barred Seyfert whose nuclear gas bar aligns with the nuclear stellar bar and x2 orbits.

quotes.quote: The two early-types Sy2 galaxies NGC 5728 and NGC 2273, mapped with OVRO (Petitpas Wilson 2002) reveal very different gas morphologies, although they are both double-bar galaxies. NGC 2273 shows evidence of a nuclear gas bar, aligned with the nuclear stellar bar. Both are perpendicular to the primary bar, and therfore aligned on its x2 orbits.

gmw_00000824.name: NGC 2549
gmw_00000824.overview: NGC 2549 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1002.0760] that hosts a double, nested peanut-shaped structure [arXiv:1603.00019, arXiv:2003.00015] with an inner and outer bar [arXiv:1603.00019]. Its complex kinematics feature a multi-component velocity map with co-rotating central fast and outer slower rotating components [arXiv:astro-ph/0512200].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2549 as the worked early-type galaxy, observing it with NIFS and SAURON to constrain Schwarzschild models and estimate a central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: In order to test what correction of the PSF could be expected for typical early-type galaxies, we chose NGC524 and NGC2549 which both satisfy the basic Altair requirement for nuclear guiding. Specifically, NGC524 has a core-like light profile, while NGC2549 a cusp-like profile at the HST resolution. In this proceeding we will consider the observations of NGC2549 only, the smaller of the two galaxies.

The best fit model using SAURON and NIFS kinematics gives M _ =( 1.4^ +0.2 _ 1.3 ) 10^7 M _ sun . It is evident that when using only SAURON data it is not possible to determine the lower limit of the M _ , simply because the resolution of the SAURON data is 1.7 arcsec, while R _ sph =0.05 arcsec, using the above best fit model. Using the NIFS dataset only it seems possible to determine even the lower limit, in spite of 0.17 arcsec resolution, but the uncertainty on M _ is very large because, due to the small field-of-view, NIFS data alone are not able to constrain the total orbital distribution and give the right mass-to-light ratio. These are, however, well constrained using SAURON large field-of-view (FoV) observations. Combining the two data sets, the high resolution and large FoV, one can constrain Schwarzschild models and determine the M _ .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2549 as a SAURON velocity-map example, finding a multi-component map with central fast rotation, outer slower rotation, and co-rotating components.

quotes.quote: NGC 2549 has a multi-component velocity map. Its central region ( <5 ) is described by a linear rise in the velocity and a low flattening, rising up to 7 8 where the velocity reaches its maximum. Further away the velocity drops and beyond 10 so does the flattening (changing again at >17 ). These features are also recognisable on the velocity map. The behaviour of the k_5 term is, however, not obvious from the map.

Precisely the same behaviour is visible in Figs.  f:residuals and  f:mosaic for NGC 2549 and NGC 4473, respectively. The two co-rotating velocity components are clearly visible in the map of NGC 2549: a central fast rotating component with high flattening and an outer slower rotating component with somewhat smaller flattening. On Fig.  f:residuals it can be seen that along the major axis the contribution of the residuals is in the same direction as the bulk rotation, confirming the co-rotation of the components.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2549 as the second nested-peanut galaxy, measuring inner and outer X/P structures from HST and SDSS imaging and connecting them with bars.

quotes.quote: NGC 2549 -- This is the second galaxy where we detect both an inner and an outer X/P structure. As for NGC 128, we denote the inner peanut with (a) and the outer with (b) . Also similarly to NGC 128, we measured the diagnostics for (a) from an HST image, this time taken with the Wide Field (WF) + Planetary Camera (PC), WFPC 2. We refer the reader to Fig. 8 in C15 to see the image, reconstruction and residual, and their Fig. 10 to see the B_6 profile. However, because the peak in the outer peanut coincided spatially with the gap between the WF and PC chips, we again opted to model peanut (b) using the SDSS -band image.

A second example of a profiler decomposition is shown for NGC 2549 in the top panel of Figure fig:n2549_decomp , where the model consisted of a bulge (S ersic function, red curve), a disc (exponential, blue curve) and a bar associated with each of the two peanuts (Ferrers functions -- Ferrers1877 , cyan curves), once again nicely confirming the bar-peanut connection in this second galaxy with a nested peanut structure.

sources.summary: NGC 2549 is modeled as an X-shaped-bulge case where isophotes transition from boxy to disky, and higher Fourier harmonics quantify the galaxy's peanut-shaped structure.

quotes.quote: If we observe the isophote contours of NGC 2549 in the upper panels (centre and right) of Figure fig:n2549 , we notice how the isophotes transition from boxy (inner) to disky (outer). The shapes at the transition are not elliptical however (even though B_4 = 0 ), but rather a combination of boxy and disky (green contour), reminiscent of the B_6 corrected ellipse illustrated in Figure fig:coef246 (bottom right panel). The transition occurs precisely in the radial range where the X-shape/peanut feature is most obvious in the image. Since it is clear that higher-order harmonics are necessary to capture this morphological feature, the harmonic coefficients actually `measure' the peanutness in this way. This is an important result: with the degree of peanutness quantifiable this way, it is now possible to directly and quantitatively compare observations of real galaxies with simulations (such as e.g., Athanassoula2005 , Athanassoula2014 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2549 as a double nested peanut galaxy with a low 3:1 peanut scale ratio and as the main observational analogue for the simulated structure.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, two galaxies in this sample, NGC 128 and NGC 2549, were found to host a double, nested peanut configuration, i.e., a small scale X/P bulge embedded within a larger one. While only recently simulations of barred galaxies are seen to develop double X--shaped features under certain circumstances (e.g., Debattista+2017 , Smirnov&Sotnikova2018 , Parul+2020 ), the face-value interpretation of CG16 at the time was that each of the two co-existing X/P structures is associated with a distinct bar component, as double bar galaxies had been known for long to occur ( Friedli+1996 , Erwin+2001 , Erwin2011 ). Indeed, in a recent paper have detected a double peanut configuration in the face--on galaxy NGC 1291 (using the face - on signature of peanut galaxies, as introduced in Debattista+2005 ), which indeed hosts a large--scale, and a second embedded nuclear, bar. Nevertheless, the scale ratio of the two peanuts in NGC 128 and especially in NGC 2549 ( 3:1 ) is rather low for a (large scale+nuclear) bar configuration, and thus warrants further investigation.

For comparison with our work, we show a large-scale observation of NGC 2549 in the SDSS -band, and a higher-resolution zoom-in observation taken with the Hubble Space Telescope, at 702 nm, in Figure fig:ngc2549 . NGC 2549 can indeed be regarded as an observational analogue of our simulation, the two displaying many structural similarities (compare Figure fig:decomp_145 with the photometric decomposition of NGC 2549 shown in CG16, their Fig. 9).
The grey symbols correspond to NGC 128 (downward triangles) and NGC 2549 (upward triangles), filled for the outer and empty for the inner peanut, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 2549's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 2549: The M/L (already corrected for G.E.) is scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 . The SDSS band image is calibrated to the R- band using the aperture photometry in the R_ SV and V_ SV band of Sandage1978 , using the color transformations of Prugniel1993 and Jordi2006 . We performed an Imfit bulge (function Sersic ) plus disk (function EdgeOnDisk ) decomposition (see Figs. fig_ngc2549 and fig_imfitiso ). The parameters of this fit are given in Table tab:2549decomp .

gmw_00000825.name: NGC 2748
gmw_00000825.overview: NGC 2748 is a highly inclined SAbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1309.5166, arXiv:1311.1530] with tightly wound spiral arms [arXiv:1311.1530] and a rotating disk that displays non-circular motions [arXiv:1309.5166, arXiv:1504.06282]. The galaxy's 20 cm radio emission is mostly diffuse and likely traces star formation across the disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0407511].

sources.summary: The VLA H I data describe NGC 2748 as a nearby SAbc disk galaxy with the sample's highest SNR, a 1482 km/s systemic velocity, rotating-disk kinematics, and possible slight warping.

quotes.quote: NGC 2748 is an SAbc galaxy RC3 at a distance of 21.6 1.4 Mpc. The observation of this galaxy has the highest SNR among our five galaxies, with an H 1 based inclination of 72.6 , compared to 68.1 from optical data in HyperLeda Paturel03 . The fitted systemic velocity is 1482 km s ^ 1 . The kinematics show the typical signature of a rotating disk (Fig. fig:ngc2748 ). The asymmetric velocity field suggests that slight disk warping may exist.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 2748 as an S4G galaxy with a previously proposed polar ring, but adopts the view that the ring is too subtle or ambiguous to accept.

quotes.quote: Other polar rings have been found in the S ^ 4 G sample galaxies NGC 2748, NGC 6870, and NGC 7465, by buta14 . Their tentative contours are marked in the appendices of comeron14 . However, those three polar rings are exceedingly subtle and/or hard to interpret. Therefore, they may actually not be polar rings. This is the position adopted in this paper.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 2748's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 2748: We performed an Imfit bulge (function Sersic ) plus disk (function Exponential ) plus double ring (functions GaussianRing ) decomposition of the 3.6mu band Spitzer image (see Figs. fig_ngc2748 and fig_imfitiso ). The resulting values of the parameters of the fit are listed in Table tab:2748decomp . The residual image shows that the galaxy has a low surface brightness polar ring, that we masked while fitting the main galaxy. The position angle of the bulge component (which contributes 15 of the total light) is different from the rest of the galaxy; this probably stems from the modeling of the combination of a small bar (in which case the two rings make sense as an inner ring and an outer ring) and whatever small pseudobulge the galaxy may have. The Spitzer image does not really have the resolution to be sure about that.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC2748, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC2748 (Fig. fig:NGC2748 ) is a spiral galaxy seen at high inclination. The spiral arms are tightly wound and can be traced at semi-major axis radii between 6 and 40 undergoing a full 360 rotation. Inside 6 they are not well defined.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2748 velocity products, maps its non-circular motions, and measures residual arm velocities from the residual map.

quotes.quote: In Fig. ngc2748plots , we present the resulting moment maps for NGC 2748 in the top images.
In panels d) and e) of Fig. ngc2748plots , we have shown the velocity model and the non-circular motion maps respectively for NGC 2748.

In Fig. cdf we present the statistical distribution of the residual velocities of the arm region in NGC 2748. The adopted value for the residual velocities there is 23.2 1.8 km s ^ 1 ; which corresponds to the representative value in the residual map of NGC 2748 (panel e in Fig. ngc2748plots ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2748's 20 cm emission spans nearly the whole disk, is mostly diffuse rather than core-dominated, and likely traces star formation in the host disk.

quotes.quote: PRC C-28 (NGC 2748) The emission in this galaxy extends nearly the entire length of the galaxy disk at 20 cm. There is a bright central core, but it contains only 5 \ of the total emission; nearly all of the 20 cm emission comes from the diffuse component in the galaxy disk. None of the emission appears to be associated with the candidate polar ring (see Figure 4); it is probably due to star formation in the disk of the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2748's profile initially suggested a nuclear cluster, but NICMOS inspection and the final summary argue dust made the central excess misleading.

quotes.quote: An example of the necessary subjectivity in surface brightness profile fitting can be seen for NGC 2748.
However, closer inspection of the NICMOS images in figure images shows that dust is the likely cause of the inflexion in the SBP for NGC 2748.
In the case of NGC 2748 the apparent excess is misleading and is probably due to obscuring dust causing an inflexion in the surface brightness profile.

gmw_00000826.name: NGC 2787
gmw_00000826.overview: NGC 2787 is an isolated lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:1311.1530] featuring a strong bar [arXiv:1311.1530, arXiv:2010.12208], a ring [arXiv:1311.1530], and a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0209607]. The galaxy is completely devoid of star formation [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:2010.12208] and contains outer neutral hydrogen accreted from its environment [arXiv:1907.07261].

sources.summary: The paper reports decoupled gaseous kinematics in NGC 2787, tracing [NII] rotation to 20 arcsec, with outer neutral hydrogen accreted from outside and no star formation across the galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 2787. It is one more galaxy where we searched for inner polar disk because of the circumnuclear dust lane perpendicular to the isophote major axis polars0 . Presently, by exploiting the scanning Fabry-Perot interferometer, we succeeded to trace the gas rotation in the emission line [ NII ] 6583 up to R 20^ , and we saw a turn of the kinematical major axis over this radius range (Fig. fig_fpi ). However, in the emission line [ OIII ] 5007, by comparing the rotation velocity projections for the stars and for the ionized gas exposed with the long slit in two different position angles (Fig. fig_ls ), we see also decoupled gaseous kinematics at R>20^ quite certainly.

The gaseous disk isovelocity contour twist in the central part of NGC 2787 may be explained by a strong bar aligned in PA= 160 ; perhaps the gas when inflowing to the center is settling into a main plane of the triaxial potential orthogonally to the bar major axis. But the outer neutral hydrogen strongly decoupled from the stellar body is evidently accreted from outside though NGC 2787 is recognized as a completely isolated S0. Again, any star formation is absent over the whole galaxy extension.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 2787's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 2787: Erwin2003b presented evidence for a composite bulge in this galaxy. Although their ``inner disk'' may perhaps be better understood as the projected box/peanut structure of the bar (rather than a ``disky pseudo bulge''), we use the parameters of their decomposition (their Table 5) giving R_e=3.5'' , but shift the resulting total surface brightness to the 3.6mu band to match the total profile derived from Spitzer images (coming from Program 30318, PI Giovanni Fazio, Observer ID 2; Fig. fig_maxfits ). Sani2011 derive R_e=12.3'' , not distinguishing between the classical and the pseudo component of the bulge. The M/L is computed by fitting the kinematics of Bertola1995 (see Appendix C and Fig. fig_kinprofile ), using the sum of the bulge and disk profiles derived above. The distance comes from KormendyHo2013 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2787 X-ray faint relative to its optical line emission and argues that its radio core, broad H component, and variability favor an obscured LLAGN.

quotes.quote: The nuclei of NGC 2787 and NGC 4203 are embedded in soft diffuse emission with diameters of 30^ and 50^ , respectively. One object --- NGC 2787 --- has only 8 detected photons in the 0.5--8 keV band and is too faint to obtain spectral information.

The four objects NGC 2787, NGC 4550, NGC 5866, and NGC 6500, however, have much lower / ratios ( / 0) than expected from the correlation ( / 1-2 ), and their X-ray luminosities are insufficient to power the H emission (Terashima et al. 2000a). The first possibility, i.e., an obscured low-luminosity AGN as the source of the X-ray emission, seems preferable for NGC 2787, NGC 5866 and NGC 6500, although some other source(s) may contribute to the optical emission lines.

Additional lines of evidence which support the presence of an AGN include the fact that all three of these galaxies (NGC 2787, NGC 5866, and NGC 6500) have VLBI-detected, sub-pc scale, nuclear radio core sources (Falcke et al. 2000), a broad H component (in NGC 2787, and an ambiguous detection in NGC 5866; Ho et al. 1997b), a variable radio core in NGC 2787, and a jet-like linear structure in a high-resolution radio map of NGC 6500 with the VLBA (Falcke et al. 2000).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2787 as having a hard X-ray nucleus in diffuse emission, with simultaneous Chandra and XMM fits favoring a lightly absorbed soft power law.

quotes.quote: ho01apjl , after studying a \ snapshot, gave this source a class III X-ray morphology, showing hard X-ray nucleus embedded in diffuse emission. terashima03apj:rloud derived a 2-10 keV flux of about 3 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , after assuming a photon index of 2 and a Galactic absorption. gonzalezmartin09aa analyzed both \ and \ long observations and fit the \ spectrum with a power-law with a rather soft of 2.3 and fit the \ spectrum with a combination of two power-laws and a thermal component with a power-law absorption of 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . We found a good fit for both \ and \ spectra simultaneously with a single absorbed power-law and found little absorption of 2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 and a soft power-law photon index, =2.4 , and a 2-10 keV flux of 4 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC2787, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC2787 (Fig. fig:NGC2787 ) is a lenticular galaxy which shows a strong ring and large bar, requiring an improved model. As suggested by a visual inspection of the image, comparing various models confirms that the bar does not extend all the way through the center and requires an inner truncation. The same applies to the (large-scale) disk in order to model the ring.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2787 as an instrument-limited case: annular contamination prevents long-term comparison, and short-term variations are not detected.

quotes.quote: NGC 2787 : One observation per instrument is available. When they are compared, the emission from the annular region contributes with 53 in Chandra data. Thus, we do not perform a simultaneous fit and we can not use this object to discuss long term variations. Short term variations are not detected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2787 as a CIR-correlation outlier, citing its strong bar, absent star formation, relatively low-mass black hole, and possible non-AGN quenching.

quotes.quote: In this case, six galaxies ( IC 3773 (marked 6 in Figure fig:figure1 ()) NGC 0404 (1), NGC 0855 (7), NGC 1316 (8), NGC 2787 (9) and NGC 5866 (5)) are showing deviation from the linear relation.

NGC 2787 is reported to have a strong bar aligned in PA = 160 ^ and star formation is absent over the whole galaxy extension 2019ApJS..244....6S . The galaxy could be experiencing a non-AGN-related quenching process as it is reported to contain a relatively low-mass BH (10 ^ 7.6 M _ ) at its centre 2016ApJ...832L..11M .
Three galaxies (NGC 2787 (marked 9 in Figure fig:figure1 (d)), NGC 5128 (4) and NGC 7457 (10)) are outliers in the correlation.

sources.summary: The review compares decompositions of NGC 2787 and NGC 3945, noting small central bulge masses and inner-disk or barlens components much larger than classical bulges.

quotes.quote: We can compare the decompositions by lauri2014 with those obtained by erwin2003 for NGC 2787 and NGC 3945. Using a completely different decomposition approach they ended up with similar small relative masses for the central bulges in these two galaxies, just different names were used for the bulges. What is a barlens in lauri2014 , is called as an ``inner disk'' by Erwin et al., which disks are a magnitude larger than the central classical bulges, manifested as peaks in the surface brightness profiles.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 2787 as a low-mass-end disk-galaxy outlier whose leverage would torque the regression lines for disk-bearing early-type galaxies.

quotes.quote: NGC 1277 (peculiar morphology) and NGC 2787 are two disk galaxies which are potential outliers at the high- and low-mass end of our relations, respectively. They have a torquing effect on our regression lines, especially for the sub-category of galaxies with a disk (ES/S0). We have therefore excluded these galaxies from our regressions to avoid biasing the slope of our scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 2787's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has a LINER nucleus, very compact SER ( 40 pc) that peaks at the nucleus and extended emission that shows a rotation pattern with KMA tilted by 34^ relative to the PMA. Its kinematics and excitation have been investigated in brum2017 . The [ N ii ] is high at the nucleus and its distribution is elongated to the NW approximately along the kinematic minor axis, which could be due to an outflow along this direction.

gmw_00000827.name: NGC 2974
gmw_00000827.overview: NGC 2974 is an early-type galaxy classified variously as an elliptical or lenticular system [arXiv:1204.3879, arXiv:1702.02013] that hosts a Seyfert AGN [arXiv:1511.07446] or LINER nucleus [arXiv:1708.02683]. It features a fast-rotating disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0412186, arXiv:astro-ph/0508199], a bar-like component [arXiv:1511.07446], and multiple star-forming rings [arXiv:astro-ph/0608212, arXiv:0912.0274].

sources.summary: The paper reviews NGC 2974 as an E4/SA0-a system with rotating H I, gaseous spiral structure, an outer young stellar ring, and arc-like features it excludes as likely non-shells.

quotes.quote: NGC 2974: This object is classified as an elliptical galaxy, E4 ( devaucouleurs91 ), isolated elliptical ( sandage94 ), and an SA ( )0/ a galaxy by buta10 using the 4g data. tal09 identified multiple shells in this system. NGC 2974, however, shows an HI velocity field consistent with a rotating disk and the rotation axis of the galaxy is well aligned with the optical minor axis of the galaxy ( kim88 ). Additionally, according to emsellem03 , this galaxy has gaseous spiral structure and a possible inner bar within a radius of 200 pc.

From the near- and far-ultraviolet study ( jeong07 ), NGC 2974 hosts an outer ring at a radius of 60 with a young stellar population ( 1 Gyr), and we confirm the arc-like ring features at a radius of 55 (corresponds to 7 kpc) in the unsharp masked image of 3.6 (Figure ngc2974_usm ). A bright star near NGC 2974 makes it difficult to determine whether this feature is a ring or a shell, but the arc-like feature encompasses almost 180 , which can be seen in Figure 1 of jeong07 . It also hosts a regular HI ring aligned with the stellar component of galaxy ( serra10 ). Therefore, this arc-like feature may not be a shell as previously claimed ( tal09 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2974 as a decomposition example, identifying it as a misclassified spiral with a Seyfert nucleus, disk, bar-like component, faint ring, and compact bulge.

quotes.quote: As an illustration, we consider the galaxy NGC 2974, a spiral galaxy which has been misclassified as an elliptical galaxy in the RC3 catalog RC3 . This galaxy hosts a Seyfert AGN veroncettyveron2006 and filamentary dust in its center tran2001 . NGC 2974 is classified as a fast rotator by the ATLAS ^ 3D survey, and indeed the velocity map obtained by the SLUGGS survey shows that the galaxy kinematics is rotation-dominated well beyond three effective radii ( R>150'' ), as expected from a large-scale disk. From an inspection of the unsharp mask, we identified a ring at R 50'' , which might be a residual of two tightly wound spiral arms, and an elongated bar-like component within R 30'' , that is in addition to the more spherical bulge and produces a peak in the ellipticity and position angle profiles at R 20'' .

Our 1D galaxy decomposition for NGC 2974 (Figure fig:n2974ex ) consists of a S ersic bulge, an exponential large-scale disk, a Ferrer bar, a Gaussian nuclear component (AGN) and a Gaussian ring. Although the ring is extremely faint, it is important to account for it in the galaxy decomposition. A model without the ring component results in a ``steeper'' exponential profile for the disk (i.e. the exponential model has a smaller scale length and a brighter central surface brightness) and produces bad residual structures within R 40'' .

sources.summary: The paper presents UV and optical imaging of NGC 2974, finding young stellar populations, rings, and star formation likely driven by a large-scale rotating bar-like asymmetry.

quotes.quote: Hyunjin Jeong, ^ 1,2 E-mail: hyunjin@galaxy.yonsei.ac.kr Martin Bureau, ^2 Sukyoung K. \ Yi, ^ 1,2 Davor Krajnovi ^2 , and Roger L. \ Davies ^2 ^1 Department of Astronomy, Yonsei University, Seoul 120-749, Korea ^2 Sub-Department of Astrophysics, University of Oxford, Denys Wilkinson Building, Keble Road, Oxford OX1 3RH We present Galaxy Evolution Explorer ( GALEX ) far (FUV) and near (NUV) ultraviolet imaging of the nearby early-type galaxy NGC 2974, along with complementary ground-based optical imaging. In the ultraviolet, the galaxy reveals a central spheroid-like component and a newly discovered complete outer ring of radius 6.2 kpc, with suggestions of another partial ring at an even larger radius. Blue FUV NUV and UV optical colours are observed in the centre of the galaxy and from the outer ring outward, suggesting young stellar populations ( 1 Gyr) and recent star formation in both locations. This is supported by a simple stellar population model which assumes two bursts of star formation, allowing us to constrain the age, mass fraction and surface mass density of the young component pixel by pixel.

Overall, the mass fraction of the young component appears to be just under 1 per cent (lower limit, uncorrected for dust extinction). The additional presence of a nuclear and an inner ring (radii 1.4 and 2.9 kpc, respectively), as traced by [ O III ] emission, suggests ring formation through resonances. All three rings are consistent with a single pattern speed of 78 6 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 , typical of S0 galaxies and only marginally slower than expected for a fast bar if traced by a small observed surface brightness plateau. This thus suggests that star formation and morphological evolution in NGC 2974 at the present epoch are primarily driven by a rotating asymmetry (probably a large-scale bar), despite the standard classification of NGC 2974 as an E4 elliptical. galaxies: elliptical and lenticular, cD -- galaxies: evolution -- galaxies: individual: NGC 2974 -- galaxies: photometry -- galaxies: structure -- ultraviolet: galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper dynamically models the elliptical galaxy NGC 2974 with an isotropic oblate Jaffe model, fitting Gauss-Hermite kinematics and predicting its luminous mass.

quotes.quote: In this paper we analyse the relations between a previously described oblate Jaffe model for an ellipsoidal galaxy and the observed quantities for NGC 2974, and obtain the length and velocity scales for a relevant elliptical galaxy model. We then derive the finite total mass of the model from these scales, and finally find a good fit of an isotropic oblate Jaffe model by using the Gauss-Hermite fit parameters and the observed ellipticity of the galaxy NGC 2974. The model is also used to predict the total luminous mass of NGC 2974, assuming that the influence of dark matter in this galaxy on the image, ellipticity and Gauss-Hermite fit parameters of this galaxy is negligible within the central region, of radius 0.5R_ e .

For this model, the mean absolute error values between these fit parameters (v, , h_3, h_4) and their corresponding observations are, respectively, 12.75 kms ^ 1 , 12.00 kms ^ 1 , 0.005 and 0.004. Therefore this fitting can be regared as good. The error bars in Fig. NGC2974_MJ_60 represent the major axis Gauss-Hermite fit parameters of NGC 2974 [ see Cinzano and van der Marel 1994, figure 1 or figure 5(a) ] .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2974 as a barred S0 with disk evidence, inner bars, spiral arm-like UV structure, and shell features indicating a minor merger.

quotes.quote: The sample includes five S0 galaxies: NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 (interacting with NGC 5638, an elliptical galaxy). Notice that NGC 2974 is erroneously classified as elliptical both in RC3 and RSA, although an exponential disk is evident from the optical and UV luminosity profiles see also Jeong07, Jeong09, Marino10a . In addition, in NGC 2974 Krajnovic05 found, with SAURON observations deZeeuw02 , the existence of non-axisymmetric perturbations consistent with the presence of inner bars.

NGC 1533 UV image shows an incomplete, clumpy ring-like structure, while NGC 2962, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 which have well defined and complete rings, although not uniform, in the FUV and NUV bands. Spiral arm-like structures are visible in both NGC 2962 and in NGC 2974. Both the ring and the arm-like structure appear bluer than the nucleus and the galaxy body.
Tal09 report the presence of a shell system surrounding NGC 2974, a signature of a minor merging event Dupraz86,Ebrova09 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2974 as an early-type galaxy with regular H I, H emission, rapid rotation, and gas-disc kinematics useful for dynamical inclination modeling.

quotes.quote: This paper presents a case study of the early-type galaxy NGC 2974. It is one of the few elliptical galaxies known to contain an extended disc of neutral hydrogen in regular rotation 1988ApJ...330..684K . It also hosts extended H emission 1993A&A...280..409B, 1998A&AS..128...75P , and belongs to the `rapid rotators' 1988A&A...193L...7B .

Inclination of the gas disc The existence of the emission-line gas disc in NGC 2974 can be used to infer the inclination of the galaxy assuming an equilibrium dynamical configuration. This has been attempted before and in all studies the inclination of the gas disc was consistent with 55 60 ( 1993sdce.conf..225A 55 ; 1993A&A...280..409B 59 ; CvdM94 1994MNRAS.270..325C 57.5 ; 1998A&AS..128...75P 60 ). The high quality of the two-dimensional kinematics allows us to estimate the inclination of the emission-line gas disc more accurately.

sources.summary: NGC 2974 serves as the prototypical fast-rotator test case, with gas-kinematic inclination constraints and Jeans models showing anisotropy-inclination degeneracy.

quotes.quote: Our prototypical test case is the fast-rotator E4 galaxy NGC 2974. The inclination of this galaxy was studied extensively by a number of authors using the kinematics of the gas e.g. weijmans08 . From accurate models of the \ gas kinematics in the regions where we have our stellar kinematics krajnovic05 find a best fitting inclination i=60 3 . A sequence of model predictions for the velocity second moments ( v_ los ^2 )^ 1/2 as a function of anisotropy _z and inclination i is shown in fig:models_beta_inc . A strong similarity is evident between the effect on the models of the anisotropy and of the inclination.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2974 as an outlier early-type galaxy with a large-scale star-forming ring seen in PAHs, UV, and ionized emission, while otherwise resembling circumnuclear systems.

quotes.quote: Outliers While the two classes described above neatly bring together two groups of early-type galaxies that have a large number of similar properties, several ``outlier" galaxies in our sample (NGC 2685, NGC 2974, NGC 4477) resist such straightforward classification.

NGC 2974 also contains a large-scale star-forming ring, detected in PAHs, broadband UV emission, and \ Figure profiles a ; Jeo+07,Wei+08 . In this system, the ring is morphologically and kinematically aligned with the stars Figure Main-SF ; Wei+08 . A probable interaction between this gas and a putative large-scale bar is responsible for generating the ring morphology Kra+05,Jeo+07 , suggesting that here, as with \ galaxies, there is a deep connection between stellar kinematic structures and the spatially distinct morphology of star formation. This galaxy resembles \ systems in other ways, including the co-rotation of the gas and the stars as well as the old SSP ages (11 Gyr; Kuntschner et al. in prep ), and we consequently include NGC 2974 in our discussion of the \ galaxies in discussII (see blue-magenta symbol in Figure starsgas ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2974 in its made-to-measure dynamical modeling, deriving its MGE potential, showing velocity contours, and comparing observed and modeled velocity maps.

quotes.quote: All the galaxies are modelled as axisymmetric galaxies with their gravitational potentials calculated from deprojecting the multi-Gaussian expansions (MGEs) of their surface brightness recorded in Cappellari2006 , and Krajnovic2005 for NGC 2974. The multi-Gaussian expansion technique is described in Emsellem1994 and is not repeated here. The galactic kinematic and photometric symmetry axes are assumed to align.
As an example, we show the resulting velocity contours for NGC 2974 in Figure fig:icsNGC2974 .

In Figure fig:galaxyvelocity , we show the observed and model line-of-sight velocity maps for a selection of 4 galaxies (NGC 2974, NGC 3414, NGC 4550 and NGC 5813) covering the major galaxy types (elliptical and lenticular), rotation (fast and slow), orientation (inclined to the line-of-sight and edge-on) and core features.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2974 as an E4 or S0/a galaxy whose faint optical ring or arm-like structure is bright in UV and appears in NUV luminosity profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 2974 This galaxy is classified as E4 in both the RSA and the RC3. However, Buta15 classified it as SA()0/a with type T=0.0 A bright star superposed on S-W side of the galaxy hampers the study of this galaxy mainly at optical wavelengths. The CGS optical image atlas shows the presence of a faint extended ring/arm-like structure in NGC 2974 Ho11,Li11 . This structure is very bright in the GALEX Far-UV Jeong09,Marino11a,Marino11b,Marino11c and in our NUV images (top right panel of Figure NGC2974-sb ). The CGS optical and NIR luminosity profiles do not show signatures of this structure which appears in our NUV luminosity profiles.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2974 as an early-type galaxy with a co-rotating ionised-gas disk, ring and spiral-arm structure, moderately high line ratios, and dust tracing those features.

quotes.quote: NGC 2974 reveals a fast rotating gas disk co-rotating with respect to the stars. Despite the regular appearance of the ionised-gas distribution, the equivalent width (EW) map of the \ line (see Fig. fig2 ) highlights the presence of an inner ring and two spiral arms that extend all the way out to the limits of our field-of-view (see Krajnovi et al. 2005a for a detailed analysis of this galaxy using \ data).

The ratio is moderately high in NGC 2768, NGC 2974, and NGC 4278, while it is very low in NGC 4526. In this last galaxy, the location of the low / \ values corresponds to the location of a prominent dust disk (as shown in the unsharp-masked image), and suggests ongoing star formation. In this respect, there seems to be a clear link between the ionised structures found in the / \ maps with those seen in the \ equivalent width and kinematic maps (i.e. ring in NGC 2974, or integral-sign pattern in NGC 4278).
The ring-like structure and spiral arms in NGC 2974 are also followed by dusty structures at the same locations.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 2974's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 2974: The M/L is scaled to the distance of Sani2011 and from I to 3.6mu- band using the (I-3.6mu) color from Hyperleda. The Spitzer images come from Program 30318, PI Giovanni Fazio, Observer ID 2. The BH mass comes from the uncertain determination of Cappellari2008 . We exclude this galaxy from the fits reported in Tables tab_1dimstrict and tab_2dimstrict .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2974 as a low-dark-matter-fraction early-type galaxy, comparing its globular-cluster dynamical mass estimate with an H I study and noting limited GC sample size.

quotes.quote: The results we have obtained for NGC 2974 may appear to be at odds with that reported in the HI study of Weijmans_2008 where they obtained a dark matter fraction of 0.55 within 5  and a M_ tot (< 5  ) = 2.7 10^ 11 \ for their maximal disk model, compared to our values (for the isotropic orbit case) of 0.08 0.45 and M_ tot (<5  ) = 0.8 0.4 10^ 11 \ . The stellar mass and galaxy size used in the two studies are comparable. If we assume = 0 (i.e. the isothermal case as suggested by their flat rotation curve), we would derive a somewhat higher dark matter fraction of 0.15. The main limitation on our DM fraction measurement for NGC 2974 is likely the limited sample size of 26 GCs; this results in a relatively large error.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 2974 as an E-type galaxy with a velocity-broadened LINER nucleus, faint rotating emitting disk, deep wide Na D absorption, and existing stellar/gas dynamical modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 2974: This E-type galaxy contains a velocity broadened LINER nucleus embedded in a faintly emitting disk with a strong rotational signature. The Na D absorption is deep and wide. The dynamics of this galaxy were studied by Bettoni92 . In addition there is SAURON data and detailed modeling of both stars and gas by Krajnovic05 , and HST data has been obtained by Emsellem03 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2974 as a regular SAURON velocity-map example, with near-axisymmetric kinematics, small higher-order terms, and photometric-kinematic agreement.

quotes.quote: We used the SAURON 2001MNRAS.326...23B velocity map of NGC 2974 presented in 2004MNRAS.352..721E and its measurement errors to construct one hundred realisations of the velocity map. The velocity in each bin was taken from a Gaussian distribution of mean equal to the observed velocity and standard deviation given by the corresponding measurement error.

NGC 2974 is largely consistent with axisymmetry (but see 2003MNRAS.345.1297E and 2005MNRAS.357.1113K for bar signatures), and the simplicity of the velocity map is reflected in the kinemetric coefficients: the k_5 term is below 1-2 , while the flattening and position angle are both constant over the map except in the central few arcsecs where the departures from the axisymmetry are the strongest. The photometric and kinematic position angles and, especially, the flattenings agree very well.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 2974 as an early-type galaxy whose rings can be modeled as resonances in an S0 galaxy with an extended stellar bar and fast pattern speed.

quotes.quote: The pattern speed of early-type galaxy NGC 2974 has been measured by fitting the properties of three rings of recent star formation jeong_etal_07 . This galaxy is normally classified as E4, but the authors argue that all three rings can be accounted for as occurring at resonances if this galaxy is a S0 galaxy with an extended stellar bar and pattern speed, _p 78 6 . This pattern speed is only slightly slower than that of fast bars.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2974 as an elliptical-galaxy test case with an HI-traced flat circular velocity curve, but notes its shape and photometry limit its usefulness.

quotes.quote: As an example, the circular velocity in NGC 2974, which can be traced by an HI disk, becomes constant at around 5 kpc and has the value 300 kms ^ 1 , which would correspond to a Jaffe scale radius of 23 kpc. Unfortunately, NGC 2974 is neither spherical nor does its photometry reach large radii so that it does not serve well as a test object. In any case, Weijmans et al. (2008, their Fig. 20) showed that the reverse procedure (going from the density to the circular velocity curve) leads to a very good fit.

sources.summary: NGC 2974 is described as an E4 galaxy with spiral and shell substructure, rapid inner rotation, and a major-axis rotation plateau to about 3 effective radii.

quotes.quote: Though classified as an E4, this galaxy exhibits some spiral structure in addition to more subtle substructure in the form of shells Tal:2009ht . It rotates rapidly within 1 \ with corresponding anti-correlated h_3 values Cinzano:1994tl,Emsellem:2004kr . At larger radius the rotation amplitude plateaus out to 3 \ along the major-axis, and also remains anti-correlated with h_3 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 2974 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is an elliptical (E4) field galaxy. Its kinematics was mapped by the SAURON survey 2004MNRAS.352..721E , showing that the galaxy is rotating at 120 along the photometric major axis, while the velocity dispersion has a steep decreasing profile. PN kinematic data show that the galaxy halo rotates at least at 70 along the photometric major axis, and has a velocity dispersion of 150 .

gmw_00000828.name: NGC 3079
gmw_00000828.overview: NGC 3079 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1503.01836] classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1505.01166, arXiv:1811.00838] and a starburst galaxy [arXiv:1811.00838]. It features a heavily obscured, Compton-thick core [arXiv:1502.07353, arXiv:1905.06964] that hosts water megamasers [arXiv:1802.05133, arXiv:1811.00838] and drives a biconical nuclear outflow [arXiv:1509.00350, arXiv:2110.01766].

sources.summary: The paper newly analyzes NGC 3079, finding bimodal opening-angle fits and concluding that a sphere-model solution implies a heavily buried, nearly fully covered source.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 10 local ( z<0.1 ) CT AGN observed by : NGC 424, NGC 1068, 2MFGC 2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386, NGC 3079, IC 2560, Mrk 34, NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy. With the exception of 2MFGC 2280, these sources were selected to be known CT AGN and to cover a wide range in X-ray luminosity. Particular attention was paid to selecting sources in the low ( 10^ 42 ) and high ( 10^ 44 ) luminosity regimes, thus enveloping the bulk of the local CT AGN population, which is at moderate luminosities, so that our sample could be as representative as possible, despite its small size.

In two sources, NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, we discover bimodality in statistic space for , with local minima at small and large opening angles. We show their \ as a function of \ in Figure fig_thetacon . For NGC 3079, the large opening angle provides a marginally better fit, whereas for NGC 4945 the small opening angle is a significantly better fit. For two sources, NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, we find that the best-fit \ reaches the lower limit on this parameter (26 ), which implies that both the sources are highly covered. We also find that the values for these fits deviate significantly from the canonical value of 1.9 (steep at 2.52 for NGC 1386 and flat for NGC 4945 at 1.58). The fact that the deviations from the canonical value occur at the edge of the \ parameter space suggests that the true \ of these sources lies outside of the range of the torus model (i.e. < 26 ). also present a model of a fully covered source (i.e. 0 \ opening angle), which includes variable iron and elemental abundances, known as sphere . We fit this model, with the iron abundance free, in place of the torus model for NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, and present the results in Table table_specpar . This is also done for NGC 3079 since the small opening angle also provides a good fit to that source. An improvement in the fit statistic is found for both NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, where the spherical model produces a significantly better ( > 90 significance) fit than the torus model for both ( ^2 =4 and 136, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper presents deep H I observations of NGC 3079, finding an extended lopsided disk, gas streams, a bridge to MCG 9-17-9, and a nuclear atomic outflow.

quotes.quote: We present deep neutral hydrogen ( ) observations of the starburst/Seyfert galaxy NGC 3079 and its environment, obtained with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope. Our observations reveal previously unknown components, both in \ emission and in absorption, that show that NGC 3079 is going through a hectic phase in its evolution. The \ disk appears much more extended than previously observed and is morphologically and kinematically lopsided on all scales with evidence for strong non-circular motions in the central regions. Our data reveal prominent gas streams encircling the entire galaxy suggesting strong interaction with its neighbours. A 33-kpc long \ bridge is detected between NGC 3079 and MCG 9-17-9, likely caused by ram-pressure stripping of MGC 9-17-9 by the halo of hot gas of NGC 3079.

We also detect, in absorption, a nuclear \ outflow extending to velocities well outside what expected for gravitational motion. This is likely an atomic counterpart of the well-studied outflow of ionised gas present in this galaxy. This may indicate that also large amounts of cold gas are blown out of NGC 3079 by the starburst/AGN.

sources.summary: Deep Herschel imaging of NGC 3079 reveals X-shaped far-infrared dust filaments and central biconical heating aligned with its wind, while spectroscopy shows cool gas interacting with nuclear outflows.

quotes.quote: We present the results from an analysis of deep Herschel far-infrared observations of the edge-on disk galaxy NGC 3079. The PSF-cleaned PACS images at 100 and 160 m display a 25 25 kpc ^2 X-shape structure centered on the nucleus that is similar in extent and orientation to that seen in H , X-rays, and the far-ultraviolet. One of the dusty filaments making up this structure is detected in the SPIRE 250 m map out to 25 kpc from the nucleus. The match between the far-infrared filaments and those detected at other wavelengths suggests that the dusty material has been lifted out of the disk by the same large-scale galactic wind that has produced the other structures in this object. A closer look at the central 10 10 kpc ^2 region provides additional support for this scenario.

The dust temperatures traced by the 100-to-160 m flux ratios in this region are enhanced within a biconical region centered on the active galactic nucleus, aligned along the minor axis of the galaxy, and coincident with the well-known double-lobed cm-wave radio structure and H X-ray nuclear superbubbles. PACS imaging spectroscopy of the inner 6-kpc region reveals broad [ C II ] 158 m emission line profiles and OH 79 m absorption features along the minor axis of the galaxy with widths well in excess of those expected from beam smearing of the disk rotational motion. This provides compelling evidence that the cool material traced by the [ C II ] and OH features directly interacts with the nuclear ionized and relativistic outflows traced by the H , X-ray, and radio emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3079 as a nearby edge-on SBc LINER observed with XMM-Newton, finding a huge soft X-ray halo, thermal outflow signatures, extranuclear spurs, and NEI wind conditions.

quotes.quote: The nearby (distance 17 kpc) edge-on (inclination to the line of sight about 85^ ) spiral galaxy NGC3079 has been observed with XMM-Newton for a total of 25 ksec. The galaxy is classified as an SBc LINER with distinct nuclear activity. The object is an ideal target for analyzing the diffuse X-ray emission in the disk and the surrounding galactic halo, since galactic foreground absorption is low (column density N_H < 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ). The target was chosen to investigate both the problem of starburst AGN connection, and to see if there was a wide-spread extranuclear X-ray emission, for which we wanted to derive its spectral properties.

We detected a huge soft ( 0.2 - 1.0 keV) X-ray halo (s. Fig. fig:n3079_img_soft ) extending perpendicular to the galaxy disk to about 17.5 kpc (Breitschwerdt et al., 2003). The emission is therefore expected to be dominated by thermal lines, which are abundant in the soft band. The spectrum of a conspicuous emission region indeed proves this point. Moreover it clearly shows that the galactic starburst must drive a thermal outflow, since we have strong indications for collisionally excited oxygen and iron L line complexes in the spectrum (s. Fig. fig:n3079_spfit ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as an active edge-on Seyfert/LINER galaxy whose nuclear OH absorption separates into systemic disk gas and a blueshifted outflow linked to starburst activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 is an edge-on Sc galaxy housing a LINER (Low Ionisation Narrow Emission Line Region) nucleus heck80 with a long history of observations at various wavelengths. The nucleus is also classified as a type 2 Seyfert nucleus ford86 . The galaxy has a systemic velocity of 1116 irwi91 , which gives the distance of 16 Mpc and hence 1 milliarcsec (mas) corresponds to approximately 0.08 pc, adopting H _0 = 75 Mpc ^ 1 . Observations of optical emissions with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) have shown a number of outflowing filaments in a 'superbubble', produced by a combination of stellar winds and supernova explosions in a site of massive star formation.

The broad OH absorption towards the nuclear continuum source in NGC 3079 was imaged using VLBI techniques. The EVN observations reveal two kinematically independent OH absorption components, where OH1 is the blue-shifted absorption, and OH2 is the absorption at the systemic velocity of the galaxy. The understanding of the kinematics of these components is limited by the fact that the 18-cm background continuum and the OH components have not been spatially resolved.

The blue-shifted component OH1 appears to be associated with molecular outflows and is possibly associated with the nuclear super-bubble due to circum-nuclear starburst activity at components E and F and possibly at other locations.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3079 as a Seyfert/LINER with a powerful outflow and a thick, clumpy maser disk orbiting a likely black hole and forming stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 is a relatively nearby ( v_ sys =1125 6 km s ^ 1 ), edge-on spiral galaxy classified as Seyfert 2 Ford et al. 1986; Ho1997 Ford1986 or a LINER Heckman1980 . Observations of large-scale structure in this galaxy indicate the presence of a powerful outflow that appears to originate within the nucleus. Along the minor axis of the galaxy, at a position angle (P.A.) of 80 , a bipolar super-wind inflates a kpc-scale superbubble visible in radio continuum emission Duric1988, Baan1995, Irwin2003 , [ NII ] + H emission Veilleux1994 , and soft X-ray continuum emission Cecil2002 . The apex of the superbubble is embedded in a dense molecular kpc-scale disk traced by CO Koda2002 , HCN, and HCO ^+ emission Kohno2001 . The circumnuclear molecular disk is aligned with, and rotates in the same sense as, the much larger galaxy disk such that the north side is approaching and the near side is projected to the west of its major axis.

\ In what follows, we propose that the inner parsec in NGC 3079 contains a nearly edge-on, massive, thick, and flared disk. The disk orbits a mass of 2 10^6 M_ enclosed within 0.4 pc, most likely a supermassive black hole, and it is aligned with the kpc-scale molecular disk. The disk is most likely self-gravitating, clumpy, and forming stars.

sources.summary: The paper reports two 6.7 GHz methanol absorption components toward NGC 3079, likely arising against nuclear continuum sources A, B, and E, with poorly constrained A-methanol column density.

quotes.quote: Using the Effelsberg 100 m telescope, two absorption features were identified toward the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 3079. Both components probably originated on lines-of-sight toward the central region, presumably absorbing the radio continuum of the nuclear sources A, B, and E of NGC 3079.

The spectrum obtained toward the starburst and H _2 O megamaser galaxy NGC 3079 shows two absorption components with line-to-continuum ratios of about 1/50 and 1/150. This is the signature of a Class I environment, where the absence of a strong infrared radiation field inhibits the inversion of the level populations. Instead, the line is characterised by ``anti-inversion'' or ``overcooling''. Most of the absorption likely occurs toward the nuclear continuum sources A, B, and E. With no information on the density and kinetic temperature of the gas, the A-methanol column density is poorly defined and may range between a few times 10 ^ 13 and a few times 10 ^ 15 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper presents deep HI observations showing NGC 3079 has complex extended gas, a warped disk, a large stream, and cold gas outflows linked to starburst/AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Very deep neutral hydrogen (HI) observations of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 3079 with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) are presented. The galaxy has been studied extensively in different wavelengths and known for its several unique and complex features.
