However, the new data still revealed several new features and show that the galaxy is even more complicated and interesting than previously thought. In the new data a large stream of gas, encircling the entire galaxy, was discovered, while the data also show, for the first time, that not only hot gas is blown into space by the starburst/AGN, but also large amounts of cold gas, despite the high energies involved in the outflow.

The HI emission from NGC 3079 appears to be more complex than thought in previous studies, with a much more extended and highly asymmetric distribution (see Fig. f:totHI ). The HI disk also shows an asymmetric warp in the outer regions. A large stream of gas, encircling the entire galaxy was discovered, making an HI bridge between NGC 3079 and MCG 9-17-9, which indicates interaction between the two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports NH3 absorption in NGC 3079, notes a puzzling non-detection of NH3 (3,3), and infers hot components in two velocity features.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079: The NH _3 absorption toward NGC 3079 possesses the same two-component spectral structure as that observed in the H _2 CO 2_ 11 2_ 12 measurements reported in Mangum2013 . The H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 measurements reported in Mangum2008,Mangum2013 also have the same velocity structure as that observed in our NH _3 measurements, with the exception that there is a tentative, weak H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 absorption component at v_ hel 1500 \ that is just outside the bandwidth of our NH _3 measurements. We are not aware of any previous NH _3 measurements toward this galaxy. The background continuum source in NGC 3079 is estimated to be 0.5^ in extent see Duric1983,Kukula1995 . Unexplained is the non-detection of NH _3 (3,3) toward this galaxy, even though we detect the higher-excitation (6,6) and (9,9) transitions.

One possibility is the existence of a separate physical component within NGC 3079 which has physical conditions which result in NH _3 (3,3) maser emission which cancels any NH _3 (3,3) absorption from the physical structures which also produce absorption in the other NH _3 transitions.
Our NH _3 (1,1) through (9,9) spectra provide only lower limits of 100 and 150 K to the kinetic temperature in both of the v _ opt 1020 \ (NGC 3079G1) and 1115 \ (NGC 3079G2) velocity components.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC3079 with multiple X-ray datasets, modeling its transmission and MYTorus spectra and confirming heavy Compton-thick obscuration in the source.

quotes.quote: NGC3079 spectral fits Given the absence of significant flux and spectral variability among the several datasets available for NGC3079, they were all fit simultaneously in the up to 45 keV (due to the good SNR of the \ spectra) using a transmission model. Figure fig:NGC3079_spectra (top-left panel) shows the fitted spectra (and data-model residuals in units of ) and the relative model in transmission (top-right panel); pn data are shown in black, the MOS summed spectra are in red, ACIS-S data are in green, and blue and light blue show the \ FPMA and FPMB spectra, respectively. The MOS data reproduce well all the spectral features identified in the pn spectrum, although some residuals remain at the energy of the Fe K fluorescence emission line.

Figure fig:NGC3079_spectra (bottom-left panel) shows the full-band spectra fitted with MYTorus model. This physical model, which can be reproduced within xspec as phabs(mekal + zpowerlaw + MYTorus ) , is shown in Figure fig:NGC3079_spectra (bottom-right panel). Also for NGC3079 we can compare the main spectral results obtained from our analysis to those reported in recent literature works. In particular, the source is reported as heavily obscured in N_ H =3.20^ +0.54 _ 0.43 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 IG2019 , Marchesi2018 ( N_ H =2.47 0.23 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ), Masini2016 ( N_ H =2.5 0.3 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ), and in Brightman2015 , where the TORUS and SPHERE models provide a column density in the range (1.5-2.7) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , pointing towards a fully-covered picture for the obscuration in NGC3079.

sources.summary: The paper reports broad 6 GHz OH absorption in NGC 3079, with systemic and blueshifted components that may trace outflowing or rotating gas near the Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: The spectra of two of them, NGC 3079 and NGC 5793, are shown in Fig. 1. Absorption is detected towards the centre of the sources, with wide velocity components and narrow troughs at the systemic velocity of the two lines. In NGC 3079 (Fig 1, left panel), the absorption width is 800 km/s, with two deeper components corresponding to the two transitions (6031 MHz and 6035 MHz) at the systemic velocity. The maximum line opacity is = 0.05. Two further lines are also observed corresponding to the two transitions blueshifted relative to the systemic velocity by 100 km/s, indicating the presence of some outflowing gas.

Alternatively, these two troughs, symmetric around the 6035 MHz line, might suggest the presence of gas rotating around a central source. The VLBI jets in this source, a Seyfert 2 galaxy 16 Mpc away, extend to 1.5 pc from the central engine ; the 1.6 GHz absorption in this source has been found to come from nuclear region and in addition to the broad velocity width, suggest that the 6 GHz absorption is also likely to come from the central region, where the torus is expected to be.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 3079 with VLBI, interpreting nuclear radio-component decelerations and brightening as jet compression in a clumpy dense medium.

quotes.quote: We report the results from a six-year, multi-epoch very long baseline interferomertry monitoring of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 3079. We have observed NGC 3079 during eight epochs between 1999 and 2005 predominantly at 5 GHz, but covering the frequency range of 1.7 GHz to 22 GHz. Using our data and observations going back to 1985, we find that the separation of two of the three visible nuclear radio components underwent two decelerations. At the time of these decelerations, the flux density of one of the components increased by factors of five and two, respectively. We interpret these events as a radio jet component undergoing compression, possibly as a result of a collision with ISM material.

This interpretation strongly supports the existence of jets surrounded by a clumpy medium of dense clouds within the first few parsecs from the central engine in NGC 3079. Moreover, based on recently published simulations of jet interactions with clumpy media, this scenario is able to explain the nature of two additional regions of ageing synchrotron material detected at the lower frequencies as by-products of such interactions, and also the origin of the kpc-scale super bubble observed in NGC 3079 as the result of the spread of the momentum of the jets impeded from propagating freely.

sources.summary: The paper finds complex dense-gas profiles in NGC 3079, with saturated/self absorption, disk rotation near systemic velocity, and a blue-shifted absorption component interpreted as a dense nuclear outflow.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3079 the lines show complex profiles as a result of self-absorption and saturated continuum absorption. H ^ 13 CN reveals the continuum absorption profile, with a peak close to the galaxy's systemic velocity that traces disk rotation, and a second feature with a blue wing extending to 350 km s ^ 1 that most likely traces a nuclear outflow.

The spectrum of NGC 3079 is dominated by numerous absorption lines, which are characterised by a double peak profile. The component closest to systemic is caused by absorption in the approaching side of the inner disk, which lies in front of a radio continuum knot in the north-west jet. Based on the depth of the H ^ 13 CN (1-0) feature (since H ^ 12 CN (1-0) is both saturated and blended with emission), we estimate the hydrogen column density in the disk to be N_ H 6 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . The other absorption component, with a broad blue-shifted wing extending to 350 km s ^ 1 , suggests the existence of a dense nuclear outflow.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3079 as an AGN megamaser comparison, describing its 183 and 439 GHz water masers and denser central gas than NGC 4945.

quotes.quote: To date extragalactic mm/submm water maser emission has been detected for the 183 GHz transition towards NGC 3079 using the SMA Humphreys2005 , Arp 220 using the IRAM 30 m and APEX (Cernicharo2006; Galametz et al. 2016), and for the 321 GHz transition towards Circinus and NGC 4945 using ALMA (Hagiwara et al. 2013; Pesce et al. 2016; Hagiwara et al. 2016). There has also been a tentative, 5 detection of 439 GHz H _ 2 O water maser emission towards NGC 3079 using the James Clark Maxwell Telescope Humphreys2005 . NGC 3079, Circinus, and NGC 4945 display 22 GHz megamaser emission, and the mm/submm water maser emission towards these targets has been interpreted as also arising from the AGN central engine.

The 183 GHz maser emission detected towards NGC 3079 has a significantly lower isotropic luminosity and narrower velocity range of emission than the emission detected towards NGC 4945, even though both are attributed to arising from the AGN. There may be many reasons for the difference. However, in the case of NGC 3079, it appears that the strongest 22 GHz emission occurs for a velocity range where 183 GHz emission is weak or absent. From the results of Gray2016 , this may indicate that a significant proportion of the gas in the NGC 3079 central engine is too dense for strong 183 GHz emission to arise, while it can still produce strong 22 GHz emission Figure 2 Humphreys2005 i.e. n(H _ 2 ) > 10 ^ 10 cm ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3079 as the weakest CHANG-ES circular-polarization detection, with a radio-lobed AGN, maser emission, variable VLBI components, and no reliable Stokes-V spectral index.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 NGC 3079 D = 20.6 Mpc, wie15 has a unique radio structure in the form of two kpc-scale radio lobes on either side of the NW-SE major axis, the (Fig. fig:allgalaxies b, colour image), first detected by deb77 . The AGN in this galaxy has been detected in VLBI, showing linear features related to a jet, luminous H _2 O maser emission and a time variable component . X-ray observations support the presence of an obscured AGN cec02, iyo01 and mid07 have detected variably brightening components in VLBI due to jet-cloud interactions. Recently, sha15 have found a rich interplay between NGC 3079 and its nearby companions.
This is our weakest CP signal (Fig. fig:allgalaxies b) and only the central core is represented. We were unable to measure a reliable in-band spectral index in Stokes V .

sources.summary: NGC 3079 is presented as a Seyfert-starburst composite with polarized filamentary radio lobes, superwind evidence, and likely interplay between AGN jet activity and starburst winds.

quotes.quote: We present results from multi-frequency polarization-sensitive Very Large Array observations of the Seyfert-starburst composite galaxy NGC 3079. Our sensitive radio observations reveal a plethora of radio ``filaments'' comprising the radio lobes in this galaxy. We analyze the origin of these radio filaments in the context of existing Chandra X-ray and HST emission-line data.

NGC 3079 is a well known nearby ( z=0.003723 ) Seyfert 2 / LINER galaxy with a nuclear starburst, that is a member of an interacting galaxy pair. It is an edge-on spiral galaxy with several regions of star-formation along the disk. Its 8-shaped radio structure with a ring-like feature (``ring'' hereafter) inside its north-east lobe Duric88,irwin2003,wiegert2015,irwin2019 , its slow-moving ( v 0.1c ) parsec-scale radio jet irwinseaquist1988,baanirwin1995,Middelberg05 , copious amount of hot X-ray gas and emission-line filaments cecil2001,cecil2002 , have been well documented.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3079 as a starburst example with strong silicate absorption, AAB PAH classification, possible 8.6 micron extinction effects, and low relative 8.6 micron flux.

quotes.quote: However, some sources do present higher silicate absorption as exemplified by NGC 3079 (Fig. fig:splines ). In such cases, the 8.6 m band fitting could be compromised if an extinction correction is not applied.

On the other hand, the starburst galaxy NGC 3079 received the classification of ``A A B'' for the bands, respectively. Finally, the ULIRG + LINER source IRAS 03250+1606 has its three bands classified as ``B'' object. The 8.6 m band flux of NGC 3079 could be affected by the extinction that reduces the flux of the band. In comparison to the other galaxies shown if Fig. fig:gauss1 , this source presents the lowest relative flux intensity for the 8.6 m band and also the deepest silicate absorption at 9.7 m. Indeed, if we compare the relative intensities of the 8.6 m band of the four presented objects, we note that IRAS 03250+1606 and NGC 3079 possess lower values than the other two sources.

sources.summary: NGC 3079 is one of the Seyfert AGN compared to TXS 0506+056, with literature neutrino associations, hard-X-ray fluxes, and edge-on column-density corrections.

quotes.quote: These included two blazars (PKS 1502+106, 2019ATel12967....1T ; PKS 1424 41, 2016NatPh..12..807K ) and other two Seyfert AGN NGC 4151, NGC 3079; 2023arXiv230715349G,Neronov2023 . AGNs NGC 1068, NGC 3079 z=0.00399 2022ApJS..261....6K and NGC 4151 were indeed suggested to have comparable intrinsic hard X-ray (15--195 keV range) and all-flavor neutrino fluxes Neronov2023 . They found the observed hard X-ray fluxes as F_ 15-55,obs 2.29^ +0.16 _ 0.30 10^ 11 for NGC 1068 and F_ 15-55,obs 1.70^ +0.08 _ 0.29 10^ 11 for NGC 3079.

For NGC 3079, assuming an edge-on configuration, Marchesi2018 favors a value of 2.47 0.24 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 for N_H (along the line-of-sight), which is in agreement with the value N_H=3.2^ +0.54 _ 0.43 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 suggested by 2019A&A...621A..28G , also relying on observations. We combine the two results to get the range for the column density of the observer as N_H=(2.23:3.74) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . Using the above numbers for the observed hard X-ray fluxes and line-of-sight column densities, the intrinsic hard X-ray flux emerges as F_ 15-55,intr =(0.15 : 2.51) 10^ 8 for NGC 1068, F_ 15-55,intr =(1.26^ +1.20 _ 0.56 ) 10^ 10 for NGC 3079.

sources.summary: The paper reports millimeter and tentative submillimeter water maser emission from NGC 3079, tied to its nucleus, dust continuum, gas-to-dust ratio, and known 22 GHz maser velocity range.

quotes.quote: With peak S _ 2.5 Jy, 439 GHz emission is of comparable strength to typical values for peak 22 GHz emission towards NGC 3079 and occurs within the 22 GHz velocity interval. Millimeter Dust Emission The millimeter continuum of NGC 3079 is probably dominated by thermal emission from optically thin dust. As in the 1.2 mm observations of Braine1997 at 11 '' resolution, we find dust concentrated in the nuclear region of NGC 3079 (Fig. f:continuum ) and place an upper limit of 8 mJy (3 ) on emission associated with the radio lobes 1 kpc from the emission peak.

Using CO Nobeyama observations by Koda2002 , who find a gas mass of M_ gas 10 ^ 8.5 M _ within the central 150 pc, we estimate a gas-to-dust ratio of 150 , which is comparable to the Galactic value of 160 and to the ratios derived in bright local galaxies Dunne2000 . 183 GHz Emission The 183 GHz emission we detect in the nucleus of NGC 3079 is likely from a maser process. It arises from one, and possibly more, narrow unresolved features in the same range of velocity as that of known 22 GHz maser emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3079 as an observed starburst example whose soft X-ray spur spectrum is better fit by non-equilibrium outflow models, implying a substantial mass-loss rate.

quotes.quote: Fig. spectra (left) shows as an example the spectrum of the north eastern part (``spur 1'') of the starburst galaxy NGC 3079, observed with XMM-Newton. The fit (solid line) is the result of a dynamically and thermally self-consistent model as discussed by Breitschwerdt and Schmutzler (1999).

If the photon statistics is sufficiently high, XMM-Newton observations permit to identify emission line complexes, such as O vii and O viii and others, which in the cases of NGC 3079 and NGC 253 gives poor CIE fits of the whole spectra. Therefore the reduced ^2 is much better in Fig. spectra (left) for the NEI fit. Our best fit model implies a mass loss rate per unit area of 0.055 M _ /kpc ^2 /yr or 3.5 M _ /yr for the whole spur region.

sources.summary: The paper reports weak lines in NGC 3079, the largest CH3OH/HCN ratio, low inferred molecular-gas density, and the least dense gas among the modeled galaxies.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 and NGC 6240 did not show strong lines, so we spent additional time on these sources to lower the noise to better than 0.5 mK.

The spread in this ratio was relatively small, ranging from 3.3 in NGC 3079 to 7.8 in Maffei 2. The largest CH _3 OH/HCN ratio was measured in NGC 3079, and thus the chemistry in NGC 253, IC 342, Maffei 2 and NGC 3079 may all be strongly influenced by shocks.

Based on the line ratios, and ignoring any chemical differences, the data suggest that the molecular gas in Arp 220 is the densest and the molecular gas in NGC 3079 the least dense. Based on model comparisons shown in Figures 2 and 4, IC 342, Maffei 2 and NGC 3079 would be expected to have the lowest average gas density of 10 ^4 cm ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper turns NGC 3079 from a prior non-detection into a 1_10-1_11 detection and describes complex H2CO velocity components with distinct excitation behavior.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079: Reported as a nondetection in Mangum2008 , additional integration resulted in a detection of the 1_ 10 1_ 11 line (Figure fig:NGC2146M82M82SWNGC3079FormSpec ), consistent with the absorption line reported by 2.1 mJy Baan1986 . Perez2007 detect CO J= 1-0 and J= 2-1 , CN N= 1-0 and 2-1 , and HCN and HNC J= 1-0 and J= 3-2 emission from the nucleus of this galaxy. From the HCN J= 1-0 image presented by Kohno2000 Perez2007 derive a source size of 5^ 5^ . 450 m, 850 m Stevens2000 , and 1200 m continuum imaging reveals an unresolved core 20 in extent. NGC 3079 is one of two galaxies in our sample which shows complex absorption and emission structure in its H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3079 as a late-type galaxy with a 3-5 kpc UV scale height, asymmetric halo flux, and possible young stars above 10 kpc.

quotes.quote: These include the late-type galaxies NGC 5775, NGC 5907, and NGC 3079, and the early-type galaxies NGC 4594, NGC 5866, and NGC 3613. We find H 2.5-3.5 kpc for NGC 5775, H 2-3 kpc for NGC 5907, and H 3-5 kpc in NGC 3079. In NGC 5907 and NGC 3079, we measure more flux on one side of the galactic center than the other.

A similar transition is also seen in NGC 891 and NGC 5301, but not in NGC 3079 (or NGC 24 and NGC 4088, which are less inclined, so the halo extraction boxes slice through multiple heights). If the light is from younger stars, the scale heights of 3--5 kpc for NGC 3079, NGC 5775, and NGC 5907 indicate that there are some young stars over 10 kpc from the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a type 2 Seyfert with dust lanes, water maser, parsec-scale radio jets, diffuse soft emission, and uncertain Compton classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert . Broad lines were not detected in polarized light gu2002 . The HST image shows dust lanes (Appendix multiimages ). A water maser and parsec-scale jets were observed at radio frequencies with VLBI trotter1998 . The X-ray image in the 0.6-0.9 keV energy band shows strong diffuse emission, while a point-like source is detected in the 4.5-8.0 keV energy band (see Appendix multiimages ).

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3079's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079: We model the Spitzer 3.6mu band image of this pseudo bulge galaxy with four components using Imfit : a pseudo bulge (function Sersic ), a disk (function Exponential ), a bar (a second Sersic function) and a ring (function GaussianRing ). The results are shown in Fig. fig_ngc3079 and fig_imfitiso . The parameters of the fit are listed in Table tab:3079decomp . All four components are very flattened and have almost the same position angle. The small ( a_e=4.5'' ) pseudo bulge component contributes 26 of the total light of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3079 cautiously because map quality, its strong nuclear source, and nuclear/outflow contamination affect scale heights and spectral-index estimates.

quotes.quote: The L-band values for NGC 3079 may be underestimated because of the poor quality of the map (see Table sample ). In NGC 3079, the scale heights of the central strips have also been omitted in the averaging procedure because of the strong nuclear source.

As the determined spectral index value for NGC 3079 is strongly influenced by the nucleus and its outflow, we simply assumed a value of 1.0 for this galaxy. Remarkably, the ratios for all galaxies range between 1 and 2, except for the outliers NGC 3003 and NGC 3079, mentioned already at the beginning of this section. Nevertheless, from the frequency analysis the clearest candidates for having an escape-dominated halo with convective propagation are NGC 4157, NGC 4217, and NGC 4302; the others are in the range of a synchrotron energy loss-dominated or escape-dominated halo, both with diffusion, except NGC 3003 and NGC 3079.

sources.summary: The paper reports FUV and X-ray evidence for a 60 kpc biconical wind from NGC 3079, linking shocked, enriched gas to the galaxy's starburst and AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of a wind from NGC 3079 to at least 60 kpc from the galaxy. We detect the wind in FUV line emission to 60 kpc (as inferred from the broad FUV filter in ) and in X-rays to at least 30 kpc. The morphology, luminosities, temperatures, and densities indicate that the emission comes from shocked material, and the O/Fe ratio implies that the X-ray emitting gas is enriched by Type II supernovae. If so, the speed inferred from simple shock models is about 500 km s ^ 1 , which is sufficient to escape the galaxy.
NGC 3079 is a well known starburst and AGN galaxy with an extensive radio halo Irwin2003 , a nuclear H and X-ray bubble Cecil2001 , and diffuse nuclear hard X-rays JiangtaoLi2019 .

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-parsec VLBA imaging and radio SED results for NGC 3079, describing its central radio morphology and spectral shape in the FRAMEx AGN sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 [ 10 ^ h 01 ^ m 57 804, + 55 40 47 239 ] : NGC 3079 is one of the two sources in our sample with multicomponent (A and B; mentioned in trotter_1998ApJ...495..740T ) radio sources. In addition, a third component lying between A and B was tentatively identified in C and X bands, but its characteristics were not well constrained by the observation. Source A was detected in L, C, and X bands, whereas component B was seen only in C and X bands. Including non-detections upper limits (5 ), both components showed a GHz peaked spectrum and slightly resolved point-like structures. Component A showed a turnover peak at 4.46 GHz, and B showed a turnover peak at 5.60 GHz.

sources.summary: NGC 3079 is described as centrally active, with a biconical minor-axis wind, central absorption, nearby dwarf galaxies, extraplanar radio plumes, and a possible tidal-debris structure.

quotes.quote: Well known for its central activity, this galaxy produces a bi-conical wind along the minor axis, although of limited angular extent. The corresponding ``figure-eight'' structure seen in the radio continuum on smaller scales (Duric Seaquist ) is not resolved in our 22-cm image, but is apparent as a protusion East of the nucleus. The H i map exhibits strong central absorption and two dwarf galaxies towards the West and North-West, which were previously reported by Irwin et al. ( ). Away from the plane, there are plumes of radio continuum emission in the North-East and in the South-West, while in the H i line a linear structure is apparent, which was not visible in the lower-resolution VLA maps of Irwin et al. This feature is suggestive of tidal debris of a recent merger (cf. NGC 4631 below).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3079 has a warped disk, nuclear superbubble, extraplanar gas, and large-scale outflows extending from the disk rather than solely from the nucleus.

quotes.quote: Many dusty regions and strong H ii regions are visible in the R band image of NGC 3079 (Figure f:ngc3079 ). 13 Our image shows emission-line regions scattered throughout a warped disk, as well as a superbubble in the nuclear region. Both the superbubble e.g., vei94,cec01,cec02 and the fueling of active nucleus e.g., vei99b have been the subject of several previous studies. An enlargement of the inner central region of NGC 3079, tracing the superbubble and the outflow cone, is shown in Figure f:ngc3079z . 14 The fainter extraplanar gas and large-scale outflows emanating from the disk are shown in an enhanced contrast map (see Figure f:fourha ).

sources.summary: The paper excludes the barred galaxy NGC 3079 because its stellar velocity dispersion varies sharply and its boxy peanut bulge is affected by unmodelled bar streaming motions.

quotes.quote: Following G ultekin et al. \ (2009b), we exclude the barred galaxy NGC 3079. Although NGC 3079 has a well determined black hole mass (Kondratko et al. \ 2005), the observed stellar velocity dispersion drops rapidly from a central value of 150 km s ^ 1 to 60 km s ^ 1 or less within just 1 kpc (Shaw, Wilkinson, Carter 1993). The dynamics in this Sc galaxy's boxy peanut-shaped bulge are known to be affected by bar streaming motions (Merrifield Kuijken (1999; Veilleux, Bland-Hawthorn, Cecil 1999) which were not modeled by Shaw et al. \ (1993). Such additional motions are a general concern in barred galaxies, as detailed in Graham et al. \ (2011), and it is not clear what velocity dispersion should be used for the bulge component of this galaxy

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a starburst galaxy whose UV halo has FUV filaments, multiwavelength extraplanar emission, H I/UV filament coincidence, and height-dependent FUV-NUV color.

quotes.quote: Filaments are most easily visible in the FUV, and they are seen in most starburst galaxies (M82, NGC 253, NGC 3079, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, and NGC 5775), as well as two normal galaxies (NGC 4522, NGC 4330). The filaments in the latter two are associated with gas stripped by ram pressure in the Virgo cluster.

The starburst galaxies all have eDIG, soft X-ray halos, extraplanar thermal dust emission (except for NGC 3628), and radio halos. H 1 emission is also seen around all galaxies in the sample with high resolution maps, although in some cases the beamsize makes it unclear how much of the emission is truly extraplanar.

The UV and H 1 filaments do coincide near the disk in the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, NGC 5775, and NGC 3079 (not all shown in Figure figure.multiwave_sb ). The UV halo is also brighter and more vertically extended on the side of the galaxy with the large H 1 filament in NGC 891, and a similar phenomenon is seen in NGC 3044.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3079 as a Seyfert/starburst composite where only a minority of radio flux is wind-like, while VLBI reveals misaligned jet-like features and possible wide-angle outflow.

quotes.quote: We examined the starburst-wind contribution to the radio emission in NGC 6764 and another Seyfert + starburst composite galaxy, NGC 3079 see Irwin03 . We found that only about 25-30 of the total radio flux density appears to arise in clear equatorial emission that could be attributable to stellar winds, in both these sources. VLBI observations of NGC 3079 have revealed multiple misaligned jet-like features, which are not all consistently along one position angle with respect to the KSR; this lead to invoke a wide-angle parsec-scale outflow in NGC 3079 see also Mukherjee18 . In Mrk 6 and NGC 6764 and possibly NGC 3079, precessing jets launched from their black hole accretion disk systems, could be powering the KSRs and dissipating on sub-kpc or kpc-scales.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 3079 as a nearby type II Seyfert edge-on spiral with AGN and starburst activity, expanding its molecular-line inventory and finding HC3N poverty.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079, classified as a type II Seyfert galaxy 2001ApJS..136...61S , is an edge-on spiral galaxy with optical and radio nuclear activity at the distance of 16.1 Mpc 2015yCat..51500043T . The central region not only hosts a AGN, but also hosts a powerful starburst. Dense gas tracers (CN, HCO ^+ , and HNC 3-2 1-0 lines) of this source were reported by , which concluded that emissions of HNC and HCN emerge from the same gas and the ratios of HCN/HNC and CN/HCN favor a PDR scenario, rather than an XDR.

We detected 10 molecular species in NGC 3079, 11 molecular species in NGC 4258, 6 molecular species in NGC 6240, and 6 molecular species in VII Zw 31, which expanded the molecular line data set for these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a Seyfert with a central starburst and loop-like radio lobes, resolving the lobes into filaments shaped by jet, superwind, and magnetic-field effects.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 NGC 3079 is another Seyfert galaxy with a type 2 nucleus veron2006 that shows loop-like radio lobes. NGC 3079 also hosts a central starburst rendering the delineation of the role played by starburst or AGN in the observed radio morphology difficult. Our high-resolution image resolves the ``ring''-like lobes into filamentary structures. We carried out a detailed study of NGC 3079 and we conclude that the lobe morphology is a result of the central jet, the superwinds, and the galactic magnetic fields sebastian2019 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 3079 as one of two X-ray/Hα scale-height outliers, known for enormous Hα and X-ray filaments whose differing structures can bias scale-height comparisons.

quotes.quote: Although there are only six galaxies with reliable measurements in both H and X-ray, it seems that most galaxies tend to have an X-ray scale height smaller than the H scale height, except for two outliers: NGC 4388 and NGC 3079 (Fig. fig:hvshX ). These two galaxies are well known for their extremely large H and X-ray filaments extending to a few tens of kpc above the disk (e.g., Cecil01, Yoshida02, Iwasawa03, Strickland04a, Kenney08, Li19, HodgesKluck20 ). However, the H filaments always appear much thinner than the coherent X-ray filaments, because they are intrinsically denser and less volume filling, and also because the H images typically have higher angular resolutions than the X-ray images. Therefore, these large scale filaments may not contribute significantly in our H scale height measurements, but they could largely affect the X-ray scale height.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 3079 with NGC 4151 as an NGC 1068-like galaxy expected to be detectable by IceCube and showing a reported neutrino excess.

quotes.quote: There, a list of nearby galaxies similar to NGC 1068 was constructed and their expected neutrino luminosities were estimated. Taking into account these luminosity estimates and positions in the sky, it was found that the current IceCube's sensitivity should be sufficient to detect neutrino signal only from two more galaxies (besides NGC 1068 itself), NGC 4151 and NGC 3079. The authors analyzed a 10-year public catalog of IceCube events and found excesses of neutrinos from these sources with statistical significances of 3.0 and 3.9 , correspondingly. Note that, according to , both these sources also have a soft spectrum, though the values of power-law indices are not given in the paper.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as an edge-on Seyfert 2 with a nuclear starburst and wind, dominated in this sample by strong PAH features and low-ionization lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 is a Seyfert 2, but the AGN classification is not conspicuous in the optical and was found only through a targeted search of nearby galaxies for low luminosity AGN ho97 . This is an edge-on galaxy having a nuclear starburst with a supernovae-driven wind emerging out of the disk vei94 . This has by far the strongest PAH features of any object in the current sample and also has strong low-ionization emission lines. Because of the strong PAH features, the underlying continuum fit, free from any PAH contribution, is very uncertain shortward of 18 (panel A in Figure 8).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a starburst-dominated Seyfert 2 example with enhanced PAH emission, red-continuum behavior, and Fe II contamination affecting oxygen-line measurements.

quotes.quote: Apart from these variations, there are spectra that show enhanced contribution from star forming features like PAHs (Mrk 622 and NGC 3079).
Most Seyfert 1.8s and 1.9s are mid-way (in comparison of spectral shapes) between Seyfert 1 types (broken power law, NGC 4151 and NGC 2622) and starburst type spectra like NGC 3079.

There could be two measurement errors that can affect this correlation as pointed out by the referee: (1) the [ Ne II ] line fluxes have a contribution from adjacent 12.7 PAH in the low resolution measurements, and (2) the [ O IV ] line is blended with [ Fe II ] 25.99 , and hence starburst dominated systems will have a contribution from the [ Fe II ] line as in the case of NGC 3079 2005ApJ...633..706W .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3079 as an example where high-latitude water maser features indicate clouds rising above a rotating disk in an outflow.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3079, four maser features were found significantly out of the plane of the maser-traced disk yet their line-of-sight velocities reflect the velocity of the most proximate side of the disk. note that this can be explained if, as proposed by , maser clouds rise to high latitudes above the rotating structure while still maintaining, to some degree, the rotational velocity imprinted by the parent disk. Because the detected maser emission involves cloud-cloud amplification that requires precise alignment in both position and velocity along the line-of-sight, the discovery of four high-latitude maser features implies the existence of many more such clouds partaking in the outflow in this source.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as highly inclined, cold-dust dominated at long mid-infrared wavelengths, and starburst dominated with residual excess emission at 6.2 and 7.7 microns.

quotes.quote: Mrk 938 and NGC 3079 show very red long-wavelength continua in our sample, and both of these galaxies are highly inclined to our line of sight (see Table tab:objdata ), their continua are dominated by the emission from the cold dust.
This conclusion is supported by objects that show very red long-wavelength continua (NGC 3079 and Mrk 938).

On the other hand, in sources clearly dominated by starburst contribution such as Mrk 938 and NGC 3079, we find an excess of emission at 6.2 and 7.7 , after PAH bands at 11.3 and 17 are cleanly subtracted.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a Seyfert with jet components compressed by clumpy ISM and a kpc-scale superbubble, later noting that its bubble shape resembles the studied bubbles.

quotes.quote: Interferometric monitoring of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 3079 has found evidence of radio jet components undergoing compression by collision with the clumpy ISM, within a few pc of the central engine. This result supports the idea that the kpc-scale superbubble originates from the spread of the momentum of jets impeded from propagating freely.

The wide opening angle of the bubbles is not a problem in this case; the bubble shape is similar to that observed in NGC 3079, and the X-ray features observed by \ are similar to those observed in other nearby starburst galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3079 as a pseudobulge host and places it among galaxies that are outliers below the luminosity relation and displaced from inferred regressions.

quotes.quote: By means of the S e rsic index, we identify nine disk galaxies as hosting pseudobulges: Circinus, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368, NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594.

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.

These are CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079 and NGC3998; therefore there is a good overlap (4/6 where the exceptions are NGC3368 and NGC3998) with the deviant pseudobulges found in the previous sections.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a comparison megamaser galaxy with aligned molecular structures, a misaligned pc-scale jet, and diffuse radio emission aligned with the disk.

quotes.quote: However, in the megamaser galaxies NGC 1068, NGC 3079, Circinus, and NGC 2273, detailed gas observations do exist.

NGC 3079 contains a kpc-scale molecular disk that is aligned with the major axis of the galaxy, and then a 600-pc scale molecular oval that is aligned in PA both with the kpc-scale disk and the pc-scale masing disk kodaetal2002,kondratkoetal2005 .
In fact, the pc-scale jet in NGC 3079 is misaligned trotteretal1998 , while the diffuse radio emission on pc scales is aligned with the rotation axis of the disk duricseaquist1988 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as an edge-on spiral Seyfert with a nuclear megamaser, powerful outflow affecting NGC 3073, extincted continuum, and shocked molecular hydrogen.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 (S2) This edge-on spiral galaxy is known for its nuclear H _2 O megamaser Henkel84 . It also contains a powerful outflow that appears to be influencing its satellite galaxy, NGC 3073 Irwin87,Filippenko92 . The GNIRS spectrum of NGC 3079 (Fig.

sources.summary: For NGC 3079, the paper finds that mytorus-based intrinsic luminosity estimates give bolometric corrections that better match established luminosity and Eddington-ratio relationships.

quotes.quote: However, for NGC 424 and NGC 3079 the \ values given the \ estimate from the mytorus model provide better agreement.
For our analysis of \ henceforth, we use the \ estimate from the torus model with the exception of NGC 424 and NGC 3079 where we use the \ estimate from the mytorus model.
We found that for NGC 424 and NGC 3079, the \ estimate from the mytorus model gave a \ value that is in better agreement with the relationships.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3079 as a starburst transition toward AGN activity, with a central focused wind and bow-shock radio structure, plus evidence of high internal extinction.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 and NGC 3310 are starburst which can be considered as transition phases towards AGN (active galactic nucleus) activity. NGC 3079 exhibits a focused wind emerging from its centre and giving rise to a `figure-8' bow-shock structure seen in the nonthermal radio continuum 1983ApJ...273L..11D, 1988ApJ...326..574D .
Compared to the \ data they would require internal extinctions of 2 5 (for NGC 3079) and 1 0 (for NGC 4038) which seems rather low for these edge-on, highly extinguished galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports RadioAstron detections of NGC 3079 as an extragalactic megamaser, with projected baselines up to 2 Earth diameters and about 115 microarcsecond resolution.

quotes.quote: It was detected in a number of star-forming regions in the Galaxy and megamaser galaxies NGC 4258 and NGC 3079.
As a result, the signals from extragalactic masers NGC3079 and NGC 4258 were detected along with star-forming regions.
Projected baselines in these observing sessions were up to 2.0 ED (for NGC3079) and exceeding 25 ED (for NGC 4258) corresponding to angular resolutions of 115 as and 8 as, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports that structured magnetic fields suppress NGC 3079 flux in Prim2R, while NGC 3079 still helps populate HS3 for light and intermediate compositions.

quotes.quote: The Prim2R EGMF model suppresses the energy flux of NGC 5055, NGC 3628, NGC 3627, NGC 4631, M51, NGC 3556, NGC 3079, and Vir A.
HS3 is mostly populated by NGC 2903 and NGC 3079 independently of the EGMF model in the case of light and intermediate composition, as shown in figure fig:contribution_HS3 .

Beyond the previous results, we showed here that the Prim2R EGMF model suppresses the flux arriving at Earth from several possible sources: NGC 5055, NGC 3628, NGC 3627, NGC 4631, M51, NGC 3556, NGC 3079, and Vir A.

gmw_00000829.name: NGC 3245
gmw_00000829.overview: NGC 3245 is an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1108.3662, arXiv:astro-ph/0012213] classified as an intermediate LINER and transition object [arXiv:2001.02955, arXiv:astro-ph/0012213] that hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0012213]. Its structure features a clear disk without spiral arms [arXiv:1311.1530], a possible bar [arXiv:1108.3662, arXiv:1311.1530], and a satellite system containing at least 13 secure dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1808.03410].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3245 as a LINER needing a broad H component; one-component and three-component treatments differ but rotation may explain secondary kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3245 was classified as an intermediate case between a LINER and a transition object Ho1997 . HST /STIS data was analyzed in first place by Barth2001 , who studied the dynamics of the ionized gas in this galaxy. They suggested the possible presence of a broad component in H , which was modeled by C15 (see Sect. C15 ).

In our case, visual inspection indicates that only one narrow component is sufficient to fit the lines, although with a secondary component the ^ 2 of the fit improves approximately a 16 . We have prioritized the simplest model that is able to describe the observed spectrum (S-method; see Fig. Panel_NGC3245 ). In either case, a broad component in H was necessary to model the line profiles.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3245's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3245: We use the decomposition of Beifiori2012 in the i- band, which gives R_e=3.5'' (with B/T=0.27 ) and agrees with the 'classical fit' of Laesker2014 . The 'best fit' of Laesker2014 , which includes a bar, gives R_e=1.5'' . Since what Laesker2014 call a 'bar' could also be an 'oval' component, we stick to the decomposition of Beifiori2012 . We calibrate the fit to the R- band from the original SDSS i- band using the (R-I) color estimated as follows and the (i-R) conversion equation in Jordi2006 . Since only the (B-V) color was available in Hyperleda, we derived (R-I) by linearly fitting the (V-I) vs.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3245's outer profile as III-s and describes bar evidence, rings, antitruncation shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3245 (III-s; SA()0 ^0 ) : The ellipticity profile shows a peak at 15 (1.5 kpc), which coincides with a ring with inner and outer semiaxis lengths 12 and 1.9 , respectively. This ring appears diffusely distributed along the minor axis ( michard94 Michard Marchal 1994 call it a ``curious grey asymmetry'') so that it might perhaps be considered a bar, but this is not clear. The surface brightness profile shows an antitruncation with break point at 120 (12 kpc). The ellipticity decreases continuously, from 0.48 at 50 (5 kpc) to 0.2 at 160 (16 kpc), so we classify this as Type III-s.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC3245, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC3245 (Fig. fig:NGC3245 ) has a clear disk component but it lacks any obvious spiral structure. Besides the bulge and the exponential disk, the radial profile shows that the disk is partially truncated beyond 60 , followed by a low-surface-brightness extended disk (or halo) beyond 120 . A small inflection at 15 may suggest a bar.

sources.summary: The paper treats N3245 as a Milky-Way-mass HSC pilot target, finding 13 secure and 2 possible dwarfs and a satellite luminosity function more than twice as rich as the Milky Way's.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we focus on two of the observed targets, N2950 and N3245. Their physical properties are summarized in Table tab:phys_props .

In total, we identify 9 secure dwarfs and 4 possible dwarfs around N2950. N3245 has 13 secure and 2 possible dwarfs. These are raw counts from the observation and we apply the completeness correction described below when we discuss LFs in Section sec:luminosity_function .
On the other hand, N3245 has more dwarfs than the MW by more than a factor of two.

sources.summary: The paper models HST/STIS gas dynamics in NGC 3245, an S0 transition/LINER galaxy, and reports a massive dark object consistent with a central black hole.

quotes.quote: This paper presents an analysis of \ observations of NGC 3245, as part of a STIS program designed to study the dynamics of ionized gas in nearby, low-luminosity active galactic nuclei (AGNs). NGC 3245 is an S0 galaxy with a mildly active nucleus classified as a LINER/ H 2 ``transition'' type hfs97a . Ground-based spectra displayed by hfs95 show double-peaked profiles of \ and [ N 2 ] 6584, indicating spatially unresolved, rapid rotation near the nucleus. VLA observations reveal an unresolved nuclear radio source, with no apparent jet wh91 . NGC 3245 lies at a distance modulus of 31.60 0.20 (a distance of 20.9 Mpc), as determined by measurements of surface-brightness fluctuations ton01 .
We have detected a massive dark object, presumably a BH, in the nucleus of NGC 3245.

gmw_00000830.name: NGC 3414
gmw_00000830.overview: NGC 3414 is a peculiar lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:2201.05080] featuring a bulge [arXiv:1601.00974, arXiv:2201.05080] and a bottom-heavy central stellar initial mass function [arXiv:1611.09859]. Its neutral hydrogen and ionized gas kinematics are completely decoupled from the stars [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:2201.05080], likely forming an accreted polar disk or ring [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:1007.2059].

sources.summary: The paper frames NGC 3414 as a structurally ambiguous S0 whose gas kinematics are fully decoupled from stars, with central gas alignment favoring a polar-disk interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3414. A real structure of this galaxy remains a complete mystery up to now. It looks like a round homogeneous body (a spheroid? a face-on disk?) with an overlaid thin filament-like structure in the orientation of PA 20 . There were various interpretations of such a view: it might be a face-on S0 with a very strong bar bag,chitrejog , but there were also suggestions that it was a face-on S0 with a polar ring projected onto the center whitmore . In any case, we were not surprised that the gaseous kinematics in NGC 3414 is completely decoupled from the stellar one (Fig. fig_ls ).

In the very center, R<10 , the gas rotation plane starts to turn, and the kinematical major axis traced by the [ OIII ] 5007 emission-line velocity field aligns with the `bar' (Fig. fig_fpi ). In Fig. fig_pa we have plotted the ionized-gas kinematical major axis orientation by fat black squares (our Fabry-Perot data) and by pale squares (SAURON [ OIII ] 5007 velocity map); both data sets consistently indicate strong turn of the kinematical major axis by 90 , toward PA<50 at R<3 . This `polar' velocity structure is aligned with the morphological feature in PA 20 which is sometimes described as a bar and sometimes -- as a polar ring whitmore . Since the stellar photometric and gas kinematical major axes coincide at R<3 , the data are more consistent with the hypothesis of a polar disk; however this polar disk seen strictly edge-on lacks any gas at R>10 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3414 among three additional early-type galaxies selected for high Na/Fe, with shorter minor-axis spectra that still support a bottom-heavy central IMF trend.

quotes.quote: In addition to these galaxies we observed three others, with the slit oriented along the minor axis: NGC 3414, NGC 4552, and NGC 4564. These galaxies were selected because of their relatively high [ Na/Fe ] values in data we had obtained previously. These earlier, as yet largely unpublished, data did not cover the Na D doublet. Exposure times were shorter for these objects (although still substantially longer than in van Dokkum Conroy 2012), and we cannot measure their IMF gradients to the same distance as for the primary galaxies.

For all six galaxies, the central M/L ratio is much higher for a varying IMF than for a Kroupa (2001) IMF. That is, all galaxies prefer a bottom-heavy IMF that is steeper than that of the Milky Way at low masses. The IMF parameter falls off steeply, from =2-3 at R=0 to 1 at 0.3 R_ e .

sources.summary: NGC 3414 is categorized as a disk/ring system with an H I polar ring or disk, probably accreted, and used as a possible endpoint for NGC 2768.

quotes.quote: The final category (denoted D, for disc/ring) refers to galaxies where most of the \ is found in a fairly regularly rotating disc or ring. This category contains the galaxies NGC 2685, NGC 3032, NGC 3414, NGC 3489, NGC 4150 and NGC 4278, while also the only cluster galaxy detected (NGC 4262) falls in this category. In NGC 2685 and in NGC 4278 the \ disc is large, i.e. extending beyond the bright optical body, while in NGC 3032, NGC 3489 and in NGC 4150 the \ forms a small, inner gas disc.
In NGC 3414, the \ appears to form a polar ring or disc.
The disc in NGC 2685 is heavily warped while the disc in NGC 3414 is polar, hence it is likely that also in these galaxies the gas has been accreted.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3414's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3414: We performed an Imfit bulge (function Sersic ) plus bar (function Sersic GenEllipse ) plus disk (function Exponential ) decomposition on the SDSS i- band image of the galaxy (see Figs. fig_ngc3414 and fig_imfitiso ). The parameters are listed in Table tab:3414decomp . We converted the i- band zeropoint into a Cousins I- band using two equations in Jordi2006 and the (-i) SDSS color. The M/L of Cappellari2006 is scaled to the distance of Sani2011 . Our decomposition gives R_e=23'' and agrees with Sani2011 .

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 3414 as a peculiar S0 galaxy with Bubble H-alpha morphology, asymmetric nuclear emission, dust-distorted ionized gas, and gas rotation perpendicular to the stellar component.

quotes.quote: NGC 3414 (see Fig. Figure_AB2 ): This is a peculiar S0 galaxy, with a faint disc over a prominent bulge Koopmann2006 . In our images, the H morphology is classified as an Bubble . Our results show the nuclear emission with a non-symmetric shape that could be due to the presence of dust in the south-west direction, as previously reported. Specifically, the analysis in the R band with HST data done by suggest the presence of dust in the nucleus. This is also confirmed by , as they found centrally concentrated ionised gas distorted by the presence of dust.

sources.summary: The paper reports Arp 162 has a stable inner center, small A1 and A3, rising A2, low-to-rising A4, and nearly constant phase for m=2 and m=4.

quotes.quote: Arp 162: The centre co-ordinates x0, and y0 are constant in the inner 14". The values of A _1 and A _3 are extremely small. The amplitude A _2 starts off at about 0.2 between 6"-20" and then increases to 0.3 beyond that, while A _4 is low in the inner 20" and then increases. The phase angle is nearly constant throughout for the m=2 and 4 components.

gmw_00000831.name: NGC 3608
gmw_00000831.overview: NGC 3608 is a low-luminosity core elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612348, arXiv:1601.00974] that contains a counter-rotating, kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1601.02597, arXiv:1310.2607] and polar-rotating ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0612348]. This dust-poor galaxy [arXiv:1301.4621] is largely devoid of neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1007.2059] and hosts a central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0604487].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3608 as a low-luminosity elliptical with a counterrotating core, no circumnuclear stellar disk, polar ionized gas, and a magnesium-enriched triaxial core.

quotes.quote: Central structure of the neighbouring low-luminosity elliptical galaxy NGC 3608 is also very interesting: it has a counterrotating core. From their long-slit cross-section taken along the major axis of NGC 3608 js88 found that at approximately R=10 (1.1 kpc) the stellar rotation changes its sense; the maximum rotation velocity of the inner core, achieved at R 4 , was however rather low, of 15 km/s. car97 inspected high-resolution images obtained with the WFPC2/HST through two filters, F555W and F814W, for the sample of elliptical galaxies with kinematically decoupled cores. In NGC 3608 they have not found any evidence for a circumnuclear stellar disk presence.

In NGC 3608 the stellar rotation, though proceeding with the expected spin orientation, is too slow for the visible ellipticity of the magnesium-index isolines. If we attribute the Mgb-isoline ellipticity, 0.33, to a hypothetical circumnuclear disk, then the minimum v_ max / ratio appropriate to oblate spheroids or disks would be 0.7 ill , and we have 0.1 in the center of the galaxy. The ionized gas rotates in the polar plane with respect to the stellar rotation in the very center of the galaxy. So we conclude that the chemically decoupled cores in NGC 3607 and NGC 3608 look like small magnesium-enriched triaxial structures with some kinds of polar disks around them -- only gaseous one in NGC 3608 and stellar-gaseous one in NGC 3607.

sources.summary: The paper assigns NGC 3608 to class E, linking its slow-rotator behavior, old stellar population, and known kinematically decoupled core to gas-poor merger-driven growth.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC 3608, 4365 and 4473) have been assigned class E. The mass growth of class E galaxies is expected to be dominated by recent gas-poor major mergers, although minor mergers also contribute. They should contain predominately old stars with little, or no, younger stars. This class of slow rotators includes simulated galaxies that reveal kinematic twists, minor axis rotation, KDCs and the rare 2- galaxies.

Indeed, NGC 3608 and NGC 4365 are known to harbour KDCs, and NGC 4473 is one of only 4 of galaxies in the ATLAS ^ 3D sample to reveal a double peak in the 2D velocity dispersion map (i.e. a 2- galaxy). All three have old mass-weighted ages (McDermid et al. 2015).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3608 gains no improvement from a dark halo, with poor minima likely caused by a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion profile and possible NGC 3607 contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 3608 Including a dark halo does not improve on stellar-only fits. The minima -- which have very high values of _r^2 - either imply a very compact halo ( R 1 R_ e ) with F_ d >9 or an extended halo ( 500 R_ e ) with F_ d =1 . We tentatively blame this outcome on the centrally peaky _ los profile. If the S ersic profile describes the stellar mass distribution poorly, then the fitting routine compensates by seeking a high central dark density. coccato2009 remark that this galaxy had few PN detections, and possible contamination from NGC 3607 nearby. We omit NGC 3608 from further detailed examination.

sources.summary: NGC 3608 has nearby intergalactic H I clouds but no on-galaxy H I detection; its stars and ionized gas are centrally kinematically decoupled.

quotes.quote: NGC 3608 In the vicinity of NGC 3608 we detect a large \ structure, about 40 kpc in size and about 70 kpc from the galaxy. No \ was detected on NGC 3608 itself. A smaller \ cloud is also detected, closer to NGC 3608.

The stars and the ionised gas in NGC 3608 are kinematically decoupled, at least in the centre, where the stars show a regular rotation pattern. No CO was found in this galaxy in the survey of Combes07 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3608 as a dust-poor comparison galaxy, noting few visual dust features and lower measured contrast than NGC3081.

quotes.quote: In some cases (e.g., NGC3608), these galaxies have few dust features.

We measured the contrast parameters for two representative galaxies in the sample, NGC3081 and NGC3608. The IUM technique appears to work very well in detecting the dust clumps in NGC3081, whereas NGC3608 was largely devoid of dust clumps according to the visual inspection. For each of these galaxies, we measured the contrast values for three detected dust clumps, using two relatively large but smooth ``sky'' regions (Area 100---200 sq. pixels). The mean contrast, ( f_ dust = f , the average flux associated with the dust feature) measured for NGC3081 and NGC3608 equals 6 and 2 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper explains NGC 3608 as an old elliptical produced by a 1:1 merger, with simulated magnitude, SED, morphology, gas content, and passive state matching observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3608.       The merging of two triaxial collapsing systems with a mass ratio of 1:1 and a total mass slightly larger than that of NGC 3607 (Table table2 ) accounts for the global properties of this E. Its simulated M_B (Table table3 ) agrees well with observations (Table table1 ), as well as its SED and morphology (Figures figure1 and figure3 ). The spins of the systems are equal ( =0.06) and both are aligned with the shorter of their principal axes. They merged into a unique configuration about 12.5 Gyr ago. The SFR proceeded about 1.3 Gyr before the merger and contributes to about 2 of the total SFR.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3608's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3608: The galaxy is a core elliptical Rusli2013b . We calibrate the i- band SDSS image to the I- band using the (R-I) color from Hyperleda and the tranformation of Jordi2006 . This gives a more extended profile than the one derived by Rusli2013b , which comes from the g- band image. We transform the M/L_V of Rusli2013b to the I- band using the (V-I) color of Hyperleda and scaling it to the distance of KormendyHo2013 . We measure R_e=43'' , while Beifiori2012 obtain R_e=161.9'' .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3608 with NGC 3607, finding NGC 3608 has a higher dark-matter fraction within five effective radii and a denser dark-matter halo.

quotes.quote: Our study also includes the two most dominant members of the Leo II group (NGC 3607 and NGC 3608) with intriguing f_ DM measurements.

The next most luminous member of the group with M_ K =-23.78 , NGC 3608, however, has a higher f_ DM of 0.8 within 5  . Within 5  , NGC 3607 has an average dark matter density of _ DM 6.2 \ kpc^ 3 , the lowest in our sample, unlike NGC 3608 with a denser DM halo with _ DM 7.2 \ kpc^ 3 . This suggests that both galaxies have DM haloes that are structurally different, with implications for their assembly time, such that the galaxy with the denser DM halo assembled earlier Navarro_1996, Bullock_2001, Thomas_2009 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3608 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is an E2 galaxy in the Leo II group. It is known to have a counter-rotating core 1988ApJ...330L..87J in the central 0.27 , aligned with the photometric major axis and rotating at 20 2001MNRAS.326..473H,2004MNRAS.352..721E . From 1 galaxy starts to rotate at approximately along the photometric major axis 2016MNRAS.457..147F . The PN kinematics shows that the growing trend continues at larger radii, where the amplitude of rotation reaches 65 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3608 as a core-profile outlier in CIR relationships, noting its reported recent merger may have enhanced star formation and reduced its X-ray gas luminosity.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies (NGC3608 and NGC3115) appear to be a misfit in the core-coreless classification. NGC3608 ( CIR=1.21 ) has a definite core profile, and has been reported as a recent merger Pinkney2003 . The merger event could increase the star formation of the galaxy and affect the value of CIR The reduced L _ X,GAS value of this galaxy might also be connected to the recent star formation Boroson.et.al.2011 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a bright central source embedded in diffuse emission in NGC 3608, with limited radio constraints and a possible AGN contributing much of the X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3608 (L2, E2) has two point sources with the brightest one at the center, embedded in diffuse emission. The spectrum of the brightest source does not have enough counts to fit a variety of models to it but its luminosity was estimated to be L_ 0.5-10 keV = 8.7 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . There is no detection of this source at 5 GHz but only an upper limit of 0.5 mJy WH91 . We can then put a limit on the radio loudness: R_ X < -2.7 .

sources.summary: NGC 3608 is described as a slightly flattened low-luminosity elliptical with mild outer major-axis rotation and a central counter-rotating kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: This slightly flattened low luminosity elliptical galaxy (E1--2) shows only mild outer rotation along the major axis. It is one of the first known galaxies with a central counter-rotating kinematically decoupled core Jed:1988a . As such, it is one of the few non-regular rotators in the \ sample Krajnovic:2011jj .

gmw_00000832.name: NGC 4026
gmw_00000832.overview: NGC 4026 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy located in the Ursa Major group [arXiv:1907.07261]. It features a central ionized gas disk that is highly inclined relative to its stellar disk, while its outer neutral hydrogen gas corotates with the stars [arXiv:1907.07261].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4026 as an edge-on S0 in Ursa Major with neutral hydrogen in a disk and external filament, central highly inclined gas, and outer gas corotating with stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 4026. This edge-on S0 galaxy belongs to a very rich galaxy group, almost cluster, Ursa Major odenwald . The Ursa Major cluster lacks meantime any hot intergalactic medium so neutral-hydrogen streams survive easily within its volume. NGC 4026 has been studied in the frame of the ATLAS-3D survey atlas3d_1 , and a substantial amount of neutral hydrogen has been detected not only within its disk, but also as a narrow external filament connecting just to the galaxy center atlas3d_13 .

So we expected decoupled gaseous rotation in this galaxy. Indeed, in the very center the [ OIII ] 5007 demonstrates rapidly rotating gaseous disk strongly inclined to the stellar disk -- it is seen in our Fig. fig_fpi , as well as in the IFU SAURON emission-line data polarsau . However, beyond the central region, the ionized gas is strictly confined to the stellar disk, and its rotation follows the stellar component one exactly (Fig. fig_ls ).

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 4026's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 4026: We correct the M/L for G.E and scale it to the distance of KormendyHo2013 . The SDSS g- band image was calibrated to the V- band using the transformation of Jordi2006 and the mean (B-V) colors from Hyperleda. We performed an Imfit bulge (function Sersic ) plus disk (function EdgeOnDisk ) decomposition (see Figs. fig_ngc4026 and fig_imfitiso ). The best fit parameters are given in Table tab:4026decomp . We set b=0.5 in Table tab_data , since the galaxy is too edge-on to be certain about the presence or the absence of a bar.

gmw_00000833.name: NGC 4889
gmw_00000833.overview: NGC 4889 is a supergiant cD elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1612.08089, arXiv:1810.01424] and the brightest member of the Coma Cluster [arXiv:0906.0287, arXiv:1811.11794]. It is an undisputed slow rotator [arXiv:1308.6581] that hosts a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0906.0287, arXiv:0808.1521] and an exceptionally massive central black hole [arXiv:2202.04099].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4889 as a dominant Coma central galaxy whose velocity matches lower-velocity, higher-metallicity core gas and helps trace stripped or residual merger material.

quotes.quote: The Coma cluster , Abell 1656 , contains two dominant galaxies, which are NGC 4874 and NGC 4889 . The two galaxies have a large velocity difference of 700 km s ^ 1 between them FitchettWebster87 , likely caused by a major merger in the cluster.

A map of the gas velocity (Fig.  fig:coma_map bottom-left panel), shows that there is lower velocity material extending from NGC 4889 to the south-east and to the south-west of the cluster core, while the material to the north and east of the core better matches the NGC 4874 velocity. This material with velocities similar to NGC 4889 also has higher metallicity and lower temperature. An alternative origin for the high velocity gas is that it is material stripped from NGC 4911/4921 group, although the cool arms seen in the cluster core appear to be lower velocity material. The higher metallicity north-south filament might be stripped material or a residual core from a merging subcluster, possibly the one which contained NGC 4889 , considering the similarity in velocity.

These arms make a U-shaped structure, however, with NGC 4889 at the tip of one of the arms and the group at the bend. found that the galaxies within their cluster-core subcomponent have a velocity distribution peaking between the velocities of the two central galaxies. In contrast, found that the main component of the intracluster planetary nebulae within the cluster core have a peak velocity of 6500  km s ^ 1 ( z 0.022 ), close to that of NGC 4889 , rather than NGC 4874. Our velocity for the gas in the central region (regions 5 and 6) is similar to NGC 4889 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC4889 as a central Coma galaxy associated with a merging group and localized X-ray excess, while contrasting it with NGC4874 and the relaxed core.

quotes.quote: We unambiguously confirm the excess emission around the two central galaxies (NGC4874 and NGC 4889) and the tail of gas in the direction of NGC4911, revealed by the wavelet analysis of Vikhlinin ( ). However, contrary to Vikhlinin result, this filamentary structure does not seem to be directly connected to the Coma center, but originates 0.5 Mpc South of it.

If there is a consensus that a merging group is associated with NGC4889, the situation of NGC4874 is less clear (see in particular Colless Dunn ; Donnelly ). As already proposed by Colless Dunn ( ), our data suggest that NGC4874 is simply the central galaxy of the main Coma cluster, rather than being associated with a second subgroup in an early merging stage with the main cluster (as proposed by Donnelly ). Several facts support this picture. First the remarkably homogeneous temperature distribution within the central <10' region suggests the gas in that region is basically in a relaxed state. Second, there is no obvious evidence of a third peak in between NGC4889 and NGC4874, which would be associated with the `true' cluster center. Actually, apart from the excess around NGC4889, the X--ray morphology can be classified as an offset-center cluster morphology (variation of isophote centroid with scale).

sources.summary: Chandra resolves 4889 as one of Coma's dominant galaxies retaining a compact warm X-ray gas corona, with faint extended emission and measured soft X-ray flux.

quotes.quote: The total exposure was 48 ksec for 4874 and 56 ksec for 4889. The central part of the composite ACIS-I image is shown in Fig. fig:big . Notice the faint arcmin-scale X-ray enhancements around 4874 and 4889, previously detected by . At the center of these extended structures there are bright (500 counts) compact sources coincident with the optical positions of the galaxies.

The observed 0.5--2 keV flux is 3.9 0.1 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for 4874 \ and 3.2 0.1 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 for 4889, corresponding to luminosities in the same band of 9.1 10^ 40 and 7.6 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . The enlarged X-ray and optical images of 4874 and 4889 are shown in Fig. fig:panels . The X-ray sources associated with the galaxies are extended, but unlike most ellipticals, the X-ray emission is much more compact than the distribution of optical light.

sources.summary: The paper associates NGC 4889 with a nearby Coma subcluster whose elongated group, missing envelope, velocity offset, and disturbed state suggest it passed through the core earlier.

quotes.quote: There are some complications to this simple model due to the nearby presence of another subcluster associated with NGC 4889, 7 \ to the east from NGC 4874. Baier (1990) find that the group of galaxies associated with NGC 4889 is elongated along an axis from the southeast to the northwest, possibly indicating a recent dynamical/tidal interaction similar to that described by RLB97 as occurring after core passage. It is possible that the slightly high entropy in region 8 briefly mentioned above is related to this activity. The lack of an extended envelope around NGC 4889 also would be expected if the group has recently passed through the core.

As with the NGC 4874 group, CD96 and B96 find a statistically significant velocity offset for the NGC 4889. This suggests that both the NGC 4889 and NGC 4874 groups -- in that order-- may be recent arrivals in the core. We are thus seeing the debris of the final stages of one minor merger, just as another minor merger is occurring.

sources.summary: The paper classifies GMP2921 as an undisputed slow rotator in Coma, with no evidence of rotation and an unusually hot dispersion map; it is likely one of the central dominant galaxies.

quotes.quote: If we select SRs morphologically based on the absence of significant rotation we find four examples: GMP2921, GMP3329, GMP3792 and GMP2975. If we classify SRs as all galaxies with < , then there are also four examples in Fig. fig:lam-eps : GMP2921, GMP3329, GMP3792 and GMP3433.

GMP2921 and GMP3329 show no evidence of rotation and are undisputed SRs. Similarly GMP3792 and GMP2975 show no evidence of global rotation extending to large radii. All four of these morphological SRs have <0.1 .

The majority of the dispersion maps in Fig. fig:kina are blue (dynamically cold, <100 ), while one dispersion map stands out from all the others as being dynamically much hotter than the others; that of GMP2921. This galaxy is likely the most massive in the cluster and is one of the two central dominant galaxies. However, the other centrally dominant galaxy, GMP3329, has a very moderate dispersion map in comparison; indeed the dispersion map of the non-central galaxy GMP3792 is just as dynamically hot.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4889 as one of Coma's central cD galaxies, separating it from normal early types because of its stellar mass, intracluster-light evidence, and core-dominated structure.

quotes.quote: Objects assigned to class G2 (photometric ellipticals) have single S ersic index n > n_ disk max and include two known central cD galaxies, NGC 4874 and NGC 4889. We label these two sources separately as cD galaxies because they contain a disproportionately large fraction of the stellar mass.

Of the three cD galaxies in Coma, two (NGC 4874 and NGC 4889) lie in the projected central 0.5 Mpc and are therefore in our sample. The third cD (NGC 4839) lies is in the outer southwest region of Coma and is not part of this study. Definitive proof that NGC 4874 and NGC 4889 are cDs is the detection of intra-cluster light in Coma (Kormendy Bahcall 1974; Melnick et al. 1977; Thuan Kormendy 1977; Bernstein et al. 1995; Adami et al. 2005; Arnaboldi 2011). The single S ersic indices reported in Appendix decomp and Table 1cptab for the these cD galaxies are n 3-4.4 because the decompositions also include the central core. The central core is a clear deviation from the inward extrapolation of the S ersic profile that characterizes the outer galaxy structure.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4889 as a central Coma cD galaxy with archival HST imaging, using its globular-cluster luminosity function in the Coma distance comparison.

quotes.quote: For comparison, we studied two more Coma galaxies for which deep images are available in the archive: NGC 4874 and NGC 4889, two cD galaxies in the central region of Coma. We analyzed the HST/ACS F475W (g) and F814W (I) images of NGC 4874 and NGC 4889, in the archive (PIDs : 11711 and 10861). We combined the images of these galaxies with a pixfrac value of 1.0, and a pixel scale of 0 05 for F475W and 0 03 for F814W.

The total exposure times of NGC 4874 are 5,071 s for F475W and 11,825 s for F814W, and those of NGC 4889 are 4,770 s for F475W and 9,960 s for F814W. These exposure times are much shorter than those for the NGC 4921 images. However, the images of these elliptical galaxies suffer much less crowding than those of NGC 4921, which is an almost face-on spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 4889 is described as a dominant Coma supergiant, structurally closer to a normal elliptical, with about 16,000 globular clusters and a normal BCG-range specific frequency.

quotes.quote: The giants NGC 4874 and NGC 4889 are the dominant galaxies in the rich and well known Coma cluster (A1656), for which we adopt d = 100 Mpc, (m-M)_I = 35.02 , and E_ B-V = 0.01 . Reproductions of the NGC 4889 and NGC4874-F2 fields from the ACS/WFC camera are shown in Figures fig:ngc4889 and fig:ngc4874 .
By contrast NGC 4889 resembles a structurally more normal elliptical though with a supergiant-level luminosity.

For NGC 4889 the profile integration gives n = 8080 120 clusters brighter than F814W = 26.5 ( M_I = -8.53 ), a limiting magnitude which is very near the GCLF peak point. We therefore double that number to N_ tot 16000 250 , which when combined with M_V^T = -23.65 gives a specific frequency of S_N = 5.5 0.1 . This galaxy is very luminous, but relative to its size it does not have an exceptionally populous cluster system in the more typical BCG range.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4889 as the other central D galaxy in Coma, tied to an infalling disrupted subcluster and nearby blue intracluster-light clumps.

quotes.quote: A formation scenario that is currently contemplated for the Coma cluster is a merger of two sub-clusters or groups associated with the two D galaxies, NGC 4874 and NGC 4889, possibly after their second core crossing briel1992,watt1992,davis1993,colless1996,arnaud2001,neumann2003,adami2005b,gerhard2007 . Most dynamical and X-ray studies suggest that the group related to NGC 4874 could have been at the center of the main cluster potential in the past, being displaced out of the bottom of the potential well by the infall of the group associated with NGC 4889 e.g. davis1993,colless1996,gerhard2007 . Moreover, analyses of X-ray substrutures and radio emission indicate that this second subcluster is partially disrupted and have ejected NGC 4889 colless1996 .

We do see the absence of an emission peak centered on NGC 4889. Instead, the diffuse light is preferentially concentrated on two clumps, east and west of this giant galaxy, evident in all the filters although most intensely in the bluer narrow bands (marked in Fig. ICLcontours as B and C).
To our knowledge, the clump C, east of NGC 4889, was never reported.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4889 as a Coma BCG requiring HST-based dynamical modelling; its dark matter models differ strongly, and its fitted black-hole mass matches prior work.

quotes.quote: For the two BCGs, NGC 4874 and NGC 4889, additional steps were taken to ensure high-quality dynamical models can be created. For these dynamical models, we replace the MGE results taken from the SDSS photometric data with that from the higher-resolution HST data. These new models permit us to generate higher resolution dynamical models of the galaxies in the region covered by the IFU.

Next, we focus on the two BCGs in the cluster, NGC 4889 and NGC 4874, which are the two most massive galaxies in Fig. dm_frac . We observe that the dark matter fraction for NGC 4874 (the less massive of the two BCGs) is consistent for both sets of dynamical models, but for NGC 4889 we see a drastic difference between the models.

Importantly, our best-fitting BH mass of 2.0 10^ 10 M_ for NGC4889 is entirely consistent with the best-fitting value by McConneletal2011 and McConnelletal2012 for the same galaxy, with independent data and models.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4889 is a Coma brightest cluster galaxy with a significantly bottom-heavy core IMF, differing from NGC 4874 and possibly reflecting assembly history.

quotes.quote: The two Coma BCGs have similar velocity dispersion and metallicity, but we find the IMF of NGC 4874 is consistent with that of the Milky Way while NGC 4889 presents evidence for a significantly bottom-heavy IMF.

However, for NGC 4889 we find evidence for a bottom-heavy IMF, in contrast to Z17. The difference is unlikely to be due to aperture effects. As the spectral extraction is weighted toward pixels with the highest S/N, our effective aperture within the longslit is 0.7'' 4.12'' for NGC 4889 and 0.7'' 3.51'' for NGC 4874, corresponding to <0.07R_ e for NGC 4874 and <0.11R_ e for NGC 4889 (Table tab:sample ).

Our analysis therefore suggests that the cores of NGC 4874 and NGC 4889 indeed have different IMFs. This difference may be due to different assembly or merger histories, since the BCGs likely originate from separate clusters that have since merged Gu_2018 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4889 as a Coma supergiant/cD galaxy whose associated subcluster has likely fallen in from a filament and mostly dissolved.

quotes.quote: In combination with galaxy redshift and X-ray data this argues strongly that the cluster is currently in the midst of a subcluster merger, where the NGC 4874 subcluster core may still be self-bound, while the NGC 4889 subcluster core has probably dissolved. The NGC 4889 subcluster is likely to have fallen into Coma from the eastern A2199 filament, in a direction nearly in the plane of the sky, meeting the NGC 4874 subcluster arriving from the west. The two inner subcluster cores are presently beyond their first and second close passage, during which the elongated distribution of diffuse light has been created.

Optical and X-ray data have shown that the Coma cluster contains major substructures around the infalling giant galaxy NGC 4839 as well as around the central pair of supergiant galaxies NGC 4874 and NGC 4889 Fitchett87,Colless96,Neumann03 .
