In addition, a variety of smaller substructures have been identified in position-radial velocity data Colless96,Adami05b . These findings, combined with the modern understanding of structure formation in the hierarchical Universe, have lead to the view that the cluster continues to accrete groups from the surrounding large-scale structure, and possibly has recently had a larger accretion of a subcluster centered around NGC 4889 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4889 as the brightest Coma BCG with an extended envelope, HST imaging, a central KDC, and SSP parameters compared to literature values.

quotes.quote: NGC4889 A large BCG with a very extended envelope, and the brightest galaxy of the Coma cluster. The surface brightness profile was published by Peletier et al. \ (1990), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile clearly shows a KDC (Paper 1). Table Toorberg shows the SSP-equivalent parameters compared to that of the literature. Similarly to the other Coma BCGs, the ages fall in a very large range, and the value derived in this study is within this range.

sources.summary: The paper reports Subaru spectroscopy of Coma BCG NGC 4889, measuring halo absorption indices and inferring rapid central star formation followed by accretion-built halo growth.

quotes.quote: Long slit absorption line spectroscopy of the two BCG galaxies in the Coma cluster, NGC 4889 and NGC 4874, was performed at the Subaru 8 m telescope, with the FOCAS spectrograph. The integrated light spectra had adequate S/N so that absorption line indices for NGC 4889 could be determined out to 65 kpc. The results show that within 1.2 R_e the stars of NGC 4889 must have formed over a very short time scale ( < 1 Gyr), with little subsequent merging, as shown by the large enhancement and the measured steep metallicity gradient. The stars in the halo were formed on a longer time scale, as shown by the lower /Fe values. This is compatible with formation in smaller systems which were subsequently accreted onto the halo of the BCG.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4889 as part of a pair with reported ultramassive black holes that may be outliers and excludes it from final black-hole scaling fits.

quotes.quote: It is apparent that there is something unusual with the two ultramassive black holes reported for NGC 3842 and NGC 4889 by McConnell et al. \ (2011a). Figure Fig1 reveals that they may be are outliers biasing the M_ bh - relation defined by the remaining data. Performing a symmetrical linear regression on the core-S ersic galaxies, including NGC 3842 and NGC 4889, yields a slope of roughly 7.0 1 (see Table Tab3 ). Uncertain as to whether there is an error in the black hole masses for NGC 3842 and NGC 4889, or perhaps with their velocity dispersion measurements, or if instead these values are fine and these galaxies are representative (of a possibly new class) of galaxies which deviate from the M_ bh - relation as we know it, in the following section we have elected to repeat the M_ bh - regression analysis without these two galaxies. If these galaxies are outliers, then this additional approach provides a more robust statistical analysis of the M_ bh - relation.

sources.summary: Chandra data show NGC 4889 is a Coma brightest cluster galaxy with a small central X-ray cavity whose inferred AGN heating power exceeds its X-ray cooling losses.

quotes.quote: We use deep Chandra X-ray Observatory observations to examine the coronae of the two brightest cluster galaxies in the Coma cluster of galaxies, NGC 4874 and NGC 4889. We find that NGC 4889 hosts a central depression in X-ray surface brightness consistent with a cavity or pair of cavities of radius 0.6 kpc. If the central cavity is associated with an AGN outburst and contains relativistic material, its enthalpy should be around 5 10^ 55 . The implied heating power of this cavity would be around an order of magnitude larger than the energy lost by X-ray emission. It would be the smallest and youngest known cavity in a brightest cluster galaxy and the lack of over pressuring implies heating is still gentle.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4889 as a Coma BCG/cD galaxy anchoring one subcluster; ICL1 traces its massive dark halo and remains distinct from NGC 4874's subcluster.

quotes.quote: We present optical spectra of three ICL regions in the Coma cluster, located between 100-180 kpc from their nearest brightest cluster galaxies (BCGs): NGC 4889 and NGC 4874.

Furthermore, the core of the cluster comprises two giant cD galaxies, NGC 4889 and NGC 4874, and X–ray observations Adami2005 suggest that these galaxies are likely associated with two distinct substructures. The line-of-sight velocity distributions of intracluster planetary nebulae in the central region of Coma Gerhard2007 also suggest an ongoing merger of distinct substructures associated with NGC 4889 and NGC 4874.

This shows that stars in ICL1 are tracing a massive dark matter halo around NGC 4889, while stars in ICL2 and ICL3 are tracing a distinct halo around NGC 4874. Our results confirm that NGC 4889 and NGC 4874 not only originate in separate clusters, but that those clusters are still distinct at the present day.

sources.summary: The paper associates NGC 4889 with Coma globular-cluster overdensities, identifying it as Coma core’s brightest galaxy and the location of the cluster’s largest globular-cluster surface density.

quotes.quote: These new pointings are crucial, as they include NGC 4889 and IC 4051. NGC 4889 is one of the two giant ellipticals in the core of Coma and its brightest galaxy (see Figure acspointings and Table 1).

Similarly, NGC \ 4889 and NGC \ 4882 are enshrouded by one common subgroup of globular clusters. The globular cluster overdensity cospatial with NGC \ 4889 extends southwest of NGC \ 4889 into a region where no large galaxies are present. The largest surface density of globular clusters in Coma, 0.477 GC/arcsec ^2 , is spatially coincident with one of the two cD galaxies: NGC 4889.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4889 as the brightest Coma-cluster galaxy, a large cD with an extended envelope, and finds a clear kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: NGC4889: A large cD galaxy with a very extended envelope. This is the brightest galaxy of the Coma cluster. The surface brightness profile was published by Peletier et al. \ (1990), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile clearly shows a KDC.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4889 as one of the Coma core galaxies whose subcluster merger helps explain elongated diffuse light and intracluster-star kinematics.

quotes.quote: This section provides a short summary of the PNs kinematics in the Coma cluster core and the on-going merging of NGC 4889 and NGC 4874.

The NGC 4889 sub-cluster is likely to have fallen into Coma from the eastern A2199 filament, in a direction nearly in the plane of the sky, meeting the NGC 4874 sub-cluster arriving from the west. The two inner sub-cluster cores are presently beyond their first and second close passage, during which the elongated distribution of diffuse light has been created.

In the Coma cluster, the elongated morphology and kinematics of the ICL, the galaxy morphology and X-ray data are consistent with the merging of NGC 4889 and NGC 4874 along a binary orbit, the two galaxies being currently observed beyond their first and second close passage. the Hydra and Fornax clusters show evidence for un-mixed stellar components coming from accretion/tidal stripping events in their cores.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4889 as a Coma central galaxy example with a flat core, little expected hot-gas accretion, and a black hole likely shaped by quasar growth and SMBH merging.

quotes.quote: We want to illustrate this idea on the basis of NGC 4889 and NGC 4874, the central dominating galaxies in the Coma cluster. While NGC 4889 is indeed the brightest member, it has a nearly flat density core with a slope =0.03 and a break radius of R_ b = 970 pc 2007ApJ...664..226L . Due to the flatness of its innermost surface profile, we do not expect, on the basis of the results presented in this paper, a significant phase of hot gas accretion throughout its life. Its central black hole of = 6-37 10^ 9 2011Natur.480..215M was likely grown during a massive quasar phase whereas subsequent SMBH merging activity has later shaped its core.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4889 as a Coma-cluster galaxy whose supermassive black hole is much larger than expected from the M-sigma relation.

quotes.quote: The most compelling examples are NGC 4889 McConnell:2011 and NGC 1600 McConnell:2011,Thomas:2016 in the centres of the Coma and Leo clusters respectively. These have SMBH masses M = 2 10^ 10 and 1.7 10^ 10 . But using Equation msig even with the most favourable value f _g = 1/2 , their observed velocity dispersions = 350 km s^ 1 and 293 km s^ 1 give M_ = 7 10^9 , 3 10^9 respectively. For more likely values f_g 0.16 the predicted masses are 3.5 10^9 and 1.5 10^9 , an order of magnitude below the measured masses.

sources.summary: The paper compares the simulated Coma central galaxy analogue to NGC4889, finding lower-end stellar bulge and black-hole masses and noting the real galaxy hosts an exceptionally massive black hole.

quotes.quote: At the centre of the Coma cluster lies NGC4889, its brightest galaxy. The analogue for NGC4889 has a stellar bulge mass of M_ *,bulge = 8.6 10^ 11 and hosts a supermassive black hole of mass M_ BH = 5.3 10^ 9 . Compared to the compilation data from Sahu2019 (plotted in fig:K_band_lum ), with observed masses of M_ *,bulge = (0.9 - 1.7) 10^ 12 and M_ BH = (0.6 - 3.7) 10^ 10 , this puts the analogue right at the lower mass estimate for both the galaxy and the central black hole.

However, it is worth noting that NGC4889 hosts one of the most massive black holes ever discovered in the nearby Universe, lying far above the predicted black hole mass for the mass of the stellar bulge McConnell2011 , which would be challenging for any model of galaxy formation to reproduce exactly.

sources.summary: The paper relates NGC 4889 to the main Coma intracluster planetary-nebula velocity peak, noting that local Coma galaxy velocities and the ICPN histogram are centered on its systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: The main peak in the LOS velocity distribution of PN candidates is close to the systemic velocity of NGC 4889, v_ sys = 6495 kms ^ 1 . Figure fig:vel b shows the v_ LOS velocity distribution of the Coma galaxies in a 6' 6' field centred on the MSIS position. This has multiple peaks: the largest peak is coincident with the v_ sys =6495 kms ^ 1 of NGC 4889, and two less prominent peaks are at v_ LOS < 5000 kms ^ 1 and v_ LOS 8100 kms ^ 1 . The main peak of the ICPN velocity histogram coincides with that of the Coma galaxy velocities around the MSIS field, and it is centered on the systemic velocity of NGC 4889 at v_ sys =6495 kms ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper constrains NGC 4889 as a massive black-hole host, noting large mass-measurement uncertainties and requiring any PTA-band companion to have a small mass ratio.

quotes.quote: For the black holes with 5 10^9 M_ at the centers of NGC 1600 , NGC 4889, NGC 4486 (M87) and NGC 4649 (M60), any binary companion in orbit within the PTA frequency bands would have to have a mass ratio of a few percent or less .
This is particularly true for NGC 4889, whose mass measurement has the largest observational uncertainties.

Therefore, even if there is a very unequal-mass SMBH binary in NGC 1600, NGC 4889, NGC 4486, or NGC 4649, its orbit will likely be highly eccentric and render a detection improbable in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4889 velocity-dispersion profiles, comparing previous flat or falling measurements with MASSIVE data showing a substantial rise to at least 30 kpc.

quotes.quote: daviesillingworth1983 found NGC 4889 and NGC 6909 to also have flat dispersion profiles out to 10 kpc.

On the other hand, of the five galaxies with data in both this paper and loubseretal2008 (NGC 2832, NGC 3842, NGC 4839, NGC 4874, and NGC 4889), only NGC 3842 does not show a rising outer profile in our data.
In our sample, the situation is reversed, with NGC 4839 measured to less than 10 kpc while NGC 4874 and NGC 4889 are measured to at least 30 kpc and show a substantial rise in .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4889 as the Coma Cluster central galaxy with an unusually massive black hole and low atmospheric temperature, interpreted as evidence for earlier baryon lifting and quenching.

quotes.quote: For example, the most massive black hole in the Gaspari2019 sample resides in NGC 4889, the central galaxy of the Coma Cluster. Its atmospheric temperature ( T_ CGM 2.4 keV ) is considerably lower than the cluster's atmospheric temperature ( T_ halo 7.2 keV ), which indicates M_ halo 10^ 15 M_ . Presumably, the baryon-lifting event that quenched star formation in NGC 4889 predated the Coma Cluster's growth to such a large mass, explaining why its black hole's mass falls below the power-law M_ BH - M_ halo relation followed by lower-mass halos in the right panel of Figure fig:MBH-TCGM-Thalo .

gmw_00000834.name: IC 2574
gmw_00000834.overview: IC 2574 is a gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy located in the M81 group [arXiv:1204.1066]. It features a clumpy H I distribution with numerous H I holes [arXiv:1105.2572, arXiv:1204.1066], including a northeastern supergiant shell associated with active star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0610114, arXiv:1512.08820].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 2574 as a dwarf irregular comparison, finding clumpy H I, no coherent pattern, and no improvement from a radially dependent pattern-speed term.

quotes.quote: To explore this possibility, a test was performed by applying the solution method to Holmberg II and IC 2574. Both are dwarf irregular galaxies (RC3) that have no coherent or well organized patterns, and show material clumped around expanding H I bubbles (Puche et al. 1992; Walter Brinks 1999). Results for their pattern speeds would represent the averaged angular rotation of the individual clumps of material, and are therefore expected to differ somewhat from .

IC 2574 Figure 14 shows the centered and rotated specific intensity map of IC 2574 overlaid with contours of constant v_y . Like Figure 11, one can see the clumpy distribution of the H I and the lack of a coherent or well organized pattern.

Consistent results were obtained using different regions of implementation and larger spacings between different y_j . lccccc Statistics for IC 2574 [ 7.5 pt ] &&&& P & R^2 Equation& n & _ ^ 2 & F &( )&( ) [ 7.5 pt ] (2), (3)& 0 & 1.83 & & & [ 2 pt ] [ 7.5 pt ] (2)&1 & 2.82 & & & 7.0 [ 2 pt ] [ 7.5 pt ] (3) &1 & 1.92 & & & 1.2 The statistics for the results are shown in Table 9. For both forms of _p , the n = 1 term did not provide an improvement in _ ^ 2 . The solution when n = 0 is therefore adopted as the best estimate of _p .

sources.summary: The paper models IC 2574 as a core-dominated outlier, arguing that its extended dark-matter-dominated core is difficult for CDM but tractable with SIDM thermalization.

quotes.quote: We turn then our attention to two particular examples highlighted in Oman_2015 , UGC 5721 and IC 2574. These two galaxies have similar circular velocity measured at the outermost point of the rotation curve 80 , which probably indicate that they populate dark matter halos with similar mass, but the shapes of the inner regions are remarkably different: whereas UGC 5721 is consistent with a `cuspy' NFW profile, IC 2574 has a very extended core. This pair is an interesting case of the aforementioned diversity and we can use it as benchmark for our SIDM scenario.

For IC 2574 we use the rotation curve, gas and stellar densities from Oh_2011 . We multiply the stellar velocity contribution by 0.5 (mass-to-light ratio lower by a factor 4 ), so the stellar contribution lies between that in Sanders_2002 and Oh_2011 . For both cases our SIDM halo is run using a cross section for self-interactions of = 3 following Kamada2016 .

On the other hand, Fig.  fig:ICgal presents the rotation curve fit for IC 2574 with an extended and slowly rising core in its centre. The gas has a disturbed profile, but the baryons as a whole contribute little to the total density except within the innermost 0.5  kpc, and make a modest contribution at > 6  kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses IC2574 as a challenging dwarf-galaxy rotation-curve case and argues that a CDM model with baryonic physics can reproduce its observed curve and baryonic mass.

quotes.quote: Of particular interest, IC2574 can be mistaken as an outlier if the error bars, of 5-10 km/s , are not taken into account. However it lies in the outskirts of the distribution and we re-simulated it as we did previously for the clusters of our model to obtain an appropriately similar galaxy, within some percent, of the mass distributions of IC2574. Note that no further tuning was required.

As an example, in Fig. fig:RC , we show the RC of IC2574, the same reproduced by SIDM in . Similarly to the case of clusters, we run several simulations for this galaxy until the final halo and baryonic masses Also, here, we refer not only to the total halo and baryonic masses but also the observed radial density distribution (RC). differ by at most 10 from the observational data. The plot shows the observational RC (dots with error-bars), while the model prediction for the RC, the contribution to the RC given by gas, stars, and the total baryonic mass, are represented by the grey continuous line, red dashed line, green dotted line, and blue continuous line, respectively. As can be seen here, the RC of IC2574 (advocated to be problematic for the scenario of core formation ) is very well described by the simulated galaxy, as well as the baryonic mass, as seen comparing with Blais-Ouellette2001 or with the SPARC mass models . We want to stress that the differences between the IC2574 RC's baryon contribution (gas, stars) in from that of SPARC or Blais-Ouellette2001 arise from the different DM properties.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 2574 as a slowly rising rotation-curve outlier whose H I asymmetries, holes, nearby H I complex, and possible merger features may complicate mass-profile inference.

quotes.quote: IC 2574 and UGC 5721 are frequently referenced in the literature as examples of the two extremes of the diversity problem: IC 2574 has a slowly-rising rotation curve with a roughly linear slope out to 12 kpc, while UGC 5721 has a very steep rise in circular velocity within the inner few kpc.

In Figure fig:rc_IC 2574 we plot the RC of IC 2574 against the RC reconstructed from the perfect centrifugal moments of FIRE-2 galaxy m11e. We choose this FIRE-2 galaxy for comparison due to its RC reconstruction's similarity in linear slope and overall magnitude to IC 2574. conduct a detailed analysis of the HI distribution and kinematics of IC 2574 and its nearby HI complex, HIJASS J1021+68, and find that this galaxy is highly asymmetric in both its HI density distribution and its first and second kinematic moments. Moreover, the HI distribution in IC 2574 is notable for containing multiple HI holes 2007ApJ...661..102W .

Regardless, IC 2574 and HIJASS J1021+68 are clearly a system with a significant degree of non-equilibrium behavior. That m11e-2, a simulated galaxy with similar morphological features, has a strikingly similar reconstructed RC suggests that these non-equilibrium effects are likely essential to characterizing the circular velocity and underlying dark matter profile for IC 2574. These features may render IC 2574 a poor choice of galaxy to characterize the diversity problem, and possibly provide evidence for a merger.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 2574 as an extreme slowly rising rotation-curve outlier whose large implied dark-matter core can be reconciled with NIHAO feedback simulations.

quotes.quote: Galaxies IC 2574 and UGC 5750 have been extensively studied elsewhere in the context of \ e.g., Oman15,Oman16,Creasey17 : their RCs are so slowly-rising that their implied DM core is even larger than that predicted by other hydro-simulations that form cores. While issues about non-circular motions have been raised by and for the case of IC 2574, such concerns do not apply to UGC 5750. Therefore, it has been argued that these galaxies pose a problem to the core-formation scenario Oman15 . We show here that this is not the case.

In Fig. fig:coreRC we closely compare the observed RC of IC 2574 and UGC 5750 with that of their most-similar NIHAO counterpart (g9.59e10 and g1.59e10, respectively). SPARC data is presented with error bars. For NIHAO galaxies we show the circular velocities from the DM-only run V_ circ DMO , the spherical circular velocities V_ circ-spherical (as in top left panel of Fig. fig:v2vrlast ), and the true circular velocties computed from the gradient of the gravitational potential at the disc plane V_ circ-potential (as in bottom left panel of Fig. fig:v2vrlast ). Although IC 2574 and UGC 5750 present a higher than 3 difference with respect to NIHAO galaxies on the V_ 2kpc V_ Rlast plane when using V_ circ-spherical , the use of the true circular velocity that takes into account the precise mass distribution of the galaxy provides a remarkable agreement with observational data, proving that when simulations include effective feedback, dwarf galaxies IC 2574 and UGC 5750 can be reconciled with predictions based on the \ model.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 2574 as a gas-dominated dwarf with a large core, finding an excellent RAR fit after distance and inclination adjustments but a low disk mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: Figure IC2574 illustrates the MCMC fit of a gas-dominated dwarf galaxy (IC 2574). This object is often considered a problematic case for CDM because it has a large core extending over 8 kpc e.g., Oman2015 . Moreover, Navarro2017 claimed that this galaxy strongly deviates from the RAR (their Figure 3). We find an excellent fit for IC 2574 after adjusting its distance and inclination by 1 _D and 1.5 _i , respectively. The adjusted mass-to-light ratio is rather low ( _ disk =0.07 M _ /L _ ), perhaps uncomfortably so.

This object is also present in the THINGS database deBlok2008 , where the rotation curve is consistent with but slightly higher than that adopted here. If we apply the same MCMC technique to the THINGS data, we find a good fit with _ = 0.25 M _ /L _ , illustrating the sensitivity of this parameter to even small changes in the rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper maps gas between IC 2574 and the M81 system, finding velocity continuity and H I clouds that suggest IC 2574 may have interacted with the group.

quotes.quote: Located at a projected distance of 193 kpc from the centre of M81 group, the galaxy IC 2574 has a systemic velocity coinciding with that of the M81 system, which suggests a possible kinematical association with the system. Moreover, the existence of an ``optically dark'' galaxy -- HIJASS J1021+68 -- located west of IC 2574 has been reported in the recent blind \ HIJASS survey \ Jodrell All-Sky Survey, Boyce2001 . This hints at the existence of more low density clouds in the space between the M81 system and IC 2574, and a possible \ connection of the two entities through discrete clouds.

There seems to be a velocity continuity between the western side of IC 2574, the gas cloud HIJASS J1021+68, and the region of the system containing NGC 3077 and the eastern bridge of the system. This, together with the fact that there exists a peak of an apparent cloud in the space between HIJASS J1021+68 and the NGC 3077, may suggest that IC 2574 is not completely isolated from the system.

Indeed, multi-wavelength studies of IC 2574 have revealed that the galaxy has undergone two significant starburst events in the past 300 Myr Pasquali2008,Weisz2009 . Although these starbursts were attributed to stellar feedback in the galaxy, there is a possibility that its recent interaction with the system may have somehow contributed to enhance its star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper maps IC 2574 in H I, finding an extended rotating gas envelope and deriving an extended rotation curve used to constrain its dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: We analyse the eastern region of a 5 5 deep \ survey of the M81 group containing the dwarf galaxy IC 2574 and the \ complex HIJASS J1021+68, located between the dwarf and the M81 system. The data show that IC 2574 has an extended \ envelope that connects to HIJASS J1021+68 in the form of a collection of small clouds, but no evident connection has been found between IC 2574 and the central members of the M81 group. We argue, based on the morphology of the clouds forming HIJASS J1021+68 and its velocity distribution, that the complex is not a dark galaxy as previously suggested, but is instead a complex of clouds either stripped from, or falling onto the primordial \ envelope of IC 2574. We also use the deep \ observations to map the extended \ envelope around IC 2574 and, using a 3D tilted-ring model, we derive the rotation curve of the galaxy to a larger extent than has been done before. Combining the obtained rotation curve to higher resolution curves from the literature, we constrain the galaxy's dark matter halo parameters.

Furthermore, the velocity distribution in the envelope ( fig:ic2574_ext_vel ) suggests that most of the low density gas, especially the southern and western concentrations, is broadly rotating with the galaxy. The extension to the south is more or less aligned with the minor axis of the galaxy, and the gas located in that region is at systemic velocities; the gas along the western edge sits on the approaching side of the galaxy, although there appears to be a gradient in its velocity distribution. This further supports the idea that the low density gas detected around IC 2574 forms an extended envelope around the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 2574 as an isolated M81-group dwarf with 23 GC candidates, a corrected GC population of 27, measured halo mass, and unusually rich globular-cluster content.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of 23 globular cluster (GC) candidates around the relatively isolated dwarf galaxy IC 2574 within the Messier 81 (M81) group, at a distance of 3.86 Mpc. We use observations from the HST Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) to analyse the imaging in the F814W and F555W broadband filters. Our GC candidates have luminosities ranging from 5.9 M_V 10.4 and half-light radii of 1.4 r_h 11.5 pc. We find the total number of GCs ( N_ GC )=27 5 after applying completeness corrections, which implies a specific frequency of S_N = 4.0 0.8 , consistent with expectations based on its luminosity.

We make an independent measurement of the halo mass of IC 2574 from its kinematic data, which is rare for low mass galaxies, and find log M_ 200 = 10.93 0.08 . We place the galaxy on the well-known GC system mass-halo mass relation and find that it agrees well with the observed near-linear relation. IC 2574 has a rich GC population for a dwarf galaxy, which includes an unusually bright Cen-like GC, making it an exciting nearby laboratory for probing the peculiar efficiency of forming massive GCs in dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 2574 with UVIT, finding 782 young FUV clumps, larger 26-150 pc sizes, stronger hierarchical splitting than WLM, elongated clumps, and no preferred clump orientation.

quotes.quote: We present an ultra-violet study of two nearby dwarf irregular galaxies WLM and IC 2574, using the Far-UV and Near-UV data from the Ultra-Violet Imaging Telescope (UVIT). We used the F148W band Far-UV images and identified 180 and 782 young star-forming clumps in WLM and IC 2574, respectively. The identified clumps have sizes between 7 - 30 pc in WLM and 26 - 150 pc in IC 2574.

We noticed more prominent hierarchical splitting in the structure of star-forming regions at different flux levels in IC 2574 than WLM. We found that the majority of the clumps have elongated shapes in the sky plane with ellipticity ( ) greater than 0.6 in both the galaxies. The major axis of the identified clumps is found to show no specific trend of orientation in IC 2574, whereas in WLM the majority are aligned along south-west to north-east direction.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for IC 2574, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: IC 2574 IC 2574 (Fig. fig:ic-2574 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] IC 2574 has neither a central radio continuum source, nor a clear nuclear source in the 3.6 m IRAC image. The dynamics of this galaxy show clear evidence of random non-circular motions, showing themselves as kinks in the iso-velocity contours. For the center determination, we make use of a velocity field where most non-circular features were removed as presented in se-heon . We make a \ run with all parameters free, using the center position derived by as an initial center estimate.

sources.summary: The BEC model nearly reproduces IC 2574’s rotation curve, better than the NFW fit, while the paper notes possible non-circular motions and the highest rotation velocity in the sample.

quotes.quote: IC 2574: For this galaxy the BEC model gives an almost perfect prediction for the rotation curve. The NFW velocity also gives a good fit. However, is is not as good as the BEC model prediction. The galaxy may be affected by non-circular motions , but the rotation velocity, with a maximum of around 80 km/s is the highest in the sample. The galaxy is also the most massive in the considered sample, with M=1.462 10^ 10 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS galaxy in Group B, where BDM fits outperform NFW, imply r_c roughly comparable to r_s, and favor a strong dark-matter core.

quotes.quote: In the second case, Group B, (IC 2574 (B1), NGC 2366 (B2), NGC 2903 (B3), NGC 2976 (B4), NGC 3198 (B5), NGC 3521 (B6), and NGC 925 (B7)), we get a fitted value of r_c r_s and BDM profile fits better than NFW. In fact, the NFW profile in some cases over-predicts the velocity in the inner parts of the galaxy or does fit with unphysical values for r_s > O (10^4) or _0 < O (10^3) M_ /kpc^ 3 , see Table tab:onlydm_bdm and tab:onlydm_nfw . The BDM profile gives r_c r_s for all stellar mass models, having an average r_c value of 6 and 5 kpc for Min.Disk and Min.Disk+Gas, respectively. Since r_c r_s , implying that our BDM profile is of the type (r_c + )^ 3 and hence having a strong core behavior. For this set of galaxies the Burkert and ISO profiles, as well as the BDM, fit much better than NFW, indicating that a core is needed.

sources.summary: The paper fits IC2574 with an SIDM rotation-curve model and finds it among galaxies preferring the largest cross sections unless halo concentration scatter is allowed.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show the SIDM fit to the rotation curve of IC2574 in Fig. fig:density ( Bottom ). The inferred values of v /m for the galaxies, shown in Fig. fig:sigmav , evidently prefer a larger /m than the cluster measurement. Fitting all twelve galaxies with a constant cross section, we find /m = 1.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.6 cm^2/g .

If we fix the _s r_s relation to its median in CDM cosmology in our analysis, the galaxies UGC 5750 and IC 2574 prefer the largest cross sections, /m 10 cm^2/g , while M81 dwB prefers the smallest cross sections, /m 0.1 cm^2/g . However, if UGC 5750 and IC 2574 halos are 2 less concentrated and M81 dwB halo 2 more concentrated than the median halo, the inferred /m become consistent, within errors, with 1 cm^2/g .

sources.summary: The paper says IC 2574 has overpredicted outer MOND velocities, while adding an external field sharply improves the fit and points to NGC 3031 as the main nearby source.

quotes.quote: (2) IC 2574 : With luminosity about L_V=8.45 10^ 8 L_ ic-lu and position of ( 10^h28^m23.5^s, 68^d24^m44^s ) in the sky, IC 2574 is located at the distance of about 3.56 Mpc from us with the minimum and maximum distance estimation of 2.25 and 5.37 Mpc, respectively ic-dm, ic-md . The Milgromian dynamics curve predicts significantly higher velocities in the outer regions of this galaxy. Taking the EFE into account, our results show a good improvement in the Milgromian dynamics fits such that the reduced ^2 decreases from about 3.9 to 1.1. The best fit is obtained with an external acceleration of a_e=0.330^ +0.090 _ 0.075 a_0 . NGC 3031 is the most important source that we find near IC 2574.

sources.summary: The paper models IC 2574 with falling H I velocity dispersion and finds a break in its exponential gas-scale-height profile tied to the disk surface-density distribution.

quotes.quote: In addition to assuming a constant value, we use a smoothly falling velocity dispersion to model those two galaxies of our sample, Ho II and IC 2574, for which this more complete velocity dispersion information is available tam09 .

The curves for DDO 154 and IC 2574 display a break, necessitating two different slopes across two radial regimes, i.e., the gas scale height increases exponentially with radius with different scale lengths across the two regimes. It is interesting to compare this figure with Figure fig4 which shows the disk surface density of gas and stars combined versus radius. Knees in the graphs of DDO 154 and IC 2574 occur at the same radius as the breaks in the exponential curves in Fig. fig3 , confirming that the gas scale height depends strongly on the radial disk surface density distribution.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2574 as a nearby low-metallicity, gas-rich dwarf irregular in the M81 group, hosting numerous H I holes and a well-studied star-forming supergiant shell.

quotes.quote: IC 2574 is a low metallicity miller96,masegosa91,croxall09 , gas-rich, dwarf irregular galaxy located 4.0 Mpc distant 1 = 19 pc karachentsev02 in the M81 group of galaxies. The galaxy hosts numerous \ holes and shells walter98,walter99 , one of which is located in the most prominent region of current and recent star-formation and is near the outskirts of the galaxy. This 1000 500 pc supergiant shell has been studied intensively at UV stewart00 , optical pasquali08,weisz09 , mid-infrared cannon05 , and radio wavelengths walter98,walter99 . Recent analysis of resolved stars imaged with \ weisz09 shows that the most significant episodes of star formation began nearly 100 Myr ago and isolated bursts along the shell periphery are as young as 10 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 2574 as a dwarf irregular with a northeastern supergiant H I shell surrounding a star-forming hole and recent triggered star formation along its walls.

quotes.quote: The well known ``supergiant shell'' (  35 in the list of walter99 ) in the dwarf Irr galaxy IC 2574 is located at the north-east outskirts of its disc. This SGS surrounding a 1000 500 pc hole in the most prominent region of current and recent star-formation activity in the galaxy represents the impressive example of giant HI shells with triggered SF along rims (see Fig.  fig:ic2574 ). The analysis of resolved stars based on the HST data weisz09b shows that the last most significant episodes of star formation in SGS began 100 Myr ago and the recent bursts of star formation along the walls of the shell are as young as 10 Myr. The ages of the younger star-formation events are consistent with those derived from broadband photometry and are younger than the estimated kinematic age of the HI SGS, 14 3  Myr walter98, walter99, ic2574 .

sources.summary: IC 2574 is used as a baryon-dominated, large-core rotation-curve example; NIHAO can approach its curve shape but not its inner baryon importance.

quotes.quote: An example (IC 2574) is shown in the bottom-right panel of Fig. FigRCDiv .

In other words, NIHAO galaxies may come close to matching the shape of the rotation curves of UGC05750 and IC2574, but are unable to reproduce, simultaneously, the importance of baryons in their inner regions.

This illustrates the effect on the inner dark matter profile of two rather different baryonic distributions; that of NGC 1075 (left) and that of IC 2574 (right), when added to two randomly drawn SIDM halos with maximum velocities matching the observed values of each of these galaxies. The solid black lines indicate the dark-matter-only SIDM profile; the dashed black lines are the resulting profiles after the baryons have been added.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2574 as an M81-group galaxy surrounded by H I clouds, poorly fit by the axisymmetric model, with larger inclination-driven uncertainties.

quotes.quote: IC 2574 This galaxy is a member of the M81 group and is surrounded by a number of substantial HI clouds So20 . It is not well fitted by our axisymmetric model Figure  fig.IC2574 . Some of the velocity residuals are large, but there is no tell-tale pattern in them to suggest a non-axisymmetry in the flow and allowing a bar/oval component to the flow made only a marginal improvement. Our best fit inclination and systemic velocity (Table  tab.summary ) are in good agreement with the values obtained by So20 from their direct fits to their low resolution 3D data cube, but both differ significantly from the values given in table 2 of dB08, which are i=53.3^ and 53.1 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: IC 2574 is described as a gas-dominated dwarf spiral whose MOND-RAR and dark matter fits require adjustments or struggle, while RCFM fits at the reported distance.

quotes.quote: IC 2574 IC 2574 is a dwarf spiral galaxy, dominated by gas. MOND-RAR finds a good fit to this galaxy but adjusts the distance and inclination by 1 to 1.5 . This galaxy is problematic for dark matter model fits, as the model overestimates the inner RC out to 10 kpc 2017MNRAS.471.1841N . RCFM fits this galaxy successfully, at the reported tip of the red giant branch distance of 3.91 Mpc, Fig. fig:CompareIC2574 .

sources.summary: The paper reports no convincing diffuse stellar-disk radio continuum for IC 2574, but finds northeastern star-forming knots around an H i hole associated with a hot X-ray bubble.

quotes.quote: Diffuse radio continuum emission from the stellar disk is not detected convincingly. Brighter knots in the North-East are associated with regions of massive star formation in the galaxy. They embrace the small, but pronounced, H i hole in this galaxy adjoining the region of highest column density. This it is one of the few documented cases of an H i hole associated with a classical hot (X-ray emitting) bubble (Walter et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper notes IC 2574 lacks CO emission, has SFR scale height coincident with HI, and uses a lower declining velocity-dispersion profile than the authors derive.

quotes.quote: However, 2017Iorio showed that its contribution is negligible in the case of DDO 154, which is the least massive galaxy in our sample. , IC 2574, and NGC 7793 were not included in 2016Frank sample as no CO emission was detected, for these we used the mass models reported in .
Clearly, in the case of DDO 154, IC 2574, and NGC 7793, h_ SFR (R) coincides with h_ HI as CO emission is not detected.
For IC 2574, the declining radial profile of the velocity dispersion assumed by is 20 lower everywhere than that shown in Fig. fig:vdisp_fits .

gmw_00000835.name: NGC 2366
gmw_00000835.overview: NGC 2366 is a barred Magellanic-type irregular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0811.2724] located in the M81 group [arXiv:0811.2724, arXiv:astro-ph/0204192]. It features a gas-rich, regularly rotating disk [arXiv:1711.09112, arXiv:1404.6252] that exhibits large outflows of neutral and ionized gas [arXiv:0811.2724].

sources.summary: The paper studies neutral and ionised gas in the irregular dwarf NGC 2366, finding large outflows and concluding the gas remains gravitationally bound.

quotes.quote: We therefore performed a detailed kinematic analysis of the neutral and ionised gas in the nearby star-forming irregular dwarf galaxy NGC 2366 in order to make predictions about the fate of the gas and to get a more complete picture of this galaxy. A deep \ image and Fabry-Perot interferometric data of NGC 2366 were obtained. They were complemented by \ synthesis data from the THINGS survey. We searched for line-splitting both in \ and \ by performing a Gaussian decomposition. To get an idea whether the expansion velocities are high enough for a gas blow-away, we used the pseudo-isothermal halo model, which gives us realistic values for the escape velocities of NGC 2366.

NGC 2366 is classified as a barred Magellanic-type irregular (IB(s)m) dwarf galaxy deVaucouleurs1991 . We adopt a distance of 3.44 Mpc from Tolstoy1995 that places NGC 2366 in the M81 group. The nearest neighbour is NGC 2403 at a projected distance of 290 kpc, which makes any kind of recent interaction unlikely.

sources.summary: GBT observations detect NGC 2366 over the Hunter et al. velocity range, show no extended gas skirt, and measure a normal dwarf-galaxy gas extent smaller than the Effelsberg report.

quotes.quote: NGC 2366 We detected \ emission from NGC 2366 from 30 \ to 172 , very close to what Hunter \ (2001) found. Our integrated \ map of NGC 2366 is shown in Figure fig-n2366m0 , and contours are plotted on the V band image in Figure fig-n2366hionv . No extended skirt of \ is seen in this map.

We detect \ at a column density of 10^ 19 \ around NGC 2366 at a diameter of 23.4 . Corrected for the 8.7 \ beam, this is an extent of 21.7 . This is similar to the extent reported by Hunter \ (2001) from VLA observations, but less than the 30 \ diameter reported by Huchtmeier \ (1981). Hunter \ report an \ diameter of 23.4 \ at 1.7 10^ 19 , and their surface density profile yields 25 \ at the level of 10^ 19 \ that we are using for comparison here. Our \ extent gives a ratio of R_ HI /R_ Holm of 2.3, which is normal compared to other dwarfs.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 71/NGC 2366 as the nearest Green Pea analog and LyC-emitter candidate, using its proximity and integrated properties to study possible LyC escape mechanisms.

quotes.quote: Mrk 71/NGC 2366 is the closest Green Pea (GP) analog and candidate Lyman Continuum (LyC) emitter. Recently, 11 LyC-leaking GPs have been detected through direct observations of the ionizing continuum, making this the most abundant class of confirmed LyC-emitters at any redshift. High resolution, multi-wavelength studies of GPs can lead to an understanding of the method(s), through which LyC escapes from these galaxies. The proximity of Mrk 71/NCG 2366 offers unprecedented detail on the inner workings of a GP analog, and enables us to identify the mechanisms of LyC escape.

Mrk 71/NGC 2366 is one such galaxy. It is the closest GP analog and a LyC candidate, at a distance of 3.4 Mpc, i.e., close enough for the detection of individual stars. The NGC 2366 galaxy hosts the giant H II region Mrk 71, which dominates the ionization properties of the galaxy, and drives the similarities to GPs Micheva2017b . The integrated properties of Mrk 71/NGC 2366 are fully consistent with GPs in terms of morphology, excitation properties, specific star-formation rate, kinematics, absorption of low-ionization species, dust reddening, and chemical abundances.

sources.summary: The paper searches the nearby irregular galaxy NGC 2366 for optical SNRs, identifying four candidates and describing the galaxy as a dwarf irregular/cometary system at about 3.4 Mpc with large HII regions.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA(J2000)= 07^ h 28^ m 24^ s , Dec.(J2000)= angle-symbol-over-decimal 69;11;00

quotes.quote: The results of an optical search for supernova remnants (SNRs) in the nearby irregular galaxy NGC 2366 are presented. We took interference filter images and collected spectral data in three epochs with the f/7.7 1.5 m Russian Turkish Telescope (RTT150) at T U B I TAK National Observatory (TUG) located in Antalya, Turkey. The continuum-subtracted H and continuum-subtracted [ S ii ] 6716, 6731 images and their ratios were used for the identification of SNRs. With [ S ii ] /H 0.4 criteria, four possible SNR candidates were identified in NGC 2366 with [ S ii ] /H ratios of (0.68, 0.57, 0.55 and 0.75), H intensities of (2.10, 0.36, 0.14, 0.11) 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 [ S ii ] 6716/ 6731 average flux ratios of (1.01 and 1.04), electron densities of N_ e (582 and 513) cm ^ 3 and [ O iii ] 5007/H 4861 (3.6 and 2.6) line ratio values are obtained for two of the SNR candidates.

In this work, we searched for SNRs in the nearby dwarf, irrregular galaxy NGC 2366 using the criterion [ S ii ] /H ratio 0.4. It is described as a ``cometary'' galaxy by looseandthuan1986 . karachentsevaetal1985 reported its distance to be D =3.44 Mpc, which is consistent with tikhonovetal1991 who reported D =3.4 Mpc. The optical and radio studies by Kennicuttetal1980 showed evidence of very large H ii regions in NGC 2366.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2366 as a late-type dwarf near NGC 2403, comparing their H I velocities and noting its disturbed gas, outflows, and recent starburst.

quotes.quote: (using a major axis position angle of 17^ ; cf. \ Fig. \ fig:momslice ) we find that it passes close to the dwarf spheroidal galaxy DDO 44 (at about 1.5 ^ or 85 kpc from NGC 2403) and also the late-type dwarf galaxy NGC 2366 (at 3.5^ or 190 kpc). The velocities of the \ in NGC 2366 completely overlap with those of the \ in NGC 2403. In NGC 2403, \ is found from 5 to 270 km s ^ 1 . In NGC 2366, this ranges from 40 to 170 km s ^ 1 walter08 .

NGC 2366 is a gas-rich dwarf-galaxy which is known to have a disturbed \ velocity field and an outer gas component that does not follow regular rotation deblok08,oh08 . It is undergoing a star-burst and shows evidence for major gas outflows eymeren09 . mcquinn , from modeling of the stellar population, find that the star formation burst started and peaked 450 Myr ago.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2366 for detailed star-formation and gas-turbulence correlation tests, finding only weak or insignificant correspondence between FUV and kinetic-energy-density images.

quotes.quote: We also applied alternate methods on one galaxy, NGC 2366 with a max \ of 0.3, to examine the robustness of our approach. This galaxy was chosen for initial and special tests because it has a giant \ region and the \ velocity dispersion is high around this region, making it an interesting candidate for looking for a star formation-turbulence correlation.

Applied to NGC 2366, ZNCC is 0.26. Like or coefficients, +1 is perfect correlation, 1 is perfect anti-correlation, and so 0.26, which is what we found for the max , implies a not very significant degree of correlation. One way to deal with pixels with low S/N is to use a weighted normalized cross-correlation (WNCC). For this test, we weighted the pixels by the ratio of their signal to the standard deviation of values in the map, which is effective at down-weighting background noise pixels. Using this method, we obtain a WNCC value of -0.023 -- effectively zero, implying no significant correlation between the two images.

sources.summary: The paper applies the pattern-speed method to NGC 2366, finding regional differences, evidence against a constant pattern speed in several regions, and H I clumps corotating or rotating slowly.

quotes.quote: Unlike NGC 3031, different regions of NGC 2366 produce different results. Results are presented for four different regions: |y| 400 , |y| 200 , 400 y 0 , and 0 y 400 . These are hereafter referred to as regions A , B , C , and D , respectively. They provide 26, 14, 13, and 13 independent calculations of Equation (1), respectively.

From the results for the F test, the results of the KS test for the constant _p model, and the plots of _p , it is possible to infer that the clumps of H I in NGC 2366 are corotating with the material in the inner half of the disk, and that there are clumps of H I in the outer half of the disk in region C that are rotating more slowly than the rest of the material. The results for NGC 2366 show that the solution method can detect different values of _p in different regions of a galaxy.

sources.summary: The study finds NGC 2366 has an extended regularly rotating disk with a northwestern kinematic distortion, while rejecting earlier declining outer rotation-curve claims.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

NGC 2366 has an extended disk with a strong kinematic distortion to the North-West (see its velocity field in Appendix app:Atlas ). Our rotation curve is in overall agreement with previous results Hunter2001, Thuan2004, Oh2008, Swaters2009, vanEymeren2009b , but we do not confirm the declining part of the rotation curve found by Hunter2001 and vanEymeren2009b at R 5' . This latter result appears to be caused by an anomalous cloud that lies at V_ l.o.s 130 km s ^ 1 along the major axis ( 7' from the galaxy center to the North, see the PV-diagram in Fig. fig:PVmodels and Appendix app:Atlas ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 2366 as a lopsided dwarf with a southeastern extension, northwestern kinematic distortion, disturbed gas, and a probable ongoing minor merger with NGC 2363.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).

NGC 2366 has a disk with a broad extension to the South-East and a strong kinematic distortion to the North-West. Fig. fig:n2366 (left) shows an optical image overlaid with the emission at 15 '' resolution, integrated over a narrow velocity range near the systemic velocity (between 90 and 115 km s ^ 1 ). The gas to the North-West does not follow the rotation of the disk see also Fig. 3 of Oh2008 and may be associated with the secondary star-forming body to the South-West (NGC 2363). Intriguingly, the PV-diagram displays a steep velocity gradient coinciding with the spatial position and optical systemic velocity of NGC 2363 (indicated by the star). This may indicate that this velocity gradient is due to rotation in a local potential well. However, given the overall rotation of the disk of NGC 2366, this conclusion is uncertain. The NGC 2363/NGC 2366 system probably is an on-going minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2366 to demonstrate that its logarithmic-normal disk model can fit the dwarf galaxy rotation curve, terminal rise, and density profile.

quotes.quote: In contrast to an exponential distribution, these characteristics match the rotation curves for both dwarf galaxies such as NGC 2366 and large galaxies such as M31 (Figs. fig:NGC2366 (a) and fig:M31 respectively), and satisfy the observed disc density distributions for many spiral galaxies 2012msma.book.....F . The ability of the LN model to generate realistic RCs with a good fit to observations is demonstrated for the dwarf galaxy NGC 2366 in Fig. fig:NGC2366 (a), with _0=1.15 10^8 M _ /kpc ^2 , =1.16 , r_ =2.85 kpc, R_ max =8.3 kpc. NGC 2366 has a relatively flat F-type curve, but shows a strong terminal rise beyond 7.5 kpc.

This is a feature of many RCs with an abrupt termination 2008AJ....135...20E , and the fitted LN curve fits the observations well, including the terminal rise. Fig. fig:NGC2366 (b) also compares the NGC 2366 LN density curve used to generate the RC with the best-fitting exponential density curve. The LN curve (red solid line) is seen to overlie the exponential curve (blue dashed line) for some portion of the radial distance, implying a possible mechanism for the S e rsic exponential models.

sources.summary: The paper studies ionized gas structures in NGC 2366 with deep H imaging and echelle spectroscopy, comparing shell and filament expansion velocities with dark-matter-halo escape velocities.

quotes.quote: We want to investigate whether the ionized gas detected in two irregular dwarf galaxies (NGC 2366 and NGC 4861) stays gravitationally bound to the host galaxy or can escape from it by becoming a freely flowing wind. Very deep H images of NGC 2366 and NGC 4861 were obtained to detect and catalog both small and large scale ionized gas structures down to very low surface brightnesses. Subsequently, high-resolution long-slit echelle spectroscopy of the H line was performed for a detailed kinematic analysis of the most prominent filaments and shells. To calculate the escape velocity of both galaxies and to compare it with the derived expansion velocities of the detected filaments and shells, we used dark matter halo models. We detected a huge amount of both small scale (up to a few hundred pc) and large scale (about 1--2 kpc of diameter or length) ionized gas structures on our H images.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST photometry across NGC 2366 to map stellar populations, infer thin and thick disk sizes, and identify a stellar halo beyond the thick disk.

quotes.quote: Hubble Space Telescope archive data are used to perform photometry of stars in seven fields at the center and periphery of the galaxy NGC 2366. The variation of the number density of stars of various ages with galactocentric radius and along the minor axis of the galaxy are determined. The boundaries of the thin and thick disks of the galaxy are found. The inferred sizes of the subsystems of NGC 2366 ( Z_ thin = 4 kpc and Z_ thick = 8 kpc for the thin and thick disks, respectively) are more typical for spiral galaxies. Evidence for a stellar halo is found at the periphery of NGC 2366 beyond the thick disk of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 2366, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 2366 NGC 2366 (Fig. fig:ngc-2366 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 2366 is classified as a dwarf irregular and belongs, like NGC 2403, to the M81 group. As for all the other dwarf galaxies in the sample presented here, the 3.6 m IRAC image and the radio continuum map do not show a nuclear source. We derived the center of the emission in the IRAC image by using ellfit at varying isophote levels. The IRAC center is offset from the pointing center by =7 8 0 6 and =-18 0 1 8 .

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC3851 as a difficult low-surface-brightness rotation-curve case with an unusually high mass-to-light ratio and a sharp inner-to-outer slope change.

quotes.quote: It is easy to see that 10 galaxies have values compatible with the prescribed range by vandenHoek00 ; one (UGC3851) has a higher value which is hard to explain in term of reasonable population synthesis; and four (UGC4325, UGC5721, UGC7603, UGC8286) have values smaller than 0.5 . Among these, one is particularly problematic, i.e. UGC7603, because it has a mass-to-light ratio too much small, Y_ = 0.017 , while the others can be again considered acceptable. UGC3851 is a real challenge for both our approach and the more traditional one deBlok02 .

The very linear rise in the inner region rapidly changes in a flat part at larger radii so that it is very hard to reproduce this sharp change in the slope. There are two possibilities for this behavior: the HI curve in outer points is underestimated, or there are non-circular motions related to a bar-like structure and a star forming region in the center which affect the H observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2366 as having a complicated rotation curve, possible non-rotational motions, uncertain dark-halo radius, and a BEC mass estimate close to the observed mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 2366: The rotation curve of this galaxy is rather complicated, indicating that disturbances caused by non-circular motions could be present in the outer regions with > 5 kpc. Non-rotational motions may also be present for <5 kpc, distorting the inner region of the rotation curve. It is difficult to give an estimate of the radius of the dark matter distribution, because of the increasing trend in the velocity field for between 6 and 8 kpc. The adopted values for the radius and central density give an estimate of the mass of the galaxy as M_ NGC2365 ^ BEC =4.54 10^ 9 M_ , while the observed mass is M=4.29 10^9M_ . The BEC velocity profile gives a good description of the rotation curve for (4.5,6.5) kpc.

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS galaxy in Group B, where BDM fits outperform NFW, imply r_c roughly comparable to r_s, and favor a strong dark-matter core.

quotes.quote: In the second case, Group B, (IC 2574 (B1), NGC 2366 (B2), NGC 2903 (B3), NGC 2976 (B4), NGC 3198 (B5), NGC 3521 (B6), and NGC 925 (B7)), we get a fitted value of r_c r_s and BDM profile fits better than NFW. In fact, the NFW profile in some cases over-predicts the velocity in the inner parts of the galaxy or does fit with unphysical values for r_s > O (10^4) or _0 < O (10^3) M_ /kpc^ 3 , see Table tab:onlydm_bdm and tab:onlydm_nfw . The BDM profile gives r_c r_s for all stellar mass models, having an average r_c value of 6 and 5 kpc for Min.Disk and Min.Disk+Gas, respectively. Since r_c r_s , implying that our BDM profile is of the type (r_c + )^ 3 and hence having a strong core behavior. For this set of galaxies the Burkert and ISO profiles, as well as the BDM, fit much better than NFW, indicating that a core is needed.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC2366 significantly below cluster-formation trends, arguing its extended star-forming pockets likely reflect genuinely low cluster formation rather than hidden clusters.

quotes.quote: There is one galaxy that falls significantly below the _ SFR trend line in both age ranges: NGC2366. This galaxy also falls nearly 3 below the SLUG simulations in the < 100 Myr age range as well. also found this galaxy to have an unusual dearth of cluster formation compared to other galaxies with similar star formation properties.

NGC2366 has spatially extended pockets of star formation where a lack of cluster formation could be due to a lower gas density. This implies that NGC2366 has truly formed fewer clusters than predicted by the _ SFR relationship.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2366 as an observed dwarf-irregular comparison, matching its star-formation rate, circular velocity, and central dark-matter density against the fiducial radiative-feedback dwarf simulation.

quotes.quote: In particular, for NGC2366, which resembles #dwALL_1# in stellar mass and circular velocity (see Section sec:circularvelocity ), Weisz11a find a present-day SFR 4 10^ 2 yr ^ 1 , also in excellent agreement with the value of 2.6 10^ 2 yr ^ 1 that we obtain for this run.

In Table rotcurvetable we directly compare our fiducial dwarf galaxy with full radiative feedback with two galaxies from Oh11a , NGC2366 and DDO154, which have similar maximum circular velocities to our models. The circular velocity profile of the fiducial model with full stellar radiation feedback is a very good match to rotation curves of observed galaxies of similar mass, NGC2366 and DDO154, except perhaps in the central 1 , where the rise in the circular velocity of the fiducial run is slightly steeper. In addition, Oh11a determine maximum circular velocities for the stars and cold gas in NGC2366 of 15 and 28 , respectively.
For instance, the dark matter density of NGC2366 at a distance of 500 pc is _ DM 0.04 pc ^ 3 , while for DDO154 the density at the same radius is 0.05 pc ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper presents a Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point-source catalog for NGC 2366, reporting 505 detected sources and common completeness limits corresponding to sources brighter than M_ 3.6 = -10.

quotes.quote: We present Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point source catalogs for mosaics covering the fields of the nearby ( 4 Mpc) galaxies NGC 55, NGC 253, NGC 2366, NGC 4214, and NGC 5253. We detect a total of 20159 sources in these five fields. The individual galaxy point source breakdown is the following: NGC 55, 8746 sources; NGC 253, 9001 sources; NGC 2366, 505 sources; NGC 4214, 1185 sources; NGC 5253, 722 sources. The completeness limits of the full catalog vary with bandpass and were found to be m_ 3.6 =18.0 , m_ 4.5 =17.5 , m_ 5.8 =17.0 , and m_ 8.0 =16.5 mag. For all galaxies, this corresponds to detection of point sources brighter than M_ 3.6 =-10 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2366 already has a good MOND rotation-curve fit, but an external field slightly improves it and yields a more plausible stellar mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: (3) NGC 2366 : With the reduced ^2 value of 0.54, the Milgromian dynamics curve without the EFE is in good agreement with the observed rotation curve of this dwarf galaxy, except that in the middle parts Milgromian dynamics predicts somewhat higher rotational velocities. The measured distance to NGC 2366 is 3.34 Mpc with the minimum and maximum estimation of 1.75 and 6.15 Mpc ic-dm, ic-md and the spatial location of this galaxy is determined by right ascention 7^h28^m54.6^s and declination 69^d12^m57^s . As can be seen in Table 2, adding the external field effect slightly improves the MOND fit but significantly increases the best-fitted M_*/L_K ratio from 0.04 to 0.33 in solar units. This new M_*/L_K ratio is more consistent with the SPS prediction than the extremely low value inferred with MOND without the EFE. The inferred external field from the rotation curve fit is a_e=0.240^ +0.125 _ 0.155 a_0 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2366 as a late-type dwarf irregular with an extended HI disk, rotational support, and a dense compact dark-matter halo suppressing small-scale spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We select five late-type dwarf irregular galaxies, namely, DDO 154, NGC 3741, DDO 43, NGC 2366 and DDO 168 in which we study the constraining influence of dominant dark halo on the formation of small-scale spiral arms. Two of these (DDO 154, NGC 2366) are a part of the THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Wal08 , whereas DDO 43 and DDO 168 are a part of the LITTLE (Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes) THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Hun12 . Our sample galaxies have the following characteristics: These have a large HI disk (extending to several optical radii), and in the inner regions as well as in the outer regions the larger part of the baryonic contribution comes from HI gas, and not stars. The galaxies are rotationally supported. We find that for these low luminosity galaxies, the corresponding dark matter halo is dense and compact, i.e., the central density ( _0 ) is few times 10^ 2 M _ pc ^ 2 and the core radius (R _ ) is less than two-three times the exponential disk scalelength (R _ d ).

sources.summary: The paper adopts the THINGS H I cube of NGC 2366 as a dynamical template, describing its barred irregular dwarf classification, disturbed rotating disk, warp, and line profile.

quotes.quote: As an input model, we adopt the HI emission cube from the THINGS survey of the galaxy NGC 2366 (Walter et al. 2008). The galaxy is classified as a barred irregular dwarf galaxy. This galaxy shows a rotating HI disk that is modestly disturbed, with a possible outer warp (van Eymeren et al. 2009). The integrated line profile is flat-top, not a clear double horn. Such a profile is more characteristic of those seen for CO emission from high redshift disk galaxies, for which classic double horn line profiles are uncommon (Gonzales-Lopez et al. 2019).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2366 as a THINGS dwarf irregular, derives its H I scale-height profile, and attributes its especially strong flaring to a concentrated dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: We present in the top four panels of Figure fig1 the resulting H i scale height versus radius for DDO 154, Ho II, IC 2574 and NGC 2366, assuming for each galaxy the constant H i velocity dispersion as given in Table table1 . For each galaxy, the scale height calculations were done twice: first by neglecting the radial term in the Poisson equation, mimicking the approach used for spiral galaxies nar02,ban07 , and a second time including the radial term. The two results differ from each other by about 10--20 percent, indicating that the exact shape of the rotation curve only enters as small, systematic correction.
The rate of flaring is highest in NGC 2366 and DDO 154 which can be ascribed to these two galaxies having a more centrally concentrated dark matter halo than the other two systems.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2366 as the sample's only low-mass, gas-rich galaxy, noting that it modestly affects the BTFr fit and is not a luminosity-based TFr outlier.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, the only low--mass galaxy in our sample (NGC 2366) appears to be very gas rich (see Figure fig_finbtfr ). If we remove this galaxy from the fit, the statistical properties of our BTFr change slightly, as the observed tightness changes from _ =0.052 to _ =0.048 dex. However, there is nothing unusual about NGC 2366 that would allow us to remove it from the BTFr. Moreover, it clearly demonstrates that inclusion of the gaseous component in the TFr increases its scatter, as NGC 2366 is not an outlier on the luminosity--based TFr of our sample.

sources.summary: The paper reports superbubbles and structured ionized emission in NGC 2366, with velocities suggesting material may outflow while remaining bound and mixing outer gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 2366 From Figure fig:ngc2366 , NGC 2366 has two significant superbubbles of ionized gas traced by \ emission in the south and several other slightly smaller bubbles in the north and central region of the galaxy. These regions are spatially coincident with the sites of BHeB stars with ages less than 100 Myr, and in the case of the northern region less than 25 Myr McQuinn2012a . Spectroscopically derived radial \ velocities using a Fabry-Per o t interferometer for the central 4 4 southern and central \ regions range from 35 80 km s ^ 1 to 90-125 km s ^ 1 respectively Garrido2004 , compared to an \ maximum circular velocity of 54 km s ^ 1 . In contrast to the smoother distribution of diffuse ionized gas seen in, for example, NGC 4068 and NGC 4163, the \ emission outside the bubbles shows significant structure with many arcs and filaments.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 2366 as a high-probability model leaker, describing it as the closest known Green Pea analogue with optically thin ionized outflows that could let LyC radiation escape.

quotes.quote: Of the galaxies predicted to be leakers by our model, NGC 2366 has one of the highest probabilities of having an f_ esc 10 . NGC 2366 is the closest known analogue of a Green Pea galaxy, many of which have already been determined to be LyC leakers Izotov2016a,Izotov2016b,Izotov2018a,Izotov2018b . While an escape fraction has not yet been measured for this system, IFU observations revealed strong ionised outflows that are almost certainly optically thin and can facilitate the escape of LyC radiation Micheva2019 . NGC 4861 is another galaxy that our model predicts to be a leaker, although it is less confident about this galaxy compared to NGC 2366.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2366 lacks a credible flat-disk fit, with velocity contours, published rotation curves, and model attempts giving conflicting or unreliable results.

quotes.quote: NGC 2366 We were unable to find a credible model that fitted the velocity map of the dwarf galaxy NGC 2366. The upper panel of Figure  fig.N2366 manifests a clear velocity gradient roughly aligned with the major axis of the HI emission, but the iso-velocity contours do not appear to have the characteristic pattern of flow in flat, axisymmetric disc. Note also in the lower panel that many pixels near the centre of our velocity map have uncertainties >5 km s ^ 1 . The rotation curve reported by dB08 rises to nearly 60 km s ^ 1 around 250 , but then drops below 40 km s ^ 1 by 420 , which is a steeper than Keplerian fall off.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 2366 as a barred irregular dwarf with companion NGC 2363, known noncircular motions, and coherent anisotropies in its velocity maps.

quotes.quote: NGC 2366 NGC 2366 is a barred irregular dwarf galaxy that has a neighboring companion (NGC 2363). Results for the P.A. and inclination angle obtained by the TRM show that the P.A. does not vary by more than 10^ between the inner ( R_ 25 130'' ) and outer disk, while the inclination angle shows large fluctuations in the inner disk (as seen in Fig. NGC2366 ). As the P.A displays a moderate variation, we find that v_t(R) v_c(R) .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2366 as an example where Keck data were too shallow for a TRGB distance, despite its expected M81 group distance.

quotes.quote: One example is NGC 2366. This dwarf irregular galaxy has been estimated to lie at a distance d 3.44 Mpc, which is consistent with other M81 group members and references therein ferrarese00a . Our CMD for this galaxy (Fig.  figure:NGC2366_cmd ) shows no recognizable structure, and the luminosity function turns over at about I 23.5 mag never having any red giant branch power-law slope.
Clearly our data for this object did not go deep enough to resolve a red giant branch population.

sources.summary: UGC 3851 is discussed near UGC 3847 as an irregular involved in a possible interaction, too large for the formal sample but included for proximity context.

quotes.quote: Another possible interaction which is worthy of comment is UGC 3847 and UGC 3851 (NGC 2363 and NGC 2366, shown in Fig. Figf ), both irregular galaxies, with the 2 highest H + [ N ii ] EWs of all 334 galaxies observed in this study. Drissen et al. ( ) consider UGC 3847 to be an outlying H ii region of UGC 3851, and note that UGC 3847 is the highest surface brightness H ii region in the entire sky. UGC 3851 is not actually part of the statistical sample, since it is too large to satisfy the selection criteria, but is included in this discussion of individual objects due to its proximity to UGC 3847.

sources.summary: NGC 2366 is used to test TRGB consistency across HST fields; its inner-field CMD has a readily detected TRGB, rich RGB/AGB populations, and many blue and red supergiants.

quotes.quote: The first field covers the inner part of NGC 2366. The color-magnitude diagram of the sum of all images is presented in the top panel of Fig. f:n2366 . The TRGB is situated well above the photometric limit in this case, and it is easily detected with our procedure. The color-magnitude diagram has huge blue and red supergiant populations, as well as rich RGB and AGB populations.

sources.summary: NGC 2366 is cited as a nearby SBm low-mass spiral whose H I shows a filamentary CNM network, a disappearance of CNM beyond R25, and a large total CNM flux fraction.

quotes.quote: Several low mass spirals were observed in the high resolution sample of Braun ( , ), including the nearby SBm galaxy NGC 2366 and two of the nearest low surface brightness spirals, NGC 247 (of type Sc) and NGC 4236 (SBd). A filamentary network of CNM gas is seen in all these systems, which accounts for about 80 of the line flux from only about 15 of the surface area within R_ 25 (the radius where the average face-on surface brightness of light in the B band has declined to 25 mag arsec ^ 2 ). Just beyond R_ 25 the flux fraction of CNM gas disappears entirely, even though the WNM continues out to a radius of almost twice R_ 25 . The result is that the total \ flux fraction due to CNM in these galaxies is about 70 , substantially more than the 40 CNM fraction in the CHVCs.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes 3851 as kinematically irregular, with strongly curved northern isovelocity contours and straighter, asymmetric contours on the southern side.

quotes.quote: 3851 has an irregular kinematic structure. On the north side, the isovelocity contours curve strongly, suggesting a nearly flat rotation curve. On the south side, the isovelocity contours are almost straight, and not symmetric with respect to the major axis, but instead run more or less north to south.

sources.summary: NGC 2366 is analyzed as an irregular dwarf with close-to solid-body rotation, weak spiral arms, and non-circular motions that do not significantly affect its rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The IB(s)m galaxy NGC 2366 forms part of ``The \ Nearby Galaxy Survey'' THINGS, Walter2008 .
As NGC 2366 is dominated by close-to solid body rotation, it is difficult to determine \ and i simultaneously.
As discussed by Tikhonov2008 and vE09a, NGC 2366 shows evidence for two weak spiral arms.

sources.summary: NGC 2366 is discussed as a dark-matter-dominated dwarf/low-surface-brightness THINGS galaxy whose Einasto halo matches simulated dwarfs in density-radius space but has a lower-than-expected index.

quotes.quote: Another interesting point concerns the results for the few dwarf and low surface brightness galaxies DDO 154 and NGC 2366. Due to their dark matter dominance they are ideal candidates for a direct comparison with CDM low mass halos ( 10^ 9 \ h^ 1 ). They perfectly agree with simulated dwarf galaxy halos in the density-radius parameter space (the two closest symbols to the red triangles in Figs fig:haloparam1 and fig:haloparam2 ) but they have a low Einasto index, contrary to all simulated dwarf-galaxy-sized halos.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2366 as a low-metallicity outlier whose [C II] and [O I] SFR relations diverge while [O III] tracks SFR, suggesting ionized gas and less efficient photoelectric heating.

quotes.quote: The behavior of NGC 2366 and NGC 4861 is mainly divergent in the SFR- L_ [ CII ] and SFR- L_ [ OI ] relations, whereas the [ O iii ] _ 88 emission correlates remarkably well with the SFR, suggesting that the gas around massive star clusters is mostly ionized and that PDRs only occupy a limited volume of the ISM.

Low-metallicity galaxies often have warm FIR colors (e.g. 1999ApJ...516..783T,2004ApJS..154..211H,2009A&A...508..645G,2011A&A...532A..56G,2013A&A...557A..95R ), which could explain why galaxies such as NGC 2366 and NGC 4861 show an offset in the SFR- L_ [ CII ] relation compared to more metal-rich galaxies.

Higher values of [ O i ] _ 63 / [ C ii ] + [ O i ] _ 63 occur preferentially in galaxies of lower metal abundance (e.g. NGC 2366, NGC 4861), which might suggest that the importance of the photoelectric effect diminishes in dust deficient environments and other heatings mechanisms (e.g. soft X-ray heating) become more efficient.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2366 has lower adopted SFR than CMD estimates, remains consistent with the VSF law after rescaling, and was used for self-consistent gas-structure testing.

quotes.quote: We note that the SFR of DDO 50 (a.k.a. Holmberg II) and NGC 2366 in Table tab:sample are a factor 2 lower than the values obtained by using the CMD of resolved stellar populations.
We found that using these modified profiles does not affect our main conclusion, as both DDO 50 and NGC 2366 remain in agreement with the VSF.
This approach was tested on three dwarf galaxies, including WLM and NGC 2366, in order to derive their rotation curve, velocity dispersion, surface density, and scale height in a self-consistent way.

gmw_00000836.name: DDO 53
gmw_00000836.overview: DDO 53 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2109.08526, arXiv:astro-ph/0301056] located in the M81 group [arXiv:2109.08526, arXiv:2006.01735]. This gas-rich, metal-deficient galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0301056, arXiv:1307.4899] features a 2-kpc ionized gas supershell [arXiv:2109.08526], multiple H I holes [arXiv:2006.01735], and an accreting northern gas cloud that triggers recent star formation [arXiv:2109.08526].

sources.summary: The paper studies DDO 53 as an isolated dwarf irregular with perturbed gas, a faint ionized supershell, shocks, and an anomalous northern cloud that may fuel recent star formation.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a multiwavelength study of the nearby dwarf galaxy DDO 53 -- a relatively isolated member of the M 81 group. We analyse the atomic and ionised gas kinematics (based on the observations with Fabry-Perot interferometer in line and archival data in 21 cm line), distribution, excitation and oxygen abundance of the ionised gas (based on the long-slit and integral-field spectroscopy and on imaging with narrow-band filters), and their relation with the young massive stars (based on archival data). We detect a faint 2-kpc sized supershell of ionised gas surrounding the galaxy. Most probably, this structure represents a large-scale gas outflow, however it could be also created by the ionising quanta leaking from star-forming regions to the marginally detected atomic hydrogen surrounding the galactic disc. We analyse the properties of the anomalous in the north part of the galaxy and find that its peculiar kinematics is also traced by ionised gas.

The brightest complex of star formation in DDO 53 demonstrates excitation from shocks both in emission line ratios diagnostics and in its ionised and neutral gas kinematics. We suggest that the shocks originate from the interaction of the ISM in the galaxy with the anomalous cloud that is detected there, rather than by the influence of the feedback from massive stars. We investigated the origin of the anomalous gaseous tail to the north of the galaxy. From the analysis of its kinematics and its relation to the kinematics and excitation of the ionised gas, we argue that it represents a gas cloud falling onto the galaxy. This gas accretion event might be a reason for the enhanced star formation in the galaxy 25 Myr ago and it also could supply the fuel for sustaining star formation in the brightest complex of DDO 53.

sources.summary: The paper identifies VV 499 as an unusually blue LSB irregular, an extremely metal-deficient gas-rich dwarf, with current star formation likely triggered by M81-subgroup tides or intragroup gas.

quotes.quote: VV 499 is a 14 5 Irr-galaxy of unusually blue colour (according to LEDA, its extinction-corrected integrated (U-B) colour is -0.48). Its integrated colour (B-V)_ tot =0.45, according to Makarova ( , ), is, however, rather typical of dI galaxies. This galaxy belongs to the Low Surface Brightness (LSB) class, since its central brightness ^ 0 _ B =24.1 mag. arcsec ^ 2 (Makarova , ).

For the brightest knot ``b'' we derive value of O/H with the T _ e method -- 12+ (O/H)= 7.52 0.08, that is consistent with the value of 7.62 obtained by Skillman et al. ( ) by the empirical method. Notice that luminosity and O/H of DDO 053 match the ``O/H versus L_ B '' relationship for dIrr galaxies suggested by Skillman et al. ( ). The oxygen abundance of VV 499 assigns it to the group of very rare extremely metal-deficient gas-rich dwarfs.

Tidal effect onto VV 499 of any separate galaxy belonging to these subgroups could be rather subtle. However, the tidal role of the whole subgroup M 81 (with a total mass of 10 ^ 12 M , Karachentsev et al. ) is quite important (see, e.g., estimates in Pustilnik et al. ).
