It is one of the objects for which the detected emission extends sufficiently far out to probe the decline in the outer disk rotation curve recently discovered in z 1 - 2.5 SFGs Gen17,Lan17 .

The kinematics show a strong velocity gradient along the minor axis towards the center, characteristic of gas inflow in the disk and/or outflow. A small, faint, low-mass source is detected in continuum and H at the north edge of the disk, redshifted by 140 km s^ 1 relative to the disk rotation at that location, consistent with a 1 : 30 minor merging event Gen17 .

gmw_00000847.name: ZC 400569
gmw_00000847.overview: ZC 400569 is a star-forming main-sequence galaxy at redshift z=2.24 [arXiv:2302.00030, arXiv:2405.00103] that hosts a massive rotating disk [arXiv:1802.07276] and shows signatures of an AGN-driven wind [arXiv:1802.07276]. The galaxy's locally unstable CO disk [arXiv:2405.00103] may experience perturbations from southern satellite companions [arXiv:1802.07276, arXiv:2405.00103].

sources.summary: The paper studies zC-400569 as a z=2.24 main-sequence galaxy with regular CO/H rotating disks, clumpy ionized companions, and a relatively flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: We used the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) to obtain high-resolution data of the CO( 3-2 ) and CO( 4-3 ) lines for a main-sequence galaxy at z 2.24 : zC-400569 Mancini2011 also known as COSMOS 0488950 Capak2007, Mobasher2007 or COSMOS2015 0300906 Laigle2016 . This galaxy is one of the best observed object from the SINS IFU survey Forster2009, Forster2018, Genzel2017 .

The galaxy zC-400569 at z 2.4 Figure fig:SINFONI compares the distributions of molecular and ionized gas in zC-400569 at z 2.24 . The H emission display a ``clumpy'' morphology with a northsouth orientation. The two brightest H components to the north are encompassed by the CO emission, whereas the two weakest H components to the south are undetected in any CO line.

In terms of kinematics, CO and H emissions form regularly rotating disks, as shown by velocity fields and PV diagrams. This implies that stellar feedback does not destroy the overall kinematic regularity of the gas disk, similarly to the situation in local spirals where stellar feedback mostly drives a kpc-scale gas circulation via galactic fountains e.g., Marasco2019, LMF2021 . The possible interaction with components A and B appears to have a negligible effect on the overall regularity of the main disk, albeit some minor kinematic disturbances are visible toward the southeast of the velocity fields.

sources.summary: The paper treats zC-400569 as a z=2.24 main-sequence galaxy with CO-based molecular gas kinematics, a locally unstable CO disc, and companion-driven perturbations possibly triggering instability.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies at cosmic noon. These are the main-sequence star-forming galaxies zC-400569 at z 2.24 and zC-488879 z 1.47 . The molecular gas kinematics and the mass models in Table tab:mass_models were derived by using on CO emission line datacubes, specifically CO(3-2) and CO(4-3) lines for zC-400569, and CO(2-1) and CO(3-2) lines for zC-488879.
The gas tracer is [ CII ] for all the locally unstable systems, except zC400569, which is traced by CO.

Observations of the H emission of zC400569 revealed the presence of a chain of clumps 2017Genzel,2018FosterSchreiber , which has been ascribed to LGI e.g. 2009Elmegreen . On the other hand, suggest that the clumps visible in the optical images are low mass companions based on the lack of a CO emission associated with the H clumps. Our results are consistent with the scenario in which non-axisymmetric perturbations induced by these companions may have triggered the instability in the CO disc.

sources.summary: The paper describes ZC 400569 as a complex, deeply observed H-emitting system with a massive northern rotating disk, southern satellite knots, a dropping outer rotation curve, and AGN-wind signatures.

quotes.quote: ZC 400569 --- This target is one with most complex structure in H among our sample, and also one of the deepest SINFONI + AO data sets (22.5 h). The velocity difference across the full extent of the source reaches 570 km s^ 1 with a change of orientation of the isovelocity contours from North to South and a chain of H knots southward of the brightest northern peak. The northern component is a massive rotating disk hosting a significant bulge, while the main southern knots 1 0 and 1 5 to the south are associated with lower mass satellites with 5 and 2.3 , respectively, of the stellar mass of the main disk Tacc15a, Gen17 . It is another of the individual galaxies with best evidence for a dropping outer rotation curve discussed in detail by Gen17 , and is also one of the objects where broad line emission signatures of an AGN-driven wind are detected and spatially-resolved in the AO data FS14 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses zC-400569 as a high-redshift, solar-metallicity star-forming galaxy with elevated SFR and weak [C i] emission possibly linked to low C/O.

quotes.quote: In studying the kinematics of star-forming galaxies at cosmic-noon, Lelli23 presented ALMA observations of the zC-400569 galaxy, located at z=2.24 , in mid- J and [ C i ] (1-0) lines. This solar-metallicity galaxy has an elevated SFR=81 M _ yr ^ 1 and it is, thus, expected it will contain higher cosmic-ray energy densities and possibly also high average FUV radiation field strengths when compared to the MW. While these conditions favor the [ C i ] (1-0) emission Bisbas21 , Lelli23 reported a weak emission of this line which did not allow to perform kinematic studies. It remains to see whether zC-400569 has C/O <0 , as this would be in accordance to the results of the present work.

gmw_00000848.name: ZC 406690
gmw_00000848.overview: ZC 406690 is a rotationally dominated disk galaxy [arXiv:1704.00732] at a redshift of z = 2.196 [arXiv:1704.00732] that features a clumpy ring [arXiv:1802.07276]. The galaxy exhibits intense star formation that drives outflows [arXiv:1802.07276], and it is accompanied by a small satellite [arXiv:1802.07276].

sources.summary: The paper uses zC406690 as a high-redshift, high-SFR, rotationally dominated test case, with CO-derived gas-disc properties, circular velocity, half-mass radius, and molecular gas surface density.

quotes.quote: ZC406690 As a final test of our model, we use the high-redshift galaxy zC406690 ( z = 2.196 ) from the zCOSMOS-SINFONI sample (see forsterschreiber09,mancini11 ). This choice is motivated by its average properties for a high-redshift galaxy (see e.g. mancini11 , tacconi13 ), except for its high SFR, which is typical of a highly actively star-forming environment. Next to zC406690, there are two additional galaxies in the samples from genzel11 and tacconi13 with known clump masses and macroscopic observational parameters. However, zC406690 is the only galaxy that is classified as rotationally-dominated, which rules out the other galaxies as suitable targets for the application of our model.

In order to properly describe the host galaxy gas disc, we use the properties derived from CO data obtained in the context of the PHIBSS survey with the Plateau de Bure millimeter Interferometer (PdBI, nowadays called NOEMA) and listed in Table 2 of tacconi13 . In particular, we use the circular velocity V = 224 , the optical half-mass radius R_ 1/2 = 6.3 , and the mean molecular gas surface density contained in the half-mass radius _ g (R R_ 1/2 ) = 10^ 2.52 ^ 2 . The mean molecular gas surface density is determined as _ g (R R_ 1/2 ) = M_ mol-gas /( R_ 1/2 ^2) , where the molecular mass of the cloud is derived from CO data using the standard Galactic CO-to-H _2 conversion factor of X_ CO = 2 10^ 20 cm^ 2 (K )^ 1 ( bolatto13 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes ZC 406690 as a clumpy-ring disk with powerful star-formation outflows, a possible obscured central mass concentration, the steepest detected outer rotation-curve falloff, and a small satellite.

quotes.quote: ZC 406690 --- This disk has the most prominent clumpy ring among our targets, with very little H and continuum emission detected inside the ring. Intense star formation in the clumps drives powerful outflows, whose broad and blueshifted emission in the southwest part of the ring New12b causes the local perturbations apparent in our velocity and dispersion maps (extracted with single-Gaussian line fits). The kinematics in the inner regions of the galaxy suggest the presence of a significant central mass concentration, although its non-detection at rest-optical wavelengths would imply it is very highly dust-obscured or mostly in cold gas form Gen17, Tacc15b . ZC406690 exhibits the steepest fall-off detected in the outer disk rotation curve among the sample discussed by Gen17 .
The properties of the faint continuum and H source 1 6 west of the center imply it is a small satellite with 15 of the stellar mass of the main disk.

gmw_00000849.name: Q2343-BX610
gmw_00000849.overview: Q2343-BX610 is a massive, high-redshift rotating disk galaxy that is actively forming stars [arXiv:1907.04936, arXiv:1507.05652]. It features star-forming clumps [arXiv:2303.16227], bar- and spiral-like structures [arXiv:1802.07276], and gas outflows driven by both star formation and a central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2303.16227, arXiv:1802.07276].

sources.summary: ID14/BX610 is a well-studied rotating disk with clumps and outflow signatures, but its CO data show kinematic anomalies and misalignment, so the paper classifies it as uncertain.

quotes.quote: ID14 ID14, also known as BX610, has been extensively studied both in optical/near-infrared Forster_2009, Forster_2014, Tacchella_2018 and far-infrared wavelength Aravena_2014, Bolatto_2015, Brisbin_2019 . Its kinematics was previously analyzed using H data from VLT/SINFONI survey Forster_2018 . Based on H , ID14 is described as a rotating disk containing massive star-forming clumps Forster_2011, Forster_2018 . It also exhibits signatures of gas outflows driven by a weak or obscured AGN in the center and by star formation at the location of the bright southern clump visible both in H and UV Forster_2014 . Recently, Brisbin_2019 suggest that the high CO(7-6)/L _ IR ratio measured in BX610 can be ascribed to shock excitation caused by a recent merger event.

The CO(4-3) data show the presence of strong kinematic anomalies: the major axis PVD has the brightest emission only in the central regions; the minor-axis PVD is strongly axisymmetric, the iso-velocity contours are distorted in the south-east direction where a peak in the CO(4-3) distribution is visible (see arrows in Fig. fig:pv4 ). The latter may be due to an interacting companion. In addition, ID14 has a large difference of 65 between PA_ HST and PA_ kin .

sources.summary: The paper observes BX610 in CO and neutral carbon, deriving high gas mass, main-sequence star formation, enhanced high-J CO emission, and possible shock or dense-PDR excitation.

quotes.quote: We used the Plateau De Bure Interferometer to observe multiple CO and neutral carbon transitions in a z=2.2 main sequence disk galaxy, BX610. Our observation of CO(7-6), CO(4-3), and both far-infrared(FIR) [ C i ] lines complements previous observations of H and low-J CO, and reveals a galaxy that is vigorously forming stars with UV fields (Log( G G _0^ 1 ) 3.25); although less vigorously than local ultra-luminous infrared galaxies or most starbursting submillimeter galaxies in the early universe. Our observations allow new independent estimates of the cold gas mass which indicate M_ gas 2 10^ 11 M _ , and suggest a modestly larger _ CO value of 8.2.

In addition to gas of modest density (Log( n cm ^3) 3 ) heated by star formation, BX610 shows evidence for a significant second gas component responsible for the strong high-J CO emission. This second component might either be a high-density molecular gas component heated by star formation in a typical photodissociation region, or could be molecular gas excited by low-velocity C shocks. The CO(7-6)-to-FIR luminosity ratio we observe is significantly higher than typical star-forming galaxies and suggests that CO(7-6) is not a reliable star-formation tracer in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes VLA CO(1-0) observations of BX610, giving the observing setup, redshifted line frequency, and calibration strategy.

quotes.quote: BX610 Observations of the CO(1--0) emission line in BX610 were performed from October 2011 to November 2011 in D-array configuration (VLA project: 11B-112), and pointed to the optical position listed in Erb2006 . These observations were performed with the correlator configured in a similar fashion as the BzK-16000 C-array observations. We used two basebands formed by 8 SPWs each, and the SPWs set with 64 channels and 2 MHz per channel resolution. Both basebands overlap, with an offset of 7 channels. With this setup, the SPWs 3 and 11 covered the redshifted CO(1--0) line.

sources.summary: The paper presents CO observations of BX610, describing it as a massive z 2.3 main-sequence star-forming galaxy with a resolved CO disk and high molecular mass.

quotes.quote: Employing the parametrization of the main sequence of star-forming galaxies derived for the AEGIS and COSMOS fields by WHITAKER2012 , both MD94 and BX610 are very representative of the massive end of the main sequence at their z 2.3 redshift. Their specific star formation rates are sSFR 1.8 10^ 9 and 2.1 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 respectively, which at their inferred stellar masses (M _* 1.5 10^ 11 and 1.0 10^ 11 \ respectively) places them well within the main sequence 0.3 dex scatter. Both are very active star-forming galaxies, with respective SFR 271 95 and 212 74 \ TACCONI2013 .

BX610 is clearly resolved along its major axis, with a deconvolved CO FWHM diameter of 1 6 0 6 corresponding to 13 5 kpc (for this source we adopt the results of the C+D robust combination, which despite the larger beam is better behaved than the C-only observation).
Under these assumptions, the molecular masses inferred from CO \ for MD94 and BX610 are M_ mol 1.7 10^ 11 and 1.1 10^ 11 M _ respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q2343-BX610 as one of the best rotating disks in the sample, with no associated neighbor, outflows from an obscured or weak AGN and star formation, and bar/spiral-like morphology.

quotes.quote: Q2343-BX610 --- Another of the best rotating disk examples among our sample, Q2343-BX610 further has no evidence for a physically associated neighbour in the available data. It exhibits signatures of gas outflows driven by a weak or obscured AGN in the center and by star formation at the location of the bright southern clump FS14 . Its rest-optical morphology shows bar- and spiral-like features FS11a , which could cause the deviations from pure rotation along the minor axis observed in the kinematics.

gmw_00000850.name: Deep3a-6004
gmw_00000850.overview: Deep3a-6004 is a massive, rotation-dominated disk galaxy [arXiv:0907.4474, arXiv:1802.07276] with a face-on, clumpy star-forming ring [arXiv:1802.07276]. Inside this ring, an active galactic nucleus drives a broad outflow around a central bulge [arXiv:1802.07276], while the star-forming phase of the ring is nearing its end [arXiv:0907.4474].

sources.summary: The paper models BzK 6004 as a z~2 rotation-dominated galaxy with a broad H-alpha ring whose star-forming phase is nearing its end while a wide stellar ring grows.

quotes.quote: BzK 6004 is also a rotation-dominated galaxy with relatively faint but broader H ring ( R/R 1.0 from Figure 5 in Genzel et al. 2008). The ring radius is R_ ring =6.9 0.8 kpc and the dispersion velocity of galaxy is =240 and the surface density of star formation rate is 0.6 0.3 yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 . The current data does not allow us to distinguish the case of different we thus just use =2 as an illustration. The dynamical evolution of the galaxy is shown by the red line in the right column of Fig. 5.

The current stage of the ring is approaching the end states of the star forming. The stellar ring is still growing fast and may remain as a wide stellar ring. The Q value shows that self-gravity is also dominated.

sources.summary: The paper describes Deep3a-6004 as a massive disk-like galaxy with misaligned kinematic and morphological axes, a face-on clumpy ring, and centrally dominant AGN-driven broad emission around a bulge.

quotes.quote: Deep3a-6004 --- This massive galaxy exhibits most compelling characteristics of a disk but with nearly orthogonal kinematic and morphological major axes ( PA = 75^ 12^ ). The inclination inferred from kinematic disk modeling Gen08, Gen14a is significantly different from that implied by the H axis ratio and indicates a quite face-on orientation ( i 25^ ) such that the bright clumpy ring-like structure and possible deviations from circularity affect the kinematic misalignment more importantly. Broad H + [ N 2 ] with elevated \ ratio associated with an AGN-driven outflow strongly dominates the line emission inside the star-forming ring, causing the apparent twist in isovelocity contours and the high velocity dispersions at the center, where a massive bulge component is in place FS14, Tacc15a .

gmw_00000851.name: Q2346-BX482
gmw_00000851.overview: Q2346-BX482 is a rotating, disk-dominated star-forming galaxy characterized by a clumpy ring structure and a small central bulge [arXiv:1010.4376, arXiv:1802.07276]. The galaxy is associated with a lower-mass companion that may be responsible for kinematic perturbations on its north-western edge [arXiv:1802.07276].

sources.summary: The paper uses BX 482 as the disk-dominated SINS application case, describing a ring-like star-forming galaxy with a small bulge, measured ring radius, turbulence, and modeled rotation curve.

quotes.quote: BX 482 and BX 389 are selected for applications, whose dynamical structure and emission lines are presented by Genzel et al. (2008) and Lehnert et al. (2009), respectively. BX 482 is rotating star-forming outer ring/disk dominated galaxy while BX 389 is central bulge/inner disk dominated (Genzel et al. 2008). On the other hand, they are located in the star forming area in the BPT diagram (Figure 9 and 10 in Lehnert et al. 2009). This sets up additional constraints on the details of both dynamical structure and emission of the two galaxies.

BX 482 is a ring-like star forming galaxy with small bulge ( 2 10^ 10 ) shown by H image, which has a ring radius of R_ ring =7.0 0.8 kpc with a width R 1 kpc and a turbulence velocity V_ tur =100 km s ^ 1 (Genzel et al. 2008).

It shows two Gaussian profiles for the bulge and ring. The rotation curve measured by H line show a similar shape of rotation curve with equation (11). We set R_c=3.5 kpc and V_c=300 for the rotation curve in this object.

sources.summary: The paper applies its supernova-driven viscosity model to BX 482, treating it as a z~2 ring-dominated star-forming galaxy whose self-gravitating ring is still forming stars while fading in H-alpha.

quotes.quote: We choose two galaxies as illustrations to apply the present theoretical model. BX 482 is a ring-dominated star-forming galaxy, of which the ring radius is R_ ring =7.0 0.8 kpc with a narrower width R 1 kpc, its dispersion velocity is =210 km s ^ 1 and the surface density of star formation rates is 0.57 0.3 yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 . We hence choose the ring model (eq. 8). The bright H ring limits the lifetime of the ring, which is about 10^7 yr in light of OB stars.

The left column of Figure 5 gives the dynamical evolution of the ring. The Toomre parameter Q shows that the ring is self-gravity dominated. A stellar ring formed in the gaseous ring is still growing shown by the red line, but the H ring is becoming dimmer.

sources.summary: The paper compares its clump star-formation rates with BX 482, assuming the galaxy's global extinction and deriving clump H-alpha SFRs from 5 to 46 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: In fact, one galaxy (BX 482) in fs11b has H measurement for each of its clumps. We calculate the H derived SFRs for each of its clumps. Since fs11b did not report extinctions for individual clumps, we assume that the dust extinction of each clump is equal to the global extinction of BX 482, A_V = 0.8 , measured through SED-fitting. We find that SFR varies from 5 M_ yr^ 1 to 46 M_ yr^ 1 for clumps in BX 482, with a median (mean) of 9 (14) M_ yr^ 1 , broadly consistent with our clump SFRs.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q2346-BX482 as a disk with a prominent clumpy star-forming ring, faint central stellar material, and a lower-mass companion that may perturb its kinematics.

quotes.quote: Q2346-BX482 --- This galaxy is another example of a disk with prominent star-forming ring strongly dominated by one bright large clump although stellar light and mass at faint levels is detected around the kinematic center based on the near-IR HST data Gen08, FS11a, Gen14a, Tacc15b . It is associated with a source 27 kpc in projection to the south-east at a relative velocity of +630 km s^ 1 , which is detected in H in the wider FOV of the seeing-limited SINFONI data and in CO 3-2 line emission in IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometric observations Tac13 . With a stellar mass of 25 that of Q2346-BX482, this companion may have induced the small kinematic perturbations on the north-western edge of the main galaxy discussed by Gen08 .

gmw_00000852.name: ZC 407302
gmw_00000852.overview: ZC 407302 is a high-redshift disk galaxy [arXiv:1802.07276]. It features a bright, compact source on its northeast edge that may be either an in-situ massive clump or an accreted satellite [arXiv:1802.07276].

sources.summary: The paper describes ZC 407302 as a disk whose northeast compact source could be an in-situ massive clump or an accreted satellite that perturbs the velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: ZC 407302 --- A bright compact source lies about 4 kpc from the center, on the northeast edge of the main body of the galaxy. Because its line-of-sight velocity is fully consistent with the extension of the velocity field of the main disk part out to this radius, it could plausibly be a massive clump that formed in-situ although we cannot rule out that it may be a small accreted satellite. The clump contains 1/20 of the total stellar mass, which could have induced the perturbations seen in velocity dispersion.

gmw_00000853.name: K20-ID7
gmw_00000853.overview: K20-ID7 is a rotation-dominated disk galaxy that features a ring-like structure [arXiv:2109.10614, arXiv:1802.07276]. The system exhibits extended southern material and is likely undergoing a minor interaction with a smaller neighboring source [arXiv:1802.07276].

sources.summary: The paper classifies GOODS-S 29868 as a rotation-dominated disk with ring-like structure apparent only in high-resolution HST and adaptive-optics maps.

quotes.quote: GOODS-S 29868 (disk): This is an extended galaxy with a ring-like shape from the HST and adaptive optics H intensity maps (not visible in the natural seeing maps) that shows an ordered rotation, with only one small additional kinematic component arising from the high-resolution data. The complexity in the HST map makes the assumption of an exponential disk problematic since the kinematic and photometric centre disagree significantly. This galaxy clearly shows the effects of beam smearing in the low-resolution maps since they resemble a smooth disk with ordered rotation with considerable flux in the centre. However, in the high-resolution maps, the real shape of the galaxy (the ring-like structure as seen in the HST map) becomes more clear and the low flux at the centre becomes evident. This system is clearly dominated by rotation with v/ =3.9 (NS) and v/ =3.4 (AO) and despite the mismatch between the photometric and kinematic centres, we classify it as a disk.

sources.summary: The paper revises K20-ID7 from a seeing-limited major-merger classification to a low-inclination disk with a ring, extended southern material, a smaller source, and a likely minor interaction.

quotes.quote: K20-ID7 --- Based on seeing-limited SINFONI data, this source had been classified as major merger mainly because of the non-axisymmetry of the velocity dispersion map Sha08 . The AO-assisted data resolve this source into a prominent ring structure with similarly regular velocity field and little variations in velocity dispersion across the system. The H and rest-optical continuum emission trace extended material to the south beyond the ring, connected to a smaller source at a projected separation of 1 6 and a relative velocity of about +350 km s^ 1 , roughly in line with the extension of the velocity gradient, and with an inferred stellar mass of 15 that of the main source. Overall, the properties of K20-ID7 at 1.5 kpc resolution appear more consistent with a large low-inclination disk undergoing a 1:7 minor interaction.

gmw_00000854.name: ZC407376
gmw_00000854.overview: ZC407376 is an interacting galaxy pair and major merger with a projected separation of 8 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1802.07276]. The system's northern component is resolved as a small disk, while the southern component exhibits irregular kinematics [arXiv:1802.07276].

sources.summary: The paper describes ZC407376 as an interacting pair and major merger whose components have similar redshifts and masses; AO data resolves the northern component as a small disk.

quotes.quote: ZC407376 is an interacting pair; the stellar mass and SFR of each component are scaled from those of the total system according to the fractions estimated from the HST /WFC3-based stellar mass map and the observed H line map, respectively.
For ZC407376, an interacting system, the profiles of the compact individual components are centrally peaked, and we also derived the sizes for each of them separately.

ZC407376 --- The two components of this interacting pair have a projected separation of 1^ (8 kpc), the same redshift within 20 km s^ 1 , nearly equal stellar masses, and derived dynamical masses within a factor of two of each other, making this system a major merger. Both components appeared as dispersion-dominated objects at seeing-limited resolution; the AO data resolves ZC407376N into a small disk with projected velocity difference of 120 km s^ 1 and V_ rot / _ 0 3 while ZC407376S still has irregular kinematics.

gmw_00000855.name: B1422+231
gmw_00000855.overview: B1422+231 is a gravitationally lensed, radio-loud quasar at a redshift of z = 3.62 [arXiv:0801.1692, arXiv:1712.01205]. It is split into four images by an early-type lensing galaxy situated within a galaxy group [arXiv:1112.0005, arXiv:1511.01097].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q1422+2309 as a four-image lensed radio-loud quasar and extracts scaled Chandra and XMM spectra while accounting for image magnification and chip-gap limits.

quotes.quote: Q1422+2309. --- This is a gravitationally-lensed, radio-loud quasar, consisting of 4 images. Kormann, Schneider, Bartelmann (1994) explored lensing models based on elliptical mass distribution and derived a total magnification factor of _t = 15.38 . Three \ (Obs-ID: 367, 1631, and 4939 PI: Garmire) and \ observations (Obs-ID: 0143652301 PI: Georgantopoulos) were carried out over a span of four years. Among the four lensed images resolved by , the brightest two images are probably affected by pileup at least in their central few pixels (Grant et al. 2004).

Since the faintest image is not bright enough to be detected in the \ data, we extracted a spectrum only from image C, the third brightest image. The magnification factor for this particular image, according to Kormann et al. is _C = 3.43 , thus we scaled down the luminosity accordingly. The spectra from the three \ observations were binned with a minimum of 25, 15, and 50 counts per bin, respectively. In our analysis of the \ observation, we used only the MOS data because in the pn the image fell on a chip gap. We summed all the counts from the MOS images since the four lensed images were not resolved. We binned the extracted spectrum with a minimum of 10 counts per bin and we scaled down the luminosity by a factor of _ tot = 15.38 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes JVAS B1422+231 radio light curves and finds published delays broadly confirmable, but multiple solution groups and method disagreement require new observations.

quotes.quote: JVAS B1422+231 For this quadruply lensed quasar, we used the data published by 2001MNRAS.326.1403P , consisting of flux density measurements at two frequencies, 8.4 and 15 GHz. Their results for the time delays were based only on the 15 GHz data without image D , which is too faint. These data are shown in Fig. fig:B1422 . They obtained t_ BA = 1.5 1.4 days, t_ AC = 7.6 2.5 , and t_ BC = 8.2 2.0 days when comparing the curves in pairs.

The NMF method allows time delays to be tested for the three light curves simultaneously, thus imposes coherence on the results. Given the error bars in the published results, which are large compared to the time delays, we can confirm the results here, but we emphasize that they include two distinct groups of solutions between which we cannot decide based on the actual light curves: for the shortest delay, we either have t_ BA 1.0 day or t_ BA 2.0 days, as shown in Fig. B1422_BA_tot_sum .

The MD method gives a completely different result: it converges towards time delays that invert the BAC-order into CAB but with error bars large enough not to exclude the BAC order of t_ BA = -1.6 2.1 days, t_ AC = -0.8 2.9 , and t_ BC = -2.4 2.7 days. New observations are clearly necessary to reduce the uncertainties in both the different solutions and the error bars that are too large in comparison with the time delays to be useful to any further analysis.

sources.summary: The paper presents a single-epoch XMM-Newton spectrum of lensed quasar B1422+231, finding Seyfert-like absorption, reflection/partial-covering models, and thermal Comptonization from a corona.

quotes.quote: We thus picked-up the z=3.62 quasar B1422+231 whose flux, enhanced by gravitationally lensing, is proven to be among the brightest lensed quasars in X-rays (F _ 2-10 keV 10 ^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ). The X-ray spectrum of B1422+231 is found to be very similar to the one of a typical nearby Seyfert galaxy. Neutral absorption is clearly detected (N _ H 5 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 at the redshift of the source) while a strong absorption edge is measured at E 7.5 keV with an optical depth of 0.14. We also find hints of the FeK line in emission at E 6.4 keV line (EW 70 eV) and a hump is detected in the E 15-20 keV energy band (rest-frame) in excess of what predicted by a simple absorbed power-law.

The spectrum can be best-modeled with two rather complex models; one assumes the presence of ionized and partially covering matter along the line of sight while the other is characterized by the presence of a reflection component. We argue that the reflection seems more plausible here on a statistical basis. In this scenario, the primary emission of B1422+231 is most probably dominated by the thermal Comptonization of UV seed photons in a corona with kT 40 keV. We also detected a reflection component with relative direct-to-reflect normalization 1.

sources.summary: B1422+231 is a z=3.62 lensed, moderately radio-loud quasar whose X-ray spectra imply a low high-energy cutoff and an optically thick, low-temperature corona.

quotes.quote: To fill this observational gap, we selected two luminous (log( ) >47.5 erg/s) quasars, 2MASSJ1614346+470420 (z=1.86) and B1422+231 (z=3.62), and obtained \ and \ deep observations for them. We performed detailed spectral analysis of their quasi-simultaneous soft and hard X-ray data, in order to constrain the parameters of their coronae. Using a phenomenological cut-off power-law model, with the inclusion of a reflection component, we derived rest-frame values of the high energy cut-off of =106^ +102 _ 37 keV and =66^ +17 _ 12 keV, respectively. Comptonization models consistently give as best-fit parameters electron temperatures of 45 keV and 28 keV, respectively, and optically thick coronae ( >1 ).
The brightest lensed source in this catalog is B1422+231 (B1422 hereafter). The lensing factor is estimated to be 20 (Assef et al. 2011).

We note that B1422 is classified as moderately radio loud ( R=90 , Dadina et al. 2016) with a steep radio continuum ( _r=0.9 , Orienti et al. 2007), indicating that the source is highly inclined in the plane of the sky. In this case, the radio emission should be dominated by the lobes, and not by the jet, and the X-ray spectrum is not strongly contaminated by the jet component (see discussion in Dadina et al. 2016).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes B1422+231 as a quadruply lensed radio-loud quasar whose ALMA flux-ratio anomaly can be reproduced by PBH microlensing models.

quotes.quote: We examine the flux density ratio anomaly in the quadruply-imaged strong gravitational lens, B1422+231, and consider the contribution of 10-10^3M_ primordial black holes (PBHs) as a potential dark matter constituent. We describe the first flux density ratio measurement of B1422+231 in the millimeter-wave band using the Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA). This fills an important multi-wavelength gap in our knowledge of this key lensed system. The flux density of the quasar at 233 GHz is dominated by synchrotron emission and the source size is estimated to be 66.9 pc. The observed flux density ratios at 233 GHz are similar to those measured in radio, mid-infrared and optical bands, which cannot be explained by a simple smooth mass model of the lens galaxy.

B1422+231 is a quadruply-lensed radio-loud quasar that is known for its flux density ratio anomaly. It has previously been observed in radio, infrared, optical and X-ray wave bands. We observed B1422+231 for the first time in the mm-wave band with ALMA.

sources.summary: The paper identifies B 1422+231 as the strongest substructure candidate, with cusp flux-ratio violations and failed physical smooth-model fits to its astrometry and flux ratios.

quotes.quote: The instance that they selected, the quadruplet B 1422+231, has three highly magnified bright images and one much fainter image. Three highly magnified images occur generically near a cusp, and a Taylor expansion gives a universal relationship that the sum of the fluxes of the two outer images should equal the flux of the middle image. This is strongly violated in B 1422+231 leaving substructure on top of the smooth model as the believable culprit. This result is supported by the detailed models of B 1422+231 by Bradac et al. (2002) which find that the discrepancy requires substructure on the mass scale 10^6 .

Using our SVD method, it was impossible to reproduce the reported errors in the relative astrometry, together with the assumed uncertainties in the flux ratios (5 per cent for the radio, 10 per cent for the optical) at least with physical models. Unphysical models are readily identified as the equidensity contours are self-intersecting. Accordingly, for this lens only, the errors in the relative astrometry were inflated by a factor of 10 to 30 mas. Figure fig:thirdlens now shows the results of our fitting procedure in four flux bands, but none of the results can be said to be at all satisfactory.

sources.summary: The paper says B1422+231 changes from inconclusive to downward curvature, but its lens-galaxy measurement is less reliable because a nearby point source leaves uncertain subtraction artifacts and large errors.

quotes.quote: B1422+231 goes from an inconclusive result to displaying a downwards curvature. However, on its data frames, one of the point sources appears close in projection to the galaxy. Therefore, separating signal from that point source and from the lens is highly uncertain. We address that problem by applying a weighting mask to B1422+231 with null weights for the ill pixels. However, in spite of that treatment, some artefacts from the sources subtraction may remain on the B1422+231 data frames.

As can be seen in Tables tab_errreff and tab_errflux , the uncertainties coming from the positions and intensities of the sources are high for that system, and it has particularly large relative error bars. We therefore consider it less conclusive regarding the de Vaucouleurs profile test than the other systems.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VLA monitoring of the four-image gravitationally lensed quasar JVAS B1422+231, reporting significant 8.4/15 GHz variability, difficult short time-delay measurements, and the system's z=3.62 source plus elliptical lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of two seasons of archival, multi-frequency VLA monitoring of the quad lens system JVAS B1422+231, the 15-GHz data of which have previously been published. The 8.4- and 15-GHz variability curves show significant variability, especially in polarization, but lack features on short time-scales that would be necessary for an accurate measurement of the very short predicted time delays ( 1 d) between the three bright images. Time delays can only realistically be measured to the very faint image D and for the first time we detect its long-term variability and determine its polarization properties.

JVAS B1422+231 patnaik92b is a four-image gravitational lens system discovered as part of the Jodrell Bank/VLA Astrometric Survey JVAS -- patnaik92 . A so-called `cusp' lens e.g. keeton03a , three of the images (A, B and C) are bright ( 100 mJy at 8.4 GHz) and approximately collinear whilst the fourth is more than thirty times fainter than the brightest image and lies close to and on the other side of the lensing galaxy. The maximum angular separation is 1.3 arcsec. The background source is a z = 3.62 quasar and the lensing galaxy is an elliptical yee94,impey96 that forms part of a group at z = 0.338 kundic97a,tonry98 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q1422+231 as an ultrabright quadruple gravitationally lensed QSO and uses Keck HIRES spectra of its A and C images to compare Lyman-forest absorption.

quotes.quote: The most suitable object known for this sort of study is the ultrabright, quadruple, gravitationally lensed QSO Q1422+231 ( z_ em = 3.61 ; Patnaik et al 1992). This object has been observed for similar purposes previously at low resolution. Bechtold Yee (1995) concluded from ground-based spectra that there are differences of typically about 50 \ in HI column density for beam separations on the order of 100 parsec. In contrast, Petry, Impey Foltz (1998), from HST FOS spectra found very little difference both in column density and velocity between the various lines of sight.

In section 2 we describe Keck HIRES (Vogt et al. 1994) spectra of the A and C images of Q1422+231. The properties of this lens system are summarized on the CASTLES web site (http://cfa-www.harvard.edu/castles/B1422.html). The observations of images A and C of Q1422+231 and the methods used to reduce the data are discussed in section 2.

sources.summary: The paper models B1422+231 as a gravitationally lensed system whose image fluxes and shapes require mass substructure in the lens galaxy beyond smooth lens models.

quotes.quote: In this work we investigate the gravitationally lensed system B1422+231. High-quality VLBI image positions, fluxes and shapes as well as an optical HST lens galaxy position are used. First, two simple and smooth models for the lens galaxy are applied to fit observed image positions and fluxes; no even remotely acceptable model was found. Such models also do not accurately reproduce the image shapes.

In particular, the gravitational lens system B1422+231 was explored in detail, and as was first mentioned by ma98 , mass substructure in the lens galaxy might provide an explanation for the failures in flux modelling. In this paper, we will study the influence of the substructure on the lens system B1422+231, using VLBI radio measurements of the system by pa99 . The present work is differs from the afore mentioned in that slightly different models for the lens in B1422+231 system are used and that we also include image shape constraints in the fit.

sources.summary: The paper uses B1422+231 as a QSO-galaxy lensing system with anomalous flux ratios, modeling it to test submillimeter millilensing detection of CDM substructure.

quotes.quote: As an example, we choose a typical QSO-galaxy lensing system, B1422+231, with cusp-caustics in order to check the observational feasibility of mapping substructures. The typical QSO-galaxy lensing system B1422+231 shows an anomalous flux ratio. The images consist of three highly magnified images A, B, and C, and a faint one D located near the lens galaxy. The observed mid-infrared flux ratios are A/|B|=0.94 0.05 and C/|B|=0.57 0.06 (Chiba et al. 2005).

However, B1422+231 shows a 20 deviation from the f=0 relation. Assuming that such an anomaly is caused by the presence of an SIS perturber in the macrolens halo, the anomaly might be in image A (Dobler Keeton 2005). The redshifts of the source and the lens are z_S=3.62 and z_L=0.34 , respectively.

sources.summary: B1422+231 is treated as a four-image radio-loud quasar lens with a luminous elliptical lens galaxy, group environment, significant shear, X-ray detection, and anomalous flux ratios.

quotes.quote: B1422+231 (hereafter B1422) is a four-image lens discovered by patnaikb1422-92 while searching for small-separation lenses among flat spectrum radio sources in the Jodrell Bank--VLA Astrometric Survey JVAS; patnaik-92, browne-98,wilkinson-98,kingb2114-99 . The source is a radio loud quasar at z_s=3.62 patnaikb1422-92 , and the lens is a luminous elliptical at z_l=0.3374 impeyb1422-96, kundicb1422-97 . The lens galaxy and five nearby galaxies form a group at z_g = 0.338 with a rest-frame line-of-sight velocity dispersion of _r = 550 50 \ kundicb1422-97 .

Lens models for B1422 require a significant shear 0.20 -0.26, which may be attributable to the group environment hoggb1422, keeton-97,dobler . Indeed, from their estimate of the group's velocity dispersion and centroid, kundicb1422-97 estimated =0.23 and pointed out that the group will also create some convergence that may affect the lens potential. grantb1422-04 detected B1422 in X-rays (0.5--2 keV) and determined a temperature of kT=1.0^ + _ 0.3 keV, which is consistent with the value expected for a poor group (MZ98). B1422 is another lens with ``anomalous'' flux ratios mao,chiba .

sources.summary: The paper presents B1422+231 as a giant elliptical lens system whose source structure complicates rotation measures, but whose differential RM resembles Q0957+561.

quotes.quote: Both the lens systems Q0957+561 and B1422+231 have a giant elliptical galaxy as the dominant lens. While the former is part of a galaxy-cluster, the latter might be associated with a group of galaxies. An analysis of the Faraday Rotation measurements in these and similar systems could provide evidence for possible existence of coherent large--scale magnetic fields in elliptical galaxies.

Possibly the source structure in the lens system B1422+231 has similarities with B0218+357 at VLB scales, and, not surprisingly, straightforward estimation of the absolute Faraday Rotation along individual images results in a large value of RM for both these systems. The source in B1422+231 has smaller fraction of polarization and hence, the change of polarization direction within the milliarcsecond scale structures is expected to affect the rotation measure estimates. Interestingly, the differential RM is coincidentally similar to the value arrived at for 0957+561, where the rotation is probably caused by a similar giant elliptical galaxy at comparable redshift.

sources.summary: The paper describes B1422+231 as a cusp-caustic quadruple lens with a z=3.62 quasar, z=0.34 primary lensing galaxy, radio/HST constraints, and the strongest flux-ratio anomaly.

quotes.quote: B1422+231 The cusp-caustic lens B1422+231 consists of three bright images A, B, C, and a faint image D. Images A and C are minima, and B and D are saddles. The quasar redshift z_S=3.62 is the largest in our four samples and the primary lensing galaxy is at z_L=0.34 kundic1997b,tonry1998 and the measured ellipticity and the position angle of near-infrared (near-IR) light distribution are e=0.39 0.02 and _e=-58.90 0.80(^ ) sluse2012 . We use the radio flux ratios koopmans2003 of four images at 5 GHz averaged over a period of 8.5 months, which are consistent with the MIR counterparts chiba2002 . We also use the astrometry of lensed images and the centroid of the primary lensing galaxy in sluse2012 obtained from the use of Magain-Courbin-Sohy (MCS) deconvolution algorithm applied in an iterative way (ISMCS) to near-IR Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images.
Using three lensed images ( N_ image =3 ), we find that for B1422+231 is non-zero at 20 level, implying that the flux-ratio anomaly is most prominent.

sources.summary: The paper measures the B1422+231 lensing galaxy profile but finds its half-light radius especially uncertain because a nearby projected point source complicates galaxy-light separation.

quotes.quote: In particular, in the case of B1422+231, a point source appeared close in projection to the elliptical galaxy. Distinguishing the light from that specific point source and from the galaxy pixels on top of it is extremely uncertain. The PSF subtraction produces spurious artefacts in regions where the lensing galaxy is bright, yielding to systematic errors in our half-light radius measurement. This problem is treated by using a mask cancelling the value of the ill pixels.

First, our r_ eff of B1422+231 is smaller than both MCS and IMFITFITS results. As explained above, one of the point sources lies very close in projection to the lens, making this measurement very tricky.

sources.summary: The paper applies its lens-model selection method to JVAS B1422+231, using image positions and ellipticities to test SIS-plus-external-shear models for the quadruple lens.

quotes.quote: JVAS B1422+231 In , we already analysed the quadruple-image lens JVAS B1422+231, first described by . Fig. fig:B1422 shows the image labels chosen. Using the image positions measured by the , the distances in mas between the image pairs are _ AB1 &= 389.25 , & _ AB2 = 319.98 , _ AC1 &= 333.88 , & _ AC2 = 747.71 , _ BC1 &= 723.13 , & _ BC2 = 1067.70 . The image ellipticities as determined by are _A = 0.80 0.07 , _B = 0.70 0.07 , _C = 0.55 0.09 .

Matching these intervals with the potential-derivative ratios of an SIS+E model, with the possible parameter ranges determined as described in sec:implementation_sise , we immediately conclude that a 1, 9 10^ 4 , _1 0.03, 0.3 can be excluded. We find possible parameter combinations for a and _1 on the semi-major axis, i.e. \ ^ (0) = /2 , as shown in Fig. fig:parameters_B1422 ^ (0) = 0 can be excluded from time delay information as measured in . .

sources.summary: The paper treats B1422+231 as a four-image lensed quasar whose wavelength-dependent flux ratios reveal substructure: a massive clump near image A and a smaller variable perturber near image B.

quotes.quote: The optical and radio flux ratios in the four-image lens B1422+231 imply that there is a 10^ 4 - 10^ 7 \ mass clump in the lens galaxy in front of image A; this is the first constraint on the mass of a particular clump lying in a distant galaxy and detected by its mass. The data also indicate that a small clump, perhaps a star, is passing in front of image B and making the optical flux ratios variable. Both of these hypotheses can be tested with new observations. B1422+231 demonstrates how data at different wavelengths can be used in individual lenses to probe individual mass clumps in distant galaxies.

B1422+231 consists of a radio-loud quasar at redshift z_s=3.62 that is lensed into four images by an elliptical galaxies at redshift z_l=0.34 (Patnaik et al. \ 1992). The lens galaxies lies in a poor group of galaxies that contributes an important tidal shear to the lensing potential (Hogg Blandford 1994; Kundi et al. \ 1997).

sources.summary: The paper models B1422+231 as a quadruply imaged quasar lens whose flux ratios challenge smooth lens models, motivating substructure in the main lens galaxy.

quotes.quote: The system B1422+231 is a well studied example of a quadruply imaged quasar, with high-quality VLBI data available. Very accurate data on image positions, fluxes and deconvolved image sizes provide good constraints for lensing models. We discuss here the failure of smooth models in fitting the data.

The lensing galaxy has been observed in the optical; its redshift has been determined to be 0.338 and its position relative to image B has been measured im96 . The main lens galaxy is a member of a compact group with a median projected radius of 35 h^ 1 kpc and velocity dispersion of 550 km s^ 1 ku97 .
The resulting ^2 = 5.6 remained high; this family of models considered does not seem to be adequate for the description of the galaxy in B1422+231 lens system.

sources.summary: The paper finds B1422+231 consistent with the cusp relation despite previous detailed models showing a flux-ratio anomaly, illustrating the limits of its generic analysis.

quotes.quote: B1422+231 Patnaik Narasimha (2001) report 61 measurements of the radio fluxes for B1422+231 at 8.4 and 15 GHz. Koopmans et al. \ (2003a) report 41 measurements at 5 GHz that yield =0.187 0.004 , in reasonable agreement with the value from the Patnaik Narasimha (2001) data. The optical and near-IR data from CASTLES yield =0.223 0.055 in V-band, 0.222 0.038 in I-band, and 0.175 0.015 in H-band. The weighted average of the optical/near-IR results is =0.184 0.014 , in remarkably good agreement with the radio results. Rcusp-obs joint show that B1422+231 is not an outlier in the cusp relation according to our analysis.
This result is interesting in light of detailed modeling that shows B1422+231 to have a flux ratio anomaly (Mao Schneider 1998; Brada et al. \ 2002; Metcalf Zhao 2002).

sources.summary: The paper models B1422+231 with multipole lens-galaxy potentials, finding that shear makes a physical solution possible, but higher-order fits still require implausible localized density wiggles.

quotes.quote: To motivate the need for external shear, we first study B1422+231 using a model that does not include shear. To facilitate comparison with the results of Evans_flux_ratios , we consider a fifth order multipole model, which fits the data of Impey_1422 and Patnaik_1422 exactly. It is clear from Figure fig: Zero shear that this model is unphysical.

In other words, away from the images the model is smooth thanks to the minimum wiggle criterion. But there must be small-scale structure in the vicinity of the images in order to explain the observed flux ratios. Since the wiggles in the isodensity contour are not dramatic, it is not clear whether they should be interpreted as real features or just as approximations of other sorts of structure (such as CDM clumps).

sources.summary: The paper finds B1422 + 231 retains anomalous mid-infrared flux ratios, implying lensing by a subhalo with an estimated Einstein-aperture mass near 200 solar masses.

quotes.quote: PG1115 + 080 at redshift z_ S =1.72 and B1422 + 231 at z_ S =3.62 are lensed by foreground ellipticals at z_ L = 0.31 and 0.34, respectively. The latter shows the colinear, three highly magnified images, A, B, and C, and this configuration emerges if the QSO is close to and inside a cusp caustic.

The mid-infrared flux ratios are given as A2 / A1 =0.93 0.06 , B / A1 =0.16 0.07 , and C / A1 =0.21 0.04 for PG1115 + 080, and (A + C )/ B =1.51 0.06 , A / B =0.94 0.05 , and C / B =0.57 0.06 for B1422 + 231. This suggests that the mid-infrared flux ratio for A2 / A1 of PG1115 + 080 is consistent with the prediction of a smooth lens model ( 1 ), while the optical flux ratio is much smaller ( 0.65 ), and that the mid-infrared flux ratios for (A, B, C) images of B1422 + 231 remain anomalous, contrary to the prediction of a smooth lens. For B1422 + 231, the presence of flux anomaly even in mid-infrared waveband suggests M_ E 200 M _ , i.e., lensing by a subhalo is most likely.

sources.summary: The paper treats B1422+231 as a cusp lens with nominally strong time-delay anomalies, but says the large timing uncertainties prevent firm conclusions and motivate further study.

quotes.quote: The anomalies in RX J1131 1231 provide strong evidence for substructure in the lens potential, while at this point the apparent anomalies in B1422+231 mainly indicate that the time delay measurements need to be improved.

For 1422, the nominal values of the time delays Patnaik-1422-tdel among the three cusp images A, B, and C indicate strong anomalies. However, the uncertainties in the claimed time delays are not much smaller than the time delays themselves, so conclusive statements are impossible at this point. (Notice, for example, that the range of P-values for the AB pair spans 0 to 1.00 given the uncertainties.) The system clearly warrants further study, especially since it is well-known to have anomalous flux ratios Mao-flux, Bradac-1422 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Keck LRIS spectra for B1422+231, interpolates two absorption troughs before measuring line widths, and flags possible problems in Greene et al. measurements.

quotes.quote: B1422+231 --- We use the LRIS Keck II UV/optical spectrum of tonry98 . We interpolated over the two small absorption troughs near 6875 \ and 7020 \ before measuring the \ widths directly from the data. Our treatment of these regions did not affect the resulting GH fit to the data. From the greene10 data, we cannot assess the reliability of their fit to the H profile, because they plotted the \ spectrum of HE1104--1805 in place of the spectrum of B1422+231. In order to be conservative, we therefore flag B1422+231 as one of the objects with possible problems in the sample of greene10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses B1422+231 as a cusp-caustic quasar lens with four images, z_S=3.62 and z_L=0.34, incorporating Subaru MIR fluxes to study flux-ratio anomalies.

quotes.quote: The cusp-caustic lens B1422+231 consists of three bright images A, B, C, and a faint image D. Images A, C are minima and B, D are saddles. It was first discovered by patnaik1992 using the Very Large Array. The quasar redshift is z_S=3.62 and the lensing galaxy is at z_L=0.34 kundic1997b,tonry1998 . The MIR image fluxes were measured by chiba2005 using the Subaru telescope.

sources.summary: The paper describes JVAS B1422+231 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: JVAS B1422+231 (herein B1422) is a four image lens discovered in the Jordell Bank -- VLA Astrometric Survey JVAS, patnaik92a, patnaik92b, king99 . The source is a z_s=3.62 radio-loud quasar lensed by a z_ l =0.338 luminous elliptical galaxy impey96, kundic97b . Using the radio light curves, patnaik01 measure the time delays between the images to be 1.5 1.4 days (between B and A), 7.6 2.5 days (between A and C), and 8.2 2.0 days (between B and C). The lens belongs to a group of 16 galaxies with =470^ +100 _ 90 kundic97b, my . grant and fassnacht08 detect the diffuse X-ray emission of the group.

sources.summary: The paper identifies B1422+231 as an early-type lens in a galaxy group, noting uncertain time-delay measurements and X-ray emission from the corresponding group.

quotes.quote: B1422+231 (hereafter 1422 ) is an early-type galaxy 1992MNRAS.259P...1P,1994AJ....107...28Y . Although time delays have been reported 2001MNRAS.326.1403P , it is possible that the measurements are aliases of the actual values 2003AJ....126...29R and hence are not included in our analysis. The lens is part of a group with 16 spectroscopically confirmed member galaxies 2006ApJ...641..169M . Using newer data, additional members could be identified 2011ApJ...726...84W . 2004ApJ...610..686G also detect X-ray emission from the corresponding group originating from a position angle of about 145 ^ at a temperature of 1.0 keV.

sources.summary: The paper treats B1422 as a narrow-line quad, uses a population-mean halo-mass prior, and finds accepted realizations with dark matter near the merging triplet image.

quotes.quote: We use this procedure to construct a prior for the halo mass of each quad, with the exceptions of B1422, PS J1606, and WGD J0405. The stellar velocity dispersions implied by the Einstein radii of these systems is significantly lower than the stellar velocity dispersion in the sample of quads used to calibrate the halo-to-stellar mass ratio in Lagattuta++10 , and as such the estimate of the halo mass using the above procedure may not be accurate for these systems. For B1422, PS J1606, and WGD J0405, we therefore assume the population mean of 10^ 13.3 0.3 inferred by Lagattuta++10 .

The top-ranked realizations for B1422 shown in Figure fig:best_realizations each have a relatively massive dark matter halo, or several smaller ones, located near the top left merging triplet image with (normalized) flux 0.88. This is in agreement with the analysis by Nierenberg++14 , who find that a blob of dark matter near this image brings the model-predicted flux ratios into agreement with a smooth lens model.

sources.summary: The paper uses Q1422+231 as a high-redshift lensed quasar target, describing its four images, lensing geometry, luminosity, unresolved A/B sightlines, and suitability for probing intervening absorption.

quotes.quote: Q1422+231 ( z_ em = 3.625 ) is a well-studied quasar and actually consists of four closely spaced lensed images with separations of 0.5 -- 1.3 arcsec; the lensing galaxy is at z_ lens = 0.34 (Patnaik et al. \ 1992; Kundic et al 1997; Tonry 1998). Gravitational lenses enhance the emission from high redshift QSOs making them more powerful probes of the intergalactic and interstellar medium, but obviously the sight lines will sample different spatial regions of the intervening material. Its redshift and luminosity (V=16.5) have made Q1422+231 an ideal candidate with which to probe intervening material through closely spaced sightlines (Petry et al. \ 1998; Rauch et al. \ 1999). The observations reported here are of the closely spaced A and B components. In our observations the sightlines are unresolved.

sources.summary: The paper uses B1422+231 as a detailed four-image lens case where optical and radio flux ratios require a mass clump, supporting constraints on dark subhalos.

quotes.quote: In B1422+231, the optical A/C flux ratio is largely consistent with smooth lens models while the radio A/C flux ratio is not (Fig. 2). Simultaneously explaining the optical and radio flux ratios and the shape of the radio image requires a mass clump in front of image A. A highly concentrated, point mass clump must have a mass 10^ 4 - 10^ 5 \ M_ , while a more extended isothermal sphere must have a mass 10^ 6 - 10^ 7 \ M_ . This is the first measurement of a particular clump lying in a distant galaxy ( z_l=0.34 ) and detected by its mass.

In the future, detailed analyses of individual clumps as in B1422+231 will be combined with statistical analyses like to constrain not only the substructure mass fraction but also the masses, densities, and sizes of dark subhalos, and the substructure mass function.

sources.summary: The paper uses B1422+231 as a lensed QSO system whose elliptical lens galaxy dark halo is modeled for MASCO microlensing detectability with future high-resolution VLBI.

quotes.quote: A possible target lens system is B1422 + 231 in which the source is a radio-loud QSO at redshift z_s=3.62 and the lens is an elliptical galaxy at redshift z_L=0.34 . Although the method we propose here is similar to that of Wambsganss Paczy'nski (1992) in which the target compact object mass is set to M 10^ 6 , the lensing effect by MASCOs can be quite different since the Einstein radius is much smaller than the size of a source and the total number in a dark halo is large. In this Letter , we show our simulation results for the lens system B1422 + 231 microlensed by MASCOs, that would be observable by the future VLBI network.

Because the ellipticity of the lens in B1422+231 is modest, it can be approximated by that of a singular isothermal sphere (SIS) with surface mass density (R)= ^2/2 G R where R is the projected radial distance from the lens center. The radial distances of the images A, B, and C from the center are roughly equal to the linear Einstein radius, _0 D_L _E = 3.8 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper models B1422+231 as a radio cusp-caustic lens and finds its observed cusp violation consistent with extragalactic CDM halos.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:r_prob1422 shows the distribution of R_ cusp for B1422+231 with the expected population of extragalactic halos only. The first thing to note is the marked asymmetry in the distribution. As previously seen ( ; ; ), the magnifications of negative magnification images are affected by substructure differently than positive magnification images. When substructure is added, R_ cusp is biased toward positive values. Also shown in figure fig:r_prob1422 is the observed value of R_ cusp for comparison. There is a perfectly reasonable probability of 0.28 that R_ cusp would be even larger than the observed value.

sources.summary: The paper models B1422+231 as a four-image radio/optical lens with anomalous flux ratios, identifying image A as perturbed and estimating the minimum substructure mass needed.

quotes.quote: The 4-image radio and optical lens B1422+231 was the first system identified as likely to contain substructure based on its anomalous flux ratios MS . The fluxes of images A, B, and C violate the relation f_A-f_B+f_C 0 generically expected for a lens in a `cusp configuration' corresponding to a source lying near a cusp caustic ( sch ; mao2 ; but see cuspreln ). To model the lens, we use the image positions and fluxes from the 8.4 GHz observations by pa .
However, if we consider A to be perturbed, then we get a good fit with ^2/ = 3.1/3 . We then apply our substructure analysis to this model to find the minimum clump mass required to perturb image A.

sources.summary: The paper images the lensed QSO B1422 + 231, finds its mid-infrared image flux ratios agree with radio ratios, and argues the anomaly likely reflects a CDM subhalo.

quotes.quote: We present mid-infrared imaging at 11.7 m for the quadruple lens systems, PG1115 + 080 and B1422 + 231, using the cooled mid-infrared camera and spectrometer (COMICS) attached on the Subaru telescope. These lensed QSOs are characterized by their anomalous optical and radio flux ratios as obtained for (A1, A2) images of PG1115 + 080 and (A, B, C) images of B1422 + 231, respectively, i.e., such flux ratios are hardly reproduced by lens models with smooth mass distribution. Our mid-infrared observations for these images have revealed that the mid-infrared flux ratio A2 / A1 of PG1115 + 080 is virtually consistent with smooth lens models (but inconsistent with the optical flux ratio), whereas for B1422 + 231, the mid-infrared flux ratios among (A, B, C) are in good agreement with the radio flux ratios. We also identify a clear infrared bump in the spectral energy distributions of these QSOs, thereby indicating that the observed mid-infrared fluxes originate from a hot dust torus around a QSO nucleus.
For B1422 + 231, we obtain M_E 209 M_ , indicating that a CDM subhalo is more likely, having the mass of M_ 100 ^ SIS 7.4 10^4 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper uses B1422+231 as a radio-detected cusp-caustic violation lens: its flux anomaly motivates substructure tests, its R_cusp value sets the violation threshold, and globular clusters were estimated to perturb it.

quotes.quote: One extreme case of flux-ratio anomalies is the cusp-caustic violation, which has been observed in five lens systems of a cusp geometry: B0712+472 ( Jackson2000B0712,Jackson98B0712 ), B1422+231 ( Impey1996B1422,Patnaik2001B1422 ), B2045+265 ( Fassnacht1999B2045 ), RXJ1131-1231 ( Sluse2003aaJ1131 ) and RXJ0911+0551 ( Bade1997aa317, Burud1998apjl ). In the first three cases (those detected in radio observations) these have been attributed to substructure lensing, while the remaining two are thought to be due to microlensing ( Anguita2008Microlensing, Morgan2006RXJ1131 ).

Any cusp source with R_ cusp 0.187 is defined as a cusp-violation case, as 0.187 is the observed value for B1422+231, which shows the smallest violation (smallest value) among the five observed cusp lenses.

A simple estimate of their contribution was made by MS1998mn , who concluded that a surface density fluctuation of a few per cent ( 0.01 ) from GCs would be enough to cause the observed flux-ratio anomaly in B1422+231.

sources.summary: The paper presents B 1422+231 as a bright four-component lensed quasar that entered the GRI inclusion region but was marked extended rather than rejected by ELQS morphology cuts.

quotes.quote: (4) B 1422+231 This lensed quasar has a total of four components and is measured to be at z=3.62 Patnaik1992 . It is very bright with m_ i =15.31 . While it's image quality flags are not raised and it actually falls into the GRI inclusion region, it is marked as extended ( type =3 ) by SDSS. However, it would not have been rejected by our selection for it's morphology (see Sec. sec_gal_rejection ).

sources.summary: The paper describes JVAS B1422+231 as a quadruple lens from JVAS whose lensed nature was supported and whose early-type lens lies in a group.

quotes.quote: JVAS B1422+231 (g) . This quadruple system was discovered in Patnaik1992 by Patnaik1992 during the Jodrell/VLA Astrometric Survey. The lensed nature of JVAS B1422+231 was first noticed by Lawrence1992 and then supported by Remy1993 . According to Tonry1998 , as first published by Kundic1997b , the lens, an early-type galaxy Impey1996 , is part of a group at a redshift of z_ g =0.339 0.002 .

gmw_00000856.name: NGC 6340
gmw_00000856.overview: NGC 6340 is a symmetrical lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1509.02356] that is likely the remnant of a major merger [arXiv:0904.4606]. It features a bright bulge [arXiv:0801.3969], an inclined, star-forming polar disk [arXiv:0904.4606], and a system of rings or tightly wound spiral arms [arXiv:1509.02356].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6340 as an old S0 merger remnant with a young inclined polar disc, dust lanes, shock-ionized gas, and a metal-rich compact pseudo-bulge.

quotes.quote: We confirm the existence of an inclined gaseous disc mentioned as a ``starforming polar ring'' in Silchenko00 . Given its orientation obtained from the kinematical data and asymmetry in the profiles of NGC 6340 stellar age and emission line fluxes, we conclude that it is rotating counter-clockwise with the SW part above the plane of the main stellar disc of NGC 6340. Its plane is inclined by 40--65 deg with the respect to the large scale stellar disc. If we consider that the polar disc contains dusty ISM observed in the colour maps, and a little amount of young stars, it will explain why the NE part of the galaxy looks older: the old population in the SW part is partially obscured by the dusty young disc in front of it. On the other hand, the main old stellar disc and pseudo-bulge are also expected to contain significant quantities of dust Driver+07 even lacking the current star formation, which will explain why the emission line intensities in the part of the young star-forming polar disc behind the main stellar disc are lower.

As it was argued above, NGC 6340 is probably a result of a major merger of two galaxies having unequal masses and/or different morphologies, which happened over 12 Gyr ago and induced a major star formation event. This hypothesis is supported by the internal structure of the galaxy, distributions of its stellar content, and significant disc overheating. The central region of NGC 6340 corresponding to the innermost S ersic component of the brightness profile with n=1.6 , i.e. pseudo-bulge, and having metal-rich stellar population ( [ Fe/H ] =-0.02  dex) with no radial gradients, was probably created by the strong star formation event triggered by a major merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6340 as a symmetrical lenticular galaxy whose disk shows stellar-population asymmetry, anomalous two-color position, and visible rings or tightly wound spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 6340. This is a symmetrical lenticular galaxy. The structure, evolution, and the composition of the stellar population of NGC 6340 were studied in detail in chi09 based on photometric and spectrosopic data. chi09 detected an asymmetry in the composition of the stellar population of the disk of NGC 6340: the ages of stars in the southwest part of the disk are half the ages of those in the northeast part (5 Gyr in the southwest and 10 Gyr in the northeast). The nucleus, bulge, and disk occupy a region in the lower part of the NCS in two-color diagrams, where they are indistinguishable from the components of other early-type galaxies.

One exception is the position of the NGC 6340 disk on the (U-B) (B-V) diagram, where it is shifted upward and to the right of the NCS. Anomalously low values of U-B are characteristic of stellar systems with moderate ( M_2/M_1 0.1 ) secondary bursts of star formation with durations to several tens of millions of years. Such photometric parameters are characteristic of spiral arms. A system of rings (or tightly wound spiral arms) is clearly visible in the images of NGC 6340 presented in chi09 , obtained after subtracting the contributions of the bulge and disk from the image of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6340 as an S0/a group galaxy with a lens, faint spiral arms, unusual dust lanes, ring-like structures, a bright bulge, and a chemically decoupled nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 6340. It is an S0/a galaxy, a member of a group, although there are no other galaxies of comparable luminosity in its immediate vicinity. The image of the galaxy features a bright inner disk---lens---and low-contrast, outer spiral arms. Long, conspicuous dust lanes of unusual shape can be seen in the central part of the disk, which are most likely located outside the plane of the disk. However, the most striking features are thin fragments of rings or spiral structures similar to those observed in NGC 1167, but somewhat more fuzzy and confined by a narrower zone of the disk. The galaxy has a bright bulge with a luminosity almost half the disk luminosity.

Two-dimensional spectroscopy of the circumnuclear region of the galaxy revealed a chemically decoupled nucleus consisting of old stars with a relatively high abundance of heavy elements, and probably a circumnuclear polar ring located within =500  pc  [ 24, 25 ] . Following Sil'chenko  [ 24 ] , we adopt for this galaxy a distance of 19.8 Mpc.

gmw_00000857.name: HIZOA J1353 58
gmw_00000857.overview: HIZOA J1353 58 is a newly detected galaxy with a low neutral hydrogen mass and a narrow velocity width [arXiv:1602.02922]. Located near the Circinus galaxy, it is considered a possible companion to Circinus and is assigned the same redshift-independent distance of 4.2 Mpc [arXiv:1602.02922].

sources.summary: The survey identifies HIZOA J1353 58 as a new low-H I-mass, narrow-line galaxy near Circinus, probably related to it and assigned the same redshift-independent distance.

quotes.quote: A notable new galaxy is HIZOA J1353 58, a possible companion to the Circinus galaxy.

The narrow velocity width, low- mass ( M_ HI = 7.24 ) detection HIZOA J1353 58, less than 8 from Circinus, has a recessional velocity that is only about 200 \ different from the large galaxy. At the low recession velocities of these objects of only a few hundred , Hubble flow distances are very uncertain, but it seems likely this newly-detected object is a previously-unknown galaxy which is related to the Circinus galaxy. As its velocity corrected to the Local Group frame is negative, and thus a Hubble distance is undefined in this frame, we adopt a redshift-independent distance measurement to Circinus, 4.2 Mpc (Tully 2009), as the distance to both HIZOA J1413 65 and HIZOA J1353 58.

gmw_00000858.name: Arp 299
gmw_00000858.overview: Arp 299 is a nearby luminous infrared system undergoing an intermediate-stage major merger [arXiv:2010.05072, arXiv:1407.2055]. The system consists of two interacting galaxies, IC 694 and NGC 3690 [arXiv:1101.0682], which host dual dust-enshrouded active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1311.3446] and experience intense, widespread starburst activity [arXiv:2010.05072, arXiv:1407.2055].

sources.summary: The review treats Arp 299 as a key LIRG case study: an intermediate-stage merger with obscured nuclear starbursts, a western Compton-thick AGN, strong molecular gas, and prolific supernova activity.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is one of the nearest examples of a LIRG with an IR luminosity almost qualifying it as a ULIRG. However, it is particularly well suited as a case study example for hosting a wide range of phenomena discussed throughout this review. It consists of two interacting galaxies that are in an intermediate merger stage Haan2011 and whose nuclei are separated by approximately 5 kpc (Fig. fig:casestudy ). It has both highly obscured nuclear starbursts with their associated SN factories, and less obscured circumnuclear SF on much larger scales.

It also hosts a Compton-thick AGN in the Western component, observed directly in hard X-rays dellaceca02, ballo04 . Its proximity allows for detailed studies of the link between the merger process, nuclear obscuration, molecular gas distribution and gas properties and supermassive BH accretion and feedback. Finally, Arp 299 is clearly above the main sequence of star forming galaxies in the local Universe (see Fig. fig:pereira ), similar to starbursting ULIRGs at z 1 - 2 . Furthermore, almudena09 have shown the integrated mid-IR spectrum of the whole Arp 299 system to resemble those of z 1.5 ULIRGs making it also a useful laboratory of the conditions where the bulk of SF took place at high- z .

sources.summary: Arp 299 is described as an interacting LIRG system with two galaxies, distinct A and B/C components, young starburst activity, and far-infrared line measurements for both components.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is an interacting system comprising two galaxies. The nuclear regions of the galaxies are named A (eastern component) and B (southwestern component), separated by about 20". The component C located at 10" north of B is part of the overlapping/external star-forming region. see 2004ApJ...611..186N . The A source is the brightest IR continuum source in the system and hosts a young starburst estimated to have peaked 6 - 8 Myr ago 2000ApJ...532..845A . B is the second brightest in the far-IR continuum, but interestingly the [ O iii ] line coming from B is significantly weaker than its close neighbor C.

Because B and C are only separated by 10 arcsec and both appear extended, they are blended in both SOFIA/FIFI-LS and Herschel observations, thus they are treated as one source ``B C'' in the rest of the paper. But the majority of the line emission is actually from component C. Both A and B C are observed in our SOFIA/FIFI-LS mapping.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton abundance measurements for Arp 299's hot atmosphere, finding subsolar Fe/O, slightly supersolar Ne/O and Mg/O, and tensions with simple SN enrichment models.

quotes.quote: Here we measure the elemental abundances of the hot atmosphere of luminous infrared galaxy Arp 299 observed with XMM-Newton . To measure the abundances in the hot atmosphere, we use a multi-temperature thermal plasma model, which provides a better fit to the Reflection Grating Spectrometer data. The observed Fe/O abundance ratio is subsolar, while those of Ne/O and Mg/O are slightly above solar. Core-collapse supernovae (SNcc) are the dominant metal factory of elements like O, Ne, and Mg. We find some deviations between the observed abundance patterns and theoretical ones from a simple chemical enrichment model.

Arp 299 hosts a hot atmosphere that dominates the soft X-ray spectrum below 2 keV e.g., ana16 . About 20 ultraluminous X-ray sources zez03,ana16 and at least one Compton-thick nucleus pta15 dominate the hard X-ray spectrum at 2-10 keV and >10 keV, respectively. Previous X-ray studies do not focus on accurate abundance measurement of O, Ne, and Mg, which are mainly enriched by SNcc.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 299 as a nearby luminous infrared star-forming merger whose Chandra emission includes diffuse star-formation components, 18 discrete sources, and an obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Arp299 is one of the nearest (D=40.4 Mpc Throughout this paper we assume an H_ o =75 km/s/Mpc and q_ o =0.0 ) Luminous Infrared Galaxies (LIRGs; Sanders Mirabel 1996; L_ IR =5 10^ 11 L_ ) and is therefore an excellent candidate for detailed studies of their energy source In some papers Arp299 is referred to as a ULIRG, based on the use of H_ o =50 km/s/Mpc . Optical, infrared (IR) and radio observations show that it hosts galaxy-wide star-formation with estimated rates for discrete regions ranging from 20 up to 140 and typical ages of 10 Myr (Alonso-Herrero 2000 henceforth referred to as AH00; Zhao 1997). Mid IR observations indicate that the major part of this star-forming activity is obscured by large amounts of dust (Charmandaris 2002).

X-ray observations show that Arp299 is one of the most X-ray luminous star-forming galaxies in the nearby universe, with an observed luminosity of 4 10^ 41 in the 0.1-10.0 keV band (Zezas 1998; Heckman 1999). Its X-ray spectrum is well represented by a two component model with either a double thermal plasma or a thermal plasma and a power-law, and was found to be very similar to that of other much less luminous star-forming galaxies. However, recent observations of Arp 299 with Beppo-SAX revealed a heavily obscured AGN (Della Ceca 2002). In order to further study the origin of the X-ray emission from this exceptionally luminous star-forming galaxy we obtained a relatively short exposure with the Chandra X-ray observatory (Weisskopf 2000). In this paper we report on the properties of the discrete sources in Arp 299.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 299 as a luminous merging system with tidal tails, active nuclei, large molecular reservoirs, and broad luminous H2O megamaser emission.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is a merging system at D 40 Mpc, composed of two main sources, IC 694 and NGC 3690 (for an alternative nomenclature, see Sect. 4.2.3), that are separated by 22 \ in east-west direction (e.g. Sargent Scoville 1991). A FIR luminosity of several 10 ^ 11 L _ (Casoli et al. 1992) places Arp 299 near the boundary between luminous (LIRGs) and ultraluminous (ULIRGs) infrared galaxies. Supporting the merging scenario, two highly extended \ tails have been identified by Hibbard Yun (1999). Radio and infrared observations reveal three main regions of activity (e.g. Gehrz et al. 1983; Aalto et al. 1997; Casoli et al. 1999), the nuclear regions of IC 694 and NGC 3690 and an interface where IC 694 and NGC 3690 overlap. NGC 3690 contains a deeply enshrouded active galactic nucleus (AGN), while the situation with respect to the similarly obscured nuclear region of IC 694 is less clear (e.g. Della Ceca et al. 2002; Ballo et al. 2004; Gallais et al. 2004).

In Arp 299, water maser profiles are extremely broad ( 200 km s ^ 1 ), with peak flux densities of 30 mJy (Fig. arp299a ).
