Adopting a distance of 42 Mpc, the total isotropic luminosity is 250 L _ , placing the object among the more luminous H _2 O megamaser sources. The maser line is centered at a velocity of 3100 km s ^ 1 , i.e. close to the systemic velocity of the entire complex of sources constituting Arp 299.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 299 as an extreme-star-formation supernova factory, a localized hyperflare search target, and a possible site of very young magnetars.

quotes.quote: These are Arp 299 n2004 and NGC 3256 l2004 , two galaxies with extreme star formation rate (``supernova factories''). The total star formation rate in these galaxies is few times lower than that in the Virgo cluster, therefore they are a less probable source of HFs in the BATSE data. Nevertheless, these galaxies are of great interest since they are well-localized and can lead to measurements with a better angular resolution. A number of candidates for HFs from these galaxies is given by Popov (private communication See the e-print astro-ph/0502391.

In the four near-by star-forming galaxies there should be SGRs 10 times younger than the Galactic ones; in galaxies like Arp 299 and NGC 3256 we expect to find magnetars with ages about few tens of years. If they produce frequent bursts, then non-detection should mean that their luminosities are lower than those exhibited by the galactic sources. As it is noted by h2005 , Swift gives an excellent opportunity to observe extragalactic GFs and HFs of SGRs.
Arp 299 and NGC 3256 can be good targets for such observations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp299 as a colliding starburst system and potential AGASA-triplet source, observes it with MAGIC, and reports no significant VHE gamma-ray excess with flux limits.

quotes.quote: Arp299 (Mrk 171, VV118; and references therein) is a starburst galaxy and a system of colliding galaxies (NGC3690+IC694). It is characterised by an extreme star formation rate. The supernova rate is also as high as 0.5 per year. Arp299 is pointed out to be a potential source to explain the AGASA triplet .

Following up with AGASA-HiRes quartet detection, we searched the possible UHE cosmic ray sources, nearby quasar remnants NGC3610 and NGC3613 and a starburst galaxy Arp299, for VHE emission. In 200 - 500 GeV energies, the upper limits on the gamma ray flux from each source is placed against each object and were 8 12 Crab flux at a 95 CL. Over the region observed, there are no positive signals for the VHE emission by effectively 15 hour observation.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 299 as an interacting luminous infrared system with two galaxy nuclei, intense star formation, and evidence for dual dust-enshrouded AGN before full merger.

quotes.quote: The interacting galaxy Arp 299 (Mrk 171, IC 694+NGC 3690) was identified as a mid-infrared (MIR) luminous source Rieke1972 more than 40 years ago. Subsequently, detected two bright 10 m sources coincident with the galaxy nuclei see also Telesco1985 , referred to as Arp 299-A or the nucleus of the eastern component (IC 694) and Arp 299-B or the nucleus of the western component (NGC 3690). Source B is further resolved into B1, the MIR-bright nucleus, and B2, the optical-bright source (see Fig. fig:acquisitionimages ). Two other bright MIR sources are in the system overlapping region: Arp 299-C and Arp 299-C ^ .

The interaction of the Arp 299 system started at least 750 Myr ago Hibbard1999 but the system has not yet fully merged. The projected nuclear separation is 4.5 kpc and the system is experiencing intense SF spread out over several kpc (Charmandaris et al. 2002, AAH09). Our work provided evidence of dual dust enshrouded AGN, probably indicative of an early phase of AGN activity.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 299 as an ongoing luminous infrared merger whose young massive clusters are numerous, massive, and shaped by environment-dependent formation and disruption.

quotes.quote: Archival WFC3/UVIS imaging of Arp 299 (NGC 3690E + NGC 3690W) is retrieved to investigate the young massive cluster (YMC) population of this ongoing merger. We extract 2182 cluster candidates, including 1323 high confidence photometric sources. Multiband photometry is matched with Yggdrasil models to estimate the age, mass, and extinction of each cluster. A Schechter fit of the truncated cluster mass function results in a characteristic mass M_ = 1.6 10^6 . Our results confirm that intensely star-forming galaxies such as Arp 299 host more massive clusters than quiescent dwarf and normal spirals.

The merger generally follows the star formation rate density relation from the literature. The YMC photometric study of Arp 299 thus reveals that both formation and disruption mechanisms of the star cluster population are most likely environment-dependent.

sources.summary: The paper detects GeV emission from Arp 299, describes it as a powerful local merging LIRG with buried AGN activity, and finds tentative gamma-ray variability.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: For the galaxy Arp 299, we selected the events in the energy range 0.3-500 GeV within a rectangular ROI of size 17^ 17^ centered at the galaxy IR center ( _ 2000 =172.136^ , _ 2000 =58.561^ ).

quotes.quote: Two new galaxies, M33 and Arp 299, are detected significantly . The two galaxies are consistent with the empirical correlation between the ray luminosity and total infrared luminosity, suggesting that their ray emissions should mainly originate from CRs interacting with ISM. Nevertheless, there is a tentative evidence that the flux of the ray emission from Arp 299 is variable.

Arp 299 is one of the most powerful merging galaxy system in the local Universe, at a distance of 44 Mpc 1999ApJ...517..130H . The system consists of two galaxies in an advanced merging state, NGC 3690 to the west and IC 694 to the east, plus a small compact galaxy 1999AJ....118..162H . The total IR luminosity of both galaxies(NGC 3690+IC 694) are L_ IR = 5.16 10^ 11 L_ 2002ApJ...571..282C , so it belongs to the class of Luminous IR Galaxies (LIRGs).
If the variability is true, part of the ray emission should come from the obscured AGN in Arp 299.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 299 as the LIRG host of Arp 299-B AT1, connecting its nuclear IR transient to a dust-obscured TDE, radio jet, concealed AGN torus, and possible boosted TDE rate.

quotes.quote: Mattila2018 , hereafter Mattila2018 , presented the discovery of an IR luminous transient (Arp 299-B AT1) coincident with the nucleus B1 of the LIRG Arp 299, a result of systematic monitoring of a small sample of nearby LIRGs for dust-obscured supernovae (SNe) in the near-IR K band see Mattila2001 . Multi-wavelength follow-up included data in the near-IR, mid-IR, and radio, with non-detections at optical and X-ray wavelengths. The transient was extremely energetic, with more than 10 ^ 52 erg radiated in the IR over 10 years of evolution. Based on high-resolution radio very-long-baseline interferometry observations, Mattila2018 find Arp 299-B AT1 to be consistent with a TDE with a resolved and expanding radio jet. The viewing angle of the torus associated with the concealed AGN within this nucleus is constrained to be edge-on AlonsoHerrero2013 , and the inferred angle of the jet was not polar with respect to this torus, providing strong evidence that the transient was a TDE.
We use the names given by the RBGS except in the case of NGC 3690/IC 694, which we refer to as Arp 299, in order to make clear the association with the transient Arp 299-B AT1.
The NEOWISE data show the mid-IR light curve decline continuing at a similar rate as shown by the pre-2016 light curve.

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 299 as a HEX-P dual-AGN case study, modeling its two nuclei and predicting that HEX-P would detect and spatially resolve both AGNs.

quotes.quote: One illustrative case study for examining the effectiveness of HEX-P over NuSTAR , in terms of spectroscopic and imaging constraints, is Arp 299, a local (44 Mpc, or z 0.01 ) dual AGN comprising an X-ray bright and heavily obscured AGN in the southwest nucleus (Arp 299-B, detected by Chandra and NuSTAR , ballo2004,ptak2015 ) and an AGN in the northeast nucleus (Arp 299-A) identified as a flat spectrum radio source pereztorres2010 , and tentatively identified via mid-IR SEDs alonsoherrero2013 and ionized Fe K emission ballo2004 . As reported by ptak2015 , Arp 299-B is detected by NuSTAR with a hard X-ray flux of F_ 10-30 keV =3.5 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , while Arp 299-A is dominated by X-ray binaries below 10 keV and contains no hard X-ray emitting AGN above a 10-30 keV limit of <3.5 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Here we demonstrate that HEX-P would not only clearly detect both of the reported AGNs in Arp 299 in the 0.2-80 keV band but would spatially resolve the AGNs in both HET and LET imaging.

For Arp 299-B, we assume =1.95 , N_ H =4 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 (for simplicity we assume the average torus column density and the line-of-sight column density are equal), covering factor cos( _ open )=0.85 , and we normalize the 10-30 keV emission to 3.5 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . For Arp 299-A, we assume =1.8 , N_ H =5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , and covering factor cos( _ open )=0.5 ; while ptak2015 placed an upper limit of <3.5 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on the 10-30 keV flux of Arp 299-A ( <10 of the observed flux of Arp 299-B), we conservatively assume that Arp 299-A (here, AGN 2) exhibits an observed 10-30 keV flux of 7 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (i.e. 2 of the 10-30 keV observed flux of Arp 299-B). For both AGNs 1 and 2, we assume =1.8 and a cutoff energy of 7 keV for the power law component driven by X-ray binaries; for simplicity, we assume these components contribute equally to the total 2-10 keV XRB-driven flux ( 8.1 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ) found by ptak2015 , and we therefore normalize both components to 4.05 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in the 2-10 keV band. For our spatial models, we adopt the positions given in Table 1 of ptak2015 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 299 as a nearby interacting starburst LIRG and argues it is the best candidate source for the AGASA ultra-high-energy cosmic-ray triplet.

quotes.quote: However, as we shall show, there is another favourable candidate for the origin of this UHECR cluster - Arp 299 (Mrk171, VV 118a/b), a member of LIRG class of extragalactic objects. This system (RA =171.4^ , =58.8^ ), consisting of two interacting starburst galaxies, is the closest extragalactic object (distance 42 Mpc for H_ 0 =75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ) with IR luminosity greater than 5 10^ 11 L ( 2 10^ 45 ergs s^ 1 ) and it is the brightest IR source within 70 Mpc. Such high IR luminosity is related to young and violent star forming regions.

So far Arp 299 is the best candidate as a member of LIRGs, the brightest infrared source within 70 Mpc and a system of colliding galaxies showing intense, violent starburst activity at only 42 Mpc away. It should be noted that Arp 299 appeared earlier as VV 118 in the list of candidates for the AGASA triplet presented by Takeda but was not recognized as a colliding, energetic system, and, as a result, was not given enough attention. These authors point to another object VV 141 being a colliding system at z=0.02. However, as Arp 299 is at a distance two times smaller and fulfils the necessary criteria for CR acceleration, we think that it is this object which could be the most probable CR source.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 299 as a complex interacting galaxy pair and starburst system hosting SN 2010O, where archival X-ray data are searched for progenitor emission.

quotes.quote: The supernova resides in the Eastern part of the interacting galaxy Arp 299, which is often referred to as IC 694 in the literature, designating the Western part as NGC 3690. However, as this convention is somewhat confusing 1999AJ....118..162H discuss that it is likely, although not conclusively so that Swift referred to the compact dwarf to the northwest of Arp 299 for IC 694. All supernovae in either Eastern or Western part of Arp 299 (including SN 2010O) are classified as ``in NGC 3690'' by the Central Bureau for Astronomical Telegrams of the IAU. , we use the name Arp 299 to refer to the whole system and use Eastern and Western part to identify the two galaxies. Arp 299, at a distance of about 41 Mpc, is a pair of interacting galaxies giving rise to a spectacular starburst e.g.

Clearly Arp 299 is a very complex stellar system with tremendous activity, making association of different sources with each other difficult. Nevertheless, we will analyse the pre-supernova X-ray data of Arp 299 to search for possible emission of the progenitor of 2010O.

sources.summary: Arp 299 is described as an IR-luminous merger of IC 694 and NGC 3690 with intense star formation and molecular emission, but no p-H3O+ detection.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is an IR-luminous merging system of two galaxies, IC 694 and NGC 3690. Strong \ emission has been detected from the nuclei of IC 694 and NGC 3690 and from the interface between the two galaxies e.g. sargent91, aalto97 . The two nuclei, as well as the western overlap region, currently undergo intense star formation activity e.g. gehrz83 . Bright HCN 1--0 emission is emerging from both nuclei as well as from the overlap region e.g. aalto97 . Water emission from NGC 3690 suggests that it also harbours an AGN tarchi07 .
p- \ line emission was detected towards IC 342, NGC 4418, NGC 253, NGC 1068 and NGC 6240, while Arp 299 and IRAS 15250 were not detected.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 299 as a luminous infrared interacting system, using VLA variability to estimate nuclear core-collapse supernova rates and compare them with infrared expectations.

quotes.quote: Received 2011 April 5; in original form 2011 February 14 firstpage - lastpage 2011 We present a study of the core collapse supernova (CCSN) rate in nuclei A and B1, of the luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) Arp 299, based on 11 years of Very Large Array (VLA) monitoring of their radio emission at 8.4 GHz. Arp 299 is a system composed of the pair of galaxies IC 694 (east component) and NGC 3690 (west component), whose interaction is inducing a powerful star formation and references therein alonso00 . At a luminosity distance of 44.8 Mpc dist for H_0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , Arp 299 has an infrared (IR) luminosity ( L_ IR =L [ 8 - 1000 m ] ) 6.7 10^ 11 L _ as resulting from scaling the value for the same wavelength interval given by to the distance we adopted here sanders03 , which makes it one of the most luminous LIRGs of the local Universe.

Over the past 20 years, Arp 299 has been the target of many SN search campaigns which can also provide important insight into its dominating energy source (AGN and/or starburst). The detection of seven SNe over a period of less than 20 years, already indicates a very high SN rate in the circumnuclear regions of Arp 299. The wealth of data available for Arp 299 in the VLA archive, makes this galaxy a unique target to perform such studies.

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 299 as an early-stage interacting system near the ULIRG threshold, with intense recent star formation, high supernova frequency, and infrared emission concentrated in IC 694.

quotes.quote: An extremely prolific supernova factory in Arp 299 Arp 299 consists of two interacting galaxies (IC 694 and NGC 3690), which are in an early merger stage. At a luminosity distance of 44.8 Mpc fixsen96 for H_0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , Arp 299 has an infrared luminosity L_ IR 6.7 11 L_ , which almost qualifies it as a ULIRG. The innermost 150 pc nuclear region of Arp 299-A (see top left panel in Fig. fig3 ) is heavily dust-enshrouded, thus making the detections of SNe very challenging even at near-infrared wavelengths. Yet, Arp 299 hosts recent and intense star-forming activity, as indicated by the relatively high frequency of supernovae discovered at optical and near-infrared wavelengths in its outer, much less extinguished regions.

Numerous H II regions populate the system near star-forming regions, which implies that star formation has been occurring at a high rate for past 10 Myr almudena00 . Given that IC 694 accounts for most of the infrared emission in Arp 299, it is the region that is most likely to contain new SNe condon92 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 299 as a nearby interacting LIRG with central star formation, multiple components, a high CCSN rate, and seven optically detected supernovae.

quotes.quote: The Arp 299 system of multiple galaxy components is commonly adopted as a prototypical LIRG due to its IR luminosity ( L_ IR = L [ 8 1000 m ] 6.7 10^ 11 L_ ; sanders03 ), relatively nearby distance ( 44.8 Mpc; huo04 ) and interacting nature. Arp 299 consists of two main components, Arp 299A and Arp 299B (sometimes referred to as IC 694 and NGC 3690, respectively), both likely to host an active galactic nucleus see e.g. dellaceca02, perez-torres10 . Most of the star formation in LIRGs is concentrated in their central regions, which is also the case in Arp 299. The milliarcsecond resolution of very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) radio observations has proven that there is a rich population of radio supernovae (SNe) and young SN remnants in the nuclear regions of Arp 299A and Arp 299B, within only 100 150 and 30 pc from the centre, respectively perez-torres09,ulvestad09,romero11,bondi12 .

The most recent estimate for the total CCSN rate of Arp 299 is 1.6 1.9 yr ^ 1 derived by mattila12 based on IR and radio observations. So far, Arp 299 has hosted seven optically detected SNe and has been continuously the target of multiple systematic SN search programmes in the optical and near-IR (NIR) wavelengths during the last 15 yr for a summary see mattila12 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 299 as a nearby luminous infrared major merger with overlapping components, merger-induced star formation, dense molecular gas, and starburst conditions across much of the system.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 (NGC 3690 + IC 694, Mrk 171, VV 118, IRAS 11257+5850, UGC6471/2) is a nearby (42 Mpc ) LIRG (L _ FIR = 5 10^ 11 ) currently undergoing a major merger event. Arp 299 is dominated by intense, merger-induced star formation and is made up of three main components 2000ApJ...532..845A . Although the core regions of these components can still be resolved, there is a large overlap in their disks.

2000ApJ...532..845A observes Arp 299 in high-resolution with the Hubble Space Telescope in the near-infrared and also find that over the past 15 Myr, Arp 299 has been undergoing intense merger-related star formation. This star formation is fueled by large amounts of dense molecular gas: 8 10^5 M _ pc ^ 2 for Arp 299 A, 3 10^4 M _ pc ^ 2 for Arp 299 B, and 2 10^4 M _ pc ^ 2 for Arp 299 C 1991ApJ...366L...1S . Most of the star formation responsible for the high far-infrared luminosity is spread over 6-8 kpc 2009ApJ...697..660A , resulting in most of Arp 299 having typical starburst properties.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 299 A as an AGN-harboring component with brighter high-J CO emission; its molecular transitions require mechanical plus UV heating and imply about 3e9 solar masses of molecular gas.

quotes.quote: We study Arp 299, a colliding galaxy group, with one component (A) harboring an AGN and two more (B and C) undergoing intense star formation. For Arp 299 A, we present PACS spectrometer observations of high-J CO lines up to J=20-19 and JCMT observations of ^ 13 CO and HCN to discern between UV heating and alternative heating mechanisms. There is an immediately noticeable difference in the spectra of Arp 299 A and Arp 299 B+C, with source A having brighter high-J CO transitions.

Therefore, we determine that the high-J CO and HCN transitions are heated by an additional mechanism, namely cosmic ray heating, mechanical heating, or X-ray heating. We find that mechanical heating, in combination with UV heating, is the only mechanism that fits all molecular transitions. We also constrain the molecular gas mass of Arp 299 A to 3 10^9 M _ and find that we need 4 of the total heating to be mechanical heating, with the rest UV heating.

sources.summary: The paper includes new high-resolution mid-infrared imaging of Arp 299, resolving its nuclei, star-forming regions, and extended emission as part of an IR-bright galaxy study.

quotes.quote: We obtained new mid-IR high angular resolution imaging observations of the interacting LIRG Arp 299 with GTC/CanariCam on the night of 2016 January 26 in queue mode. These observations were part of our GTC/CanariCam AGN guaranteed time program (PI: C. Telesco). We used the Si-2 filter which has a central wavelength of _ = 8.7 m and a width of _ cut = 1.1 m, at 50 cut-on/off.
We therefore required two pointings to cover the nuclei and bright star forming regions of Arp 299. The mid-IR spectroscopy of the bright nuclei of Arp 299 was presented in .

The GTC/CanariCam 8.7 m images of Arp 299 show emission stemming not only from the bright mid-IR sources (A, B1, B2, C and C') but also from H ii regions in the spiral arms of the eastern component and to the north west of B1, as well as extended emission around B1 see Fig. fig:Arp299 and AAH2009 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 299 as a luminous infrared source coincident with the AGASA triplet and models it as a possible source of ultra-high-energy cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: In particular, the AGASA triplet [ 7 ] coincides well with the brightest (up to 70 Mpc) extragalactic IR source, Arp 299, 42 Mpc away. Here, we analyse further the possibility of Arp 299 being the source of the triplet particles, allowing for their changing directions in extragalactic and Galactic magnetic field. We also predict what the southern sky would look like in the highest energy cosmic particles, as measured by the Pierre Auger Observatory in Argentina [ 3 ] .

The positions of the three shower directions of the AGASA triplet, together with the direction towards Arp 299 are shown in Fig fig:1 . The particle energies are 54, 55 and 78 EeV correspondingly for shower 1,2 and 3. As the distance to Arp is 'only' about 42 Mpc, let us assume that these particles may have arrived from this source with negligible energy losses and follow the consequences.

sources.summary: Arp 299 A is identified as the strongest additional CON candidate, with PAH equivalent-width behavior and optical depth suggesting a formerly buried AGN.

quotes.quote: The strongest candidate of these is Arp 299 A as it has a low enough 12.7/11.3 PAH EW ratio which is more reliable than the 6.2/11.3. This object also almost meets the optical depth criterion of _N > 3.5 with _N = 3.3 .

Another interesting trend with aperture size can be seen in Arp 299 A, where the 12.7/11.3 PAH EW ratio increases as the aperture decreases. The high optical depth measured for this object means that the continuum will cause the PAH EW ratio to move in the opposite direction therefore changes in the 12.7/11.3 PAH flux ratio must drive this change and so the PAH properties may be different in the nuclear region. The flux ratio of PAHs are known to be sensitive to changes in the properties of PAHs such as the size, charge and hardness of the incident radiation field See for a review Li2020 . There is evidence that the PAH molecules in the vicinity of AGN are larger and more neutral Garcia-Bernete2022b, Garcia-Bernete2022c and may even be excited by AGN on very small scales Jensen2017 . It, therefore, is plausible that Arp 299 A represents an AGN that used to be completely obscured and has expelled some dust exposing PAH molecules to radiation from the central engine and therefore changing the properties of PAH molecules in the nuclear region.

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 299 as a nearby, dynamically young interacting infrared galaxy system where tidal forces trigger star formation and a buried AGN may contribute in one nucleus.

quotes.quote: At a distance of 41 Mpc ( v_ hel = 3080 km s ^ 1 , H_ 0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ), Arp 299 (Mrk 171 = IC 694 + NGC 3690) is one of the nearest interacting galaxies. Because of its proximity, its spatial extent (8 kpc), and its high infrared (IR) luminosity We use the standard definition of L_ IR (8-1000 m ) = 5.62 10^5 D^2_ Mpc (13.48f_ 12 + 5.16f_ 25 + 2.58f_ 60 + f_ 100 ) L_ see Sanders96 . ( L_ IR = 5.16 10^ 11 L_ ), it is one of the prime candidates for exploring the effects of triggered star formation activity as the tidal forces during an interaction lead to instabilities in the galactic disks and rapidly funnel large quantities of gas into the dynamical centers of the galaxies Mihos96,Sanders96 . Similar to the prototypical interacting pair of galaxies NGC 4038/39, this system is dynamically young, as shown by the prominent 180 kpc ( 13^ ) long tidal tail Hibbard99 .

One of the recurrent questions though in all studies of Arp 299 is whether this galaxy is a pure starburst as was proposed by Weedman72 or whether some luminosity could be contributed by Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). The fact that it is one of the most X-ray luminous galaxies L_ 0.2-4.0 keV 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , see Fabbiano92,Zezas98 along with the flatness of its radio continuum emission had given some indications that an enshrouded AGN could be lurking in one of the nuclei.

sources.summary: The survey describes Arp 299 as an interacting system with compact radio and X-ray sources, likely AGN in both nuclei, and possible non-thermal millimeter emission.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 (VV 118, NGC 3690, Mrk 171, IC 694) High-resolution radio images from 1.4 to 8.4 GHz reveal five bright compact sources as well as extended emission n04 . The bright compact radio source in the nucleus of IC 694 breaks up into five candidate radio supernova remnants when observed with the VLBA; one of the sources may possibly be a low-luminosity AGN n04 . X-ray images reveal a population of compact sources, including sources in the nucleus of each component which are likely to be AGN z03 . Further evidence for an AGN in NGC 3690 comes from gm06 , who identify a conical region with Seyfert-like excitation emanating from the B1 region.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 299 as a luminous infrared merger system with a powerful starburst, star-formation-dominated sub-10 keV X-rays, and a Compton-thick AGN.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 (NGC 3690 and IC 694) It is a luminous infrared ( L_ IR = 6 10 ^ 11 L_ ) merger system. It hosts one of the most powerful starbursts in local galaxies AAH09Arp299 . The X-ray emission below 10 keV is dominated by star-formation, however a Compton thick AGN is found in the system DellaCeca2002 . The hard X-ray spectrum of the nucleus of NGC 3690 indicates that the obscured AGN is probably located there Zezas2003 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 299 as an interacting luminous infrared galaxy and prolific supernova factory whose circumnuclear regions motivate radio monitoring of recent supernovae.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is a LIRG with an IR luminosity ( 6.7 11 L _ sanders03 , at an adopted luminosity distance of 44.8 Mpc, assuming H_0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . The system is composed of two interacting galaxies, whose major nuclei (A and B1) and core components in the interacting region (C ^ and C) are bright radio and NIR emitters gehrz83, alonso00 . Arp 299 is a very prolific SN factory as proved by the detection of several radio SNe and SN remnants (SNRs) in the innermost nuclear regions of Arp 299A and Arp 299B perez09b, ulvestad09, rocc11, bondi12 , and the detection within the last 20 years of seven optical/NIR SNe in the circumnuclear regions of the system see anderson11, mattila12 . Many attempts have been made to detect at radio wavelengths the SNe occurring in the circumnuclear regions of Arp 299, mostly under observing programmes with the Very Large Array (VLA): AS333 carried out from 1990 May to 1993 December; AS525 on 1994 February to detect SN 1993G; AS568 on 1999 January, February, April and October to detect SN 1999D; AW641 on 2005 February, June and August to detect SN 2005U.

sources.summary: The paper reports no significant AME in Arp 299, groups it with strong AGN non-detections, and gives an upper limit rather than a detected AME component.

quotes.quote: Combining with the ancillary data, we investigated the radio-IR spectral energy distribution (SED), while searching for the AME signals in five galaxies. The AME in NGC 2903 was well detected at a significant confidence level, whereas that in NGC 2146 and M82 was marginal. NGC 1068 and Arp 299 indicated no significant hints, and we provided the upper limits for the AME.

However, we did not observe AME in strong AGNs such as NGC 1068 and Arp 299, indicating that AME favored non- or rather weak-AGN hosts. This may imply that ultra-small dust grains are easily destroyed by strong AGNs.
For non-detection galaxies, NGC 1068 and Arp 299, we also reported the upper limits for AME.

sources.summary: The paper identifies merger galaxy Arp 299 as the best candidate source for the AGASA C2 UHECR triplet, placing it at the triplet direction and 42 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: Despite the limited amount of data, we find that the C2 triplet events observed by the AGASA may originate from the source within 100 Mpc from us at 2 confidence level. Merger galaxy Arp 299 (NGC 3690 + IC 694) is the best candidate for their source.

Indeed, show that there are merger galaxies Arp 299 (NGC 3690 + IC 694) at the direction of the AGASA triplet at 42 Mpc. Considering the analysis presented here, Arp 299 is the best candidate of the UHECR source.

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 299 as the benchmark dual-megamaser merger, noting water emission in multiple regions, similarity to IIZw96, and HII-nucleus classification for both systems.

quotes.quote: However, water emission has since been discovered in multiple regions in Arp 299 including Arp 299-A (IC 694), a galactic nucleus also hosting an OH megamaser (T11).

IIZw96 resembles Arp 299 morphologically: Goldader et al. (1997) observes that both galaxies possess similar projected size (10-15 kpc), and are composed of distinct nuclei which are strongly interacting and are in a similar, very short ( 10 Myr) ``intermediate" stage of merging.
With the exception of UGC 5101, both systems (Arp299 and IIZw96) hosting dual megamasers are classified as HII nuclei.

gmw_00000859.name: ESO 540-032
gmw_00000859.overview: ESO 540-032 is an intermediate-type dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0105144] with a low surface brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/0302045] located in the Sculptor group [arXiv:astro-ph/0105144]. It contains neutral hydrogen [arXiv:astro-ph/0508062], an unresolved central source [arXiv:0811.0329], and a stellar population dominated by old, metal-poor stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0105144].

sources.summary: The paper presents first VI photometry for ESO 540-032, deriving its stellar populations, TRGB distance, Sculptor-group membership, surface-brightness profiles, and dwarf-galaxy structural properties.

quotes.quote: We present the first VI CCD photometry for the Sculptor group galaxy ESO 540-032 obtained at the Very Large Telescope UT1+FORS1. The (I, V-I) colour-magnitude diagram indicates that this intermediate-type dwarf galaxy is dominated by old, metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] 1.7 dex) stars, with a small population of slightly more metal-rich ( [ Fe/H ] 1.3 dex), young (age 150-500 Myr) stars. A discontinuity in the I band luminosity function is detected at I_0 = 23.44 0.09 mag. Interpreting this feature as the tip of the red giant branch and adopting M_ I = -4.20 0.10 mag for its absolute magnitude, we have determined a Population II distance modulus of (m - M)_ 0 = 27.64 0.14 mag (3.4 0.2 Mpc).

This distance confirms ESO 540-032 as a member of the nearby Sculptor group but is significantly larger than a previously reported value based on the Surface Brightness Fluctuation (SBF) method. We produce the surface brightness profiles for ESO 540-032 and derive the photometric and structural parameters. The global properties follow closely the relations between metallicity and both absolute magnitude and central surface brightness defined by dwarf elliptical galaxies in the Local Group.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 540-032 as a reddish, low surface-brightness dSph/dIrr-like galaxy, undetected in H I, with mostly RGB stars and a TRGB distance of 3.42 Mpc.

quotes.quote: Like KDG 2, FG 24 (Feitzinger Galinski 1985) has a reddish color and a low surface brightness, typical of dSph or dSph/dIrr galaxies. The galaxy was not detected in H i by Huchtmeier et al. (2000). Surface photometry of FG 24, performed by Jerjen et al. (1998, 2000), yields an integrated magnitude of B_T = 16.44 mag, an integrated color (B-R)_T = 1.08 mag, and a central surface brightness 24.5 ^m/ in the B band. Most of the stars detected by us in FG 24 (see Fig. 2) are likely RGB stars, although some faint blue stars are present in the galaxy's central part. The TRGB I band magnitude derived by us, 23 67 0 17 , corresponds to a distance of 3.42 0.27 Mpc, which exceeds the distance 2.21 0.14 Mpc estimated by Jerjen et al. (1998) from SBF, but agrees excellently with the new distance estimate, 3.4 0.2 Mpc, obtained by Jerjen Rejkuba (2001) from the TRGB.

sources.summary: The paper detects neutral hydrogen in ESO540-G032, finds its H I mass consistent with passive stellar-outflow accumulation, and notes its gas position is consistent with the optical center.

quotes.quote: Four of these objects, ESO294-G010, ESO410-G005, ESO540-G030, and ESO540-G032, were detected in with neutral hydrogen masses in the range of 2-9 10^5 ( M_ HI /L_ B = 0.08, 0.13, 0.16, and 0.18 M_ /L_ , respectively). These masses are consistent with the gas mass expected from stellar outflows over a large period of time.
In the third case, ESO540-G032, the apparent offset is smaller than the uncertainty of the position and thus consistent with being centered.
The masses for ESO294-G010, ESO410-G005, ESO540-G030, and ESO540-G032 are all consistent with passive, undisturbed evolution where all the gas from stellar winds has condensed in the form of H i .

sources.summary: The paper identifies a central unresolved source in ESO540-G032, weak CMD evidence for upper-AGB and blue main-sequence populations, and its 3D separation from NGC253.

quotes.quote: ESO540-G032 There is an unresolved source (FWHM 2.4^ ) at the centre of this galaxy, for which M_ HI =9 10^5 bouchard2005 . The CMD of this object shows weak evidence for upper-AGB and blue main sequence populations jerjen2001a . It is situated 640 160 kpc (3D) from NGC253.

gmw_00000860.name: NGC 1381
gmw_00000860.overview: NGC 1381 is a massive, edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1901.04310, arXiv:1906.08187] located in the densest region of the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1904.01260, arXiv:1901.04310]. It features a fast-rotating thin disk [arXiv:1901.04310, arXiv:1105.4809], a thick disk [arXiv:1901.04310], a boxy bulge [arXiv:1503.08105, arXiv:1906.08187], and a highly flattened nuclear star cluster [arXiv:1903.10918].

sources.summary: The paper uses FCC 170 as the inner Fornax comparison S0, contrasting its older population, greater mass, and cluster location with FCC 153 and FCC 177.

quotes.quote: These two galaxies are located outside of the densest region of the Fornax cluster where FCC 170 resides. This other edge-on S0 galaxy was studied by Pinna2019 . We compare and discuss our results with this previous study. The differences between these three galaxies, at different distances from the cluster center, suggest that the environment can have a strong effect on the galaxy evolutionary path.

Our stellar-population analysis points clearly to different evolutions for FCC 170 from FCC 153 and FCC 177. First of all, they have different total masses. FCC 170 is the most massive, followed by FCC 153 and then FCC 177, according to their maximum circular and rotation velocities. Koleva2011 observed the down-sizing phenomenon in the Fornax cluster.

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 1381 as an SA0 galaxy with a significant, highly elliptical disk; its model profile matches the observed galaxy within 0.1 magnitude and recovers disk properties consistently.

quotes.quote: NGC 1381 NGC 1381 is a galaxy of Hubble type SA0 and total magnitude in the B system m_ B = 12.60 (RC3). We used images of this galaxy in the ,v, and g Gunn filters. Information about how this image was normalized was not available to us, so our estimate of _ ^ 2 should be taken as a relative measure only. It has a significant, highly elliptical disk, serving as a good test of our program's ability to extract parameters when the isophotes are highly non-elliptical, and one-dimensional fitting programs are inapplicable (BF95).

Figure fig:1381R shows the R band decomposition of the this galaxy. As in NGC 661, the difference between our model profile and the galaxy profile is very difficult to see with the eye, and is never more than 0.1 magnitude. The bulge intensities are very similar both when the central pixels are masked out and when they are unmasked. The bulge scale length varies by 25 in the v and bands. The disk intensities are recovered consistently as well.

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 170 as an S0 galaxy whose MUSE kinematics reveal nuclear, bar, thin-disk, and thick-disk components, with thick-disk chemistry suggesting environmental processing and accretion.

quotes.quote: We present and discuss the stellar kinematics and populations of the S0 galaxy FCC 170 (NGC 1381) in the Fornax cluster, using deep MUSE data from the Fornax 3D survey. We show the maps of the first four moments of the stellar line-of-sight velocity distribution and of the mass-weighted mean stellar age, metallicity and [ Mg/Fe ] abundance ratio. The high-quality MUSE stellar kinematic measurements unveil the structure of this massive galaxy: a nuclear disk, a bar seen as a boxy bulge with a clear higher-velocity-dispersion X shape, a fast-rotating and flaring thin disk and a slower rotating thick disk. Whereas their overall old age makes it difficult to discuss differences in the formation epoch between these components, we find a clear-cut distinction between metal-rich and less [ Mg/Fe ] enhanced populations in the thin-disk, boxy-bulge and nuclear disk, and more metal-poor and [ Mg/Fe ] enhanced stars in the thick disk. Located in the densest region of the Fornax cluster, where signs of tidal stripping have been recently found, the evolution of FCC 170 might have been seriously affected by its environment.

The thick disk displays a composite star formation history, as a significant fraction of younger stars co-exist with the main older thick-disk population. The former sub-population is characterized by even lower-metallicity and higher- [ Mg/Fe ] values, suggesting that these stars formed later and faster in a less chemically evolved satellite, which was subsequently accreted.

sources.summary: The paper places FCC 170 among the most massive sample galaxies, with a very massive, old, metal-rich nuclear star cluster interpreted through rapid early central enrichment and efficient formation.

quotes.quote: Most massive galaxies The most massive galaxies in our sample ( M_ gal > 10^ 10 M_ ), FCC 47, FCC 170, and FCC 193, also host the most massive NSCs ( > 10^8 M_ ). Their NSCs are significantly more metal-rich than the hosts and any GCs, and are older than the host galaxies. This could be explained by an inside-out formation scenario, where the central regions of the galaxies assembled first and were rapidly enriched due to efficient star formation, while enrichment in the outskirts was slower and less efficient.

In , we argued that the old age and high metallicity of the NSC in FCC 47 indicates an efficient formation at early times. Similar arguments were made in the detailed analysis of FCC 170 by using the same F3D MUSE data which showed that FCC 170 assembled very rapidly and efficiently at early times (see also Poci2021 ). Their SFH of the central region of FCC 170 is similar to the one presented here.

sources.summary: NGC 1381 is a fast-rotating S0 with an edge-on disc, non-elliptical isophotes, central kinematic artifacts, and an inner metallicity decrease.

quotes.quote: NGC 1381 = FCC 170 This is a S0 galaxy with a prominent edge-on disc in fast rotation ( V_ max 170 ). The isophotes are not elliptical and the use of as a spatial scale may be questioned. The kinematical GMOS profiles displays a drop and a displacement of the kinematical centre in the central 2 arcsec. As this is not seen in the FORS2 profiles, this is an obvious artifact due to the wide slit. The metallicity decreases from [ Fe/H ] 0.05 dex to -0.15 dex within 3.5 arcsec.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 1381 as a boxy-bulge galaxy with non-cylindrical rotation, a metallicity gradient, positive alpha enhancement, and evidence for multiple bulge components.

quotes.quote: They found that NGC 1381 has a boxy bulge, with stellar rotation neither cylindrical (as would be expected for bars seen edge-on) nor strongly non-cylindrical and with a double hump on the rotation curve. The galaxy shows a metallicity gradient but no age gradient and a positive [ / Fe ] . They explain the properties of these galaxies in an scenario where NGC 1381 has the three classes of bulges. The classical bulge formed their stars rapidly and explain the general trend in [ / Fe ] as a function of height, as disk light (with its lower [ / Fe ] ) contributes less and less to the integrated spectrum.

The boxy appearance is explained by the simultaneous presence of a bar (which appears boxy in projection), and the double hump of the rotation curve hints at the presence of a small disky pseudobulge see also silchenko2010 .

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 1381 with the bright S0s that follow elliptical relations and show strong Mg indices, but notes central indices cannot test whether this applies galaxy-wide.

quotes.quote: First, faint S0s have a large scatter with respect to the main relations, while the bright galaxies NGC 1380 and NGC 1381 closely follow the relations for ellipticals estimated by K00 (red dashed lines in Figure fig:indsig ).

What is clear from almost all these panels is that the three highest-dispersion galaxies in the sample (open symbols in Figure fig:FevsMg ) are not accurately described by models that assume solar abundance ratios, presenting stronger Mg indices for a given Fe index than the model predictions. Among these three galaxies, the effect seems to be stronger for NGC 1380 and 1381, while NGC 1316 (the merger remnant) is usually closer to the solar-model grids.
However, whether this picture can be applied to the entire NGC 1380 and NGC 1381 galaxies or only to their nuclei, is something that cannot be tested with central line indices alone.

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 170 as hosting the sample's most flattened nuclear star cluster, with an almost edge-on view, strong central rotation, and a flattened central dispersion drop.

quotes.quote: FCC 170 hosts the most flattened NSC in our sample. The galaxy itself is viewed almost edge-on. The strong rotation in the central 2 2 reaches 100 . The maps has a flattened drop in the centre.

sources.summary: FCC 170 is described as an edge-on lenticular ancient infaller with a boxy bulge, thin and thickened disc, and a detailed Fornax3D stellar-kinematics analysis.

quotes.quote: FCC 170 (NGC 1381) -- Fig. fig:FCC170map FCC 170 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy in the high-density region of the cluster at R_ proj =0.4^ west of FCC 213 (NGC 1399). It belongs to the north-south clump of galaxies and is an ancient infaller. It is characterised by a boxy bulge and a thin disc, which becomes thicker at larger radii ( Iodice2018 ; Pinna2019b ). The analysis of the kinematics and properties of the stellar component of this object based on F3D data is presented and extensively discussed by Pinna2019b .

sources.summary: The paper identifies a distinctive rotational feature and strong h_3 signal in NGC 1381, interpreting them as evidence that this edge-on galaxy is barred rather than unbarred.

quotes.quote: NGC 1381 Figure fig:comp1 reveals a distinctive feature in this galaxy's rotational motion which was not apparent in the previous data. There is a correspondingly very strong signal in the h_3 profile shown in Figure fig:1381dat . Such kinematic features are characteristic of a bar viewed edge-on (Bureau Athanassoula 2005), so we would seem to have identified this edge-on galaxy, previously designated as unbarred, as a barred system. The new velocity dispersion profile diverges at large radii from the D'Onofrio et al. \ (1995) data, but this would seem to be because the latter has been artificially enhanced in regions where the dispersion approaches the resolution limit of the older data.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1381 as a clear edge-on S0 whose pCMD resembles NGC 1374, using the pair to test morphology and substructure diagnostics.

quotes.quote: It is worth noticing that the pCMDs of NGC 1374, classified as E0, and NGC 1381, classified as S0, are quite similar. For example, while NGC 1381 is a clear edge-on S0 galaxy, NGC 1374 looks like an elliptical galaxy but it could also be a face-on S0 (Figure Figure4 ). For example, from the particular cases of NGC 1374 and NGC 1381, we wonder if this kind of diagrams could become a powerful tool to clarify dubious morphological classifications, besides allowing the identification of internal substructures.

gmw_00000861.name: SDP.81
gmw_00000861.overview: SDP.81 is a gravitationally lensed, dusty starburst galaxy at redshift z = 3.042 [arXiv:1503.02025, arXiv:1608.01696]. Reconstructed imaging reveals a complex, clumpy dust morphology [arXiv:1503.02025, arXiv:1503.08720] and a rotating molecular gas disk that appears to be interacting with near-infrared components [arXiv:1503.08720, arXiv:1709.01514].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDP.81 as a z=3.042 gravitationally lensed starburst whose ALMA continuum reconstruction reveals complex dust morphology and yields intrinsic star-formation and dust properties.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we present the analysis of high angular resolution imaging of the heated dust continuum emission from the gravitationally lensed starburst galaxy SDP.81 (H-ATLAS J090311.6+003906; z = 3.042 0.001; Negrello2010,Frayer2011,Bussmann2013,Dye14,Negrello14 ) that were taken during the science verification phase of ALMA. These observations were taken using the new long baseline capability of ALMA and provide a unique view of this star-forming galaxy. We determine the structure of the background source by applying a Bayesian pixellated visibility-fitting lens modelling technique rybak15 .

The reconstructed continuum emission of SDP.81 at 236 and 290 GHz possesses a complex morphology; there are three distinct regions of the source that are resolved in our pixelated reconstructions, but there is also evidence of a wider region of low-surface brightness emission (see Fig. fig:band6 ). This outer region is consistent with the extended emission that forms the Einstein ring seen in our low resolution images (see Fig. fig:images ). It is clear that the sub-kpc structure of the galaxy is not uniform, but contains several regions of intense dust emission that are presumably associated with recent star-formation.

Using our measurement of the total magnification, we have determined the intrinsic properties of SDP.81 from a fit to the UV/optical-to-sub-mm broad-band spectrum that was carried out by Negrello14 . We find a star-formation rate of 315 60 M _ yr ^ 1 , a far-infrared luminosity of 3.1 0.4 10 ^ 12 L _ and a dust mass of 6.4 1.5 10 ^ 8 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper uses SDP.81 as a lensed dusty star-forming galaxy test case, simulating IRIS stellar-continuum observations and emphasizing the high source-plane resolution.

quotes.quote: Using one of the highest resolution images ever taken by ALMA of a gravitationally-lensed galaxy (SDP.81 ), we generated an IRIS simulation of its expected stellar continuum emission. SDP.81 was imaged by ALMA for the science verification campaign of their longest baseline ( 15 km). SDP.81 is a gravitationally-lensed, dusty, star-forming galaxy at z=3.042. The lens is a galaxy at z=0.2999. The background galaxy is almost perfectly aligned behind the lens such that it produces an Einstein ring.

With this lensing configuration, SDP.81 is magnified by a factor of 11. At the 15 km baseline, ALMA's spatial resolution is 23 mas or 180 pc at z=3.042. Taking into account the lens model, the source plane is able to sample at 10 pc resolution.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDP 81 as a z~3 lensed dusty starburst, measuring clump-scale SFR density, CO velocity dispersion, gas mass, and turbulence-sensitive star-formation behavior.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage Using high-resolution (sub-kiloparsec scale) data obtained by ALMA, we analyze the star formation rate (SFR), gas content and kinematics in SDP 81, a gravitationally-lensed starburst galaxy at redshift 3 . We estimate the SFR surface density ( ) in the brightest clump of this galaxy to be 357^ +135 _ 85 , over an area of 0.07 0.02 ^2 . Using the intensity-weighted velocity of CO (5-4), we measure the turbulent velocity dispersion in the plane-of-the-sky and find _ v,turb = 37 5 km s ^ 1 for the clump, in good agreement with previous estimates along the line of sight, corrected for beam smearing.

J090311.6+003906 (hereafter, referred to as SDP 81) was detected as a lensed galaxy in the H-ATLAS survey of bright submillimeter galaxies (SMGs) by where the redshift was measured as z = 3.042 0.001 through ground based CO measurements. It falls in the popular definition of SMGs where the 850 m flux density S_ 850 > 3 mJy and infrared luminosity L_ IR 10^ 12 2006ApJ...650..592K,2008MNRAS.384.1597C,2011ApJ...743..159H . SDP 81 has also been established to be a dusty star-forming galaxy in previous works ( 2014MNRAS.440.1999N ; 2015ApJ...806L..17S (hereafter, S15); 2015MNRAS.452.2258D,2015PASJ...67...93H,2015MNRAS.451L..40R,2015MNRAS.453L..26R,2015PASJ...67...72T,2015ApJ...811..115W,2016ApJ...823...37H,2016MNRAS.457.2936I ). Even though there are significant uncertainties in determining the stellar mass of SDP 81, we note that it lies 1-2 orders of magnitude above the main sequence on the stellar mass star formation rate ( M _ SFR ) plane 2014ApJS..214...15S,2015A&A...575A..74S,2015ApJS..219....8C,2015ApJ...808L..49G . Thus, SDP 81 falls under the category of extreme starburst galaxies and is an ideal candidate to test star formation relations.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA long-baseline imaging of the lensed z=3.042 submillimeter galaxy SDP.81, resolving its Einstein-ring arcs, dust continuum, CO emission, and weak foreground-lens core emission.

quotes.quote: We present initial results of very high resolution Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations of the z =3.042 gravitationally lensed submillimeter galaxy HATLAS J090311.6+003906 (SDP.81). These observations were carried out using a very extended configuration as part of Science Verification for the 2014 ALMA Long Baseline Campaign, with baselines of up to 15 km. We present continuum imaging at 151, 236 and 290 GHz, at unprecedented angular resolutions as fine as 23 milliarcseconds (mas), corresponding to an un-magnified spatial scale of 180 pc at z =3.042. The ALMA images clearly show two main gravitational arc components of an Einstein ring, with emission tracing a radius of 1.5 .

The CO emission, at an angular resolution of 170 mas, is found to broadly trace the gravitational arc structures but with differing morphologies between the CO transitions and compared to the dust continuum. Our detection of line emission, using only the shortest baselines, provides the most resolved detection to date of thermal emission in an extragalactic source. Finally, we detect weak unresolved continuum emission from a position that is spatially coincident with the center of the lens, with a spectral index that is consistent with emission from the core of the foreground lensing galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs SDP.81 as a z=3.042 lensed source with clumpy dust, rotating CO disk, high gas and dynamical masses, interacting near-infrared components, and intense star formation.

quotes.quote: We have modelled Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array (ALMA) long baseline imaging of the strong gravitational lens system H-ATLAS J090311.6 + 003906 (SDP.81). We have reconstructed the distribution of band 6 and 7 continuum emission in the z=3.042 source and we have determined its kinematic properties by reconstructing CO(5-4) and CO(8-7) line emission in bands 4 and 6. The continuum imaging reveals a highly non-uniform distribution of dust with clumps on scales of 200 pc. In contrast, the CO line emission shows a relatively smooth, disk-like velocity field which is well fit by a rotating disk model with an inclination angle of (40 5)^ and an asymptotic rotation velocity of 320 kms ^ 1 . The inferred dynamical mass within 1.5 kpc is (3.5 0.5) 10^ 10 M _ which is comparable to the total molecular gas masses of (2.7 0.5) 10^ 10 M _ and (3.5 0.6) 10^ 10 M _ from the dust continuum emission and CO emission respectively.

Our new reconstruction of the lensed HST near-infrared emission shows two objects which appear to be interacting, with the rotating disk of gas and dust revealed by ALMA distinctly offset from the near-infrared emission. The clumpy nature of the dust and a low value of the Toomre parameter of Q 0.3 suggest that the disk is in a state of collapse. We estimate a star formation rate in the disk of 470 80 M _ /yr with an efficiency 65 times greater than typical low-redshift galaxies.

sources.summary: The review describes SDP.81 as a strongly lensed z=3.042 galaxy whose ALMA continuum, CO, and water-line maps resolve arcs, an Einstein ring, star formation, and molecular-gas structure.

quotes.quote: A remarkable object was observed to demonstrate these capabilities, with baselines up to 15km: SDP.81 ALMA2015 . This gravitationally lensed galaxy at z=3.042 was discovered by the Herschel survey H-ATLAS Eales2010, Negrello2010 . Its redshift was determined through CO lines; the lensing galaxy is at z = 0.299, and the amplification factor is = 11 Bussmann2013 . With a beam of 25mas at (180pc at z=3.042), the ALMA continuum map reveals the two gravitational arcs with unprecedented sharpness.

The two arcs are part of an Einstein ring, of radius 1.5". The continuum from the arcs comes from dust, which indicates a highly obscured high-z star forming galaxy, with an SFR = 527 M _ /yr. The three CO lines detected (from J=5, 8 and 10) show regions in the galaxy of different excitation, implying a complex structure.

sources.summary: The paper models SDP 81 as a galaxy-scale lens with ALMA science-verification data, preferred gradient regularisation, differing PL+MP shear, and altered posterior parameters.

quotes.quote: ALMA observations We obtained data from the ALMA archive for three gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxies: SPT 0532 50 (SPT S J053250 5047.1), SPT 0538 50 (SPT S J053816 5030.8), and SDP 81 (H-ATLAS J090311.6 + 003906). These are galaxy-scale lenses with known source and lens redshifts ( Negrello:2010,Spilker:2016 ; Table table:obs ). Details of the lens systems and ALMA observations are given in Table table:obs . The observations of SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0538 50 consisted of multiple epochs in compact and extended antenna configurations: we considered only the long-baseline configurations for this experiment to maintain the higher angular resolution. The data for SDP 81 were taken during the 2014 ALMA long baseline science verification campaign, using an atypical array configuration Vlahakis2015 .

We find that gradient regularisation gives the strongest evidence for SDP 81 and SPT 0538 50, while the area-weighted gradient gives stronger evidence for SPT 0532 50. 1.5 Evidence for complex angular structure Table table:nested shows the marginalised posterior of the lens parameters and the relative evidence for the three datasets and the two different lens models tested here. The maximum a posteriori PL+MP lens model is shown in Fig. fig:lens_models for each system. The differences in the convergence between the lens models are shown in Fig. fig:kappa_diff and have convergence differences up to 5 to 10 percent at the Einstein radius. The sources are not visually different between the PL and PL+MP models, as can be seen in Fig. fig:sources . Nevertheless, the PL+MP is preferred over a PL for all three lenses, with a relative Bayes factor (difference in E ) of 28, 75, and 120 for SDP 81, SPT 0532 50 and SPT 0538 50, respectively (i.e. strong to decisive evidence in favour of more complex angular structure; Kass:1995 ).

Interestingly, the shear strength is largely consistent between the PL and PL+MP models, although the shear position angle does differ by several sigma for SDP 81 and SPT 0532. Altogether, we do not see clear evidence that the PL model is not compensating for some unmodelled angular structure when multipoles are not included, in contrast with the findings of . We find that the PL+MP model posterior for SDP 81 differs significantly in ellipticity, external shear, and multipole amplitudes from that of using the same set of observations.

sources.summary: The paper reports lensing evidence in SDP.81: WFC3 shows a faint arclet, and lens subtraction reveals two arclets matching submillimeter structure.

quotes.quote: Hints of lensing are also found in the WFC3 images of SDP.81, where a faint arclet is visible 1.5 arcseconds away from the central elliptical galaxy in the west direction.

SDP.81 . The fit to the Keck image performed in N10 required two profiles: a compact elliptical S e rsic core plus a subdominant exponential disk. Two S e rsic profiles are required to achieve a good lens subtraction in both HST bands, with indexes n_ s,1 = 2.3 and n_ s,2 = 2.0 for F110W, and n_ s,1 = 2.9 and n_ s,2 = 0.9 for F160W. Once the lens is subtracted (Fig. fig:HST_images2 ), two arclets, on opposite sides with respect to the centre of the lens, are revealed. The smaller one, on the west, was barely visible before the subtraction of the lens. The remarkable similarity of the residuals to the structure revealed in the sub-mm by the SMA (see fig. fig:HST_images2 ) supports the robustness of the lens fitting procedure.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs SDP.81 as a z=3.042 lensed starburst galaxy with excitation-dependent CO structure, extended CO(5-4), compact CO(8-7), and rotating molecular gas.

quotes.quote: At the same time, strong gravitational lensing has allowed the study of high-redshift sub-mm galaxies on 50-100 pc-scales, which is comparable to what is achieved at lower redshift swinbank10 . In this Letter, we take advantage of the enhanced spatial resolution provided by the long baseline capability of ALMA, in combination with the magnifying power of strong gravitational lensing to study the molecular gas distribution and kinematics of the starburst galaxy SDP.81 (H-ATLAS J090311.6+003906; z = 3.042 0.001; Negrello2010,Negrello14,Frayer2011,Bussmann2013,Dye14 ).

fig:moment0 . These maps show clear excitation dependent structure in the molecular gas distribution of SDP.81. The CO (5-4) emission has both diffuse and multiple compact structures that extend over 3 kpc in the north-south direction, with an additional low surface brightness component that is 1--2 kpc in size towards the north-east of the source. The emission from the CO (8-7) transition is much more compact, with an extent of about 1.5 kpc, and there is only a single brightness clump.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HATLAS J090311.6+003907 as SDP.81, a lensed z = 3.04 submillimetre galaxy whose reconstructed ALMA/HST imaging resolves giant molecular clouds and interacting components.

quotes.quote: HATLAS J090311.6+003907 (HerBS-19) is also known as SDP.81, and has recently been observed by . showed SDP.81 is lensed using 880 m Sub-Millimetre Array observations. and reconstructed the galaxy from the ALMA observation, by modelling the distorting effect of the lens.

They found a magnification of 11. This reconstructed image features details on the scale of hundreds of parsecs, and the image shows resolved individual giant molecular clouds in a z = 3.04 galaxy. SDP.81 appears, through reconstructed HST and ALMA imaging, to be two interacting objects, where the dust disk is in a state of collapse.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDP 81 as a z=3 comparison galaxy, comparing three CII/SFR measurements with model predictions and adopting a literature magnification factor.

quotes.quote: At z=3 , we have three measurements for SDP 81 2011MNRAS.415.3473V,2014A&A...568A..62D,2018MNRAS.481...59Z and one for HLock01 2018MNRAS.473...20R . For SDP 81 , two of the measurements coincide ( 2014A&A...568A..62D use the values from 2011MNRAS.415.3473V ): the empty squares with (L_ C II / SFR ) 7.6 . The other measurement for SDP 81 comes from 2018MNRAS.481...59Z , which is the leftmost point in the z=3 panel. The main difference between the values of 2011MNRAS.415.3473V and 2018MNRAS.481...59Z is the different data reduction methods, and therefore we rely on the more recent results of 2018MNRAS.481...59Z . Both SDP 81 and HLock01 are close to the model predictions of Recal-L0025N0752 .
For H-ATLAS J084933.4+021443 and SDP 81 , we adopt 2.8 and 25, respectively, as magnification factors 2017ApJ...837...12W,2011MNRAS.415.3473V .

sources.summary: The paper models J090311.6 + 003906 as a cusp-caustic H-ATLAS lens whose near-infrared lensed emission matches SMA data and is well fitted with little residual structure.

quotes.quote: J090311.6 + 003906 (ID81): This is a classic cusp-caustic configuration lens. The near-IR emission in the lensed image closely matches that of the SMA data and unsurprisingly results in a consistent magnification. The reconstructed source in both HST filters shows little structure other than a slight elongation along the NE-SW direction. The centroid of the source is well aligned with that found by B13. The lens model is an excellent fit to the observed data and leaves no significant residuals.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA observations of the strong-lensing system SDP.81 to detect a roughly 10^9 solar-mass subhalo near one image and constrain low-mass dark-matter substructure around the lens.

quotes.quote: We study the abundance of substructure in the matter density near galaxies using ALMA Science Verification observations of the strong lensing system SDP.81. We present a method to measure the abundance of subhalos around galaxies using interferometric observations of gravitational lenses. Using simulated ALMA observations, we explore the effects of various systematics, including antenna phase errors and source priors, and show how such errors may be measured or marginalized. We apply our formalism to ALMA observations of SDP.81. We find evidence for the presence of a M=10^ 8.96 0.12 M_ subhalo near one of the images, with a significance of 6.9 in a joint fit to data from bands 6 and 7; the effect of the subhalo is also detected in both bands individually.

We find hints of additional substructure, warranting further study using the full SDP.81 dataset (including, for example, the spectroscopic imaging of the lensed carbon monoxide emission). We compare the results of this search to the predictions of CDM halos, and find that given current uncertainties in the host halo properties of SDP.81, our measurements of substructure are consistent with theoretical expectations.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.81 as an H-ATLAS lensed source studied with ALMA, with reconstructed lens and dust emission, measured gas kinematics, disturbed morphology, and radio emission likely from the foreground-lens AGN.

quotes.quote: SDP.81 has been the target of observations and modelling in the ALMA long-baseline campaign ( Partnership2015 , Rybak2015 , Rybak2015a , Dye2015 , Swinbank2015 , Tamura2015a , Hatsukade2015a , Hezaveh2016 ). These studies led to the reconstruction of the matter distribution of the foreground lens through the detection of low-mass substructures, together with the pixelated surface brightness distribution of dust in the lensed source. Thanks to ALMA CO and CII spectroscopic data, it was also possible to measure with high precision the gas mass distribution and the kinematics of the clumps, revealing a disturbed morphology of the stellar, gas and dust components (see also Rybak2020 ).

H-ATLASJ090311.6+003907 (SDP.81) is detected in the FIRST catalogue, was observed by the Extended Very Large Array (EVLA) at 8.4 GHz ( Valtchanov2011 , Rybak2015 ). However, its radio emission is likely to be originated from the AGN hosted by the foreground lens ( Tamura2015a , Rybak2020 ); for this reason it has been excluded a priori as a possible counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper includes SDP.81 as a Herschel-selected lensed DSFG, finding point-like K-band continuum likely from the foreground lens AGN and noting its extended CO/[C II] gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: The three sources selected from the Herschel surveys H-ATLAS survey Negrello2010, Negrello2017 and HerMES Oliver2012, Wardlow2012 are SDP.81, SDP.130, and HXMM.02 (also known as Orochi, Ikarashi2011 ). All three targets have HCN(1--0) line observable in the JVLA K-band, robust lens models based on high-resolution ALMA imaging, and existing CO(1--0) measurements.

SDP.81, SDP130, and HXMM.02 show continuum emission that is point-like and likely due to the AGN in the lensing galaxy. Namely, the 1.3-cm flux in SDP.81 is consistent with the expected signal from the foreground AGN based on the radio spectrum fit from Tamura2015 .

This is likely the case in SDP.81, which has a very extended (over 10 kpc) reservoir of CO(1--0) and [ C ii ] gas Valtchanov2011, Rybak2020b , whereas the FIR-bright starburst is only 2-3 kpc across and highly magnified.

sources.summary: The review discusses SDP.81 as a lensed DSFG observed by ALMA, used to test phase calibration effects and reported to host a candidate subhalo with inconsistent model interpretations.

quotes.quote: This was investigated using ALMA observations of the DSFG SDP.81 by , with a test of the data sensitivity to the subhalo detection both with and without a treatment for phase errors. and addressed this issue by including a single antenna-based phase correction as a free parameter in the lens modeling, self-consistently incorporating uncertainties due to this systematic effect into the model posterior.

From the analysis of the ALMA long baseline campaign data for the lens system SDP.81, Hezaveh2016b reported the detection at the 5- level a subhalo with a total PJ mass of 10^9 M_ . Inoue2016 also found a detection in this lens system. However, the inferred subhalo position and lens macro-model are inconsistent between the two analyses.

gmw_00000862.name: JVAS B1030+074
gmw_00000862.overview: JVAS B1030+074 is a strong gravitational lens system featuring a background flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1604.00173]. The primary lensing galaxy is an early-type galaxy with a nearby companion [arXiv:1604.00173], and its lensed images exhibit a polarization time delay of approximately 146 days [arXiv:1808.00757].

sources.summary: The paper uses VLBI/HSA non-detection of the central image in B1030+074 to constrain the lensing galaxy mass profile, break radius, and possible central black-hole effect.

quotes.quote: We present a 1.7-GHz High Sensitivity Array (HSA) observation of the double-image radio lens system B1030+074. The data are combined with archive VLBA and global-VLBI observations, and careful consideration is given to the effects of noise, clean ing and self-calibration. An upper limit is derived for the strength of the central image of 180 Jy (90 confidence level), considerably greater than would have been expected on the basis of a simple analysis. This gives a lower limit of 10^3 for the ratio of the brightest image to the central image. For cusped models of lens mass distributions, we have made use of this non-detection to constrain the relation between inner power-law slope of the lensing galaxy mass profile, and its break radius r_b .

For r_b>130 pc the power-law slope is required to be close to isothermal ( >1.8 ). A flatter inner slope is allowed if a massive black hole is present at the centre of the lensing galaxy, but the effect of the black hole is small unless it is 10 times more massive than that implied by the relation between black hole mass and stellar velocity dispersion.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VLA monitoring of the lensed quasar JVAS B1030+074, measuring a 146-day polarization time delay and attributing extra image-B variability to propagation effects.

quotes.quote: We have analysed archival VLA 8.4-GHz monitoring data of the gravitational lens system JVAS B1030+074 with the goal of determining the time delay between the two lensed images via the polarization variability. In contrast to the previously published total intensity variations, we detect correlated variability in polarized flux density, percentage polarization and polarization position angle. The latter includes a fast ( < 5 d) 90-degree rotation event. Our best estimate of the time delay is 146 6 d (1 ), considerably longer than that predicted by the lens model presented in the discovery paper.

No time delay is visible in total flux density and this is partially due to much greater scatter in the image B measurements. This must be due to a propagation effect as the radio waves pass through the ISM of the lensing galaxies or the Galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents deeper VLA and e-MERLIN imaging of CLASS B1030+074, using the central-image non-detection to constrain the lens galaxy mass profile and possible SMBH influence.

quotes.quote: We present new VLA 22-GHz and e-MERLIN 5-GHz observations of CLASS B1030+074, a two-image strong gravitational lens system whose background source is a compact flat-spectrum radio quasar.

The noise levels achieved in our new B1030+074 images reach 3 and represent an improvement in central image constraints of nearly an order of magnitude over previous work, with correspondingly better resulting limits on the shape of the central mass profile of the lensing galaxy.

The lens appears on optical/IR Hubble Space Telescope images as a faint and slightly irregular galaxy, with a companion (Jackson, Xanthopoulos Browne 2000) which is about half an arcsecond, or 3 kpc, distant. The primary lens is almost certainly an early-type, and is well fit by a de Vaucouleurs profile which is close to spherical ( 1-b/a=0.22 0.04 , Leh ar et al.

gmw_00000863.name: SDSS J074426.12+291607.4
gmw_00000863.overview: SDSS J074426.12+291607.4 is a quiescent galaxy [arXiv:2301.05484] located in a close pair of quiescent galaxies at a redshift of z=0.0396 [arXiv:2301.05484]. The galaxy is best modeled with an exponential disc and a bulge [arXiv:2301.05484], and it has hosted an optical and ultra-soft X-ray transient [arXiv:2301.05484].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J074426.12+291607.4 as J0744's quiescent host, analyzes its MaNGA spectrum and morphology, and finds a luminous, extended dwarf-like galaxy in a close pair.

quotes.quote: the optical transient, AT 2020uwq/ ZTF20acgrymn https://www.wis-tns.org/object/2020uwq , reported to the TNS on 2020-10-04. within the Zwicky Transient Facility survey data (ZTF; bellm_zwicky_2019,graham_zwicky_2019 ), which is associated with the centre of the smaller galaxy in Fig. fig:finder_chart (herein SDSS J074426.12+291607.4- section sec:host_properties ). As it is highly unlikely that there also exists an ultra-soft X-ray transient (section sec:xray_observations ) evolving contemporaneously in the more massive galaxy seen in Fig. fig:finder_chart , we consider the transient X-ray emission initially detected by eROSITA to also originate from SDSS J074426.12+291607.4 throughout this work. No X-ray point source was detected at the position of the host galaxy in this observation.

The host galaxy of J0744 was observed on 2015-11-10 by the SDSS Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory (MaNGA) survey bundy_overview_2014 , which provides integral field spectroscopy for galaxies with z < 0.15 . We extracted a spectrum from a 0.5 ^ x0.5 ^ spaxel with spaxel coordinates (32,17), covering the Gaia position of J0744, with this showing a quiescent galaxy-like spectrum (Fig. fig:optical_spectra and fig:ppxf_fit ). One of the most striking aspects of J0744 is its host galaxy (Fig. fig:finder_chart ), which is part of a pair of quiescent galaxies at z=0.0396 .

The inferred morphological parameters for the host of J0744 and its companion are presented in Table tab:morphology , with simard_catalog_2011 finding that an exponential disc and bulge, with n_ b =3.5 0.3 , is the best fitting model for the host of J0744. The Galactic extinction corrected SDSS g band model magnitude of the host is 17.89 0.01 , translating to an absolute g band magnitude of 18.39 0.01 .

gmw_00000864.name: ESO121-G006
gmw_00000864.overview: ESO121-G006 is a highly inclined galaxy [arXiv:0906.0772]. It is likely to host an active galactic nucleus that is optically unidentified due to its high inclination [arXiv:0906.0772].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO121-G006 as a likely optically unidentified AGN in a highly inclined host with a high inferred [OIV]-to-[OIII] flux ratio.

quotes.quote: Ten of our mid-IR classified AGNs are not unambiguously identified as AGNs at optical wavelengths: five are classified as HII galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 1448, NGC 1792, NGC 4945, and NGC 5128), four are classified as LINERS (NGC 0660, NGC 3627, NGC 3628, and NGC 5195), and one does not have good-quality optical spectroscopic data (ESO121-G006). Although ESO121-G006 does not have a good-quality optical spectrum, since it is hosted in a highly inclined galaxy we would expect it to be an optically unidentified AGN; see 3.3 and Fig. fig_agn_images . Indeed, the four optically unidentified AGNs with the highest [ OIV ] - [ OIII ] flux ratios, which imply A_V>3 mags, are all hosted by galaxies that are either highly inclined or have obscuring dust lanes; ESO121-G006 does not have good-quality optical spectroscopy, however, since it resides in a highly inclined host galaxy, it is likely to be an optically unidentified AGN with a high [ OIV ] - [ OIII ] flux ratio.

gmw_00000865.name: Mrk 709 S
gmw_00000865.overview: Mrk 709 S is a low-metallicity, star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1405.0278] that is interacting with its northern companion, Mrk 709 N [arXiv:1405.0278, arXiv:2103.05655]. The galaxy hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.05655, arXiv:2108.09307] powered by an accreting massive black hole [arXiv:1405.0278].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 709 S as the southern dwarf component with clumpy star formation, a high star-formation rate, and evidence for a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: We find that the southern galaxy, Mrk 709 S, is undergoing a clumpy mode of star formation resembling that seen in high-redshift galaxies, with multiple young clusters/clumps having stellar masses between 10^7 and 10^8 M_ . Furthermore, we present additional evidence for a low-luminosity AGN in Mrk 709 S (first identified by reinesetal2014 using radio and X-ray observations), including the detection of the coronal [ Fe X ] optical emission line. Moreover, reinesetal2014 present observational evidence at radio and X-ray wavelengths for an active massive BH in Mrk 709 S, the southern galaxy in the pair. Mrk 709 S has intense, on-going star formation with a star formation rate that is an order of magnitude higher than the Large Magellanic Cloud (and is significantly more luminous than the LMC), yet the two dwarf galaxies have similar stellar masses with M_ < 3 10^ 9 M_ reinesetal2014,vandermarel2002,Whitneyetal2008 .

The northern and southern galaxies, Mrk 709 N and Mrk 709 S, were identified by reinesetal2014 using SDSS imaging, however only Mrk 709 S had an SDSS spectrum, which indicated a redshift of z=0.052 ( d 214 Mpc). Therefore, while Mrk 709 N and Mrk 709 S are part of the same system, the western component is a background galaxy (J094917.85+165247.1) and not part of the Mrk 709 system. There is a striking bridge of star clusters between Mrk 709 N and Mrk 709 S, strongly emitting in H .

sources.summary: The paper confirms Mrk 709 N as the northern dwarf companion at the same redshift as Mrk 709 S, with lower star formation and a tidal bridge between them.

quotes.quote: The redshift of Mrk 709 N is measured in this work using Keck spectroscopy. The spectra indicate that Mrk 709 N is at the same redshift as Mrk 709 S ( z=0.052 ), but the western galaxy is at a higher redshift ( z=0.364 ) and therefore not part of the Mrk 709 system. The northern and southern galaxies, Mrk 709 N and Mrk 709 S, were identified by reinesetal2014 using SDSS imaging, however only Mrk 709 S had an SDSS spectrum, which indicated a redshift of z=0.052 ( d 214 Mpc). Therefore, while Mrk 709 N and Mrk 709 S are part of the same system, the western component is a background galaxy (J094917.85+165247.1) and not part of the Mrk 709 system.

There is a striking bridge of star clusters between Mrk 709 N and Mrk 709 S, strongly emitting in H . At the distance of Mrk 709, this corresponds to a projected physical length of 10 kpc. t We estimate the star formation rates (SFRs) of Mrk 709 S, Mrk 709 N and the bridge using H fluxes determined from our HST images and the following relation from : log SFR (M_ yr ^ 1 ) = log L_ H 41.27, where L_ H is the luminosity of H in units of erg s ^ 1 . We find SFRs of approximately 1, 0.2, and 0.5 M_ yr ^ 1 for Mrk 709 S, Mrk 709 N, and the bridge, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra, VLA, and SDSS observations of the low-metallicity dwarf system Mrk 709, finding an interacting compact pair and X-ray/radio evidence for an accreting massive black hole.

quotes.quote: Here we present high-resolution X-ray and radio observations of the low-metallicity, star-forming, dwarf-galaxy system Mrk 709 with the Chandra X-ray Observatory and the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA). These data reveal spatially coincident hard X-ray and radio point sources with luminosities suggesting the presence of an accreting massive BH ( M_ BH 10^ 5-7 ). Based on imaging from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS), we find that Mrk 709 consists of a pair of compact dwarf galaxies that appear to be interacting with one another.

The position of the candidate massive BH is consistent with the optical center of the southern galaxy (Mrk 709 S), while no evidence for an active BH is seen in the northern galaxy (Mrk 709 N). We derive stellar masses of M_ 2.5 10^9 M_ and M_ 1.1 10^9 M_ for Mrk 709 S and Mrk 709 N, respectively, and present an analysis of the SDSS spectrum of the BH-host Mrk 709 S. At a metallicity of just 10 solar, Mrk 709 is among the most metal-poor galaxies with evidence for an active galactic nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 709 S as the southern galaxy and likely black-hole host, with an SDSS spectrum, star-formation-dominated line ratios, and central hard X-ray/radio emission.
