sources.paper_coordinate_text: The spectroscopic fiber is centered at RA=09 ^h 49 ^m 18 031, Dec=+16 ^ 52 ' 44 24.

quotes.quote: However, only the southern galaxy, which we designate Mrk 709 S, has an SDSS spectrum (and redshift) so we cannot definitively rule out a chance positional alignment. The spectroscopic fiber is centered at RA=09 ^h 49 ^m 18 031, Dec=+16 ^ 52 ' 44 24. We analyzed the SDSS spectrum of Mrk 709 S to determine if the gas probed by strong emission lines is primarily ionized by massive stars or an AGN (Fig. fig:spec ).

A Candidate Massive Black Hole Our Chandra observations reveal a hard X-ray point source with a luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV (5.0 2.9) 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 at the center of Mrk 709 S. This luminosity is almost certainly dominated by a single accreting massive BH, as it is significantly higher than the expected contribution from X-ray binaries (XRBs). For star-forming galaxies with SFR /M_ 5.9 10^ 11 yr ^ 1 (such as Mrk 709 S), high-mass XRBs are expected to dominate the hard (2--10 keV) X-ray emission with the total luminosity of XRBs given by L_ 2-10 keV = M_ + SFR, where = (9.05 0.37) 10^ 28 erg s ^ 1 M_ ^ 1 and = (1.62 0.22) 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 (M_ yr ^ 1 ) lehmeretal2010 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 709 N as the northern member of the interacting dwarf pair, with no active black-hole evidence and an estimated stellar mass of 1.1e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Based on imaging from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS), we find that Mrk 709 consists of a pair of compact dwarf galaxies that appear to be interacting with one another. The position of the candidate massive BH is consistent with the optical center of the southern galaxy (Mrk 709 S), while no evidence for an active BH is seen in the northern galaxy (Mrk 709 N). We derive stellar masses of M_ 2.5 10^9 M_ and M_ 1.1 10^9 M_ for Mrk 709 S and Mrk 709 N, respectively, and present an analysis of the SDSS spectrum of the BH-host Mrk 709 S.

We obtained background-subtracted z band fluxes of 284 6 and 128 5 nMgy for Mrk 709 S and Mrk 709 N, respectively. The corresponding stellar masses are M_ 2.5 10^9 \ for Mrk 709 S and M_ 1.1 10^9 \ for Mrk 709 N.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NSA 533731 with Mrk 709S, an interacting-pair dwarf already confirmed as an LLAGN, and adds strong Fe X and radio AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: NSA 533731: This galaxy is also known as Mrk 709S, is in a pair of interacting galaxies first studied in depth by , and is one of the two objects that inspired this work. Mrk 709S was confirmed to harbor a LLAGN with a RIAF engine and strong out-flowing winds by . In this work, we find that the observed \ emission is almost 23 times higher than that seen in SN2005ip, and has an additional marker of AGN activity via strong radio emission seen in both the FIRST and VLASS surveys.

gmw_00000866.name: SXDS1 13015
gmw_00000866.overview: SXDS1 13015 is a massive main-sequence galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.45 [arXiv:2101.08396]. Observations reveal a rotating molecular gas disk with a half-light radius of 2.3 kpc, where the molecular gas is more centrally concentrated than the stellar distribution [arXiv:2101.08396].

sources.summary: The paper studies SXDS1 13015 with ALMA, deriving molecular gas distribution and kinematics, identifying a UV clump, and finding molecular gas more centrally concentrated than stars.

quotes.quote: The sample galaxy in this study, SXDS1 13015, is one of the most massive main sequence galaxies at z 1.4 in the sample galaxies by Yabe12 and Seko16 .

We presented the results of sub-arcsecond ALMA ^ 12 CO(2-1) and ^ 12 CO(5-4) observations toward a massive main sequence galaxy at z = 1.45 (SXDS1 13015). These observations enabled us to study molecular gas properties, its distribution in the galaxy, and CO(5-4)/CO(2-1) flux ratio in a UV clump detected in the rest-frame UV HST image. By fitting a rotating gas disk model to the CO(2-1) data, we derived molecular gas distribution and kinematics.

iii) By using GalPaK^ 3D , the molecular gas distribution of the galaxy traced by CO(2-1) is well represented by the rotating disk model (Figure fig:Galpak ). The half-light radius of the modeled gas disk is 2.3 \ kpc . iv) By comparing the molecular gas distribution and stellar distribution derived by fitting S e rsic models to the rest optical HST images, the molecular gas is more concentrated in the galactic center (Figure fig:intrinsicprofile ). The gas mass fraction decreases with increasing galactocentric radius from 0.6 at the central region to 0.2 at 3 r_ 1/2 , suggesting that the galaxy is forming its bulge.

gmw_00000867.name: PSO J0309+27
gmw_00000867.overview: PSO J0309+27 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.08632, arXiv:2010.11193] and the most distant known blazar, located at a redshift of z ~ 6.1 [arXiv:2404.03671, arXiv:2209.15043]. It hosts a kiloparsec-scale X-ray jet [arXiv:2111.08632] that extends 20 kiloparsecs in projection [arXiv:2111.08632, arXiv:2209.15043].

sources.summary: The paper reports PSO J030947.49+271757.31 as a z=6.1 radio-loud AGN with the most distant resolved kiloparsec-scale X-ray jet, likely powered by IC/CMB emission.

quotes.quote: We report on the direct observation of an extended X-ray jet in the z =6.1 radio-loud active galactic nucleus PSO J030947.49+271757.31 from a deep Chandra X-ray observation (128 ksec). This detection represents the most distant kiloparsec-scale off-nuclear emission resolved in X-rays to date. The angular distance of the emission is 4 (corresponding to 20 kpc at z =6.1), along the same direction of the jet observed at parsec scales in previous VLBA high-resolution radio observations. Moreover, the 0.5-7.0 keV isophotes coincide with the extended radio emission as imaged by the VLA Sky Survey at 3 GHz. The rest-frame 2–10 keV luminosity of the extended component is L _ 2-10keV =5.9 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , about 8 of the core: this makes it one of the most luminous jets resolved in X-rays so far.

Through spectral energy distribution modelling we find that this emission can be explained by the inverse Compton interaction with the photons of the cosmic microwave background, assuming that the jet's physical parameters are similar to those in the local Universe. At the same time, we find that the radiation produced by a putative population of high-energy electrons through the synchrotron process observed at low redshift is quenched at high redshift, hence becoming negligible.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLA/VLBA observations resolving PSO J030947.49+271757.31, a z=6.10 blazar and radio-loud AGN, into a compact core and one-sided 500 pc jet with multiple subcomponents.

quotes.quote: We present Director's Discretionary Time multi-frequency observations obtained with the Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) of the blazar PSO J030947.49+271757.31 (hereafter PSO J0309+27) at z = 6.10 0.03 . The milliarcsecond angular resolution of our VLBA observations at 1.5, 5 and 8.4 GHz unveils a bright one-sided jet extended for 500 parsecs in projection. This high-z radio-loud AGN is resolved into multiple compact sub-components, embedded in a more diffuse and faint radio emission, which enshrouds them in a continuous jet structure.

On VLBI scales PSO J0309+27 is a core plus one-sided jet from 1.5 to 8.4 GHz (see Fig. fig:VLBA_images ). The jet extends at 1.5 GHz for about 80 mas at a position angle 30 degrees (east of north), which corresponds to 500 pc at the redshift of the source. At this frequency it is resolved into four steep-spectrum sub-components, which are connected by a continuous emission (Fig. fig:VLBI_spectrum , but see also Sec. sec:radio_spectrum ).

sources.summary: The paper cites PSO J0309+27 as the most distant known blazar, with a massive black hole and luminosity implying rapid early SMBH growth.

quotes.quote: Recent radio observations have reported the discovery of the most distant blazar PSO J0309+27 located at a redshift of z 6.1 when the age of the Universe was less than 1 billion years . Its central engine is found to be a black hole of mass 1.45 _ 0.89 ^ +1.89 10^ 9 M _ with a bolometric luminosity of 8 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 . This corresponds to a seed black hole mass of 10 ^6 M_ at z = 30 (when the first stars and galaxies are thought to have formed 15 million years after the Big Bang) . Such a rapid growth of SMBHs from the early stars has serious implications on various evolution models for the SMBHs in the jetted AGNs across cosmic time .

sources.summary: The paper describes PSO J0309+27 as the most distant known blazar, with Chandra revealing a 20 kpc jet whose X-rays are consistent with IC/CMB emission.

quotes.quote: To this end we performed a Chandra X-ray observation of the most distant blazar currently known, PSO J0309+27 at z=6.1 belladitta20 . This observation revealed the presence of a 20 kpc jet extending in the North-East direction (see Figure IghinaFig1 ), making it the highest-redshift large-scale jet currently known. From the detailed study of its multiwavelength emission (see ighina22 ), we found that its X-ray flux is fully consistent with the IC/CMB process with 2 , therefore without requiring the extreme bulk velocities velocities needed at low redshift.

gmw_00000868.name: NGC 2775
gmw_00000868.overview: NGC 2775 is a nearby spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0308489] classified as an SAab type [arXiv:1108.3662, arXiv:2403.04901] with a classical, inactive bulge [arXiv:1004.5393]. It features a striking flocculent disk [arXiv:2403.04901] where uniform star formation [arXiv:2403.12927] is thought to be seeded by gas accreted from its companion, NGC 2777 [arXiv:2403.04901].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2775's outer profile as III-d(?) and describes dust features, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2775 (III-d(?); SA()ab) : In this galaxy there is a bright nucleus in an elliptical genuine bulge. The disk extends in a multi-armed structure out to 85 (7.4 kpc) from the center, where there is a band of dust absorption. From this point outwards the galaxy continues without fine structure forming what may be a stellar halo. This idea is backed up by the isophotes, whose ellipticity declines from 85 (11.2 kpc) reaching a minimum at 213 (18.5 kpc). Nevertheless the ellipticity increases again and the surface brightness profile is an almost perfect exponential between 138 (12 kpc) and 345 (30 kpc), suggesting that it has disk structure.

sources.summary: NGC 2775 is a flocculent SAab galaxy with a bulge, bimodal cluster colors, low main-sequence residual, bulge/disc cluster segregation, and rejuvenation linked to accreted gas.

quotes.quote: In particular, NGC 2775 is of Type a SA()ab with an intermediate sized bulge, a flocculent disc, and a color-color distribution that appears strongly bimodal. It has the second lowest MS in the sample and flocculent structure so striking that its HST imaging has captured broad attention https://esahubble.org/images/potw2026a/ . Almost all class 1 clusters are situated in the bulge and class 2 clusters in the disc (Figure fig:spatial_dist_9 ). The bimodal distribution originates from the combination of a relatively dust free old central region with no recent star formation hogg_hot_2001 , and flocculent star formation thought to be seeded by accreted gas i.e., from the nearby companion NGC 2777, arp_properties_1991 which led to a disk rejuvenation event.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2775 among four nearby intermediate-inclination spiral galaxies observed with major- and minor-axis long-slit spectroscopy for stellar disk kinematic modeling.

quotes.quote: We observed four galaxies: NGC 1068, NGC 2460, NGC 2775, and NGC 4030. All four are nearby, spiral galaxies of intermediate inclination ranging in Hubble type from Sa to Sbc. The main characteristics of each galaxy are listed in Table t:sample . Long-slit spectra were obtained along the photometric major and minor axes of each galaxy, in multiple exposures of 30 minutes each.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2775 as a classical-bulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2775 .SAR2../Sa() Classical Bulge. There is a clear break in the disk near 22 , inside which the bulge is smooth and E-type. The decomposition yields n_b=3.5 , and the bulges is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-0.4 mag.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2775 as having an unresolved but likely stellar compact nuclear star cluster, using its best-fit Sersic radius as an upper limit.

quotes.quote: On the other hand in NGC 2775 and NGC 1440 the nuclear light appears to be stellar; specifically, the nuclear sources in both have very similar colors to the surrounding galaxy light, and the nuclear spectra ho1995 shows very little emission.
Therefore it is likely these two galaxies host compact NSCs that we just cannot resolve with HST.
The best-fit S ersic r_e are used here as upper limits; for NGC 2775 and NGC 1440 these are 3.87 pc and 4.65 pc or 0 042 and 0 048.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts flocculent NGC 2775 with NGC 4321, showing smaller azimuthal SFR and molecular-density variations and more uniform star formation around the disk.

quotes.quote: This is in contrast to the case for a flocculent galaxy, NGC 2775, where the PHANGS values are shown in Figure n2775-deltas . Note that the scale for _ SFR is blown-up by a factor of 10 compared to Figure n4321-deltas , and the scale for _ mol is blown up by a factor of 2. This difference is sensible because flocculent galaxies have much more uniform SFRs around in azimuth than galaxies with strong spiral density waves like NGC 4321, where the star formation is concentrated in the arms.

gmw_00000869.name: Arp 143
gmw_00000869.overview: Arp 143 is a tight, interacting galaxy pair [arXiv:1407.7369]. The system features a well-defined emission ring, which is offset in both its center and position angle relative to the stellar disk [arXiv:0807.1477].

sources.summary: The paper finds Arp143 has a well-defined emission ring, an offset ring and nucleus relative to the disk, and tidal streams in the companion.

quotes.quote: Arp143: Ring is very well defined by the distribution of \ regions, with most of the \ emission originating from the northwestern arc. The \ emission is also detected from the nucleus. The stellar disk can be clearly traced outside the ring, and has similar ellipticity as the ring. However, the ring is offset both in center and P.A. with respect to the disk. The nucleus is not exactly located at the ring center and is pulled towards the companion.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 143 as a tight interacting galaxy pair with low-frequency radio observations, deriving radio-emission ages and magnetic-field properties for the pair.

quotes.quote: We present results of the recent low-frequency radio observations of a tight galaxy pair Arp 143 at 234 and 612 MHz. These data are analysed together with the archive data at 1490, 4860, 8440, and 14940 MHz. From the analysis of the radio emission we derive constraints on the age of the radio emitting structures as well as on the properties of their magnetic field.

In this paper we present results from our recent observing project at the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT), in which Arp 143 has been studied at 234 and 612 MHz. The new observations are analysed together with the previously unpublished, archive VLA data at 1490, 4860, 8440, and 14940 MHz to produce high resolution maps of the radio emission and to study the magnetic field of this galaxy pair.

gmw_00000870.name: NGC 2445
gmw_00000870.overview: NGC 2445 is a distorted collisional ring galaxy that is interacting with its companion, NGC 2444 [arXiv:1407.7369]. This interaction has created a 150 kpc neutral gas tail, while the galaxy's core is currently undergoing starburst activity [arXiv:1407.7369].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2445 as Arp 143's distorted collisional ring galaxy, with strong magnetic fields, steep-spectrum radio emission, star formation, and disruption from interaction with NGC 2444.

quotes.quote: We show that the collisional ring of NGC 2445 hosts strong magnetic fields (reaching 12 G in its northwestern part) manifesting as a steep--spectrum, nonthermal radiation at radio frequencies. The spectral age of this structure is higher than estimates derived for the star-forming regions from the H distribution, suggesting that the radio emission might have a different origin. The galactic core is of a very young spectral age, suggesting an ongoing starburst activity.

However, it differs significantly from the usual image of a ring system, as the ring structure in NGC 2445 is distorted, similar in appearance to a trapezoid with rounded vertices. The whole visible structure is dominated by local maxima of optical emission that are regions of intensive star formation burridge, tail, multiv . All of them are relatively young, and the central region is suspected to be undergoing starburst activity multiv . Moreover, the pair is known to be "traling smoke", as the H i morphology tail reveals a 150 kpc long tail of the neutral gas emission extending towards north. All this is suggestive for a collision of NGC 2445 with a compact galaxy -- possibly its companion, NGC 2444 multiv,beirao .

gmw_00000871.name: NGC 2444
gmw_00000871.overview: NGC 2444 is an elliptical companion galaxy that forms the galaxy pair Arp 143 [arXiv:1407.7369]. It is believed to have collided with its companion, disrupting the companion's spiral disk and causing morphological distortions [arXiv:1407.7369].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2444 as the elliptical/compact companion in Arp 143, likely involved in the collision that distorted NGC 2445 and produced intergalactic radio emission.

quotes.quote: Additionally we identify a possible ridge of emission between the ring galaxy and its elliptical companion NGC 2444.

Galaxy pair consisting of NGC 2444 and 2445 is one of the few systems of ring galaxies that have been included in the Arp's Catalogue, denoted Arp 143 arp . However, it differs significantly from the usual image of a ring system, as the ring structure in NGC 2445 is distorted, similar in appearance to a trapezoid with rounded vertices.

All this is suggestive for a collision of NGC 2445 with a compact galaxy -- possibly its companion, NGC 2444 multiv,beirao . This collision disrupted the spiral disk and resulted in morphological distortions as well as in intensification of the star formation in particular regions of the collisional ring.

gmw_00000872.name: COSMOS-11494
gmw_00000872.overview: COSMOS-11494 is a massive, compact, quiescent galaxy at high redshift [arXiv:1612.02001]. It has a very low star-formation rate and exhibits strong magnesium and calcium enhancement compared to similar-mass galaxies at lower redshifts [arXiv:1612.02001].

sources.summary: The paper studies COSMOS-11494 as a massive, compact, quiescent high-redshift galaxy with strong magnesium and calcium enhancement, implying rapid early star formation dominated by core-collapse supernova enrichment.

quotes.quote: We observed the galaxy COSMOS-11494 with the near-infrared multi-object spectrograph MOSFIRE on the Keck I Telescope . It was also observed by two other programmes , and so we incorporated these publicly available archival data. COSMOS-11494 was selected from the 3D-HST survey . With a stellar mass M given by log_ 10 M/M_ =11.5 0.1 , COSMOS-11494 is among the most massive galaxies at its redshift, and it has a very low star-formation rate of less than 0.6M_ / yr (see Methods). Similarly to the typical massive, quiescent galaxy at this redshift, it is smaller than its local counterparts of the same mass, with an effective radius of 2.1 kpc .

All galaxies are fitted with the alf code. Figure fig:abun illustrates that COSMOS-11494 is more Mg-enhanced than similar-mass galaxies at lower redshift, with [ Mg/Fe ] about 0.3 dex higher. [ Ca/Fe ] is also higher compared to the values for lower-redshift massive galaxies.

gmw_00000873.name: ESO156-G029
gmw_00000873.overview: ESO156-G029 is a spiral galaxy belonging to a group located at the virial radius of the cluster Abell 3193 [arXiv:1909.01509]. The galaxy exhibits disturbed gas kinematics and a highly asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution, likely caused by group pre-processing, a past minor gas-rich merger, and early ram-pressure effects [arXiv:1909.01509].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO156-G029 as an infalling spiral galaxy with disturbed gas kinematics, asymmetric H I, evidence for group pre-processing, a past minor gas-rich merger, and impending ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: We report on observations of ESO156-G029, member of a galaxy group which is positioned at the virial radius of cluster Abell 3193. ESO156-G029 is located 1.4 Mpc in projected distance from the brightest cluster galaxy NGC1500. We show that ESO156-G029 has disturbed gas kinematics and a highly asymmetric neutral hydrogen ( ) distribution, which are consequences of group pre-processing, and possibly of ram-pressure. Based on the current data we propose a scenario in which ESO156-G029 had a minor gas-rich merger in the past and now starts to experience ram-pressure. We infer that the galaxy will undergo rapid evolution once it gets closer to the cluster centre (less than 0.5 Mpc) where ram-pressure is strong enough to begin stripping the \ from the galaxy.

gmw_00000874.name: RGB J0710+591
gmw_00000874.overview: RGB J0710+591 is an elliptical blazar [arXiv:1906.04486] classified as an extreme high-synchrotron-peak BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1110.0040, arXiv:2203.00880]. Its radio structure features a core-dominated jet with limb brightening [arXiv:0711.1158, arXiv:1801.00817], while its active nucleus exhibits hard X-ray and very-high-energy gamma-ray spectra [arXiv:1110.0040, arXiv:1906.04486] alongside spectral variability [arXiv:2311.12695].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RGB J0710+591 as an unusually high-synchrotron-peak HBL, reports VERITAS and Fermi-LAT hard-spectrum detections, and says its multiwavelength SED is well described by SSC modeling.

quotes.quote: RGB J0710+591 is an HBL at z = 0.125 with its synchrotron peak at an unusually high-frequency ( 10^ 19.2 Hz). It was observed with VERITAS for 22 h of good-quality live time between December 2008 and March 2009 . VHE ray emission was discovered from this blazar (5.5 ) during this exposure. The observed VHE flux from VER J0710+591 shows no variability (within statistics) and is 3 Crab above 300 GeV. The measured VHE spectrum between 310 GeV and 4.6 TeV is among the hardest ( = 2.69 0.26 ) observed by VERITAS from a blazar.

Following the VERITAS detection, the Fermi-LAT collaboration performed a specific high-energy analysis of their data and found ray emission with a very hard spectrum ( _ LAT 1.5 ) between 100 MeV and 300 GeV. The contemporaneous MWL SED, including both Fermi-LAT and Swift UVOT/XRT data, is well-described by an SSC model. An additional EC component does not improve the fit.

sources.summary: The paper models RGB J0710+591 among six extreme BL Lacs, explaining its broadband SED with a two-zone lepto-hadronic scenario involving inner-blob photohadronic and outer-blob SSC/EC emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, using the parameters listed in Table tab_model_parameters we model successfully the broadband SED of six known extreme BL Lac 1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0347-121, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1218-304, 1ES 0414+009. The result of our model is shown in Figure ( fig_SED_result ). It is important to mention that in this work, we have not included the contribution observed in the optical band produced by the host galaxy Costamante2018 . The extreme BL Lac behavior presents challenges for a pure leptonic or hadronic model with one-zone emission. These objects demand atypical parameters for explaining the hard spectrum shape, especially in a VHE gamma-ray band.

In particular cases, we described the six best-known extreme BL Lacs using our proposed model, and we found a feasible explanation that relaxes the atypical values required by the one-zone models. We showed that the annihilation line radiation produced in a mildly relativistic outflow pair-plasma and the DT play essential roles in forming the hard spectrum observed in the VHE gamma-ray bands. In our model, this gamma-ray bump is produced mainly in two regions: i) the inner blob, which is responsible for the highest energy contribution above TeV-energies via photo-hadronic interactions with the annihilation line photons from the pair-plasma, and ii) the outer blob, where the very-high-energy gamma rays are created by EC with seed photons from the DT, and by the SSC mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper describes EXO 0706.1+5913 as a UHBL whose radio images show a cocoon, weak jet, EVN-detected core and jet components, and a flat core spectrum.

quotes.quote: The 1.4 GHz VLA image shows that the radio core is located on the north-west edge of a roughly spherical cocoon. It was suggested that this object could be a wide-angle or narrow-angle tailed object, viewed at a small angle. The 5 GHz VLBA image shows a weak jet to the south-west gir04a . We present our EVN and MERLIN image of this source in Fig. J0710com .

The core and three jet components are detected from EVN observation, and their parameters are listed in Table table_model for all the VLBI data. We found that the P.A. of jet components from all the VLBI data ranges from -128 ^ to -170 ^ , indicating that the projected jet opening angle is likely around 42 ^ . A flat spectra is found with =0.22 for the core, using the average flux at 4.9 and 8.6 GHz from multi-epoch VLBI data (see Table table_model ).

sources.summary: The paper treats RGB J0710+591 as a hard-TeV UHBL with known GeV variability, simultaneous Fermi/VHE data, and two viable hadronic solution families.

quotes.quote: Out of the 44 HBLs detected so far in the TeV range, five sources --- 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 0347-121, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 1218+304 --- are characterized by particularly hard spectra with the high-energy peak above 1 TeV, which is consistent with their very hard and weak signal in the Fermi LAT band. Another common characteristic is the lack of observable variability on short time-scales of the very-high-energy flux with the exception of 1ES 1218+304, which showed VHE flaring activity on the scale of days, 1218_Veritas . Although the BL Lac object H 1426+428 belongs to this sub-class as well, since it was detected at VHE only during flaring activity without extensive multi-wavelength coverage 1426_hegra, Arache, 1426_whipple2, 1426_whipple, Leonardo09, wystan we decided to exclude it from our sample. The modelling of these ultra-high-frequency peaked BL Lacs UHBLs, also known as extreme BL Lacs, EHBLs, see e.g. for a more recent review Costamante01, Gunes13 with one-zone SSC scenarios, which usually yield good results for HBLs, is proving difficult in most cases, as can be seen from Table tab:uhbl , where some recent attempts at SSC modeling of the sources are summarized. A good representation of the complete SEDs requires either extremely high values of the minimum Lorentz factor of the electron distribution (1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0347-121), or an elevated Doppler factor (1ES 1218+304), or both (1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591).

For the five sources, we find two distinct sets of solutions, corresponding to the proton-synchrotron scenario and a lepto-hadronic scenario, dominated by emission from secondary particles from p- interactions. In the latter case, the SSC contribution is at a level comparable to the emission from hadronic processes, and justifies the '' lepto-hadronic '' classification.
In the top panel of Figure fig2 we show the two families of solutions for the case of RGB J0710+591, which is the only source for which there is a \ spectrum simultaneous with VHE observations.

sources.summary: The paper treats RGB J0710+591 as an extreme TeV HBL with hard VHE emission, no reported gamma-ray variability, and a viable synchrotron pair echo fit.

quotes.quote: Namely we study the HBLs 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1218+304 and RGB J0710+591 by way of demonstrating an alternative interpretation for the origin of the emission of this extreme HBL subpopulation. The HBL RGB J0710+591 was discovered in VHE by Acciari10 . It has an observed index _ VHE 2.7 , making it one of the hardest VHE detected sources to date. There is no reported variability in the gamma-ray emission of this source.

Its gamma-ray spectrum has been used to derive constraints on the IGMF strength assuming a leptonic origin Taylor11,Huan11,Arlen12 , whereas more recently a UHECR-induced inverse Compton cascade origin of the emission has been discussed Tavecchio14 . For RGB J0710+591 the synchrotron pair echo model is consistent with the data across the GeV and TeV bands and L_ cr,iso =10^ 47 erg   s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats RGB J0710+591 as an extreme hard-TeV BL Lac whose UV excess, like 1ES 0229+200, supports separate emission components in the SED.

quotes.quote: Some objects do appear extreme in both synchrotron and Compton emissions, such as 1ES 0229+200 ignacio,kaufmann , 1ES 0347-121 hess0347 and RGB J0710 + 591 veritas0710 . However, there are cases of objects which are extreme only in Compton but not in synchrotron, such as 1ES 1101-232 gerd1101 , 1ES 1218 + 304 veritas1218 and 1ES 0414 + 009 hess0414 . Others are extreme in synchrotron but not in Compton, such as 1ES 1426+428 (which is characterized by a steep VHE spectrum after correction for absorption with recent EBL calculations, see e.g. dominguez13 ).

However, this is not the case in two objects, namely 1ES 0229+200 and RGB J0710+591. Their soft X-ray spectrum is harder than the UV-to-X-ray slope by about 0.2 , well above the statistical uncertainty. The UV data therefore remain significantly above the power-law extrapolation of the soft X-ray spectrum towards lower energies.

This scenario seems indeed corroborated by our data in the two objects with an ``UV excess'' (1ES 0229+200 and RGB J0710+591). In these objects the UV flux remains above the extrapolation of the soft X-ray power-law spectrum to lower energies, indicating that they might belong to a different emission component.

sources.summary: The appendix describes RGB J0710+591 as an elliptical blazar with a nuclear point source, steady VERITAS VHE emission, hard Fermi LAT and X-ray spectra, and host-galaxy optical contribution.

quotes.quote: The blazar RGB J0710+591 was discovered in 1984 as fully resolved elliptical galaxy with a nuclear point source at redshift z = 0.125 . The first VHE emission from this source was detected by VERITAS during 2008--2009 observations with no evidence of variability in the -ray light curve above 300 GeV . The observed VHE -ray spectrum was described by a power law with a photon spectral index of 2.69 0.26 in the energy range 310 GeV to 4.6 TeV. The HE -ray spectrum in MeV-GeV band was also fitted by a power law with a spectral index of 1.46 0.17 using Fermi LAT observations. The X-ray emission from the source was observed to be consistent with the hard photon spectrum having a power law index of 1.86 0.01 indicating a synchrotron peak energy above 10 keV.
The host galaxy of RGB J0710+591 was observed to make a significant contribution to the optical emission of the source.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0706+592 as a possible small-angle tailed object whose VLBI images are core-dominated, with weak extensions and no detected polarization.

quotes.quote: 0706+592 The 1.4 GHz VLA image of Giroletti04a shows the radio core to be located on the northwest edge of a roughly spherical cocoon. They suggest that this object could be a wide-angle or narrow-angle tailed object, viewed at a small angle. Their 5 GHz VLBA image shows a weak jet to the southwest. Our two VLBI images (Fig.  fig:0706 ) are dominated by a compact core, with weak extentions to the west (in 1993) and southwest (in 1995); given the image of Giroletti04a , the former is probably an artefact. We did not detect polarization at either of our epochs.

sources.summary: The paper models gamma-ray cascades from the blazar RGB J0710+591 and uses simultaneous HE and VHE spectra to constrain the extragalactic magnetic field.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we describe the properties of the model and demonstrate its utility by exploring the gamma-ray emission from the blazar RGB J0710+591. In particular, we predict, under various scenarios, the VHE and high-energy (HE, 100 MeV E 300 GeV) fluxes detectable with the VERITAS and Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) observatories. We then develop a systematic framework for comparing the predictions to published results, obtaining constraints on the EGMF strength.

We demonstrate the cascade model by investigating RGB J0710+591, a high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae (HBL) object located at a redshift of z=0.125 , for which simultaneous HE and VHE data are available with no variability observed VERITAS_RGBJ0710 .

The application of the model to RGB J0710+591 data yields lower limits at 95 confidence that agree with the results of for the same source, further indicating that the physical picture of the cascade presented by the model is sufficiently complete to produce accurate results within the limits discussed in Sec. sec:model .

sources.summary: The paper models RGB J0710+591 as an extreme-TeV blazar, finding good generic SED solutions and satisfactory recollimation-shock representations comparable to scenario I.

quotes.quote: Equally good solutions can be found for RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0347-121, and 1ES 1101-232 (cf. Fig. fig:sed0710 to fig:sed1101 ) if, in addition to adjusting to values between 30 and 50 , one also allows for an adjustment of the size of the emission region R , typically by a factor of 1.5 . In the case of 1ES 1218+304, the Fermi LAT spectrum is flatter than for the other sources, requiring a decrease in _ e,min by an order of magnitude, in addition to adjustments of and R (cf. Fig. fig:sed1218 ). Allowing for these adjustments, satisfactory solutions can be found for all five SEDs.

As can be seen from Figures fig:sed1218_II , fig:sed0710_II and fig:sed0347_II , satisfactory representations of the SEDs can be found within this shock scenario for 1ES 1218+304, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 0347-121. While for the first two, the representation of the SED is of comparable quality to the one in scenario I, for 1ES 0347-121 the Fermi LAT flux is over-predicted for the lowest flux point, but given the uncertainties in the Fermi points, this solution can still be regarded as acceptable.

sources.summary: The paper identifies RGB J0710+591 as an extreme TeV blazar with hard spectra and reports spectral softening, a major spectral transition, and established variability in both spectral components.

quotes.quote: We present a spectral study of four eHBLs, namely 1ES 0120+340, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1741+196, and one HBL 1ES 2322-409 using new X-ray data from AstroSat, together with quasi-simultaneous Fermi-LAT and other archival multi-frequency data. Using the broad-band data we confirm the extreme synchrotron nature of these eHBLs. Three of the eHBLs are non-variable, as is typically associated with this class of sources. On the contrary, RGB J0710+591 shows spectral softening in both X-ray and GeV bands indicating a significant change in the synchrotron cut-off.

This paper reports on recent observations carried out using \ and \ of five sources: 1ES 0120+340 (redshift z=0.272 ), RGB J0710+591 ( z=0.125 ), 1ES 1101-232 ( z=0.186 ), 1ES 1741+196 ( z=0.084 ), and 1ES 2322-409 ( z=0.1736 ), each displaying a unique range of spectral characteristics. Among these, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 1101-232 are well-known for being extreme TeV sources with hard intrinsic TeV spectra.

For example, RGB J0710+591 shows a significant spectral transition with a strongly increasing spectral curvature ( increased by a factor 1.8 ) and a marginal change in its spectral index and flux over a period of one year cf., Costamante+2018, Goswami+2020 .

sources.summary: The paper uses simultaneous GeV-TeV observations of RGB J0710+591 to constrain EGMF, finding GeV flux below zero-field cascade expectations and inferring magnetic-field bounds from suppression mechanisms.

quotes.quote: Constraints on EGMF could be derived from the simultaneous GeV-TeV data on the blazars RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0229+200, and 1ES 1218+304. The measured source flux level in the GeV band is lower than the flux of the expected cascade component calculated under the assumption of zero EGMF. Assuming that the reason for the suppression of the cascade component is the extended nature of the cascade emission, we find that B 10^ 15 G (assuming EGMF correlation length of 1 Mpc) is consistent with the data. Alternatively, the assumption that the suppression of the cascade emission is caused by the time delay of the cascade photons the data are consistent with B 10^ 17 G for the same correlation length.

sources.summary: The paper treats RGB J0710+591 as an HBL TeV source with VERITAS confirmation, power-law VHE spectrum, contemporaneous Fermi/LAT/Swift/MDM data, and no SED variability timescale.

quotes.quote: RGB J0710+591. A well known HBL object, it was not detected by EGRET. It was observed at the VHE waveband by VERITAS between December 2008 and March 2009, and was confirmed to be a TeV source (Ong et al. 2009, Acciari et al. 2010b). The observed spectrum from 0.31 to 4.6 TeV can be fit by a power law with a photon spectral index 2.69 , and the integral flux above 300 GeV is 3.9 0.8 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , corresponding to 3 of Crab flux. The VERITAS observation was complemented by contemporaneous observations from Fermi /LAT, Swift and Michigan-Dartmouth-MIT observatory (Acciari et al. 2010b).

sources.summary: The paper discusses RGB J0710+591 as one of three TeV blazars previously used for IGMF lower limits, but its revised modeling finds less than 90% incompatibility with zero IGMF.

quotes.quote: We reveiw recent work in the literature which utilizes this idea to derive constraints on the IGMF for three TeV-detected blazars-1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1218+304, and RGB J0710+591, and we also investigate four other hard-spectrum TeV blazars in the same framework.

The most comprehensive work yet on constraining the IGMF was done by (hereafter referred to as TVN11), in which three sources were found (RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1218+304, and 1ES 0229+200) whose combined quasi-simultaneous IACT and Fermi-LAT dFED are not compatible with the B_ IGMF = 0 hypothesis at greater than 95 confidence level. It was suggested that B_ IGMF 10^ 17 G is required to explain observations.

The first two sources, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 1218+304 (Fig best_fit_model a and b) were found to be incompatible with the B_ IGMF = 0 hypothesis at < 90 , which does not corroborate the conclusion of TVN11 that the B_ IGMF = 0 hypothesis is rejected for these sources .

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0706+592 shows limb brightening in one image and has a component seen across five epochs, though its relatively uncertain proper motion receives a Poor quality code.

quotes.quote: We note here that we continue to observe the presence of limb brightening in those two sources in later epochs, and we also observe limb brightening in some of the new images of other sources; for example 0645+153 at 2014 August 21 and 0706+592 at 2014 March 27 (see Figure 1). 0706+592 Component 1 is observed at five epochs, with a proper motion of 0.709 0.189 mas yr ^ 1 . It is seen as a distinct feature in tapered images of this source, as is shown in the three-epoch image comparison in Figure 8. However, the relatively large uncertainty in the proper motion gives it a `Poor' quality code.

sources.summary: The paper models RGB J0710+591 as an extreme TeV blazar; its gamma-ray spectrum is compatible with zero intergalactic magnetic field under the tested cascade models.

quotes.quote: The data sets of the first three sources (RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1218+304, and 1ES 0229+200) are identical to those used in  , except that an additional data set for 1ES 1218+304 obtained by the VERITAS Collaboration just before the launch of the Fermi satellite (on August 4, 2008 or MJD 54682) is considered in this study. As an illustration of a typical result of the data and model comparison, Figure  rgbj0710_B0_fit a shows the ^2 confidence level of the dFED, assuming four fixed and three free parameters for each model tested within the given range of and _c parameters. The most favored model with value of = 1.8 and _c = 3.16 TeV is incompatible with the data at the level of about 75 .

gmw_00000875.name: 1ES 0347-121
gmw_00000875.overview: 1ES 0347-121 is an extreme BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2203.00880, arXiv:1411.5968] hosted by an elliptical galaxy that contains a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1906.04486]. The blazar features a compact radio structure with a faint jet [arXiv:2310.10206] and exhibits a hard TeV gamma-ray spectrum with no observed short-term variability [arXiv:1411.5968, arXiv:1906.04486].

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0347-121 among six extreme BL Lacs, explaining its broadband SED with a two-zone lepto-hadronic scenario involving inner-blob photohadronic and outer-blob SSC/EC emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, using the parameters listed in Table tab_model_parameters we model successfully the broadband SED of six known extreme BL Lac 1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0347-121, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1218-304, 1ES 0414+009. The result of our model is shown in Figure ( fig_SED_result ). It is important to mention that in this work, we have not included the contribution observed in the optical band produced by the host galaxy Costamante2018 . The extreme BL Lac behavior presents challenges for a pure leptonic or hadronic model with one-zone emission. These objects demand atypical parameters for explaining the hard spectrum shape, especially in a VHE gamma-ray band.

In particular cases, we described the six best-known extreme BL Lacs using our proposed model, and we found a feasible explanation that relaxes the atypical values required by the one-zone models. We showed that the annihilation line radiation produced in a mildly relativistic outflow pair-plasma and the DT play essential roles in forming the hard spectrum observed in the VHE gamma-ray bands. In our model, this gamma-ray bump is produced mainly in two regions: i) the inner blob, which is responsible for the highest energy contribution above TeV-energies via photo-hadronic interactions with the annihilation line photons from the pair-plasma, and ii) the outer blob, where the very-high-energy gamma rays are created by EC with seed photons from the DT, and by the SSC mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0347-121 as a hard-TeV UHBL whose SSC fits require extreme electron energies and whose Fermi analysis agrees with recent published results.

quotes.quote: Out of the 44 HBLs detected so far in the TeV range, five sources --- 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 0347-121, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 1218+304 --- are characterized by particularly hard spectra with the high-energy peak above 1 TeV, which is consistent with their very hard and weak signal in the Fermi LAT band. Another common characteristic is the lack of observable variability on short time-scales of the very-high-energy flux with the exception of 1ES 1218+304, which showed VHE flaring activity on the scale of days, 1218_Veritas . Although the BL Lac object H 1426+428 belongs to this sub-class as well, since it was detected at VHE only during flaring activity without extensive multi-wavelength coverage 1426_hegra, Arache, 1426_whipple2, 1426_whipple, Leonardo09, wystan we decided to exclude it from our sample. The modelling of these ultra-high-frequency peaked BL Lacs UHBLs, also known as extreme BL Lacs, EHBLs, see e.g. for a more recent review Costamante01, Gunes13 with one-zone SSC scenarios, which usually yield good results for HBLs, is proving difficult in most cases, as can be seen from Table tab:uhbl , where some recent attempts at SSC modeling of the sources are summarized. A good representation of the complete SEDs requires either extremely high values of the minimum Lorentz factor of the electron distribution (1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0347-121), or an elevated Doppler factor (1ES 1218+304), or both (1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591).

For the five sources, we find two distinct sets of solutions, corresponding to the proton-synchrotron scenario and a lepto-hadronic scenario, dominated by emission from secondary particles from p- interactions. In the latter case, the SSC contribution is at a level comparable to the emission from hadronic processes, and justifies the '' lepto-hadronic '' classification.
For 1ES 0347-121 our result agrees with the \ analysis recently presented by Tanaka14 .

sources.summary: 1ES 0347-121 is analyzed as an EHBL with hard TeV emission and weak variability whose spectrum can be reproduced by a UHECR-induced cascade model.

quotes.quote: Very-high-energy (VHE; 100 GeV) ray observations have resulted in the detections of several extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac objects (EHBLs), most notably 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1101-232, and 1ES 0347-121, which show very hard VHE spectra ( 3; dN / dE E^ ) extending to tens of TeV e.g., Aharonian2007AA470p475,Aharonian2007AA473L25,Aharonian2007AA475L9 . Moreover, these sources show weak variability, unlike most blazars. VHE rays can be attenuated by pair-creation interactions with EBL photons while propagating through intergalactic space Nikishov1962JETP14p393,Gould1966PRL16p252,Stecker1992ApJ390L49 . A possible scenario to overcome the severe absorption that would soften the TeV spectra of these sources is the UHECR-induced cascade model e.g., Essey2010APh33p81,Essey2011ApJ731p51,Murase2012ApJ749p63,Razzaque2012ApJ745p196 .

We have generally derived the constraints on the CR luminosity of a powerful source from the observed isotropy at 10^ 19 eV. In this section, we specifically focus on three known EHBLs as powerful UHECR emitters, that is, 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian2007AA475L9 , 1ES 0347-121 Aharonian2007AA473L25 , and 1ES 1101-232 Aharonian2007AA470p475 , all of which have been detected by the High Energy Spectroscopic System (H.E.S.S.). The spectra of these objects in the VHE range can be well reproduced by the UHECR-induced cascade model e.g., Essey2011ApJ731p51,Murase2012ApJ749p63 . The isotropic-equivalent CR luminosities to reproduce the observed flux are shown in Table tab:ehbls with their redshift and positions.
These results are quantitatively the same also for 1ES 0347-121, shown in Figure fig:1es0347 .

sources.summary: The paper finds 1ES0347-121 among seven blazars contributing to the excess; excluding it raises the overall significance because the fit extrapolates from two low-optical-depth points.

quotes.quote: The seven sources with measurements extending to >2 contribute about equally to the overall excess. Removing each source spectrum and re-calculating the significance given in the following list provides an estimate on the relevance of each source: Mkn 421 ( S( Mkn 421 )=3.8 ), Mkn 501 ( S( Mkn 501 )=3.7 ), H 1426+428 ( S( H 1426+428 )=3.5 ), 1ES1101-232 ( S( 1ES1101-232 )=2.8 ), 1ES0347-121 ( S( 1ES0347-121 )=4.6 ), 1ES0414+009 ( S( 1ES0414+009 )=3.6 ), 3C279 ( S( 3C279 )=3.8 ). The result demonstrates that the different objects (6 high frequency peaked Blazars and 1 flat spectrum radio quasar) at widely different redshifts ( z=0.031 , 0.034 , 0.129 , 0.186 , 0.188 , 0.287 , and 0.536 ) contribute about equally to the overall significance. A systematic effect based upon the type of source or its distance is not evident.

Note, the significance increases after excluding the spectrum of 1ES0347-121 from the overall test. This is a consequence of the extra-polation of the fit to the two points at small optical depth (see Fig. sf05 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 1ES 0347-121 as a hard-spectrum TeV blazar and finds its simulated zero-IGMF fits affected by weak Fermi bins and VHE spectral structure.

quotes.quote: The VHE data set of 1ES 0347-121 consists of a set of observations over the period of August - December 2006, during which time 25.4 hours exposure was acculumlated. A time-averaged dFED for 7 bins over the energy range from 250 GeV - 3.67 TeV was derived ( ). The flux found in the first and fourth (last) bins is weak (TS < 9) for all spectral indices tested, allowing only an upper limit to be established.

Figure  1es0347_B0 a shows the confidence level of simulated models obtained for H0 . The best fit models in the _C plane are found at _C values near 1 TeV, and the dFED for one of these models is illustrated in Figure  1es0347_B0 b. The relatively high confidence level of the 1ES 0347-121 simulated models is partially due to the poor fit of the highest energy bins of the VHE regime where the reported dFED tentatively exhibits a feature of increasing energy density.

sources.summary: The appendix describes 1ES 0347-121 as a BL Lac object in an elliptical host with a supermassive black hole, H.E.S.S. VHE detection, no monthly variability, and hard Fermi LAT and X-ray spectra.

quotes.quote: The blazar 1ES 0347-121 was classified as BL Lac object in 1993 after its discovery in 1992 by the Einstein Slew Survey . It was observed to harbor a supermassive black hole in the elliptical host galaxy at redshift z = 0.188 . The H.E.S.S. telescopes first discovered a VHE -ray emission from this source in 2006 above an energy threshold of 250 GeV . The observed differential photon spectrum was described by a power law with a spectral index of 3.10 0.23 and no evidence of short term variability on monthly timescales was observed. The Fermi LAT observations indicated high energy -ray emission in the energy range 1-300 GeV to be compatible with the power law photon index 1.65 0.17.
The X-ray emission in the energy range 0.3-8 keV was described by a power law model with a spectral index of 1.99 0.06.

sources.summary: The paper uses blazar 1ES 0347-121 as a case study for testing Fermi LAT and CTA sensitivity to extragalactic magnetic-field parameters with cascade emission models.

quotes.quote: We have performed a comparative study of sensitivity of ray instruments such as Fermi LAT and CTA to the EGMF parameters (B, ) using blazar 1ES 0347-121 (redshift z = 0.188) as an example. The primary spectrum was assumed to have a log-parabolic shape with an exponential cutoff. As a case study, we set the ``Monte Carlo true'' EGMF parameters as B = 10^ 15 G, = 1 Mpc.

For every configuration of the EGMF parameters we estimated the best-fit values of the parameters of the primary spectrum using the 1ES 0347-121 observations presented in . Finally, using the profile-likelihood method we computed statistical significance for every EGMF configuration.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0347-121 as an extreme TeV blazar whose observed SED is reasonably fit by cascade models, but angular-distribution observations disfavor the model.

quotes.quote: Indeed, some blazars such as 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian2007a,Aliu2014 , 1ES 1101-232 Aharonian2006,Aharonian2007b and 1ES 0347-121 Aharonian2007c may be classified as both ETBs (see Dzhatdoev2017 , Fig. 12, 14, 17) and EHBLs. Compared to well-known nearby blazars such as Mkn 501 and Mkn 421, ETBs, as a rule, reveal weak and slow variability in the high energy (HE, E > 100 MeV) and the very high energy (VHE, E > 100 GeV) spectral bands.

In Fig. Fig08 we show an SED of the extreme TeV blazar 1ES 0347-121 ( z_ s = 0.188 ) that was also measured by the H.E.S.S. Collaboration Aharonian2007c , and present the same two model curves as in Fig. Fig07 . In particular, the Fermi-LAT Collaboration have recently performed a search for spatial extension in some high-latitude ( |b| > 5^ ) sources, including the blazars 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0347-121 Biteau2018 . The H.E.S.S. Collaboration have presented the same conclusion in Aharonian2007c for 1ES 0347-121 ; the observable angular distribution for 1ES 1101-232 also does not show any obvious hints at an additional extension Aharonian2007b .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0347-121 as an extreme-TeV blazar, finding acceptable generic and recollimation-shock SED representations despite over-predicted low Fermi-LAT flux.

quotes.quote: Equally good solutions can be found for RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0347-121, and 1ES 1101-232 (cf. Fig. fig:sed0710 to fig:sed1101 ) if, in addition to adjusting to values between 30 and 50 , one also allows for an adjustment of the size of the emission region R , typically by a factor of 1.5 . In the case of 1ES 1218+304, the Fermi LAT spectrum is flatter than for the other sources, requiring a decrease in _ e,min by an order of magnitude, in addition to adjustments of and R (cf. Fig. fig:sed1218 ). Allowing for these adjustments, satisfactory solutions can be found for all five SEDs.

As can be seen from Figures fig:sed1218_II , fig:sed0710_II and fig:sed0347_II , satisfactory representations of the SEDs can be found within this shock scenario for 1ES 1218+304, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 0347-121. While for the first two, the representation of the SED is of comparable quality to the one in scenario I, for 1ES 0347-121 the Fermi LAT flux is over-predicted for the lowest flux point, but given the uncertainties in the Fermi points, this solution can still be regarded as acceptable.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0347-121 as a BL Lac detected by HESS with a Crab-level VHE flux, no significant variability, and simultaneous HESS/Swift/ATOM SED data.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0347-121. A BL Lac object was detected by HESS between August and December 2006 with an integral flux corresponding to 2 of Crab flux (Aharonian et al. 2007b). The photon spectrum from 250 GeV to 3 TeV can be described by a power law with a photon index 3.1 . During the VHE observation, no significant variability was detected, so we take t=12 h. The broadband SED was compiled with the data from the simultaneous observations of HESS, Swift and ATOM (Aharonian et al. 2007b).

sources.summary: The paper includes 1ES 0347-121 among hard-TeV BL Lacs, describing it as extreme in synchrotron and Compton emission and noting that its UV and X-ray spectra can fit one synchrotron component.

quotes.quote: Some objects do appear extreme in both synchrotron and Compton emissions, such as 1ES 0229+200 ignacio,kaufmann , 1ES 0347-121 hess0347 and RGB J0710 + 591 veritas0710 . However, there are cases of objects which are extreme only in Compton but not in synchrotron, such as 1ES 1101-232 gerd1101 , 1ES 1218 + 304 veritas1218 and 1ES 0414 + 009 hess0414 . Others are extreme in synchrotron but not in Compton, such as 1ES 1426+428 (which is characterized by a steep VHE spectrum after correction for absorption with recent EBL calculations, see e.g. dominguez13 ).

For two other objects, 1ES 0347-121 and 1ES 1101-232, this scenario is not strictly necessary: the UV and X-ray spectra can be fitted with a single synchrotron component (though not the WISE data in 1ES 1101-232). However, the resulting gamma-ray emission tends to slightly underestimate the frequency of the Compton peak with respect to the indication of the VHE spectra (i.e. below 1 TeV instead of above it, though the statistical uncertainty is large). The Compton peak can be better reproduced allowing again for a harder synchrotron spectrum below the X-ray band (compare for example curves a and b in Fig seds ).

sources.summary: The paper groups 1ES 0347-121 with hard-TeV EHBLs, noting its hard spectrum, use in extragalactic magnetic-field tests, and rising TeV-regime flux without a clear peak before 10 TeV.

quotes.quote: Such hard spectra similar to 1ES 0229+200 one have been reported on few other sources, like for example 1ES 0347-121 0347discovery , RGB J0710+591 0710discovery , and 1ES 1101-232 1101discovery . Such objects have been named ``hard-TeV blazars'' by Nustar_EHBLs due to the high energy peak position located above about 10 TeV.
This approach has been tested on 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 0347-121 by EGMF .

Remembering that all sources in our sample are compatible within the errors with the definition of EHBL (meaning the synchrotron peak position above 10^ 17 Hz), we see here that some of them show their second IC peak in the 0.1-1.0 TeV band and with decreasing flux above 1 TeV (e.g. Mrk 501 and Mrk 421), but some others (e.g. the already known hard-TeV EHBLs 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 0347-121) show an increasing flux up to the TeV regime, and without clear sign of peak before 10 TeV.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0347 121 as compact with a faint jet, measured opening angle and apparent speed, H.E.S.S. VHE detection, and a one-zone SED description.

quotes.quote: 0347 121: The source shows a compact structure with a faint jet at 8.4 GHz piner14 , similar to what we see in the S band. The opening angle is measured to be 21.6 , and apparent speeds of 1.7 1.2 are detected by piner18 . VHE detection by H.E.S.S. reveals an integral flux of 0.02 Crab Units (CU) above 250 GeV and a power--law spectrum with =3.10 0.33 between 250 GeV and 3 TeV 2007A&A...473L..25A . The SED can be reasonably described by a one-zone model 2007A&A...473L..25A .

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0347-121 as one of four TeV blazars with adequate data, deriving lower UHECR power limits and small Auger South detection prospects.

quotes.quote: While for a 2.2 injection spectrum, the lower limits on the powers emitted in UHECRs by 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0347-121 are lower than their respective synchrotron luminosities ( 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ); in the case of 1ES 1426+428 it exceeds the corresponding synchrotron luminosity by up to an order of magnitude. Currently there are four such TeV blazars with adequate data, namely 1ES 1426+428 hess1426 , 1ES 0229+200 hess0229,veritas0229 , 1ES 1101-232 hess1101 and 1ES 0347-121 hess0347 . The detection prospect for UHECRs with = 2.2 from the other 3 blazars within the field-of-view of the Auger South is much smaller, N_ evt = 0.04 , 0.03 and 0.02 respectively for 1ES 0229+200 ( = +20.27^ ), 1ES 1101-232 ( = -23.50^ ) and 1ES 0347-121 ( = -11.98^ ); for L_p = 10^ 45 erg/s and E = 9,000 km ^2 yr sr.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0347-121 as a representative TeV extreme BL Lac whose fluxes and SED similarity guide the candidate-selection criterion.

quotes.quote: The SED of two representative EHBL detected in the TeV band, 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 0347-121, are reported in Fig. known Note that due to a bug in the calculation of the effective area, the X-ray spectrum reported in tavecchio09 was too high.
The criterion is rather model independent, as it arises by similarity with the archetypal EHBL 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 0347-121 mentioned above.
For comparison, the magenta points report the fluxes of 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 0347-121 (although not belonging to our selected sources).

sources.summary: For 1ES 0347-121, the paper models its spectrum with an electromagnetic cascade, finding K_B>1 at 1-10 TeV and possible observable intensity above absorption-only expectations.

quotes.quote: For the case of blazar 1ES 0347-121 and the electromagnetic cascade model we performed the calculation of modification factor K_ B with respect to the absorption-only model. We found that K_ B >1 in the energy region of 1-10 TeV . Therefore, the version of the electromagnetic cascade model that was considered in this work for this source predicts that it is possible that the observable intensity is higher than in the absorption-only model for the same level of EBL without any new physics.

gmw_00000876.name: 1ES 0414+009
gmw_00000876.overview: The blazar 1ES 0414+009 is a high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1911.06011, arXiv:1201.4711] hosted by a luminous elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1911.06011, arXiv:0711.1158]. It features a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1911.06011] and a radio jet with a wide opening angle [arXiv:1205.4939, arXiv:0711.1158].

sources.summary: 1ES 0414+009 is presented as a high-redshift HBL with an elliptical-galaxy optical component, massive central SMBH, VHE observations, and improved highest-energy fits from UHECR interactions.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0414+009 : The optical spectrum of this HBL at redshift z=0.287 is described by the sum of the emission due to a standard elliptical galaxy and a relatively flat power law Halpern_91 . It was first detected by HEAO 1 satellite Gursky_78 in the energy range 0.2 keV 10 MeV. It is one of the furthest VHE blazar with very hard TeV ray spectrum and well-determined redshift. The supermassive black hole (SMBH) at the center has a mass 2 10^ 9 M_ Falomo_03 .

The HE (100 MeV 100 GeV) data is obtained from Fermi-LAT and the source is listed in the Fermi 4FGL catalog. The VHE ( E>100 GeV) ray data is taken from observations by HESS Abramowski_12 and VERITAS Aliu_12 collaborations. Despite the attenuation due to EBL background, a good fit to the observed SED is achieved using a pure-leptonic model alone. However, the fit to data points at the highest energy improves on adding the photon spectrum from UHECR interactions.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0414+009 among six extreme BL Lacs, explaining its broadband SED with a two-zone lepto-hadronic scenario involving inner-blob photohadronic and outer-blob SSC/EC emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, using the parameters listed in Table tab_model_parameters we model successfully the broadband SED of six known extreme BL Lac 1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0347-121, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1218-304, 1ES 0414+009. The result of our model is shown in Figure ( fig_SED_result ). It is important to mention that in this work, we have not included the contribution observed in the optical band produced by the host galaxy Costamante2018 . The extreme BL Lac behavior presents challenges for a pure leptonic or hadronic model with one-zone emission. These objects demand atypical parameters for explaining the hard spectrum shape, especially in a VHE gamma-ray band.

In particular cases, we described the six best-known extreme BL Lacs using our proposed model, and we found a feasible explanation that relaxes the atypical values required by the one-zone models. We showed that the annihilation line radiation produced in a mildly relativistic outflow pair-plasma and the DT play essential roles in forming the hard spectrum observed in the VHE gamma-ray bands. In our model, this gamma-ray bump is produced mainly in two regions: i) the inner blob, which is responsible for the highest energy contribution above TeV-energies via photo-hadronic interactions with the annihilation line photons from the pair-plasma, and ii) the outer blob, where the very-high-energy gamma rays are created by EC with seed photons from the DT, and by the SSC mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2E 0414+0057 as a UHBL with multi-scale jet structure, wide opening angle evidence, stable VLBI flux, and a flat radio core spectrum.

quotes.quote: The 5 GHz VLA image of this source shows a jet like feature at P.A. 40^ , while the VLBI images show a jet in P.A. 75^ kharb08 . The VLBA map resolves a jet that initially extends to the east-northeast (P.A. 68^ ) of the core and also weak, extended ( 3 - 4 ) emission to the southeast of the jet, which suggests either that the jet is collimated and bends to the south 10 pc from the core, or that the projected jet opening angle is wide ( 60^ ). The inner portion of the jet is well aligned ( P.A. = 5^ ) to the kpc-scale jet rec03 . The radio structure of our EVN and MERLIN observation for this source is shown in Fig. J0414com , as well as one VLBA image as comparison.

There are two jet components in VLBA image, however, one of them is absent in our EVN image, while the MERLIN image shows only a compact core. All the measured parameters for core and jet components are listed in Table table_model , from which it can be seen that the P.A. of inner jet component ranges from 56 ^ to 90 ^ , while it is 88 ^ 124 ^ for the outer one. Therefore, our results also suggest that the jet of this source has a wide opening angle.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0414+009 as the less extreme hard-TeV BL Lac in the sample, with a steep X-ray spectrum, low synchrotron peak, and possible Compton-state change.

quotes.quote: However, there are cases of objects which are extreme only in Compton but not in synchrotron, such as 1ES 1101-232 gerd1101 , 1ES 1218 + 304 veritas1218 and 1ES 0414 + 009 hess0414 . Others are extreme in synchrotron but not in Compton, such as 1ES 1426+428 (which is characterized by a steep VHE spectrum after correction for absorption with recent EBL calculations, see e.g. dominguez13 ). To reproduce a hard TeV spectrum most scenarios rely on electron distributions with hard features, in the form of ``pile-up'', narrow-peaked distributions or low-energy cutoffs at high energy.

1ES 0414+009 is instead the only object in our sample showing more ``standard'' HBL properties. Its X-ray spectrum is steep ( _X >2 ) over the whole band, locating the synchrotron peak energy below or around 0.3 keV (from the curvature of the spectrum).

1ES 0414+009 is characterized by the lowest synchrotron-peak energy and softer X-ray spectrum in our sample. The non-thermal emission at optical frequencies swamps almost completely the host-galaxy flux, and indeed all the WISE and UVOT data appear to stay on a single power-law spectrum. As explained in Sect. 3, this source has most likely changed its Compton peak properties between the VHE and LAT epochs.

sources.summary: H.E.S.S. detected 1ES 0414+009 as a distant HBL blazar with no TeV variability, a flat deabsorbed spectrum, and implications for low EBL density and SSC modeling.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0414+009 ( z=0.287 ): The 2005/09 observations of this HBL-type blazar have been presented in . The source was detected at the significance level of 7.8 after 74 h exposure, with the photon index _ VHE = 3.45 0.25_ stat 0.20_ sys , and the integrated photon flux F( _ >0.2 TeV ) = 1.88 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . No evidence for a variability within the TeV range was found in the collected dataset. The H.E.S.S. detection of this relatively distant object confirmed a low density of the EBL, close to the lower limit from galaxy counts, as otherwise the VHE emission of the source would have been attenuated by the photon-photon annihilation below the detection threshold. A very smooth and flat EBL-deabsorbed HE/VHE spectrum of 1ES 0414+009, best characterized as a power-law with photon index _ 1.86 , implies the position of the high-energy peak in the spectral energy distribution (SED; i.e., F( ) representation) above a few TeV (see Figure f-0414 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1ES0414+009 as one of seven high-frequency-peaked blazars whose optically thick measurements contribute comparably to the excess, with a 3.6-sigma leave-one-out relevance.

quotes.quote: The seven sources with measurements extending to >2 contribute about equally to the overall excess. Removing each source spectrum and re-calculating the significance given in the following list provides an estimate on the relevance of each source: Mkn 421 ( S( Mkn 421 )=3.8 ), Mkn 501 ( S( Mkn 501 )=3.7 ), H 1426+428 ( S( H 1426+428 )=3.5 ), 1ES1101-232 ( S( 1ES1101-232 )=2.8 ), 1ES0347-121 ( S( 1ES0347-121 )=4.6 ), 1ES0414+009 ( S( 1ES0414+009 )=3.6 ), 3C279 ( S( 3C279 )=3.8 ). The result demonstrates that the different objects (6 high frequency peaked Blazars and 1 flat spectrum radio quasar) at widely different redshifts ( z=0.031 , 0.034 , 0.129 , 0.186 , 0.188 , 0.287 , and 0.536 ) contribute about equally to the overall significance. A systematic effect based upon the type of source or its distance is not evident.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0414+009 as a distant HBL detected by VERITAS after 56 hours, with low Crab-level flux, a soft spectrum affected by EBL absorption, and SED modeling favoring a lepto-hadronic fit.

quotes.quote: The comparatively distant ( z = 0.287 ) HBL 1ES 0414+009 is a known VHE emitter and was observed with VERITAS for 56 h of quality-selected live time between 2008 and 2011. These data resulted in the significant detection of a source with flux 2 of the Crab Nebula flux . The observed spectrum is soft = 3.4 0.5 , largely due to the effects of EBL absorption. Although an HBL, its SED (see Figure blazar_plots ) is not well fit by one-zone leptonic models; a lepto-hadronic model provides a better fit.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0414+009 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0414+009 : The source is rather weak in both bands. Both light curves show no trend, but DCF shows a significant correlation with radio leading optical by 20 days. Visually it appears that the correlation is a result of a common long term behaviour, rather than correlated flares. With the trend changing direction within the studied period, the trend analysis fails, but instead the polynomial subtraction is successful and we find that at least 50 of the flux in optical originates from the common slowly varying optical-radio component (see Fig. Fig:lc6 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 0414+009 as a BL Lac hosted by a luminous elliptical, with VLA and VLBI imaging showing a jet but no detected polarization.

quotes.quote: 0414+009 This BL Lac object is hosted by a luminous elliptical that is the dominant member of a galaxy cluster FalomoTanzi91 . The 5 GHz VLA image of Reid99 shows a jet-like feature at position angle PA 40 . Both of our VLBI images (Fig.  fig:0414 ) show a jet in PA 75 , with no detected polarization.

sources.summary: ATOM monitoring of 1ES 0414+00.9 found no clear global color-magnitude correlation, while distinct diagram states and modest spectral-index changes were present.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0414+00.9: The source was observed during 285 nights in 2007--2012. The linear fit to all the collected datapoints does not show any clear CM correlation, but distinct states on the CM diagram can be noted. The flux varied within the 1 mag range, and the spectral index oscillated between _ BR 0.7 and 1.3.

sources.summary: The paper adds 1ES 0414 + 009 to the H.E.S.S. blazar sample, noting its distance, 2005-2009 observations, and restricted intrinsic spectral fit due to limited degrees of freedom.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0414 + 009 ( z = 0.287, 1991AJ....101..818H ) is also added to the sample. This distant blazar was observed by from 2005 to 2009 HESS20121ES0414 .
For 1ES 0414 + 009 the intrinsic spectrum is restricted to a power-law due to the limited number of degrees of freedom available.

gmw_00000877.name: COS-3018
gmw_00000877.overview: COS-3018 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 6.854 [arXiv:2003.06443, arXiv:1706.04614]. It is characterized as a moderate starburst galaxy [arXiv:2003.06443] with dense neutral or molecular gas [arXiv:2003.06443] and a measured velocity gradient [arXiv:1706.04614].

sources.summary: The paper applies a [CII]/CIII] method to COS-3018, describing it as a z=6.854 EoR Lyman-break galaxy with moderate starburst activity, subsolar metallicity, and dense gas.

quotes.quote: COS-3018 was first discovered by tilvi2013, bowler2014 , and then re-analyzed by smit2015 as a part of their selection of IRAC excess sources in the 3.6 or 4.5 m photometric bands deriving z_ phot =6.76 . smit2018 spectroscopically confirmed the source at z=6.854 via the detection of the [ CII ] 158 m line. The [ CII ] lu mi no si ty of COS-3018 is L_ CII =(4.7 0.5) 10^ 8 L_ smit2018 .

The best fit gas density in COS-3018 is (n/ cm^ 3 ) = 2.73^ +0.15 _ 0.12 which is in an excellent agreement with the mean gas density (n/ cm^ 3 ) 2.5 of dense neutral/molecular gas found by cosmological zoom-in simulations of prototypical LBGs at z 6-7 pallottini:2019 . Such high density value might also explain the non-detection of the CIII ] 1907 line laporte2017 in this object. The best fit burstiness parameter is = 3.16^ +1.75 _ 1.39 , implying that COS-3018 is a moderate starburst galaxy. Note that the presence of an ongoing starburst in COS-3018 has been tentatively suggested by previous studies smit2018 because of the high equivalent-width of optical emission lines (EW( [ O III ] +H )= 1424 43 ) smit2015 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS-3018555981 as a z~6.85 Lyman-break galaxy with a significant [C II] line, luminous UV star formation, extended [C II] emission, and a measured velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: Particularly important, this opens up opportunities for high angular-resolution [ C 2 ] dynamics in galaxies less than one billion years after the Big Bang. h h We have obtained spectroscopy with the Atacama Large Millimetre Array (ALMA) at 241--245 GHz of two Lyman-break galaxies (LBGs) COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247 at an estimated photometric redshift just below 7, roughly 800 million years after the Big Bang. These two sources are luminous in the rest-frame ultraviolet (UV; L_ UV 2 L^ ), but are still representative of `normal' star-forming galaxies at z 7 with a UV star-formation rate (SFR) of 19-23 M_ yr^ 1 .

We detect a line in each of the targets at 241.97 0.01 GHz and 243.42 0.01 GHz , for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247 respectively, at >5 significance in both the one-dimensional spectra and the spectral line-averaged maps (Fig. fig:COS3 ). We derive spectroscopic redshifts of z_ CII =6.8540 0.0003 and z_ CII =6.8076 0.0002 , in excellent agreement with the photometric redshift estimates of 6.76 0.07 and 6.66 0.14 for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247, and line-widths of 232 30 and 124 18 km s^ 1 respectively.

We determine the [ C 2 ] half-light radii (deconvolved from the beamsize) that extend 2.6 0.8 and 3.1 1.0 kpc for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247, nearly twice the size of the UV in the brightest LBGs at this redshift . We use the spatial extent of the [ C 2 ] detection to investigate the velocity structure of these sources, which reveals a projected velocity difference over the galaxy of 111 28 and 54 20 km s^ 1 for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247 respectively (Fig. fig:kin ), similar to the velocity gradients observed in two galaxies recently studied at z 5-6 .

gmw_00000878.name: NGC 2617
gmw_00000878.overview: NGC 2617 is a face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2241, arXiv:2103.04309] containing a changing-look active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1608.00233, arXiv:1701.05042] that transitioned from a Seyfert 1.8 to a Seyfert 1 state [arXiv:1310.2241, arXiv:1608.00233]. The galaxy features a parsec-scale northern radio jet [arXiv:2103.04309] and an elliptical extended narrow-line region surrounded by bright H II regions [arXiv:1501.02022].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2617 as a face-on Sc galaxy that underwent a dramatic outburst, changing from Seyfert 1.8 to Seyfert 1 with broad lines and a blue continuum bump.

quotes.quote: After the All-Sky Automated Survey for SuperNovae (ASAS-SN) discovered a significant brightening of the inner region of NGC 2617, we began a 70 day photometric and spectroscopic monitoring campaign from the X-ray through near-infrared (NIR) wavelengths. We report that NGC 2617 went through a dramatic outburst, during which its X-ray flux increased by over an order of magnitude followed by an increase of its optical/ultraviolet (UV) continuum flux by almost an order of magnitude. NGC 2617, classified as a Seyfert 1.8 galaxy in 2003, is now a Seyfert 1 due to the appearance of broad optical emission lines and a continuum blue bump.

NGC 2617 is a face-on Sc galaxy at a redshift of z=0.0142 paturel03 , corresponding to a Hubble flow corrected distance of 64 Mpc. moran96 classified it as a Seyfert 1.8 galaxy. The galaxy displayed only narrow emission lines except for a weak, broad base to the H line. It lacked both the broad emission lines that are characteristic of Seyfert 1 galaxies, and a bright power-law continuum. Here we report on the dramatic transformation of NGC 2617 into a Seyfert 1 and the monitoring of the (presumably) associated X-ray--NIR outburst.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 2617 as a changing-look Seyfert galaxy whose X-ray spectrum resembles a type 1 AGN, with partial covering gas and redshifted highly ionized iron absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 2617 is a nearby ( z 0.01 ) active galaxy that recently switched from being a Seyfert 1.8 to be a Seyfert 1.0. At the same time, it underwent a strong increase of X-ray flux by one order of magnitude with respect to archival measurements. We characterise the X-ray spectral and timing properties of NGC 2617 with the aim of studying the physics of a changing-look active galactic nucleus (AGN). We performed a comprehensive timing and spectral analysis of two XMM-Newton pointed observations spaced by one month, complemented by archival quasi-simultaneous INTEGRAL observations. We found that, to the first order, NGC 2617 looks like a type 1 AGN in the X-ray band and, with the addition of a modest reflection component, its continuum can be modelled well either with a power law plus a phenomenological blackbody, a partially covered power law, or a double Comptonisation model.

Most interestingly, absorption structures due to highly ionised iron have been detected in both observations with a redshift of about 0.1c with respect to the systemic redshift of the host galaxy. The redshifted absorber can be ascribed to a failed wind/aborted jets component, to gravitational redshift effects, and/or to matter directly falling towards the central supermassive black hole. In either case, we are probing the innermost accretion flow around the central supermassive black hole of NGC 2617 and might be even watching matter in a direct inflow towards the black hole itself.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the changing-look AGN NGC 2617 from X-ray to near-IR, finding multiwavelength variability, broad-line delays, high-to-low state changes, and dust recovery.

quotes.quote: We present the results of photometric and spectroscopic monitoring campaigns of the changing look AGN NGC 2617 carried out from 2016 until 2022 and covering the wavelength range from the X-ray to the near-IR. The facilities included the telescopes of the SAI MSU, MASTER Global Robotic Net, the 2.3-m WIRO telescope, Swift , and others. We found significant variability at all wavelengths and, specifically, in the intensities and profiles of the broad Balmer lines. We measured time delays of 6 days ( 8 days) in the responses of the H (H ) line to continuum variations.

Using our optical spectroscopy and multi-wavelength photometry (from near-IR to X-ray region), we have shown that NGC 2617 had been in the high state for several years (2013--2017) (see also Paper I), and from the beginning of April 2017 until the end of May 2018 the object had very low brightness and variability. In December 2017, the X-ray flux was the lowest since monitoring began. Despite the very low X-ray and UV flux observed in December 2017, the broad H line was still prominent. As it was suspected in our publication (Paper I), the dust needs at least a few years of the object being in the low state to recover and to be able to obscure broad lines.
