sources.summary: The paper presents high-cadence reverberation mapping of changing-look AGN NGC 2617, detecting velocity-resolved lags, a stratified broad-line region, and a black hole mass near 2e7 solar masses.

quotes.quote: NGC 2617 has attracted a lot of attention after the detection of the changes in spectral type, and its geometry and kinematics of broad-line region (BLR) are still ambiguous. In this paper, we present the high cadence ( 2 days) reverberation mapping campaign of NGC 2617 from 2019 October to 2020 May undertaken at Lijiang 2.4 m telescope. For the first time, the velocity-resolved reverberation signature of the object was successfully detected.

In this work, we present a new high cadence RM campaign of CL-AGN NGC 2617 during 2019--2020 with 2.4 m telescope at Lijiang Observatory. The time lags of = 6.91^ +1.58 _ 0.78 , = 5.38^ +0.98 _ 1.12 , = 4.88^ +2.05 _ 1.94 , and = 1.04^ +3.24 _ 0.82 days are successfully detected from the high cadence ( 2 days) data, which give \ and \ for the first time. The black hole mass of 2 10^ 7 M_ can be derived from both \ and . During the period of our observations, NGC 2617 is 3 times brighter than 6.5 years ago, and then shows the larger lags and the narrower profiles of BELs.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2617 as a Seyfert 1 target for broadband H reverberation mapping, noting high-cadence data, prior changing-look behavior, and consistent extracted H lags.

quotes.quote: Here we present an ICCF-Cut method for broadband PRM to obtain the H broad line lag and apply it to four Seyfert 1 galaxies, MCG+08-11-011, NGC 2617, 3C 120 and NGC 5548. All of them have high quality broadband lightcurves with daily/sub-daily cadence, which enables us to extract H lightcurves from the line band by subtracting the contributions from the continuum and host galaxy. Their extracted H lightcurves are compared with the lagged continuum band lightcurves, as well as the lagged H lightcurves obtained by spectroscopic RM (SRM) at the same epochs. The consistency of these lightcurves and the comparison with the SRM H lags provide supports to the H lags of these AGNs, in a range from 9 to 19 days, obtained by the ICCF-Cut, JAVELIN and ^2 methods.
NGC 2617 is also known as a changing look AGN 2014ApJ...788...48S from other SRM campaign 2021ApJ...912...92F and continuum RM campaign 2021MNRAS.503.4163K .

For NGC 2617 shown in Figure 6, the extracted H lightcurve is consistent with the lagged continuum and the lagged SRM H line lightcurves. The H lag distributions of the ICCF-Cut and JAVELIN are very close.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2617 as a turn-on changing-state AGN whose optical transition and persistent broad Balmer emission coincide with a strong BAT X-ray flux rise.

quotes.quote: NGC 2617 NGC 2617 (BAT 1327) was classified as type 1.8 in 2003, and transitioned to type 1 in 2013 2014ApJ...788...48S, 2017MNRAS.467.1496O . Subsequent followup 2017MNRAS.467.1496O, 2021MNRAS.503.3886Y showed the continued presence of broad Balmer line emission through 2016, similar to the type 1 behaviour seen in BASS spectra in 2017 and 2020. NGC 2617 was not included in BASS DR2 as it was not detected in the 70-month BAT survey 2013ApJS..207...19B .

Fig. fig:BAT_LCs2 shows a strong increase in the 14--195 keV X-ray flux from 2010-2012, and NGC 2617 was detected in the BAT 105-month survey 2018ApJS..235....4O . Its BAT flux remained high from 2012 through to the end of the 157-month light-curve in 2017 December. We therefore associated the increase in X-ray flux in 2010--2012 with the turn-on CS event seen in the optical spectra.

sources.summary: The paper presents radio observations of NGC 2617, finding a compact core, parsec-scale northern jet, stable radio core flux, and no jet activity tied to the 2013 outburst.

quotes.quote: The nearby face-on spiral galaxy NGC 2617 underwent an unambiguous `inside-out' multi-wavelength outburst in Spring 2013, and a dramatic Seyfert type change probably between 2010 and 2012, with the emergence of broad optical emission lines. To search for the jet activity associated with this variable accretion activity, we carried out multi-resolution and multi-wavelength radio observations. Using the very long baseline interferometric (VLBI) observations with the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 1.7 and 5.0 GHz, we find that NGC 2617 shows a partially synchrotron self-absorbed compact radio core with a significant core shift, and an optically thin steep-spectrum jet extending towards the north up to about two parsecs in projection.

The radio core had a stable flux density of 1.4 mJy at 5.0 GHz between 2013 June and 2014 January, in agreement with the expectation of a supermassive black hole in the low accretion rate state. The northern jet component is unlikely to be associated with the `inside-out' outburst of 2013.

sources.summary: NGC 2617 is described as a Seyfert 1 with very broad Balmer emission, narrower ENLR lines, an elliptical ENLR, and surrounding bright H II regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 2617: This Seyfert 1 galaxy displays extremely broad Balmer line emission with a line profile that is steeper on the blue side. By contrast the ENLR lines are relatively narrow ( 120 km s ^ 1 ). The ENLR is elliptical 9 6 arcsec ( 2.6 1.7 kpc) and orientated almost E-W, but here is a prominent ENLR / \ region knot at PA = 330 and at 15 arcsec from the nucleus ( 4.4 kpc). The nucleus is surrounded by many bright \ regions.

sources.summary: The paper shows NGC 2617 remains a type-1 changing-look Seyfert, with 2016 brightening, broad-Balmer variability, and multiwavelength monitoring of its active nucleus.

quotes.quote: Optical and near-infrared photometry, optical spectroscopy, and soft X-ray and UV monitoring of the changing look active galactic nucleus NGC 2617 show that it continues to have the appearance of a type-1 Seyfert galaxy. An optical light curve for 2010--2016 indicates that the change of type probably occurred between 2010 October and 2012 February and was not related to the brightening in 2013. In 2016 NGC 2617 brightened again to a level of activity close to that in 2013 April. We find variations in all passbands and in both the intensities and profiles of the broad Balmer lines.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC2617 as a Seyfert galaxy with puzzling Swift interband lag measurements whose wavelength dependence and X-ray extrapolation do not match simple reprocessing expectations.

quotes.quote: presented lag measurements of the Seyfert galaxy NGC2617 but the results are slightly puzzling. The lags increase with wavelength, but if the fit is forced to go through the X-ray point then, for lag ^ , they find =0.37 , which is far from the 4/3 expected from reprocessing. If the X-ray point is ignored, a fit of =1.18 is found between the other bands, but if extrapolated to the X-ray wavelength, the fit is offset from the X-ray point by a very large lag of 2.4d.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2617 against Nancay, Parkes, HIPASS, and EDD H I measurements, finding the earlier reported measurements agree with its new values.

quotes.quote: NGC 2617: Previous observations include the Nan cay telescope hyperleda and the Parkes telescope including two measurements from the HI Parkes All Sky Survey Catalogue meyer2004,doyle2005 . The HI data for NGC 2617 was also reprocessed theureau2006 for inclusion in the EDD. All previous reported measurements are in agreement with our measurements.

sources.summary: NGC 2617 is included among reverberation targets with consistent ICCF-Cut and JAVELIN lags, though its measured cut lag is larger than the predicted lag with large errors.

quotes.quote: This method has recovered H lags for four Syfert 1 galaxies (MCG+089 11-011, NGC 2617, 3C 120 and NGC 5548) successfully 2023ApJ...949...22M .
The lags given by the Pmap model are similar to that of ICCF-Cut for NGC 5548, NGC 2617, Mrk 142 and Mrk 509, while not consistent for NGC 4593 and NGC 4151.
Although NGC 2617 has larger _ cut than the prediction, the errors of _ cut are also much larger.

gmw_00000879.name: host galaxy of FRB 20201124A
gmw_00000879.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 20201124A is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2111.11764, arXiv:2307.06995] located at a redshift of approximately z = 0.098 [arXiv:2111.01600, arXiv:2111.11764]. It is a massive, dusty, star-forming system [arXiv:2106.09710, arXiv:2107.14339] with persistent radio and marginal X-ray emissions that are associated with its ongoing star formation [arXiv:2106.09710, arXiv:2307.03766].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J050803.48 + 260338.0 as a massive, dusty, star-forming FRB host whose persistent radio emission is associated with ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy is massive ( 3 10^ 10 M_ ), star-forming (few solar masses per year), and dusty. Very Large Array and Very Long Baseline Array observations of the persistent radio source measure a luminosity of 1.2 10^ 29 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 , and show that is extended on scales 50 mas. We associate this radio emission with the ongoing star-formation activity in SDSS J050803.48 + 260338.0. Deeper, more detailed observations are required to better utilise the milliarcsecond-scale localisation of reported from the European VLBI Network, and determine the origin of the large dispersion measure ( 150-220 pc cm ^ 3 ) contributed by the host. SDSS J050803.48 + 260338.0 is an order of magnitude more massive than any galaxy or stellar system previously associated with a repeating FRB source, but is comparable to the hosts of so far non-repeating FRBs, further building the link between the two apparent populations.

The burst and persistent emission are coincident with each other and the galaxy SDSS J050803.48 + 260338.0 (hereafter J0508 + 2603). No other optical source is consistent with the radio source locations. We use the burst localisation precision and PS1 catalogue to calculate the chance of association using the astropath Bayesian inference framework 2021ApJ...911...95A .

sources.summary: The paper describes the FRB 20201124A host as a nearby dusty star-forming galaxy, with intense local radio star formation near the burst and SED-derived subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a multiwavelength campaign of FRB20201124A, the second closest repeating fast radio burst recently localized in a nearby ( z = 0.0978 ) galaxy. Deep VLA observations led to the detection of a quiescent radio emission, also marginally visible in X-rays with Chandra . Imaging at 22 GHz allowed us to resolve the source on a scale of 1 in a direction tangential to the center of the host galaxy and locate it at the position of the FRB, within an error of 0.2 . EVN and e-MERLIN observations sampled small angular scales, from 2 to 100 mas, providing tight upper limits on the presence of a compact source and evidence for diffuse radio emission.

Consistent values of the SFR and the key properties of the host galaxy are derived from optical spectroscopy and the spectral energy distribution (SED) modelling (Appendices sec: opt_spectroscopy and sec: Galaxy SED modeling ). The best fit parameters describing the galaxy are: a high intrinsic extinction A_V=1.3 0.2 mag, symbolic of a dusty galaxy, a sub-solar metallicity Z_ =0.46 0.25 , an e folding time =4.3^ +3.6 _ 2.5 Gyr, a moderate stellar mass (M_*/M_ )=9.78 0.08 , an old stellar population t_m=1.5^ +0.5 _ 0.4 Gyr, and a star-formation rate SFR =4.3 0.5 M_ yr ^ 1 . The best fit model spectrum is shown in Fig. fig:broadband_SED_wRadio . From optical spectroscopy we derive a redshift z=0.0978 0.0002 and an H_ line luminosity, L(H_ )= (4.5 0.8) 10^ 41 erg \ s^ 1 , corresponding to SFR _ H _ =2.3 0.4 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J050803.48+260338.0 as a massive star-forming FRB host at z=0.098 with resolved radio star-formation emission and an FRB location offset from its optical center.

quotes.quote: The local environment of The host galaxy of , SDSS J050803.48+260338.0 or hereafter J0508+2603 day_2021_atel is a massive, star-forming galaxy ravi_2021_arxiv,fong_2021_apjl at a redshift of z=0.098 kilpatrick_2021_atel . The VLA (D-configuration; ricci_2021_atel ) detected unresolved compact persistent emission at 3 GHz and 9 GHz, in addition to the uGMRT wharton_2021_atel_prs detection of unresolved persistent emission at 600 MHz. Follow-up with 22-GHz VLA observations in C-configuration, allowed for the emission to be resolved piro_2021_arxiv . The lack of compact emission in our EVN 1.4-GHz observations (Figure fig:comp ), supports the conclusion that the radio emission seen with lower resolution instruments is from star-formation ravi_2021_arxiv,fong_2021_apjl,piro_2021_arxiv .

The milliarcsecond precision of our EVN localisation, allows us to explore where the FRB location is relative to the radio star-formation emission piro_2021_arxiv , and the centre of the host galaxy fong_2021_apjl . We find that \ is 705 26 mas (projected distance: 1.3 kpc, assuming an angular size distance of 375.9 Mpc; kilpatrick_2021_atel ), from the optical centre of the host galaxy, statistically inconsistent with originating from the galaxy centre, similar to the discussion in fong_2021_apjl .

sources.summary: The paper identifies the FRB 20201124A host as a barred, Milky-Way-sized, metal-rich spiral where the source lies in a low-density interarm disk region.

quotes.quote: We performed optical and near-infrared observations of the host galaxy SDSS J050803.48+260338.0 using the 10 m Keck telescopes, including high- and low-dispersion spectra with the Echellette Spectrograph Imager (ESI) and the Low Resolution Imaging Spectrometer (LRIS), respectively, and K^ band images with the NIRC2 camera using the laser guide-star adaptive-optics (AO) system. In the optical spectra, we detected multiple emission lines ( fig:fig3 (a)) and derived z=0.09795 0.00003 , which corresponds to a luminosity distance of 453.3 0.1 Mpc adopting the standard Planck cosmological model . Similar to the hosts of several other repeaters (e.g., FRB 20121102A , FRB 20180916B , FRB 20180301A ), this host is in the star-forming branch of the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich diagram (Methods). Our adaptive-optics (AO) image ( fig:fig3 (b)), with a full width at half-maximum resolution of 0.12'' , shows that the host is a barred galaxy with spiral features, and the FRB's apparent location is in the disc but offset from the bar and spiral arms. The galaxy's stellar mass , M_* 3 10^ 10 M_ , is about half that of the Milky Way (MW); in contrast, its star-formation rate ( SFR =3.4 0.3 M_ yr^ 1 ) is about twice that of the MW, and its metallicity ( 12+ (O/H) = 9.07^ +0.03 _ 0.04 ) is approximately twice the solar abundance (Methods).

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20201124A has marginal X-ray emission converted to a luminosity of about 4 x 10^40 erg/s, which the paper attributes to star formation rather than AGN.

quotes.quote: For FRB 20201124A, we detect 5 counts in a 1.5 \ radius aperture centered on the host position in a 29.7 ks exposure (Obs ID:25016; PI: Piro; Piro2021 ). We find a net source count rate of (1.6 0.7) 10^ 4 cts s ^ 1 (2.4 significance), consistent with a marginal detection, as reported in Piro2021 . We convert this count rate to an X-ray flux with N_ H,MW = 4.5 10^ 21 \ cm^ 2 and find F_X = (1.6 0.6) 10^ 15 \ erg \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 ( 2-10 keV).

At the distance of FRB 20201124A ( z = 0.0982 ), the corresponding X-ray luminosity is L_ X = (4 1.7) 10^ 40 \ erg \ s^ 1 , consistent with the values reported in Piro2021 . Given the small spatial offset between the FRB and the host center ( 0.7 ), we adopt the host galaxy X-ray flux as an upper limit for X-ray emission at the FRB position.

sources.summary: The paper maps the barred spiral host of FRB 20201124A, finding dust-obscured star formation, radio emission across the host, and an FRB location offset from optical arms and bar.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution 1.5--6 GHz Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) optical and infrared observations of the extremely active repeating fast radio burst (FRB) FRB 20201124A and its barred spiral host galaxy. We constrain the location and morphology of star formation in the host and search for a persistent radio source (PRS) coincident with FRB 20201124A. We resolve the morphology of the radio emission across all frequency bands and measure a star formation rate SFR 8.9 M_ yr ^ 1 , a factor of 4-6 larger than optically-inferred SFRs, demonstrating dust-obscured star formation throughout the host. Compared to a sample of all known FRB hosts with radio emission, the host of FRB 20201124A has the most significant obscured star formation. While HST observations show the FRB to be offset from the bar or spiral arms, the radio emission extends to the FRB location.

sources.summary: SDSS J050803.48+260338.0 is the host galaxy coincident with persistent 650 MHz radio emission; that emission was later resolved and linked to star formation, while scattering may arise within the host.

quotes.quote: In addition, we detect persistent radio emission of 0.7 0.1 mJy at 650 MHz GMRT-PRS-ATel coincident with both the burst position and the galaxy SDSS J050803.48+260338.0. The persistent radio emission was later found to be resolved on milliarcsecond scales EVN-localization-ATel and likely associated with star formation in the host galaxy Ravi2021arXiv,Fong2021arXiv .

P-II develops the hypothesis that the scattering arises from a screen that is located much closer to the FRB, possibly within the host galaxy, and that the scintillation is being imparted by a scattering screen in the Milky Way.
If the scattering arises at the host galaxy, we expect the scattering timescale to be constant throughout the annual scintillation modulation cycle.

gmw_00000880.name: NGC 4365
gmw_00000880.overview: NGC 4365 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1310.2607] that serves as the massive core of the Virgo W group [arXiv:1510.01929]. It features a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1310.2607, arXiv:2003.04093] and is actively interacting with NGC 4342, producing a diffuse southwest tidal tail and a stellar stream [arXiv:1510.01929, arXiv:1611.04435].

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra analysis of NGC 4365, with 322 detected sources, a 13 3 variable fraction, mostly persistent emission, twelve transient candidates, and an estimated 180 yr outburst timescale.

quotes.quote: For NGC 4697 and NGC 4365, we performed standard reduction and flare removal. We used CIAO wavdetect to detect sources (158 in NGC 4697; 322 in NGC 4365) in the combined 185 ks (NGC 4697) and 194 ks (NGC 4365) observations.

There are 124 and 232 such sources in NGC 4697 and NGC 4365; however, the number of these sources in a given observation is smaller due to varying field-of-views. The fraction of strongly variable sources between any two observations ranges from 3 - 15 , but is roughly consistent with 8 . The fraction variable considering all five observations is 16 4 for NGC 4697 and 13 3 for NGC 4365.

There are eleven transient candidates in NGC 4697 and twelve transient candidates in NGC 4365, most of which either turn on or turn off. For each galaxy, we estimate the average outburst timescale assuming all sources in a galaxy are transients undergoing long-duration outbursts and we detected the beginning or end of that outburst in all of our transient candidates. We estimate relatively consistent outburst timescales of 100 yr in NGC 4697 and 180 yr in NGC 4365; however, we note that either assumption could be violated.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4365 has an extended globular-cluster system with three color subpopulations, red clusters resembling the galaxy starlight, and kinematic peculiarities tied to formation scenarios.

quotes.quote: We determine the lower limit on the number of GCs to be 6450 110 and show that the GC system extends beyond 134 kpc (9.5 galaxy effective radii). We revisit the question of whether NGC 4365 has a bimodal or trimodal GC colour distribution and find support for three distinct GC colour subpopulations (i.e. \ blue, green and red). S e rsic profile fits to the radial surface density of each subpopulation reveal that the blue GCs are more extended than either red or green GCs.

The properties, including surface density profile, position angle, ellipticity and radial colour gradient, of the red GC subpopulation are very similar to the properties of NGC 4365's starlight. This result supports the hypothesis that red GCs are formed along with the bulk of the diffuse starlight in the galaxy. NGC 4365 has a kinematically distinct core and a significant misalignment between the photometric and kinematic major axes. We discuss the possibility that these kinematic features are related to the presence of the distinct third GC subpopulation. We briefly discuss implications for the formation of NGC 4365, finding that major merger, multi-phase collapse and accretion formation scenarios could all account for the existence of a third GC subpopulation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4365's globular-cluster kinematics, finding distinct GC subpopulation rotations, rolling stellar light, a rotationally dominated outer red population, and low-velocity GCs aligned with a stellar stream.

quotes.quote: We present a kinematic analysis of the globular cluster (GC) system of the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 4365 and find several distinct kinematic substructures. This analysis is carried out using radial velocities for 269 GCs, obtained with the DEIMOS instrument on the Keck II telescope as part of the SAGES Legacy Unifying Globulars and Galaxies Survey (SLUGGS). We find that each of the three (formerly identified) GC colour subpopulations reveal distinct rotation properties. The rotation of the green GC subpopulation is consistent with the bulk of NGC 4365's stellar light, which `rolls' about the photometric major axis. The blue and red GC subpopulations show `normal' rotation about the minor axis.

We also find that the red GC subpopulation is rotationally dominated beyond 2.5 arcmin ( 17 kpc) and that the root mean squared velocity of the green subpopulation declines sharply with radius suggesting a possible bias towards radial orbits relative to the other GC subpopulations. Additionally, we find a population of low velocity GCs that form a linear structure running from the SW to the NE across NGC 4365 which aligns with the recently reported stellar stream towards NGC 4342. These low velocity GCs have g'-i' colours consistent with the overall NGC 4365 GC system but have velocities consistent with the systemic velocity of NGC 4342.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4365 as a giant elliptical with a puzzling globular-cluster system, testing claims that intermediate-age clusters imply later star formation.

quotes.quote: The giant elliptical NGC 4365 represents a puzzling case in the literature of the field of extragalactic GCs. It became an interesting target due to the somewhat unusual colour distribution of its GC system ( dforbes96 ; larsen01 ). In general, the GC systems of giant elliptical galaxies exhibit bimodal optical colour distributions, where the colours of both peaks show a fairly tight correlation with host galaxy integrated luminosity ( larsen01 ; peng06 ). NGC 4365 has been reported to have a normal blue peak for its luminosity, but a broader read peak shifted towards bluer (V-I) colours ( larsen01 with WFPC2 data, lbs05 with ACS imaging).

Spectroscopic observations of GCs in this galaxy have so far failed to provide a conclusive answer with one study suggesting intermediate ages ( larsen03 , hereafter L03) and another yielding uniformly old ages ( brodie05 , hereafter B05). These studies analysed two sets of observations of a similar sample of clusters with the LRIS spectrograph on the Keck telescope. The L03 sample consisted of 14 GCs and the B05 one of 22 GCs. The S/N of B05 was higher than that of L03. We refer the reader to Sect. 4.8 of for a thorough discussion of a comparison between the L03 and B05 samples and analysis. Integrated light spectroscopic studies show that the galaxy light is dominated by old and metal-rich stars as in most other giant ellipticals ( davies01 ; yamada06 ). Therefore a large fraction of younger GCs would be surprising. Most giant ellipticals are thought to have at place the bulk of their GC systems at z=2 ( puzia05 ; strader05 ). The claims of numerous intermediate-age ( 3-5 Gyrs) GCs in NGC 4365 would imply that a major star formation episode occurred at a lower redshift ( z<2 ) in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4365 as a massive Virgo-background elliptical with merger signs, a distinct core, unusual minor-axis stellar rotation, and red GCs matching that stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 is a massive elliptical (E3) behind the Virgo cluster with signs of an ongoing merger (Mihos et al. 2012, in preparation). It has a kinematically distinct core Krajnovic and an unusual stellar rotation along the photometric minor axis Bender94 . Its GC system is mainly known for an odd GC colour distribution that seems to suggest the presence of three, instead of the classic two, subpopulations Larsen03,Larsen05,Brodie05,Blom . We make use of the photometric results presented in Blom based on gz HST /ACS and gri Suprime-Cam imaging.

We spectroscopically confirm 269 GCs over 6 DEIMOS masks. We study the kinematics of 87 blue and 164 red GCs respectively. We also add 9 GCs from the combined dataset of and Brodie05 as described in sec:Repeated . We find that the red GCs rotate within R<200 arcsec along the photometric minor axis, mimicking the kinematics of the stars.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4365 as a case study of early-type galaxy outskirts, finding excess X-ray sources and arguing that many are not globular-cluster LMXBs but supernova-kicked binaries.

quotes.quote: Therefore, we probe the X-ray populations of NGC 4365 as a case study. The galaxy NGC 4365 is a large elliptical galaxy with =1.8 10^ 11 \ M_ and =3.95 , which exhibits a notable source excess in the \ region. The GC population of NGC 4365 has been studied as part of the SAGES Legacy Unifying Globulars and Galaxies Survey (SLUGGS).

The galaxy and its outskirts have been observed in eight pointings with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) onboard HST , whose data has been complemented with Subaru /S-Cam observations. Based on this combined data set, Blom2012 reported more than 6000 GC candidates in and around NGC 4365. Beyond the central 0.5 ( 1 ) the obtained list of GC candidates is complete to the turnover magnitude, and is not significantly affected by contamination Blom2012 .

Thus, detailed study of NGC 4365 provides a direct confirmation of the conclusion we made in Sect. sec:subsample , that the excess sources at large galactocentric radii are not all associated with globular clusters. In this particular case of a massive galaxy (even with relatively high ), almost 2/3 of the excess sources must be of a different origin, for which, as we argue below, supernova kicks seem to be a plausible scenario.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 4365 as the dominant Virgo W cloud elliptical, with a kinematically distinct core, nearby companions, a southwest tidal tail, and an older-accretion halo plume.

quotes.quote: The giant elliptical NGC 4365 (1243 ; Cappellari 2011) lies in the lower right (southwest) corner of our imaging fields (Figures Vmosaic and Bcolor ), 5.3 \ SSW of M87 and 1.5 \ WSW of M49. It is located 6 Mpc behind the Virgo Cluster (Mei 2007) For the discussion of physical sizes and luminosities for features around NGC 4365, we adopt a distance of 23 Mpc ( m-M = 31.8 ; Mei 2007) to the system, for which 1 \ = 112 pc. and is the dominant galaxy of the Virgo \ group (de Vaucouleurs 1961, Binggeli 1985; Mei 2007), surrounded by a population of lower luminosity galaxies (see Figure  N4365 a) within v = 350 \ and a projected distance of 1 (400 kpc). NGC 4365 itself has a kinematically distinct core (Bender Surma 1992), along with evidence for multiple populations of globular clusters (Blom 2012b).

An early release of our Schmidt imaging data, shown in Bogd an (2012), revealed an extended tidal tail extending SW from NGC 4365. A number of companion galaxies are projected on or near the tail, most notably the S0 galaxy NGC 4342 (761 , Cappellari 2011). While Bogd an argued the tail was unlikely to have been stripped from NGC 4342, due to the high dark halo mass implied by the galaxy's X-ray luminosity, subsequent observations by Blom (2012a, 2014) of globular clusters in the stream showed a convincing kinematic match between the tidal tail and NGC 4342.

Finally, we identify an asymmetric plume of light in the southern isophotes of NGC 4365's halo. This feature is broad and diffuse ( =27.5), and is not obviously associated with any nearby companion galaxy. It is similar in color to the outskirts of NGC 4365 ( =0.85) and may trace an older accretion event that has deposited starlight into the galaxy's halo.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4365 as the central W' group elliptical with unusual kinematics, crossed by a stellar/GC stream, and likely stripping stars and globular clusters from NGC 4342.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 is a giant elliptical galaxy with a redshift independent distance measurement of 23.1 Mpc FCS5 and a recession velocity of 1243 km s ^ 1 vSys , placing it 6 Mpc behind the Virgo Cluster. NGC 4365 is the central galaxy in the W' group and has been noted to have rare properties for a galaxy of its luminosity. It has a kinematically distinct core and the bulk of its stars rotate along the minor axis Arnold et al. \ 2013 in prep.

In the last year, Bo12a presented a very deep B filter image of NGC 4365 showing a stellar stream extending from NE of NGC 4365, across the elliptical galaxy, bridging the gap between NGC 4365 and NGC 4342, and extending further SW. This image has prompted investigation of the nature of the interaction between NGC 4365 and NGC 4342.

We find an overdensity of globular clusters (GCs) centred on the S0 galaxy NGC 4342 that is spatially coincident with the stellar stream crossing NGC 4365, as reported by Bo12a . The photometric colours of the stream GCs match the colours of the GCs around NGC 4342 and their measured recession velocities ( V =742 23 km s ^ 1 ) match the measured recession velocity of NGC 4342 itself (V = 751 km s ^ 1 ). The evidence from these observations suggests that stars and GCs from NGC 4342 are being tidally stripped by the larger galaxy NGC 4365, situated at the centre of the W' group.

sources.summary: The paper assigns NGC 4365 to class E, emphasizing its old stars, kinematically decoupled core, minor-axis rotation, and evidence for gas-poor merger assembly.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC 3608, 4365 and 4473) have been assigned class E. The mass growth of class E galaxies is expected to be dominated by recent gas-poor major mergers, although minor mergers also contribute. They should contain predominately old stars with little, or no, younger stars. This class of slow rotators includes simulated galaxies that reveal kinematic twists, minor axis rotation, KDCs and the rare 2- galaxies.

Indeed, NGC 3608 and NGC 4365 are known to harbour KDCs, and NGC 4473 is one of only 4 of galaxies in the ATLAS ^ 3D sample to reveal a double peak in the 2D velocity dispersion map (i.e. a 2- galaxy). All three have old mass-weighted ages (McDermid et al. 2015). NGC 4365 reveals minor axis rotation (i.e. the galaxy rolls rather than spins) beyond its central KDC region (Arnold et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4365 as a giant elliptical with a blue central disc, rich globular clusters, distinct stellar components, decoupled core, and tidal tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 NGC 4365 (VCC 0731) is a bright and giant elliptical galaxy located in a group slightly behind the ``Virgo subcluster B'' centered on NGC 4472. It shows the presence of a small scale disc in the center, bluer than the surrounding galaxy Ferrarese06 . Peng06 detected a significant population of globular clusters (GCs) around this object, with a specific frequency higher than in other galaxies of comparable luminosity, probably due to a larger number of red GCs. Moreover, Blom12a, Blom12b found the GC system to consist of three subpopulations.

Surma93 found evidence for two kinematically distinct stellar components: a bulge-like component and a disc-like subsystem. The most interesting kinematic features of NGC 4365 are its kinematically decoupled core Davies01 and that the galaxy rolls rather than rotates Arnold14 . Bogdan12 found an extended faint tidal tail between NGC 4365 and NGC 4342 in deep B-band images ( _ B \ 29.5 mag/arcsec ^ 2 ), which Blom14 claimed to be a sign of ongoing interaction between the two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4365 with MUSE and argues its kinematically distinct core is not truly decoupled, but likely a projection effect from triaxial orbital structure.

quotes.quote: We study the kinematically distinct components in two early-type galaxies NGC 448 and NGC 4365 aided by integral-field observations with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) on the Very Large Telescope. NGC 448 has previously been shown to host a counter-rotating stellar disk. NGC 4365 harbours a central (apparently) decoupled core that has been suggested to not be physically distinct from the main body and instead stems from the different orbital types in the core and main body due to its triaxial nature.

By contrast, we do not find evidence for true decoupling in the two distinct kinematic components in NGC 4365. This confirms earlier work suggesting that the kinematically distinct core is likely not a separate dynamical structure, but most certainly likely a projection effect stemming from the orbital structure of this galaxy that was previously found to be intrinsically triaxial in shape.

Conversely, the presence of a kinematically distinct component in NGC 4365 is not associated to a true kinematic decoupling and is instead most likely due to projection effect stemming from the triaxial nature of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4365 as a giant elliptical with triaxial equilibrium, a prominent kinematically decoupled core, old stellar population, and modeling consistent with its observed kinematics.

quotes.quote: The upper panels of Figure NGC4365 show the stellar kinematics in the central 30'' 60'' of the giant elliptical galaxy NGC4365, derived from two SAURON pointings ( ). The velocity field clearly shows a prominent decoupled core in the inner 3'' 7'' (cf. \ ). It has a rotation axis which lies 82^ 2^ away from that of the body of the galaxy, which rotates around its long axis.

The globular cluster system of NGC 4365 shows evidence for an intermediate age population ( ). The SAURON linestrength maps, however, indicate a predominantly old stellar population ( ), suggesting that the observed kinematic structure may have been in place for over 12 Gyr and the galaxy is in stable triaxial equilibrium. We therefore applied our developed modeling software to this case, to investigate whether it is possible to reproduce all the kinematic data in detail, and to constrain M/L and the intrinsic shape and orbital structure.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4365 as an old giant elliptical, finding strong triaxiality, skew rotation, and constraints on how its central black hole affects global structure.

quotes.quote: We address whether this process operates in real systems, by modeling the stellar kinematics of the old elliptical NGC 4365. This galaxy has a mean stellar population age >12 and is known for its kinematically decoupled core and skew rotation at larger radii. We fit the two-dimensional mean velocity field obtained by the SAURON integral-field spectrograph, and the isophotal ellipticity and position-angle profiles, using the velocity field (VF) fitting approach. The models constrain the system's intrinsic shape between 0.03 and 0.5 effective radii, as well as its orientation in space.

We find NGC 4365 to be strongly triaxial ( T 0.45 ) and somewhat flatter than it appears ( /a 0.6 ). Axisymmetry or near axisymmetry ( T<0.1 ) is ruled out at >95 confidence for 1 6 < R < 3 2 ( 0.03 < R/r_e < 0.06 ), and at >99 confidence at larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4365 as a Virgo giant elliptical with an unusual globular-cluster system, favoring old intermediate-metallicity clusters over a dominant intermediate-age population.

quotes.quote: Among the GC systems of early-type galaxies, a particularly puzzling case is NGC 4365. The galaxy itself appears to be a typical giant elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster. It is among the 10 brightest early-type galaxies in Virgo (Binggeli et al. \ ), but appears otherwise unremarkable. Tonry et al. \ ( ) derived a distance modulus of m-M = 31.55 0.17 from surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) measurements, placing NGC 4365 about 0.5 mag ( 4 Mpc) behind the M49 subcluster in the direction of the Virgo W cloud.

We review the available evidence for intermediate-age GCs in NGC 4365 and conclude that, while this cannot be definitively ruled out, an alternative scenario is more likely whereby all the GCs are old but the relative number of intermediate-metallicity GCs is greater than typical for giant ellipticals.
In any case, it is puzzling that the significant intermediate-colour GC population in NGC 4365 is not accompanied by a corresponding shift of the integrated galaxy light towards bluer colours.

sources.summary: NGC 4365 is an E3 galaxy with boxy hexagonal isophotes, triaxial structure, a kinematically decoupled core, metallicity gradient, and constant age.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 = VCC 0731 The image of this E3 galaxy reveals boxy, slightly hexagonal isophotes. This galaxy is known to have triaxial intrinsic shapes and kinematically decoupled core wag1988 . Earlier stellar population studies see and references therein dav2001 have shown a kinematical distinct inner region, which hosts the same stellar population as the outskirts. The galaxy harbours a metallicity gradient and constant age along the radius. Our profiles agree with the previous kinematical and stellar populations studies e.g. dav2001 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4365 as an exception where the blue globular-cluster profile is flatter than the X-ray profile, possibly because an interaction added outer clusters.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 lies in the W ' cloud, a group lying some 5 Mpc behind the Virgo cluster. The main exception is NGC 4365 which reveals a GC profile that is significantly flatter than the X-ray one. This galaxy is currently undergoing an interaction (Mihos et al. 2012) and it is possible that the accreted galaxy has contributed extra GCs to the outer regions ( > 10 arcmin) of NGC 4365.

The galaxy profiles are from Goudfrooij et al. (1994) for NGC 720; Spitler et al. (2012) for NGC 1407; Blom, Spitler Forbes (2012) for NGC 4365; Caon et al. (1994) for NGC 4472 and NGC 4636 and MacDonald et al. (2011) for NGC 4649. Although the galaxy starlight does not extend as far as the GC data (particularly for NGC 720), the GCs and the starlight show similar slopes in their outer regions.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4365 as a giant elliptical whose stream system, globular-cluster counts, effective radius, and group assignment affect SLUGGS survey analysis.

quotes.quote: So far, we have used this approach for stellar streams around two galaxies in the survey, M87 and NGC 4365 Romanowsky12,Blom14 . These data allowed us to identify, for the first time, NGC 4342 as the origin of a stream in the NGC 4365 system.

As a case in point, the giant elliptical NGC 4365 was analyzed with a central ACS pointing supplemented by parallel WFPC2 imaging, and estimated to have 3246 598 GCs by Peng08 . Our subsequent analysis of Suprime-Cam imaging found a lower-limit of 6450 110 GCs Blom12b , which is higher than the previous result by at least 5 .
The ``Virgo'' galaxy NGC 4365 is assigned to a background group, based on SBF distance measurement.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4365 as an interacting system with NGC 4342, showing GC overdensities aligned with tidal features, possible stripped clusters, and unusually many GCs for its luminosity.

quotes.quote: Signs of encounters have also been observed in other galaxies where the MOND models did not work very well. The galaxy NGC 4365 analyzed in this work still interacts with another galaxy, NGC 4342 blom12, blom12b, blom14 . There is an overdensity of GCs between the galaxies, which is aligned to the tidal features in NGC 4365. It was suggested that the GCs in this overdensity were stripped from NGC 4365. This would be supported by the fact that NGC 4365 (=VCC 731) has an unusually large amount of GCs for its luminosity peng06 . The alleged three-peaked color distribution of the GCs in NGC 4365 (e.g., blom12 ) might be a consequence of the presence of GCs coming from NGC 4342 or another galaxy.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 4365 as an old luminous early-type galaxy with a kinematically decoupled core and an unusual trimodal globular cluster color distribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 is a luminous ETG which lies about 6 Mpc behind the Virgo cluster of galaxies. Superficially it looks like a normal ETG, although it has a "kinematically decoupled core" (the galaxy centre of the galaxy rotates in the opposite sense to the main galaxy) sometimes taken to be indicative of past merger events . The galaxy itself, however, appears very old . It turns out that the GC system of NGC 4365 is quite unusual. NGC 4365 has been identified as a galaxy with a "trimodal" colour distribution for its GC system; it has blue, "green" and red GC subpopulations .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4365 as a large Virgo-region elliptical with a kinematically distinct core, longer HST imaging, and a broad globular-cluster color distribution dominated by blue-population colors.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365 Probably located slightly behind the Virgo cluster, this large elliptical contains a kinematically distinct core ben88 which may indicate a past merger event. The first HST study was that of forb96 . From a short exposure, central pointing they detected 328 GCs but no obvious bimodality. Here we use longer exposure images of the galaxy center and a region directly to the north. We find a broad color distribution for 323 clusters brighter than V=24 , although most GCs seem to belong to a peak with the same color as the blue population in most large ellipticals.

sources.summary: The paper reports an intermediate-age globular-cluster population in NGC 4365, hidden from optical colors and diffuse light but confirmed spectroscopically.

quotes.quote: Figure ps2 shows the results of an optical/near-infrared study of two nearby early-type galaxies, NGC 3115 and NGC 4365. Our group detected a significant population of intermediate-age globular clusters in NGC 4365 which could not be detected from optical colours alone. The results were recently confirmed with spectroscopic methods discussed below.

No hint for an intermediate-age stellar population in the diffuse light of NGC 4365 was found so far . We clearly require more near-infrared data on globular cluster systems to quantify the mass fractions of such intermediate-age globular cluster sub-populations.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4365 as a giant elliptical whose globular cluster system contains a substantial intermediate-age population confirmed by deep optical and near-infrared photometry.

quotes.quote: One of the first results to come from the application of the optical to near-infrared technique was the discovery of a substantial population of intermediate age globular clusters in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4365 (Puzia et al. \ 2002, hereafter P02). This result was originally thought to be surprising, in part because spectroscopy of the integrated light of NGC 4365 was originally thought to indicate an old age and thus presented a puzzle. However, recent calculations using alpha-enhanced isochrones necessary for NGC 4365 and its large observed [ Mg/Fe ] give younger ages (Brodie et al. \ 2005, hereafter B05).

Therefore, to pin down the ages and metallicities of the GCs around NGC 4365, we obtained new, very deep NICMOS near-infrared photometry in three fields, and combine this with new ACS data. As shown in Figure 1, these independent and much higher signal-to-noise data confirm the previous result that NGC 4365 has a substantial population of GCs with optical to near-infrared colors that can only be accounted for by intermediate ages of 2-7 Gyr (Kundu et al. \ 2005).

sources.summary: The paper uses VCC 731 as an observed core galaxy whose relatively small core, typical mass deficit, and brightness profile match a lower-kick recoil model.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, VCC 731 (NGC 4365) has a relatively small core and a typical mass deficit of 1 Merritt:06 . For each galaxy, we scale the N body profiles to have the same r_b and (r_b) as the galaxy itself. Figure fig:sbp shows that the brightness profiles of both galaxies can be reasonably well fit by (at least) one of the N body models. In particular, the profile of VCC 1226 is well fit by models with 0.4 550 while VCC 731 is well fit by models with 0.1 110 .

sources.summary: NGC 4365 had been a strong claimed case for intermediate-age globular clusters, but these optical/NIR diagrams find its globular-cluster system is not exceptional among the sample.

quotes.quote: It is still an open issue in the literature whether some early type galaxies known to contain old stellar populations from integrated light studies can host a high percentage of intermediate age (2-3 Gyrs) GCs. The two strongest cases are NGC 4365 ( , ) and NGC 5846 ( ). The comparison of 2-colour plots of the GC systems with simple stellar population (SSP) models (eg. , ) suggested the presence of intermediate age GCs in these galaxies.
If one examines the case of NGC 4365, the galaxy that showed evidence for having intermediate age clusters one concludes that this galaxy is no different from any other in the sample.
NGC 4365 GC system is not found to be an exception, in general all the GC systems in the 2-colour diagrams look very much alike.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4365 as a giant E3 slow-rotator whose apparent kinematically distinct core is explained as part of a single triaxial orbital structure.

quotes.quote: One of these galaxies is NGC 4365, a giant E3 galaxy with a core that appears to rotate around the short axis (Surma Bender 1995; Davies et al. \ 2001), almost perpendicular ( 82 ) to the main body rotation around the long-axis (Fig. 1). The customary interpretation has been that this core is kinematically distinct (a so-called KDC) and a remnant of a last major accretion 12 Gyr ago. However, triaxial Schwarzschild models fitted to the \ stellar kinematics (van den Bosch et al. 2008; Fig. 1), revealed that the KDC is part of the single triaxial structure: short axis tube orbits dominate throughout, but half of them counter-rotate leading to vanishing net rotation around the short-axis, except in the center due to a small in-balance leading to the appearance of a KDC. The intrinsic shape changes from oblate triaxial in the central regions to prolate triaxial in the outer parts where long-axis tube orbits provide the net rotation seen in the observed velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper notes colour trimodality and differing GC subpopulation kinematics in NGC 4365; CaT metallicities show bimodality, but a three-Gaussian CaT model is not significant.

quotes.quote: Using GMM (Section GMM , Table tab:gmm-metal , Figure fig:metalmetal ), we detected CaT metallicity bimodality with a probability of greater than 0.95 in NGC 3115, NGC 4278 and NGC 4365 and greater than 0.90 in NGC 1407, NGC 5846 and NGC 3377.

NGC 4365 shows evidence for colour trimodality 2012MNRAS.420...37B and shows different kinematics for the different GC colour subpopulations Christina2012 so we also ran GMM with three peaks on this galaxy. Although GMM finds peaks at [ Z/H ] = -1.53 , 0.70 and 0.20 which are located at the same places as the colour peaks found by 2012MNRAS.420...37B transformed into metallicity, GMM does not find three Gaussians to be a significant improvement over one ( p = 0.671 ). Due to the smaller numbers and larger errors of the CaT metallicities compared to the colours, it is not surprising that trimodality was not found to be an improvement.

sources.summary: NGC 4365 is described as a giant elliptical with a kinematically decoupled core, minor-axis rolling rotation, and large-scale maps showing that rolling through much of the stellar mass.

quotes.quote: This giant elliptical (E3) galaxy is well known for its kinematically decoupled core rotating along the major axis and otherwise minor axis ``rolling'' rotation Davies:2001bf , which can be explained as a signature of long-axis tube orbits in a triaxial potential Statler:2004,vandenBosch:2008 . Such a configuration could arise through a major merger involving a spiral galaxy, with the central ``normal'' rotating component originating from newly formed stars, and the outer rolling component from the progenitor's cold disk Hoffman:2010dr . Our large-scale combined kinematic maps reveal that the minor-axis rolling continues to large radii and hence involves the bulk of the stellar mass. The galaxy also appears to be in the process of further halo growth via an interaction with NGC 4342 in the same group Blom:2014 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4365 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: It is an E3 galaxy, one of the brightest member of the Virgo cluster. It has a complex kinematic structure with a counter-rotating core 1988A&A...202L...5B , aligned with the photometric major axis, and a prolate rotation of 61 2001ApJ...548L..33D , which indicates a triaxial potential. Our PN velocity field extends to 6 . The inner kinematics ( R 2 ) is compatible with no rotation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4365 as the Virgo W dominant galaxy, a KDC-bearing system with major tidal structures including an S plume, NE loop, and long SW tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 4365, Fig. fig:4365 , is the dominant galaxy of the Virgo W ^ group, located 6 Mpc behind the Virgo Cluster mei07 . The galaxy is long known to have a KDC be88,bs92,bsg94,for95 and has been extensively studied also through the integral field spectroscopy (SAURON - dav01 and a3d2 and MUSE - ned19 ). Several substantial tidal features have been described, see Figure 6 in and Figure 9 in : an asymmetric S Plume; NE Loop around 26 (146 kpc) from the center; and most notably, a long SW Tail with a diffuse shell-like structure at its ending, around 40 (230 kpc) from the center. A companion galaxy, NGC 4342, sits in the middle of the tail.

sources.summary: The paper finds a younger globular-cluster subpopulation in NGC 4365, dated to 2-7 Gyr ago, with photometric ages supported by spectroscopy of selected clusters.

quotes.quote: In NGC 5846 and NGC 4365 colour-colour diagrams (V-I vs. V-K) reveal a sub-population in the globular cluster systems with an age much younger than the expected old populations (13-15 Gyr). A comparison with SSP models dates the formation of this younger population 2-7 Gyr back. Further evidence for multiple star formation events is given by the age distribution in both systems and the comparison to models.
Our photometric age determination was confirmed by spectroscopy for a small sample of the clusters in NGC 4365.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4365 as a controversial Virgo-cluster case whose globular-cluster age-metallicity relation indicates that the sampled clusters are old and formed at redshifts around z 2.

quotes.quote: An interesting, and controversial case, is NGC 4365 in the Virgo cluster. In Fig. 3 we show the age-metallicity relation (AMR) for GCs in this galaxy. Ages and metallicities have been fit using the multi-line method of Proctor, Forbes Beasley (2004) by M. Pierce with indices from Brodie et al. (2005). The AMR shows that our sample GCs are all old, i.e. forming at redshifts z 2.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4365 globular-cluster CaT measurements agree with color predictions overall, while its red-color globular clusters are weaker than predicted from their colors.

quotes.quote: At blue colours the GCs of NGC 1407 and NGC 4278 have weaker CaT values than predicted by Equation eq:CaT_colour . For red colours, NGC 1407, NGC 4278, NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 GCs have weaker CaT strengths than predicted from their colours while NGC 3115, NGC 3377, NGC 4473 and NGC 4494 have stronger than predicted CaT values. The shapes of the colour--CaT relations of NGC 1407 and NGC 4494 are radically different as first noticed by 2011MNRAS.415.3393F . As seen in Figure fig:stacking_CaT1 , at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show bluer colours compared to the other galaxies while at high CaT values NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 also show bluer colours than the other galaxies. Additionally, at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show higher Na82 strengths than the other galaxies.
This fit shows good agreement with the observations in NGC 3115, NGC 4365, NGC 4473 and NGC 4649.

Some galaxies such as NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 show agreement between the measured CaT values and those predicted from colours, while other galaxies show disagreement with the colours, predicting too few CaT weak GCs in NGC 1407 and too few CaT strong GCs in NGC 3377.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4365 as an elliptical with high inner and mid-radius ellipticities whose elongated nuclear stellar cluster likely affects the measured core-Sersic ellipticity.

quotes.quote: The elliptical galaxy NGC 4365, with _ inner 0.30 and < _ mid > 0.26 , has an elongated nuclear stellar cluster (e.g., Carollo et al. \ 1997; Lauer et al. \ 2005; C ot e et al. \ 2006; Dullo Graham 2012). Carollo et al. \ (1997) noted that this nucleus extends from 1 to 3 , compared to this galaxy's core-S ersic break radius R_ b, N4365 =1 .21 (Dullo Graham 2014). Therefore, our measurements of _ inner and < _ mid > for NGC 4365 are likely to be influenced by its nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4365 as the massive elliptical core of the Virgo W group, with a diffuse tidal tail and additional streams indicating cold tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: Lying 5.3 ^ to the SW of the Virgo core (and 7 Mpc behind; Mei \ 2007) is the infalling Virgo W ^ group, with the massive elliptical NGC 4365 at its core. Our deep imaging shows an extended, diffuse tidal tail emanating SW from the galaxy (Bogd an \ 2012; Mihos \ in prep), and GC kinematics clearly link the tail to an interaction with its companion NGC 4342 (Blom \ 2014). The tail contains 1.5 10^9 L , and a number of other streams are visible in NGC 4365's halo as well (including the loop visible to the NE of the galaxy), all indicative of cold tidal stripping in the group environment.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4365's globular clusters follow old-age models overall, while its CaT distribution has a small metal-rich bump linked to intermediate-age populations.

quotes.quote: The GCs in NGC 1407, NGC 4278, and NGC 4365 well follow old age models.

In Figure f9 , the simulated distributions for the GC systems in NGC 4278, NGC 4365, and NGC 3377 concur with the observations (see Table tab.2 ). A small metal-rich bump (at CaT_ F10 7.5 ) in the CaT distribution of NGC 4365 GCs seems related to the intermediate age GC populations reported by 2003ApJ...585..767L, 2005A&A...443..413L and 2012MNRAS.420...37B .

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 4365's globular-cluster ages, finding deep HST near-infrared data support a significant intermediate-age cluster component in this elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: We find that NGC 4365 has a number of globular clusters with bluer optical colors than expected for their red optical to near-infrared colors and an old age. The only known way to explain these colors is with a significant population of intermediate-age (2-8 Gyr) clusters in this elliptical galaxy.

Our new near-IR data of the NGC 4365 GC system with much higher signal-to-noise agrees well with earlier published photometry and both give strong evidence of a significant intermediate-age component.

gmw_00000881.name: NGC 2859
gmw_00000881.overview: NGC 2859 is a double-barred [arXiv:1302.5701, arXiv:1607.00585] lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1304.3529] featuring a rotation-dominated nuclear disk [arXiv:1607.00585] and a younger, more metal-rich inner bar [arXiv:1302.5701]. It is characterized by an outer H I ring structure [arXiv:2001.08087] and a red, dusty central region [arXiv:2001.08087].

sources.summary: NGC 2859 is one of four double-barred galaxies selected for mappable inner bars; its kinematics indicate an inner stellar disc, and its inner bar is younger and more metal-rich.

quotes.quote: The sample is composed of four double-barred galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725, and NGC 5850. These galaxies were selected from the catalogue of Erwin2004 , paying special attention to the length and size of the inner bars. The main requirement for building the sample was that the inner bars were big enough to be well mapped within the \ field-of-view (hereafter FoV), but they were also small enough to allow the mapping of the transition regions between the two bars.

However, for the three cases highlighted in Figure fig:discs (NGC 2859, NGC 4725, and NGC 5850), local velocity maxima and minima appear between 5 and 10 arcseconds of the galaxy centre, aligned also with the kinematical major axis. These decoupled structures are rotating faster than their surroundings and are probably indicating the presence of stellar inner discs. This hypothesis is supported by the h _3 maps also included in Figure fig:discs , which show a clear anticorrelation with respect to the stellar velocities exactly at the locations of those features, as expected in the case of discs BureauandAthanassoula2005 .
The main result of this work is the distinct stellar populations found for the inner bars of the four galaxies: they are clearly younger and more metal-rich than the outer bars.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2859 with a simulated double-barred galaxy, emphasizing confirmed inner bars, humps, ring-like features, a nuclear disk, and positive h4 structures.

quotes.quote: From top to bottom, they are NGC 2859 , NGC 3941 , and NGC 2950 , followed by the best matching model time step. Their inner bars are well confirmed by ellipse fitting and unsharp mask on their infrared images. Based on the observed ellipticity of the outer disks, their inclination angles are i 33^ , 58^ , and 62^ , respectively, which are obtained from HyperLeda .

For NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 , humps are accompanied by moderate ring/spiral-like features. NGC 3384 also exhibits humps, consistent with the existence of an inner bar that is parallel to the LON. Relatively lower at the ends of the inner bar can partially explain the hollows found by de_etal_08 . We have confirmed that the initial disk does not exhibit such humps/hollows, and the humps develop with the S2B formation. The qualitative agreement of high-order Gauss-Hermite moments is particularly impressive for NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 , especially the positive h_4 rings appearing over the projected regions of the inner bar.

It is clear that both NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 also have a rotation-dominated nuclear disk within 10 arcsec, in addition to the inner bar. As shown in the bottom panels of fig:vsigma , without any dynamically cold nuclear disk, the central region of the S2B model is significantly dominated by velocity dispersion, |v_ dp |/ 0.7 . Thus in NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 the central features of v and h_3 are more likely to be dominated by the nuclear disk.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2859 among six gas-rich ETGs with enormous H I ring or disc structures, red dusty inner regions, blue outer H I rings, and is described with the group as CO-poor.

quotes.quote: We note that six rich galaxies (NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 3945, NGC 4036, NGC 4278, and NGC 5582) in our sample have enormous \ ring or disc structures, while they have no \ in their very central regions (red circles in Fig. fig:derivedH ). While these galaxies show very red colours in the inner regions due to dust, they show blue colours in the outer regions where the highest \ column density rings are located 2017MNRAS.464..329Y . We would like to point out that these galaxies are also CO-poor with the exception of NGC 4278. Therefore, we conclude that the presence of a large \ disc or ring in an ETG indicates a dust structure in the centre, even if there is no \ in central regions. It would be interesting to continue to study these objects in future studies.

sources.summary: The paper presents integral-field kinematics for NGC 2859, finding inner-bar velocity-dispersion hollows explained as contrast between the hot bulge and the colder, more ordered inner bar.

quotes.quote: We present \ integral-field stellar velocity and velocity dispersion maps for four double-barred early-type galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725 and NGC 5850. The presence of the inner bar does not produce major changes in the line-of-sight velocity, but it appears to have an important effect in the stellar velocity dispersion maps: we find two hollows of amplitudes between 10 and 40 km s ^ 1 on either side of the center, at the ends of the inner bars. We have performed numerical simulations to explain these features. Ruling out other possibilities, we conclude that the hollows are an effect of the contrast between two kinematically different components: the high velocity dispersion of the bulge and the more ordered motion (low velocity dispersion) of the inner bar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2859 as having a very broad A_2 profile caused by a weak bar plus prominent oval, with a secondary bar at small radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 2859 has a very broad A_2 amplitude profile, but in this case the higher Fourier modes appear only at fairly small radial distances ( < 40''). The Q_T profile is extremely shallow showing only a modest peak at the radial distance where the higher Fourier modes are present ( 40''). Our conclusion is that the A_2 profile is broad because the galaxy has a weak bar and a prominent oval extending outside the bar, a conclusion made also by Buta et al. (2006) for this galaxy. The small peak in the A_2 amplitude profile at < 10'', visible also in the surface brightness profile and in the Q_T profile, corresponds to a secondary bar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2859 as an extreme outer-ring Type II.o-OLR system with a non-exponential inter-ring profile and updated disk-orientation parameters based on SDSS imaging.

quotes.quote: This is one of three galaxies with ``extreme outer-ring'' profiles; the profile between the end of the bar and the outer ring is in this case clearly not exponential, and so we do not attempt to fit it. The disk orientation parameters presented here are updated slightly from those in erwin03 and erwin05 , and are based on the SDSS image. laurikainen05 suggest a somewhat higher inclination (outer-disk ellipticity = 0.24 instead of 0.13) than we use, based on their deep B band image; however, the profile derived using their orientation parameters is very similar to the profile we present here.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2859 with NGC 3351, describing it as a late S0 with a prominent bulge, weaker bar, and possible ansae-bar evolution.

quotes.quote: ngc3351-2859 shows two morphologically similar galaxies that differ in a few ways: one (NGC 3351) is a clear intermediate-type spiral while the other (NGC 2859) is a `late' S0; the spiral has very little bulge while the S0 has a more prominent bulge; the bar in the S0 looks weaker than that in the spiral, while the rings in the spiral are better described as pseudorings compared to what is seen in the S0. Looking at these two galaxies, one might wonder if a galaxy like NGC 2859 might be a possible end-product of some long-term evolutionary process in a galaxy like NGC 3351.

We can ask: are ansae bars merely a distinct type of bar, related to some aspect of bar formation, or is there a process that changes a normal bar (e.g., as in NGC 4608) into an ansae bar (e.g., as in NGC 2859)?

gmw_00000882.name: NGC 2950
gmw_00000882.overview: NGC 2950 is a lenticular, double-barred galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0211105, arXiv:0711.0966] containing a pseudobulge [arXiv:1004.5393] and a large-scale outer bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271, arXiv:astro-ph/0211105]. The galaxy is surrounded by a satellite population of nine secure and four possible dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1808.03410].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2950 as a well-resolved double-barred galaxy whose humps, twists, nuclear disk signatures, and h4 rings qualitatively match the simulation.

quotes.quote: From top to bottom, they are NGC 2859 , NGC 3941 , and NGC 2950 , followed by the best matching model time step. Their inner bars are well confirmed by ellipse fitting and unsharp mask on their infrared images. Based on the observed ellipticity of the outer disks, their inclination angles are i 33^ , 58^ , and 62^ , respectively, which are obtained from HyperLeda .

For NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 , humps are accompanied by moderate ring/spiral-like features. NGC 3384 also exhibits humps, consistent with the existence of an inner bar that is parallel to the LON. Relatively lower at the ends of the inner bar can partially explain the hollows found by de_etal_08 . We have confirmed that the initial disk does not exhibit such humps/hollows, and the humps develop with the S2B formation. The qualitative agreement of high-order Gauss-Hermite moments is particularly impressive for NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 , especially the positive h_4 rings appearing over the projected regions of the inner bar.

For NGC 2859 , and NGC 2950 , significant twists also appear at positions quite far from the end of the inner bar (highlighted with white curves), which is consistent with the S2B model. It is also worth noticing that the twists can be significantly asymmetric with respect to the LON. For example, the asymmetric twists in NGC 2950 can be clearly seen along the white isovelocity contours. On the upper side of the LON, the top-left arm is slightly distorted, while, on the lower side, the bottom-left arm has a nearly 90^ twist. The S2B model gives twists very consistent with those in NGC 2950 .

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 2950 has a large-scale bar and inner isophotal twist, but its stellar velocity field remains aligned with circular rotation rather than a secondary bar.

quotes.quote: The large-scale bar ( 30-40'' in size) is seen over the distributions of the m=2 Fourier harmonic and has a turn of isophotal PA accompanied by an ellipticity peak (Fig. 2950m ). Wozniak et al. ( ) and Friedli et al. ( ) explained the inner PA turn, and the peak, at <6'' , as a secondary bar.

Surprisingly, this ``bar'' does not distort the circular rotation of stars in the circumnuclear region. The of the stellar velocity field coincides with PA_0 of the line of nodes. We do not detect any non-circular motions in this velocity field despite the strong turn of isophotes at these radii.

sources.summary: NGC 2950 is described as a lenticular double-barred galaxy whose isophotal twist does not strongly affect stellar velocities, though its outer bar broadens the velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: We determined the isophotal orientation in NGC 2950 and NGC 3786 using the HST images obtained with the WFPC2 camera through a F814W filter and with the NICMOS 1 camera through a F160W filter, respectively. The R-band image of NGC 5850 was taken from the digital atlas by Frei et al. (1996). In NGC 2950, the isophotal orientation changes by more than 50^ (Fig. 5a), but PA_ dyn for the stellar component is virtually aligned with the line of nodes. Neither the outer bar nor the inner bar have an appreciable effect on the stellar velocity field.

This is probably because here, both stellar motions within the bar and the rotation of the bulge, whose contribution in this lenticular galaxy must be significant, are observed along the line-of-sight. Since the spectral resolution was too low to separate the two dynamical components in the line-of-sight velocity distribution (LOSVD), the mean velocity field corresponds to circular rotation. As was noted in Subsect. 3.1, the stellar component associated with the outer bar in NGC 2950 shows up in the distribution of the velocity dispersion, whose increase points to a broadening of the LOSVD in the bar region.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2950 as a prototypical double-barred early-type galaxy, using it to test whether Tremaine-Weinberg measurements imply a counter-rotating secondary bar.

quotes.quote: Observationally, the only direct kinematic constraint on s , based on the Tremaine-Weinberg tre_wei_84 method, was obtained for NGC 2950 by CDA03, who showed that the primary and nuclear bars cannot be rotating at the same rate. They further suggested that the nuclear bar of NGC 2950 is either rotating faster or counter-rotating with respect to the primary bar. hereafter M06 maciej_06 , more emphatically, argued that NGC 2950 has to be counter-rotating with respect to the primary. This would raise the prospect that either NGC 2950 is atypical or that counter-rotating (or possibly librating) double bars are common.

Comparison with NGC 2950 Although we have shown that the TW method as used by CDA03 is not very accurate for secondary bars, it is not so grossly unreliable that we cannot consider the question of whether the secondary bar in NGC 2950 is counter-rotating. If it were, this would suggest a formation scenario for double-barred galaxies different from the one presented here.

sources.summary: The paper focuses HSC pilot analysis on N2950 as a Milky-Way-mass target, identifying 9 secure and 4 possible dwarfs; its satellite luminosity function is broadly Milky-Way-like but richer at the faint end.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we focus on two of the observed targets, N2950 and N3245. Their physical properties are summarized in Table tab:phys_props .

In total, we identify 9 secure dwarfs and 4 possible dwarfs around N2950. N3245 has 13 secure and 2 possible dwarfs. These are raw counts from the observation and we apply the completeness correction described below when we discuss LFs in Section sec:luminosity_function .
We find that the MW LF is broadly consistent with N2950, although N2950 hosts a larger number of satellites at the faintest magnitudes probed.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2950 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge shows a nuclear bar, but otherwise shows little spiral structure; n_b=1.3 , and the bulge is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.3 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 2950 RLBR0../RSB0 Pseudobulge. The bulge shows a nuclear bar, but otherwise shows little spiral structure. The decomposition yields n_b=1.3 , and the bulge is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.3 mag.

gmw_00000883.name: NGC 3941
gmw_00000883.overview: NGC 3941 is a barred S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1605.01638] featuring a complex inner structure with a weak inner bar [arXiv:1607.00585, arXiv:1901.08881] and a triaxial or oblate bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/9905117]. It hosts a well-resolved neutral hydrogen ring [arXiv:1509.05236] and a settled gas disk that counter-rotates relative to its stars [arXiv:1302.5701, arXiv:1605.01638].

sources.summary: For NGC 3941, the paper adopts a corrected inner-bar position angle, finds flat main-bar stellar-population gradients, and reports gas counter-rotating with the stellar component.

quotes.quote: Columns (4), (5), (6), and (7) are taken from Erwin2004 except for the PA of the inner bar of NGC 3941, which is taken from ErwinandSparke2003 . Note: there is an erratum in Erwin2004 so that the position angle of the inner bar of NGC 3941 is given as 85 ^ , which is not correct. ErwinandSparke2003 provide a value between 20 ^ and 35 ^ ; taking into account the alignment of the hollows (see Section sec:hollows ), we finally assume a position angle of 30 ^ for NGC 3941, in agreement with the measurements of ErwinandSparke2003 .

Figure fig:radial shows that three out of the four double-barred galaxies present positive age gradients and negative metallicity gradients going outwards. The exception to this behaviour is illustrated by NGC 3941, which shows flat gradients in age, metallicity and abundance ratio in the regions dominated by the main bar.

One out of the four galaxies, NGC 3941, contains gas which is counter-rotating with respect to the stellar component, whereas NGC 5850 is the only galaxy which shows differences between the [ OIII ] 5007 and H gas distributions. The main result of this work is the distinct stellar populations found for the inner bars of the four galaxies: they are clearly younger and more metal-rich than the outer bars. Moreover, we find positive age and negative metallicity gradients along the inner and outer bars for all the galaxies except NGC 3941, which only shows the gradients inside the inner bar region, whereas the profiles along the outer bars are flat.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3941 among observed double-barred galaxies, noting a weak inner bar, less significant humps and twists, and weaker high-order moment structure.

quotes.quote: From top to bottom, they are NGC 2859 , NGC 3941 , and NGC 2950 , followed by the best matching model time step. Their inner bars are well confirmed by ellipse fitting and unsharp mask on their infrared images. Based on the observed ellipticity of the outer disks, their inclination angles are i 33^ , 58^ , and 62^ , respectively, which are obtained from HyperLeda .
This likely explains the less clear structure in the h_3 and h_4 maps of NGC 3941 .

NGC 2859 and NGC 3941 were included in the sample of de_etal_08 . For NGC 2859 , the model also exhibits a similar moderate ring feature around the humps, as in the observation, which causes significant hollows appearing at the ends of the inner bar. The humps in NGC 3941 are not as significant as in the model, probably because the inner bar in NGC 3941 is much weaker than in the model, with rounder isodensity contours and a smoother v field.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 3941 in radio bands, treats it as a marginal C-band detection, and derives a steep spectral slope from two frequency points.

quotes.quote: Initially, we detected NGC 3185 and NGC 3941 only in the L-Band (at 1.5 GHz), with un-tapered maps exhibiting diffuse radio emission (see Figs. fig:contourMaps3185 and fig:contourMaps3941 ). In order to further investigate this issue, the C, X and Ku datasets of these sources have been re-imaged with a uv-taper equal to the L-band clean beam size (via the parameter uvtaper with sub-parameter outertaper = [ '1.21arcsec','1.15arcsec','-74.63deg' ] in tclean ), applied to match their respective L-band observation. This has been done in order to be more sensitive to the extended radio emission. Then, the final uv-tapered images have been smoothed to the final resolution of the L-band beam (via the restoringbeam parameter of the casa task tclean ). In the case of NGC 3941, which is fainter with respect to NGC 3185, we used a briggs weighting with robust parameter equal to 2: this value ensures that the weighting is close to a natural weighting. Natural weighting guarantees a better surface brightness sensitivity but degraded resolution, and for this reason it is more appropriate when considering diffuse emission. We have thus recovered radio emission in C band for NGC 3185 and NGC 3941 at a level above 5 , and radio emission in NGC 3185 in X band at a level 3.5 (images are shown in Figs. fig:contourMaps3185 and fig:contourMaps3941 ).

We computed the spectral index also for NGC 3941 with the only two frequency points at 1.4 and 5 GHz, and we found a spectral slope of +1.52 0.33, in which the uncertainty in the slope has been estimated as ( _ f_1 /S_ f_1 ) ^2 + ( _ f_2 /S_ f_2 )^2 /ln( f_2/f_1 ) , where _ f_ 1,2 and S_ f_ 1,2 are the uncertainties on the flux density and the flux density at the two frequencies HU01 .

The other detected sources exhibit different spectra: NGC 4477 has been found to fit an intermediate spectral index between flat and steep (0.52 0.09), NGC 3941 has a very steep ( = +1.52 0.33 ) spectrum, both compatible with optically-thin synchrotron emission.

sources.summary: Near-infrared photometry shows NGC 3941 has a triaxial or possibly oblate bulge and an unusually blue nucleus, possibly from young stars formed by bar-driven gas inflow.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the bulge morphology of two barred galaxies NGC 3412 and NGC 3941 by near-infrared photometry. The present photometry shows elongated bulges of both of the galaxies with a mean ellipticity of 0.2 . There are misalignments between the major axes of the bulges and bars, indicating that their bulges are triaxial. However, as noted by Ann (1995), there is a possibility of an oblate spheroid for the bulge of NGC 3941 because the position angle of the bulge is similar to that of the disk.

The color profile of NGC 3941 is very unusual in the sense that it shows a very blue nucleus with a steep gradient inside 8^ with the largest gradient along the major-axis profile. However, if the blue nucleus of NGC 3941 is real, it might be caused by the young stellar populations which are formed by the gas driven by the bar of NGC 3941.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3941 as a worked example, describing a well-resolved H I ring and deriving a tilted-ring inclination of 57 deg and circular velocity of 148 km/s.

quotes.quote: We illustrate this technique showing its application to the velocity field of NGC 3941. This galaxy hosts an \ ring which is well resolved, as shown in Fig. n3941nhi . The best-fitting parameters resulting from a tilted-ring fit of the outer rings are i=57 deg and v_ circ =148 km s ^ 1 . Figure n3941_c3 shows the value of c_3 as a function of inclination offset from the best fitting inclination of 57 deg and has, of course, its minimum at the best-fitting inclination. The task reswri returns an error bar _3 on c_3 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3941 as a barred S0 galaxy with counterrotating settled gas, complex inner structure, central HI absence, and a problematic rotation curve decomposition.

quotes.quote: NGC 3941 NGC 3941 is a barred S0 galaxy with a known counterrotating gas disc relative to the stellar rotation ( S0kin ). The low velocity dispersion of this gas disc as measured by implies it has settled into an equilibrium distribution despite having a likely origin due to a merger event or accretion of gas with oppositely directed angular momentum. Indeed, the neutral gas appears to have settled in a disc, although it is not a cohesive structure (Fig.  fig:n3941sum ). NGC 3941 is known to have a complex inner structure involving an inner disc with spiral arms and bar with an oval distortion ( S0phot ).
