There is no HI detected in the central 40 arcsec, which is a common feature of barred galaxies in this sample.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3941 among six gas-rich ETGs with enormous H I ring or disc structures, red dusty inner regions, blue outer H I rings, and is described with the group as CO-poor.

quotes.quote: We note that six rich galaxies (NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 3945, NGC 4036, NGC 4278, and NGC 5582) in our sample have enormous \ ring or disc structures, while they have no \ in their very central regions (red circles in Fig. fig:derivedH ). While these galaxies show very red colours in the inner regions due to dust, they show blue colours in the outer regions where the highest \ column density rings are located 2017MNRAS.464..329Y . We would like to point out that these galaxies are also CO-poor with the exception of NGC 4278. Therefore, we conclude that the presence of a large \ disc or ring in an ETG indicates a dust structure in the centre, even if there is no \ in central regions. It would be interesting to continue to study these objects in future studies.

sources.summary: NGC 3941 is used as an SG-type bar example whose Fourier and torque profiles align, with a primary bar, inner disk, bulge, disk, and bar decomposition.

quotes.quote: NGC 3941 is a typical example of a galaxy with an SG-type bar, whereas NGC 4245, NGC 2859 and NGC 1452 have DG-type bars. For NGC 3941 the maxima in the A_2 , A_4 and A_6 amplitude profiles appear at the same radial distance, a behavior which is characteristic for SG-type bars. However, in some other SG-type bars like NGC 1440, the lower modes are shifted towards slightly smaller radial distances. NGC 3941 has two amplitude maxima in the A_2 profile, the main maximum belonging to the primary bar, and the lower maximum (at < 5'') to an inner disk. The radial Q_T profile follows the A_2 amplitude profile so that the two Q_T maxima appear at the same radial distances as the two amplitude maxima. The surface brightness profile of this galaxy is well fitted when including a bulge, a disk and a bar in the fit.

sources.summary: The paper presents integral-field kinematics for NGC 3941, finding inner-bar velocity-dispersion hollows explained as contrast between the hot bulge and the colder, more ordered inner bar.

quotes.quote: We present \ integral-field stellar velocity and velocity dispersion maps for four double-barred early-type galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725 and NGC 5850. The presence of the inner bar does not produce major changes in the line-of-sight velocity, but it appears to have an important effect in the stellar velocity dispersion maps: we find two hollows of amplitudes between 10 and 40 km s ^ 1 on either side of the center, at the ends of the inner bars. We have performed numerical simulations to explain these features. Ruling out other possibilities, we conclude that the hollows are an effect of the contrast between two kinematically different components: the high velocity dispersion of the bulge and the more ordered motion (low velocity dispersion) of the inner bar.

sources.summary: NGC 3941 has the sample's highest inner-to-outer bar length ratio; the paper argues decomposition gives a similar inner-bar size but shorter outer bar radius than ellipse fitting.

quotes.quote: We note here that the highest bar length ratio in Fig. fig:lengthvsht corresponds to NGC 3941 ( a_ IB /a_ OB =0.49). For this galaxy, Erwin2004 measures a deprojected bar length ratio of 0.2 from ellipse fitting. When our bar full lengths are translated into effective radii, we obtain a similar inner bar size (as expected) but a shorter outer bar radius than from ellipse fitting, thus resulting in a still high R_ e IB /R_ e OB = 0.43 for this galaxy.

gmw_00000884.name: NGC 3945
gmw_00000884.overview: NGC 3945 is a double-barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1410.4339, arXiv:astro-ph/9906262] hosting a LINER nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9906262] and a strong nuclear disk [arXiv:1607.00585]. The galaxy features a central ionized-gas disk that counter-rotates relative to its stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271, arXiv:astro-ph/0601589], which likely resulted from the accretion of an external gas cloud [arXiv:astro-ph/0601589, arXiv:astro-ph/0211105].

sources.summary: NGC 3945 is discussed as a double-barred S0 whose nuclear disc, stellar nuclear ring, secondary bar, and large ND mass fraction are compared directly with the simulation.

quotes.quote: Comparison to NGC 3945 NGC 3945 is a double-barred S0 galaxy at a distance of approximately 19.8 Mpc (based on the Virgocentric-corrected HyperLeda redshift and a Hubble constant of 72 kpc ^ 1 ). Its orientation is very similar to that of NGC 4371 (bar PA = 88 , inclination = 55 ). Unlike NGC 4371, NGC 3945 also has a small nuclear bar erwin03 , which our model does not. The primary bar in NGC 3945 is aligned almost along the minor axis of the projected disc; the outer isophotes have an ellipticity of 0.41 , indicating an inclination of i 55 .

Interior to the bar (which has its maximum ellipticity at a 3.1 kpc, corresponding to a deprojected semi-major axis of 5.3 kpc), NGC 3945 shows a strong ellipticity peak ( a 1 kpc) due to its ND This is the same structure which was termed an ``inner disc'' by erwin03 and erwin04 . . As shown by erwin99 , there is a stellar nuclear ring within this disc, which produces the slight shoulder in the ellipticity profile at a 600 pc. Further inside is a secondary bar oriented almost parallel to the primary bar and thus close to the galaxy minor axis; the projection of this produces the ellipticity minimum at a 250 pc; a small classical-bulge component dominates the isophotes at a 100 pc erwin03, Erwin2014b . Erwin2014b estimate a ND mass of 2.7 10^ 10 \ for NGC 3945, or 36 per cent of the total stellar mass, larger than the 29 per cent we estimate for the model at 10 Gyr using the same method.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3945 as having disk-like stellar kinematics in the circumnuclear disk and a counter-rotating ionized-gas disk inside about 5-6 arcseconds.

quotes.quote: The velocity field of the stars in the circumnuclear disk shows a regular circular rotation ( coincides with the PA_0 ). That the kinematics is disk-like agrees with the conclusion by Erwin et al. ( ) that the inner disk is more luminous (and massive) than the bulge in this region. Also we find no any non-circular motions of the stars due to a possible secondary bar at <3'' suggested by Erwin Sparke ( ).

First of all, we confidently detect the [ NII ] line emission up to a distance of 10'' from the center. Secondly, inside the radius of 6'' (about 0.5 kpc), the line-of-sight velocities of the gas are inverted with respect to the stellar velocities. Thus, a counter-rotating gaseous disk is located at <5-6'' . The direction of gas rotation becomes coterminous with the stellar rotation at larger distances from the nucleus (see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3945 as a lenticular galaxy with a circumnuclear ionized-gas disk counter-rotating against the stars, likely from an accreted gaseous cloud.

quotes.quote: The characteristic case is a lenticular galaxy NGC 3945 (Fig.  mois:fig2 a). The velocity field of the stars in the circumnuclear disk shows the normal circular rotation. For the gas velocity field the situation is more complicated. In <6'' (0.5 kpc), the line-of-sight velocities of ionized gas are in the maximum amplitude to those of stars but opposite in sign. On larger distances the direction of rotation of gas changes abruptly and coincides with the stellar component rotation.

The large-scale FPI velocity field confirms the fact of normal gas rotation at large radii, up to 11 kpc. Therefore, we have detected a circumnuclear disk of ionized gas rotating in the opposite direction with respect to the stellar component, . This is probably attributable to a merger of an accreted gaseous cloud with the corresponding direction of an angular momentum .

sources.summary: The paper revises NGC 3945 from a possible triple-barred galaxy to a likely double-barred system with rings, an axisymmetric inner structure, and a LINER nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3945, Figure 2: Interpretation of structures in this galaxy depends strongly on what inclination is assumed. suggested that i = 26 , and that the galaxy is triply barred. For two reasons, we feel that an inclination of 50 is more likely, and that their secondary bar is probably an axisymmetric structure. The primary bar ( a 40 ) is surrounded by outer and inner rings (2d, g); both appear perpendicular to the bar. Inner rings are almost always elongated parallel to the bar ( buta86 Buta 1986, 1995).

At HST resolution, NGC 2681 proves to have three distinct bars; NGC 3945 has two. The relative orientation of the nested bars appears random, as expected if the components rotate independently, and in line with the findings of . Both galaxies with multiple bars have LINER nuclei, while NGC 4371 does not; this is weak evidence in favor of shlosman Shlosman et al.'s (1989) thesis that inner bars can enhance nuclear fuelling (but see ).

sources.summary: NGC 3945 is identified as a kinematically peculiar S0 with central gas counter-rotating relative to its stars, then changing sense at larger radii to align with stellar rotation.

quotes.quote: We detected various types of non-circular motions of ionized gas: radial flows within large-scale bars, counter-rotation of the gas and stars at the center of NGC 3945, a polar gaseous disk in NGC 5850, etc.

The most peculiar galaxies (in terms of the gas kinematics) are NGC 3945 and NGC 5850. In the NGC 3945 the gas line-of-sight velocities within 6'' (0.5 kpc) are close in maximum amplitude to the stellar velocities (80 and 120 , respectively) but are opposite in signAt large distances, the sense of rotation of the gas changes sharply and virtually coincides with that of the stars (Fig. 3h,i). The latter fact is also confirmed by the line-of-sight velocity measurements of the ionized gas with the IFP in several star-forming regions in the outer ring structure at distances 120-140'' (10-11 kpc) from the center. It should be noted that such counter-rotation of the gas and stars is occasionally observed in early-type galaxies; it is commonly attributed to the absorption of an outer gas cloud (Bertola et al., 1992; Kuijken et al., 1996).

Against this background, the gas counter-rotation at the center of NGC 3945, one of the four S0 galaxies in our sample (table 1) with gaseous disks observed in three of them (NGC 2681, NGC 3945, and NGC 7743), comes as no surprise.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3945 among six gas-rich ETGs with enormous H I ring or disc structures, red dusty inner regions, blue outer H I rings, and is described with the group as CO-poor.

quotes.quote: We note that six rich galaxies (NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 3945, NGC 4036, NGC 4278, and NGC 5582) in our sample have enormous \ ring or disc structures, while they have no \ in their very central regions (red circles in Fig. fig:derivedH ). While these galaxies show very red colours in the inner regions due to dust, they show blue colours in the outer regions where the highest \ column density rings are located 2017MNRAS.464..329Y . We would like to point out that these galaxies are also CO-poor with the exception of NGC 4278. Therefore, we conclude that the presence of a large \ disc or ring in an ETG indicates a dust structure in the centre, even if there is no \ in central regions. It would be interesting to continue to study these objects in future studies.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 3945 with the double-barred model, describing it as dominated by a strong nuclear disk that masks clear inner-bar kinematic signatures.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3945 , as mentioned above, the inner regions are dominated by a large nuclear disk which significantly affects the kinematic properties erw_etal_03, col_etal_14 , leaving no clear kinematic signatures of the inner bar.

As shown in fig:Atlas3Dno , NGC 3945 is a prototypical example of the kinematics of a strong nuclear disk. col_etal_14 compared this galaxy extensively with their N body+gas simulation. Within the projected nuclear disk, the reduced value in NGC 3945 is quite flat except for the very central regions, which is consistent with the N body+gas simulation described in col_etal_14 .

sources.summary: The review compares decompositions of NGC 3945 and NGC 2787, emphasizing small central bulge masses and large inner-disk or barlens components.

quotes.quote: We can compare the decompositions by lauri2014 with those obtained by erwin2003 for NGC 2787 and NGC 3945. Using a completely different decomposition approach they ended up with similar small relative masses for the central bulges in these two galaxies, just different names were used for the bulges. What is a barlens in lauri2014 , is called as an ``inner disk'' by Erwin et al., which disks are a magnitude larger than the central classical bulges, manifested as peaks in the surface brightness profiles.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 3945 as Bubble because its H-alpha image shows filamentary emission, while comparing that structure with known bars, pseudo-bulge, lens, and nuclear-disc features.

quotes.quote: NGC 3945 (see Fig. Figure_AB4 ): In our H image we see clear hints of filamentary emission (up to 15 ), which we have classified as Bubble . This emission could be co-spatial with some internal features mentioned in previous works such as a pseudo-bulge R 4 Kormendy1979 , two bars R 2-3 Erwin1999 or a lens which is coincident with the larger bar R 26 Dullo2016 . identified the barlens for the first time oriented along the minor axis of the galaxy, however stated that instead that feature resembles to be associated to a larger inner/nuclear disc.

gmw_00000885.name: WFI 2033-4723
gmw_00000885.overview: WFI 2033-4723 is a quadruply lensed quasar at a redshift of 1.66 [arXiv:1610.03732, arXiv:2009.01277] that exhibits brightness variations of approximately 0.5 magnitudes [arXiv:1610.03732]. Its host galaxy features faint optical arcs [arXiv:2009.01277], clumpy extended dust emission [arXiv:2009.01277], and CO gas [arXiv:2009.01277].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WFI 2033 4723 as a fold lens with a strong BC time-delay anomaly, possibly affected by the group environment of its main lens.

quotes.quote: We also find evidence for time delay anomalies in larger-separation image pairs in the fold lenses, B1608+656 and WFI 2033 4723, and the cusp lens RX J0911+0551.

For the fold lens 2033, Vuissoz-2033-tdel report the time delays between images B and C and between B and the combination of the close images A1 and A2. The time delay between A1 and A2 was too small to be measured; it is expected to be short enough that we can assume A1 and A2 have effectively the same light travel time for the purpose of determining the time delays for the image pairs A1C and BA2. Among the distant image pairs, the BC pair has the largest separation and also a strong time delay anomaly, while the two pairs with smaller distances are not anomalous.

The time delay in BC may be affected by the group of galaxies of which the main lens is a member 2026-2033-discovery,Vuissoz-2033-tdel . We find two lines of evidence supporting this hypothesis. First, the time delays become more anomalous as the image separation increases. Second, our predicted time delays are longer than the observed time delays; adding environmental effects to the lens model would generally reduce the predicted time delays.

sources.summary: The paper lens-corrects WFI 2033's nearby galaxy location and finds its posterior favors CDM-like mass functions with many lens-plane subhalos and visible ellipticity covariance.

quotes.quote: In the cases of HE0435 and WFI 2033, the nearby galaxy lies at a higher redshift than the main lens plane. The light from the galaxy is therefore subject to lensing by the main deflector, and its true physical location differs from its observed position. We estimate the true physical locations of these objects by sampling the macromodel parameter space using the image positions as constraints, and read out the physical position of background satellite given its observed (lensed) position. We then place the satellite at this derived physical location in the forward model sampling with uncertainties of 0.05 arcseconds.

The system WFI 2033 is the opposite, with a posterior favoring CDM-like mass functions with many lens plane subhalos. The system RX J0911 lies somewhere in between, with a peak in the posterior distribution of near 10^ 7 . For each of these systems, in particular WFI 2033, there is a visibly obvious covariance between the overall normalization of the main deflector mass profile b_ macro b_ macro has units of convergence, or projected mass density divided by the critical surface mass density for lensing. , and the parameters _ sub and . This covariance is readily understood: To reproduce the observed image positions, the macromodel responds to the addition of mass in the form of subhalos in main lens plane by decreasing the overall normalization of the main deflector mass profile, and hence these quantities are anti-correlated. Similarly, WDM models correspond to macromodels with larger b_ macro because WDM realizations contain fewer subhalos. Interestingly, there is some structure in the posterior distribution for the lens ellipticity in WFI 2033, and both and _ sub .

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs WFI J2033 4723's lensed quasar host, detecting clumpy extended dust, faint host-galaxy arcs, and CO emission whose gas is no larger than the dust continuum.

quotes.quote: WFI J2033 4723 WFI J2033 4723 is a quasar at z_ s =1.66 that is lensed by a galaxy at z_ l =0.66 Morgan:2004 into four images in a typical fold configuration. The lack of detection in the SUMSS suggests a radio flux density of <12 mJy at 1.4 GHz. No other radio observations are available for this lens system. The target was observed with ALMA at 341 GHz as part of our programme, which also observed the CO (8--7) emission line from the lensed quasar. The ALMA observations detect thermal dust emission with a total flux density of 10.4 mJy and achieve an rms noise of 30 Jy beam ^ 1 with natural weighting of the visibilities.

Imaging of WFI J2033 4723 shows a ring of extended, clumpy dust emission and compact emission from the approximate location of the quasar images (see Fig. fig:2033_images ). Arcs of faint emission from the host galaxy have also been observed at optical wavelengths, similar to the extended dust emission observed here Rusu:2020 .

sources.summary: The paper models WFI 2033-4723 as a quad lens, using multiple model families to constrain the mass structure of its lensing galaxy and environment.

quotes.quote: In this paper we model WFI 2033-4723 ( hereafter referred to as WFI2033 ), a quad lens first reported by . Our motivation for choosing it is that it is one of six systems, and its quasar point images and lens light center are poorly fit by power law and composite models rusu2020h0licow . The recent galaxy-lens modeling literature is heavily dominated by the goal of measuring H_0 . In this paper we are interested in placing constraints on the galaxy mass distribution. We elaborate on our choice of constraints in Section sec:data , and in Section sec:models we describe our fitting procedure, which generates models that fit quasar image data to observational precision.

The paper's goal is to determine what can currently be said about the structure of the lens galaxy in . Two different aspects of this goal are addressed in Sections sec:constraints and sec:comparison .

sources.summary: The paper presents V- and R-band monitoring of the quadruply imaged quasar WFI 2033-4723, reporting its redshift, lensing geometry, observing campaign, and roughly 0.5 mag brightness variations.

quotes.quote: We present V and R photometry of the gravitationally lensed quasars WFI 2033-4723 and HE 0047-1756. The data were taken by the MiNDSTEp collaboration with the 1.54 m Danish telescope at the ESO La Silla observatory from 2008 to 2012. Differential photometry has been carried out using the image subtraction method as implemented in the HOTPAnTS package, additionally using GALFIT for quasar photometry. The quasar WFI 2033-4723 showed brightness variations of order 0.5 mag in V and R during the campaign.

The quadruply imaged quasar WFI 2033-4723 , see Fig. fig:wfi2033_stamps , was discovered by ; the four lensed images at redshift z_Q=1.66 show a maximum angular separation of 2.53'' . found that the lens galaxy spectrum is consistent with an elliptical or S0 template at redshift z_L=0.661 0.001 .

sources.summary: The paper presents WFIJ2033 472 as a quadruply lensed quasar whose lens spectrum matches mixed Sb/Sc templates at z=0.658, but likely indicates a massive elliptical or group.

quotes.quote: WFIJ2033 472 : A quadruply lensed quasar, discovered by Morgan et al. (2004). The quasar redshift is 1.66 and the maximum image separation is 2 5 . The lensing galaxy spectrum, shown in Fig. WFIJ2033 , is matched by an Sb and Sc galaxy template (mix ratio of 4:1 ) with a redshift of 0.658 .

As in the cases of HE0047 1756 and HE0230 2130, above, the non-parallactic slit angle could lead to a change in slope of 30 , and as before, this would likely be in the sense of making the object redder. Between the S0 and Sb/Sc templates, the slope in the 6000-8500 range changes by 75 and therefore differential atmospheric refraction is unlikely to be the main cause of the blue color of the lens galaxy. The derived SIS velocity dispersion in this case is _ SIS =300 km s ^ 1 , corresponding to a massive elliptical or a galaxy group.

sources.summary: WFI 2033-4723 has good deconvolution, small chromatic continuum differences likely tied to deblending uncertainties, and microlensing in the blue wing or blended lines.

quotes.quote: (l) WFI 2033-4723: Deconvolution: The deconvolution is good. Residual flux under the point-like images is < 0.1 of the QSO flux. Chromaticity: The amplitude of the chromatic differences between the continuum in image B and C is small and probably caused by small uncertainties in the deblending of the QSO images and of the lens galaxy. Broad Lines: There is ML of the blue wing of   or/and of  but no ML of the  line. Notes: Our estimate of M is in excellent agreement with the H- band and K- band measurements Vuissoz2008, Blackburne2011a .

sources.summary: WFI J2033 is paired with HE 0230 as a quasar with decreasing half-light radius versus wavelength; the paper argues microlensing caustics likely explain the anomaly.

quotes.quote: Quasars with half-light radii which decrease with increasing wavelength Two quasars in B11 (HE 0230 and WFI J2033) appear to have half-light radii that decrease with increasing wavelength, albeit with large uncertainties. A temperature profile which increases with radius and then abruptly decreases can in principle reproduce the sign of this trend. (Conceptually, such a temperature profile might arise if the accretion luminosity is dominated by a shock at some radius R_b , with cooling gas undergoing radial infall within R_b .) The two quasars above have maximum r_ 1/2 = -0.61 0.54 and r_ 1/2 = -0.45 0.54 , respectively, from _ min to _ max .

B11 note that the anomalous wavelength dependence of these two objects' size estimates might also be produced by unusual microlensing caustic patterns such as those found at the center of an astroid caustic. (The magnification increases away from the center of an astroid caustic, leading to larger flux anomalies for larger sources.) That explanation for these objects' half-light radii seems more likely after consideration of the difficulty in reproducing the results with actual disc temperature profiles. We predict that further observations of these two objects will not show the anomalous size estimates, as long as the time baseline between old and new observations is sufficient for the unusual microlensing caustic pattern to have moved out of our line of sight to the continuum source.

sources.summary: The paper describes WFI J2033-4723 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: WFI J2033-4723 (herein WFI2033) is a quadruply imaged z=1.66 quasar morgan2004 discovered as part of a southern hemisphere optical survey for gravitational lenses using the MPG/ESO 2.2 m telescope. The image separation is 2.53 . eigenbrod and ofek measure the S0 lens redshift to be z_ l =0.661 0.001 and z_ l =0.658 0.001 , respectively. Lens models of the system require large shear =0.225 due to a nearby galaxy and/or a group at the lens redshift morgan2004 . Recently td_wfi2033 measure two independent time delays: t_ B-A = 35.5 1.4 days and t_ B-C = 62.6^ +4.1 _ 2.3 days.

sources.summary: The paper describes WFI2033-4723 as a quad lens with a nearby satellite and a background galaxy included in cosmography and substructure analyses.

quotes.quote: WFI2033-4723 This quad was discovered by Morgan++04 . The lens and source lie at z = 0.66 and z = 1.66 , respectively Sluse++19 . The time delays were measured by Bonvin++19 with a precision of 2 , and a maximum delay of 60 days. The main lensing galaxy has a small satellite located 2 \ arcsec away from the lens centroid. In addition, there is a galaxy located behind the main deflector at z = 0.745 situated 3.5 arcseconds away from the lens center As in the case of HE0435+1223, since this galaxy lies behind the main lens plane the coordinate listed in Table tab:baselinemodels is corrected for foreground lensing effects.

sources.summary: The WFI2033 host is reconstructed with lens modeling and multiple PSF stars; its inferred host magnitude agrees with a prior reconstructed-host measurement.

quotes.quote: We select a total of 8 initial PSF stars to model this system. The final inference results are presented in Table  tab:host_measure and Figure  fig:image_inference (). We compare our inference to the previously reconstructed host galaxies. Modelling the reconstructed host by  Rusu2019 , (i.e., the Figure 4 bottom-right plane therein) as a \ profile, we infer the mag = 21.98 0.15 , which is very consistent to our inference (i.e., mag = 21.78 0.25 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes WFI2033-4723 as a TDCOSMO lens with discovery, image modeling, time-delay measurement, VLT MUSE velocity dispersion, and line-of-sight analysis.

quotes.quote: WFI2033-4723: The discovery is presented by , the image modeling by and the time-delay measurement by . The velocity dispersion measurement from VLT MUSE is presented by with 250 10 km/s only accounting for statistical error and 250 19 km/s including systematic uncertainties. The line-of-sight analysis is presented by .

gmw_00000886.name: RX J1301.9+2747
gmw_00000886.overview: RX J1301.9+2747 is an active, post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2201.11751, arXiv:2304.00775] that exhibits quasi-periodic X-ray eruptions [arXiv:2207.06585, arXiv:2002.08967] and LINER-like nuclear emission [arXiv:2201.11751]. It features compact, variable radio emission consistent with episodic jet ejections [arXiv:2207.06585] and is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2103.00017].

sources.summary: The paper presents RX J1301.9+2747 as a supersoft active galaxy with quasi-periodic X-ray eruptions and compact, variable, steep-spectrum radio emission consistent with episodic jet ejections.

quotes.quote: RX J1301.9+2747 is a unique active galaxy with supersoft X-ray spectrum that lacks significant emission at energies above 2 keV. In addition, it is one of few galaxies displaying quasi-periodic X-ray eruptions that recur on a timescale of 13-20 ks. We present multi-epoch radio observations of RX J1301.9+2747 using GMRT, VLA and VLBA. The VLBA imaging at 1.6 GHz reveals a compact radio emission unresolved at a scale of < 0.7 pc, with a brightness temperature of T_ b > 5 10^ 7 K.

The short-term radio variability suggests that the radio emitting region has a size as small as 8 10^ 4 pc, resulting in an even higher brightness temperature of T_ b 10^ 12 K. A similar limit on the source size can be obtained if the observed flux variability is not intrinsic and caused by the interstellar scintillation effect. The overall radio spectrum is steep with a time-averaged spectral index =-0.78 0.03 between 0.89 GHz and 14 GHz. These observational properties rule out a thermal or star-formation origin of the radio emission, and appear to be consistent with the scenario of episodic jet ejections driven by magnetohydrodynamic process.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 9862-12703 as a post-starburst EELR host with archival Chandra nuclear emission, low current X-ray luminosity, and a past AGN luminosity higher than the present estimate.

quotes.quote: One of the post-starburst galaxies with an EELR (9862-12703) has archival Chandra observations (proposal ID: 10708322, PI Watson), which have been included in the Chandra Source Catalog (CSC, Evans2010 ). We assume this X-ray emission is from an AGN, though much of the X-ray flux could be from a Quasi-Periodic Eruption Giustini2020 . In either scenario, the emission would be originating from the nucleus of this galaxy. Using sherpa , we fit an absorbed power-law model to the X-ray spectrum for this galaxy to determine the current X-ray luminosity, L_X = 2.2 0.3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 .

This L_X is consistent with the range of low X-ray luminosities in the sample of SPOGs considered by Lanz2022 . This galaxy has a current X-ray luminosity significantly lower than the current X-ray luminosity of the Hanny's voorwerp host galaxy, which has a current L_x = 1-2.3 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 Sartori2018b . We can compare the current X-ray luminosity for 9862-12703 with the past peak as an alternative test to the use of the IR observations used above, to test whether this galaxy has a fading AGN. We use the calibrations of Marconi2004 to determine the bolometric luminosity implied by L_X , to be L_ bol /( erg \ s^ 1 ) = 42.4 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies RXJ1301 as a QPE source discovered in an AGN and a strong post-starburst host with stellar absorption corrections and LINER-like/AGN diagnostic evidence.

quotes.quote: Two sources (GSN069 and RXJ1301) were discovered in active galactic nuclei (AGN).

The weakness of H relative to H in eRO-QPE1 and RXJ1301 (for the latter, see Dewangan2000 ) indicates that this line is likely affected by stellar absorption. This is typical for post-starburst galaxies, which contain a significant population of A stars (leading to strong stellar absorption in the Balmer lines). For these two sources, we therefore subtract the best-fit stellar template obtained with pPXF to account for this effect (see Figures fig:sub_qpe1 through fig:sub_rxj1301_zoom for visual illustrations), before measuring the (narrow) emission line fluxes.

RXJ1301 (a strong post-starburst galaxy) is located among the retired/low ionisation nuclear emission line region (LINER)-like galaxies, which is common for galaxies with relatively weak emission lines Cid2011 . This may be a consequence of its edge-on orientation Caldwell1999 . There is ample evidence that this source indeed hosts an AGN (e.g. Dewangan2000, Giustini20 ).

sources.summary: The paper models RX J1301.9+2747 with an eccentric, inclined EMRI orbit; amplitudes and timing are reasonable, but predicted peak temperatures exceed observations.

quotes.quote: Following their discovery in GSN 069 Miniutti2019 , QPEs have been observed so far from the nuclei of other four galaxies: RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 , eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , and XMMSL1 J024916.6-04124 Chakraborty2021 . Only 1.5 QPEs were detected in the latter source, but all observed properties indicate it is nevertheless a robust QPE candidate. QPE host galaxies can be classified as relatively low-mass post-starburst galaxies Arcodia2021,Wevers2022 , a population that is very similar to the preferred tidal disruption events (TDEs) hosts 2020SSRv..216...32F with which they also share a preference towards low mass MBHs of 10^ 5-6.7  M _ Wevers2022 .

RX J1301.9+2747 has a quiescence level similar to GSN 069, i.e. 3-6 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , corresponding to m _1 0.1 for a primary MBH with M_1=10^ 6.3 M_ Giustini2020 . We assume the primary MBH spin to be =0.5 . The secondary orbits the primary on an eccentric and inclined orbit with semi-major axis a=50R_ g , giving an orbital period 1.5 hr, eccentricity e=0.4 (implying the smallest GW timescale of the four QPEs, i.e. T_ gw 400 yr) and inclination i_ EMRI =6^ with respect to the x-y plane.

The peak temperature that our model predicts is in the range 180-450 eV, therefore up to a factor 3-4 higher than the observed value, i.e. 110-130 eV Giustini2020 . Figure  fig:RXJ shows that the synthetic QPE amplitudes are compatible with the observed ones within a factor of 2 , and the complex timing properties of the light curve are reasonably reproduced.

sources.summary: The paper reports X-ray quasi-periodic eruptions in RX J1301.9+2747, comparing their timing and spectra with GSN 069 and arguing QPEs persisted for years.

quotes.quote: Following the recent discovery of X-ray quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) coming from the nucleus of the galaxy GSN 069, here we report on the detection of QPEs in the active galaxy named RX J1301.9+2747.

During a XMM-Newton observation lasting 48 ks that was performed on 30 and 31 May 2019, three strong QPEs lasting about half an hour each were detected in the light curves of RX J1301.9+2747. The first two QPEs are separated by a longer recurrence time (about 20 ks) compared to the second and third (about 13 ks).

Longer X-ray observations will better clarify the temporal pattern of the QPEs in RX J1301.9+2747 and will allow a detailed comparison with GSN 069 to be performed. The X-ray spectral properties of QPEs in the two sources are remarkably similar, with QPEs representing fast transitions from a relatively cold and likely disk-dominated state to a state that is characterized by a warmer emission similar to the so-called soft X-ray excess, a component that is almost ubiquitously seen in the X-ray spectra of unobscured, radiatively efficient active galaxies. Previous X-ray observations of RX J1301.9+2747 in 2000 and 2009 strongly suggest that QPEs have been present for at least the past 18.5 years.

sources.summary: The paper models RX J1301.9+2747 as a Seyfert 2 QPE source whose evolving X-ray flare timings may indicate an eccentric close binary black hole nearing merger.

quotes.quote: Here, we explore a gravitational self-lensing binary SMBH model for the sharp (duration 1 hr), quasi-regular X-ray flares -- dubbed quasi-periodic eruptions -- recently observed from two low mass active galactic nuclei: GSN 069 and RX J1301.9+2747. In our model, the binary is observed edge-on, such that each SMBH gravitationally lenses light from the accretion disc surrounding the other SMBH twice per orbital period. The model can reproduce the flare spacings if the current eccentricity of RX J1301.9+2747 is _0 0.16 , implying a merger within 1000 yrs.

Similar flares in the X-ray light curve of RX J1301 had previously been seen in an XMM-Newton observation from 2000, a Chandra observation from 2009 and possibly ROSAT observations Dewangan2000 in the early-mid 1990s (Earth occultations make confirmation challenging). However, since no single observation contained more than one full flare, it was not possible to determine whether or not the flares were periodic until a recent XMM-Newton observation that revealed the same large-flare-followed-by-small-flare behaviour exhibited by GSN 069, except the long and short waiting times are in this case 5.6 hrs and 3.6 hrs respectively Giustini2020 . Since the full flare in the 2000 XMM-Newton observation is preceded at the start of the exposure by the tail end of the previous flare 5 hrs earlier, it appears that the QPE spacings in RX J1301 have evolved over a 19 year timescale.

sources.summary: The paper models RX J1301.9+2747 as a QPE source with rapidly evolving recurrence times, adopting two parameter sets for its 2000 and 2019 spectra and light curves.

quotes.quote: Compared with GSN 069, the flare recurrence time of RX J1301.9+2747 appears to be more complex and evolve rapidly. We suppose that its burst mechanism is the same with GSN 069 and that the evolution of light curve may be caused by the disturbance of accretion rate or magnetic field strength. Two sets of parameters with the same black hole mass are adopted to fit the spectral evolution and light curve of RX J1301.9+2747 in 2000 and 2019, respectively.

The mass of this source was suggested as 8 10^ 5 M_ with a scatter of 0.5 dex 2013ApJ...768..167S , which is roughly consistent with the value 3 10^ 5 M_ in table 1. Since the two recurrence times in 2019 are distinct with each other (Figure fig:rxj_2019 ), we take the average interval time as the period of our model to calculate the limit-cycle.

sources.summary: The paper describes RX J1301.9+2747 as the second QPE source, notes its non-strict recurrence pattern, and estimates a one-year remaining disruption time.

quotes.quote: The second QPE source is RX J1301.9+2747 with an average 5-hour separation between X-ray peak flux, which was found in the XMM-Newton observations of 2019 and in the archival observational data in 2000 Giustini2020 . It should be noted that RX J1301.9+2747 has no very strict recurrence time since that the first two peaks are separated by a longer recurrence time (about 20 ks) compared to the second and third peaks (about 13 ks) see Giustini2020 .

These QPEs have a coincidence with the galactic nuclei, where all host galaxies are found to be dwarf galaxies with total stellar mass around m_ 10^9M_ Arcodia2021 and black hole (BH) mass of 10^ 5-6 e.g., Wevers2022 .
For RX J1301.9+2747 with M_ BH 1.8 10^6 M_ and L 1.4 10^ 42 erg/s , the remaining time is 1 year .

sources.summary: The paper gives RXJ1301.9+2747's central SMBH mass from X-ray and UV analysis, and states that a white-dwarf companion model cannot explain its QPE properties.

quotes.quote: A second QPE system was discovered in RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini_2020 .
From the X-ray and UV analysis, the centeral SMBH mass of RXJ1301.9+2747 is found to be 0.8-2.8 10^6 M_ Shu_2017 .
However, we find that the model of WD companion cannot explain other three QPEs (RXJ1301.9+2747, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2, see Section model ).

sources.summary: The paper treats RX J1301.9+2747 as an AGN-like QPE host, recovered in the archive search and showing high-amplitude variability plus a small UV dip in one observed flare.

quotes.quote: Four systems exhibiting QPEs have been found to date: the first two, GSN 069 and RX J1301.9+2747 Miniutti19,Giustini20 , are consistent with active galactic nuclei (AGN) on the basis of their emission line profiles, though it is worth noting that both galaxies show no broad components to the H or H lines, and that the observed narrow emission lines may be the signature of past nuclear activity.

Within sources showing high variability of QPE amplitude (RX J1301.9+2747, eRO-QPE1), a shorter t_ rec is associated with a larger amplitude, the inverse of the trend seen between separate QPE sources.

Similarly, no significant UV activity was seen in GSN 069 Miniutti19 , though a re-analysis of RX J1301.9+2747 from the May 2019 observation did show that one of the three X-ray flares is accompanied by a slight dip in UV (but much smaller amplitude than in J0249).

sources.summary: The paper notes D61 retains an edge-on dusty disk despite very low SFR and gas limits, implying gas removal occurred faster than morphological transformation.

quotes.quote: HST images (Caldwell et al. 1999) show that some of the PSB galaxies in Coma have retained their disk, even with a smooth spiral structure. D61 (Zw 160-104) shows two dust lanes forming an edge-on disk, and D216 (RB 160) shows clear spiral structure. Interestingly our limits to the SFR and content for these two galaxies are very low. Obviously the time scale for gas removal from these galaxies was shorter than for a morphological transformation.

gmw_00000887.name: eRO-QPE1
gmw_00000887.overview: The post-starburst galaxy eRO-QPE1 is the most distant known host of quasi-periodic eruptions [arXiv:2304.00775, arXiv:2402.08722]. Powered by a low-mass supermassive black hole [arXiv:2109.03471], it exhibits highly complex, asymmetric X-ray flares with erratic timing and amplitude variations [arXiv:2109.03471, arXiv:2402.08722].

sources.summary: The paper models eRO-QPE1 with an uncertain quiescent level, reproducing a possible 150-hour modulation linked to disc precession and a broadly consistent peak temperature.

quotes.quote: Following their discovery in GSN 069 Miniutti2019 , QPEs have been observed so far from the nuclei of other four galaxies: RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 , eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , and XMMSL1 J024916.6-04124 Chakraborty2021 . Only 1.5 QPEs were detected in the latter source, but all observed properties indicate it is nevertheless a robust QPE candidate. QPE host galaxies can be classified as relatively low-mass post-starburst galaxies Arcodia2021,Wevers2022 , a population that is very similar to the preferred tidal disruption events (TDEs) hosts 2020SSRv..216...32F with which they also share a preference towards low mass MBHs of 10^ 5-6.7  M _ Wevers2022 .

The quiescence level was not clearly detected in this source but it is estimated to be below 1.6 10^ 41 erg/s, broadly corresponding to m _1 0.05-0.001 for a primary MBH with M_1=10^ 5.8 M_ Arcodia2021 . For this source we assume the MBH to have spin =0.65 , while the secondary orbit it taken slightly eccentric and inclined, with semi-major axis a=355R_ g , giving an orbital period of 40 hr, eccentricity e=0.05 (with T_ gw 2 10^5 yr) and inclination i_ EMRI =20^ with respect to the x-y plane.

We show the light curve in Figure  fig:eRO1 . We note that there is a hint of a superposed modulation on a 150 hr period in the data of this QPE source. We reproduce a similar modulation and argue that it is linked to the disc precession frequency ( 6 days). We infer a peak temperature of 190 eV, broadly consistent with the observed value of 260 eV, again subjected to large uncertainties Arcodia2021 .

sources.summary: The paper uses eRO-QPE1 as a comparison case, roughly reproducing its high luminosity, long duration, and multi-peaked light curve with shock-emission models.

quotes.quote: Thereafter, the second and fifth QPE source, RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 Chakraborty2021 , were discovered by XMM-Newton Jansen2001 , and the third and fourth, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , by the wide-field X-ray telescope eROSITA Predehl2021 . It is remarkable that the QPEs in GSN069 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 followed flares interpreted as tidal disruption events Miniutti2019,Chakraborty2021,Miniutti2022 . Among the five QPEs, eRO-QPE1 exhibits a complex evolution of its light curve Arcodia2022 .

In model M12, the high-X-ray luminosity and the long duration as found in eRO-QPE1 are roughly reproduced due to the large values of R_ SO and _* . More precise comparisons to eRO-QPE1 would require a detailed modelling of the shock dynamics, which is beyond the scope of this paper.

Finally, for eRO-QPE1, highly complex evolutions have been found both for the luminosity and the temperature in some phases Arcodia2022 . We propose that three distinct peaks can arise in the light-curve, from emission with high temperature in planar phases, as discussed above for models M6--M8. Other peaks can be contributed by emission from shocks caused by the unbound stellar envelope as presented in Lu2022 . To our knowledge, this is the only proposed explanation for the multiple peaks seen in this event.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes eRO-QPE1 using XMM-Newton and NICER data, fitting an isolated burst and qualitatively reproducing its spectrum and light curves with the disk-instability model.

quotes.quote: eRO-QPE1 This source had four observational campaigns, i.e., one of eROSITA, two of XMM-Newton and one of NICER, of which the quality of XMM-Newton data is the best 2021Natur.592..704A . Therefore we adopt the data from XMM-Newton to analyse the spectral evolution. However, due to the short duration of XMM-Newton campaign, the data from NICER (including 15 complete eruptions) are adopted to investigate the light curve (see Figure fig:QPE_1 ).

The first one on 27 July 2020 (eRO-QPE1-XMM1) showed a complex profile that seems to be formed by the overlapping of several eruptions, which cannot be simplified explained by an instability model. We thus adopt the observation on 4 August 2020 (eRO-QPE1-XMM2) showing a single isolated burst 2022A&A...662A..49A , to compare with our spectral result. In Figure fig:QPE_1 , we compare the calculated spectrum and light curves with the observations. The mass of black hole in this source is aopted as M_ BH =1 10^ 6 M_ , which is consistent with the estimation of 2022ApJ...930..122C ( 9.1 10^ 5 M_ ) by using a empirical scaling relation. Excluding the effect of background, both the spectrum and light curves can qualitatively be reproduced by our model.

sources.summary: The paper monitors eRO-QPE1, the most distant known QPE host, finding erratic fading and reappearance of X-ray eruptions plus possible timing modulation.

quotes.quote: In this study, we report on the ongoing black 3.5 year \ black and XMM-Newton monitoring campaign of eRO-QPE1, which is known to exhibit erratic QPEs that have been challenging for the simplest EMRI models to explain. We report 1) complex, non-monotonic evolution in the long-term trends of QPE energy output and inferred emitting area; 2) the disappearance of the QPEs (within \ detectability) in October 2023, then reappearance black by January 2024 at a luminosity 100x fainter (and temperature 3x cooler) than initial discovery ; 3) radio non-detections with MeerKAT and VLA observations partly contemporaneous with our \ campaign (though not during outbursts); and 4) the presence of a possible 6-day modulation of the QPE timing residuals, which aligns with the expected nodal precession timescale of the underlying accretion disk. Our results tentatively support EMRI-disk collision models powering the QPEs, and we demonstrate that the timing modulation of QPEs may be used to jointly constrain the SMBH spin and disk density profile.

Here, we report on the black 3.5 year \ black and XMM-Newton dataset of eRO-QPE1, a source initially reported in Arcodia21 . The strikingly complex flare patterns and unpredictable recurrence times were immediately noteworthy, and the comparatively erratic QPE behavior has presented a challenge for the simplest EMRI-based models which predict roughly regular outbursts on the orbital timescale. The initial \ observations of this source were studied in-depth by Arcodia22 , who found evidence for overlapping QPEs and a similar L-kT hysteresis pattern as seen in GSN 069.

We note that \ was not able to detect the quiescent disk emission in eRO-QPE1 at any epoch. As eRO-QPE1 is the most distant known QPE ( z=0.0505 ), the quiescence is detected only at 1 by \ Arcodia21 despite a similar L_X to other QPE hosts.

sources.summary: The paper describes eRO-QPE1 as a quiescent-galactic-nucleus QPE source discovered by eROSITA, with asymmetric light curves, host-galaxy stellar mass constraints, and an inferred low-mass SMBH.

quotes.quote: More recently, two more QPEs, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2, were discovered with eROSITA instrument on the Spectrum-Roentgen-Gamma (SRG) space observatory Arcodia_2021 . Follow-up observations by the XMM-Newton X-ray telescope and NICER confirmed the bursting nature of the two sources. The eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 expand QPE durations and recurrence times towards longer and shorter timescales, respectively. The light curves of them are asymmetric, with a fast rise and a slow decay Arcodia_2021 .

The total stellar masses of the host galaxies for eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 are found to be 3.8_ 1.9 ^ +0.4 10^ 9 M_ and 1.01_ 0.50 ^ +0.01 10^ 9 M_ Arcodia_2021 , respectively. Thus, the mass of the SMBH of eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 is 10^ 5 10^ 6 \ M_ .

sources.summary: The paper obtains new medium-resolution spectroscopy of eRO-QPE1, finds Balmer absorption requiring template subtraction, and detects emission lines that earlier low-resolution spectra did not clearly show.

quotes.quote: For three QPEs (GSN069, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2) we present new, medium resolution optical spectroscopy obtained with the Magellan Echelette spectrograph (MagE, mage ), mounted on the Magellan Baade telescope at Las Campanas Observatory, Chile.

The weakness of H relative to H in eRO-QPE1 and RXJ1301 (for the latter, see Dewangan2000 ) indicates that this line is likely affected by stellar absorption. This is typical for post-starburst galaxies, which contain a significant population of A stars (leading to strong stellar absorption in the Balmer lines). For these two sources, we therefore subtract the best-fit stellar template obtained with pPXF to account for this effect (see Figures fig:sub_qpe1 through fig:sub_rxj1301_zoom for visual illustrations), before measuring the (narrow) emission line fluxes.

Our medium resolution optical spectroscopy of in particular eRO-QPE1 differs significantly from the spectrum presented in Arcodia19 . While no emission lines were clearly visible in their (low resolution) spectrum, we clearly detect emission lines of H , H as well as the typical galaxy emission lines of [ O iii ] , [ N ii ] and [ S ii ] .

sources.summary: The paper describes eRO-QPE1 as a quiescent eROSITA QPE host whose flare amplitudes can vary by a factor of ten and whose properties make it an outlier among QPE hosts.

quotes.quote: The latter two sources, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia21 , are quiescent galaxies, and may be more representative of the overall QPE population, given the blind-search nature of the eROSITA survey used to find them. The QPE flares of all four previously known systems share a number of unique observational properties which distinguish them from typical AGN variability, indicating that they likely share a common physical origin.

We note that these quantities, particularly amplitude, can be highly variable even within individual QPE sources---in the most extreme case of eRO-QPE1, the amplitudes of different flares can differ by a factor of up to 10x. Within sources showing high variability of QPE amplitude (RX J1301.9+2747, eRO-QPE1), a shorter t_ rec is associated with a larger amplitude, the inverse of the trend seen between separate QPE sources.

J0249 falls near the shorter end of QPE characteristic timescales, closely matching the durations and recurrence times seen in eRO-QPE2 in spite of its host galaxy sharing the most physical similarities with GSN 069, a comparatively intermediate QPE source. eRO-QPE1 is a conspicuous outlier compared with the four other QPE hosts in terms of characteristic timescales, quiescent luminosity, flare amplitude, and blackbody temperature.

sources.summary: The paper uses eRO-QPE1 as an eROSITA QPE source found by repeated high-amplitude variability, assigning a peak luminosity and checking rate estimates against eRO-QPE2.

quotes.quote: For instance, the variability criteria described here (namely, that significant high-amplitude variability repeated within the single eRASS) are the ones that found eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia+2021:eroqpes .

For eRO-QPE1, we use the average between the main peaks of the two XMM-Newton observations Arcodia+2021:eroqpes , which are representative of most of the time evolution shown by the source Chakraborty+2024:ero1 , namely L_ 0.5-2.0 keV ^ peak =1.2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

However, we note that eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 were the only two QPEs found with a single consistent method Arcodia+2021:eroqpes using only eRASS1 and eRASS2 data. However, performing the same calculation above for eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 with two luminosity bins of one dex (41.5-42.5 and 42.5-43.5) seems to yield consistent results (grey points in Fig. fig:rates ).

sources.summary: The paper treats eRO-QPE1 as a low-luminosity active galaxy with an 18.5-hour recurrence time and estimates its model remaining disruption time at 1.5 years.

quotes.quote: black hole physics-- accretion-- accretion disk -- white dwarf -- gravitational wave Recently discovered regular X-ray bursts are reported as quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) in five galaxies, which recur from 2.4-hour (eRO-QPE2) to 18.5-hour (eRO-QPE1) timescale with duration varies from 0.5 hour (eRO-QPE2) to 7.6 hours (eRO-QPE1) Miniutti2019,Giustini2020,Arcodia2021,Joheen2021 .
The other QPE events eRO-QPE1/2 with a 18.5/2.4 hours recurrence time were discovered in two low-luminosity active galaxies by eROSITA Arcodia2021 .
For eRO-QPE1 with M_ BH 9.1 10^5 M_ and L 3.3 10^ 42 erg/s , the remaining time is 1.5 years .

gmw_00000888.name: eRO-QPE2
gmw_00000888.overview: The host of eRO-QPE2 is a low-mass post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2304.00775] that likely contains an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2201.11751]. It exhibits quasi-periodic eruptions [arXiv:2206.03092] with asymmetric X-ray light curves [arXiv:2109.03471] and alternating recurrence times [arXiv:2304.00775, arXiv:2305.02071].

sources.summary: The paper models eRO-QPE2 as a lower-mass QPE source whose recurrence timing and amplitudes are reproduced, with a predicted peak temperature slightly below observations.

quotes.quote: Following their discovery in GSN 069 Miniutti2019 , QPEs have been observed so far from the nuclei of other four galaxies: RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 , eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , and XMMSL1 J024916.6-04124 Chakraborty2021 . Only 1.5 QPEs were detected in the latter source, but all observed properties indicate it is nevertheless a robust QPE candidate. QPE host galaxies can be classified as relatively low-mass post-starburst galaxies Arcodia2021,Wevers2022 , a population that is very similar to the preferred tidal disruption events (TDEs) hosts 2020SSRv..216...32F with which they also share a preference towards low mass MBHs of 10^ 5-6.7  M _ Wevers2022 .

For this less massive source, the bolometric luminosity of the quiescence is 5-6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , corresponding to m _1 0.01 for a M_1=10^5 M_ primary MBH Arcodia2021 . We assume the primary to have spin =0.5 . The secondary BH orbits the primary on an almost circular inclined orbit with semi-major axis a=320R_ g , giving an orbital period of 5 hr, eccentricity e=0.05 (yielding T_ gw 3000 yr) and inclination i_ EMRI =15^ with respect to the x-y plane.

As shown in Figure  fig:eRO2 , the observed alternating long-short recurrence times and strong-weak QPE amplitudes are remarkably well reproduced, as for GSN 069. The peak temperatures that we obtain 170 eV, slightly below the 215 eV inferred from observations, though these are subjected to large uncertainties Arcodia2021 .

sources.summary: The paper treats eRO-QPE2 as a low-amplitude eROSITA QPE source with measured peak luminosity, sensitivity threshold, short bursts, recurrence, and consistency checks with eRO-QPE1.

quotes.quote: For instance, the variability criteria described here (namely, that significant high-amplitude variability repeated within the single eRASS) are the ones that found eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia+2021:eroqpes .
For eRO-QPE2, the average peak luminosity in the XMM-Newton observation presented in Arcodia+2021:eroqpes : L_ 0.5-2.0 keV ^ peak =8.8 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 .

Nonetheless, we identify eRO-QPE2 Arcodia+2021:eroqpes as the QPE source with the lowest variability amplitude discovered so far with our search. In particular, we adopt as the QPE sensitivity threshold of a given visit the faintest count rate observed for eRO-QPE2 among the brightest that triggered the variability code (see Fig. 2 of Arcodia+2021:eroqpes ). This corresponds to a count rate of 0.43 0.12 in the 0.6-2.3 keV band Arcodia+2021:eroqpes . This count rate can be interpreted as the lowest eRASS count rate that a QPE bright state can have to be considered significantly variable according to our search method. Based on our experience, any variable source with a putative bright state fainter than this threshold, would not have been considered a reliable QPE candidate and would not have been followed up.

sources.summary: The paper describes eRO-QPE2 as a quiescent-galactic-nucleus QPE source discovered by eROSITA, with asymmetric light curves, host-galaxy stellar mass constraints, and an inferred low-mass SMBH.

quotes.quote: More recently, two more QPEs, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2, were discovered with eROSITA instrument on the Spectrum-Roentgen-Gamma (SRG) space observatory Arcodia_2021 . Follow-up observations by the XMM-Newton X-ray telescope and NICER confirmed the bursting nature of the two sources. The eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 expand QPE durations and recurrence times towards longer and shorter timescales, respectively. The light curves of them are asymmetric, with a fast rise and a slow decay Arcodia_2021 .

The total stellar masses of the host galaxies for eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 are found to be 3.8_ 1.9 ^ +0.4 10^ 9 M_ and 1.01_ 0.50 ^ +0.01 10^ 9 M_ Arcodia_2021 , respectively. Thus, the mass of the SMBH of eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 is 10^ 5 10^ 6 \ M_ .

sources.summary: The paper compares eRO-QPE2 with GSN 069, infers a smaller black-hole mass from its shorter periods and hotter spectra, and reproduces its spectral and timing behavior.

quotes.quote: eRO-QPE2 The timing property and spectral evolution of this source seem to be similar with GSN 069, i.e., showing a small duty cycle and alternating longer and shorter recurrence times. But the period of eruptions in eRO-QPE2 is much shorter and the temperature of spectra is much higher than those in GSN 069. We can therefore infer that the mass of eRO-QPE2 should be smaller than GSN 069. In our calculation, a black hole mass M_ BH =1 10^ 5 M_ is adopted, smaller than the mass of eRO-QPE2 adopted by 2022ApJ...930..122C ( 2.3 10^ 5 M_ BH ).
We present the results in Figure fig:QPE_2 . Both the spectral evolution and light curves are well reproduced by our model, just like the results of our previous work for GSN 069.

sources.summary: The paper describes eRO-QPE2 as a quiescent eROSITA QPE host whose recurrence times and durations resemble J0249, while the eROSITA QPEs lack corresponding optical or UV variability.

quotes.quote: The latter two sources, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia21 , are quiescent galaxies, and may be more representative of the overall QPE population, given the blind-search nature of the eROSITA survey used to find them. The QPE flares of all four previously known systems share a number of unique observational properties which distinguish them from typical AGN variability, indicating that they likely share a common physical origin.

J0249 falls near the shorter end of QPE characteristic timescales, closely matching the durations and recurrence times seen in eRO-QPE2 in spite of its host galaxy sharing the most physical similarities with GSN 069, a comparatively intermediate QPE source. eRO-QPE1 is a conspicuous outlier compared with the four other QPE hosts in terms of characteristic timescales, quiescent luminosity, flare amplitude, and blackbody temperature.

Arcodia21 showed that in the two eROSITA QPEs, there was no corresponding variability in the optical/UV. Similarly, no significant UV activity was seen in GSN 069 Miniutti19 , though a re-analysis of RX J1301.9+2747 from the May 2019 observation did show that one of the three X-ray flares is accompanied by a slight dip in UV (but much smaller amplitude than in J0249). Arcodia21 suggest that the lack of UV/optical variability may be due to particularly small accretion disks in these two previously quiescent galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies eRO-QPE2 as a blind-survey QPE source, observes it with new Magellan spectroscopy, and finds line diagnostics that strongly indicate an AGN.

quotes.quote: Subsequently, two QPEs were discovered in a blind X-ray survey (eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2, Arcodia19 ).

For three QPEs (GSN069, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2) we present new, medium resolution optical spectroscopy obtained with the Magellan Echelette spectrograph (MagE, mage ), mounted on the Magellan Baade telescope at Las Campanas Observatory, Chile.
eRO-QPE2 is well beyond the extreme starburst line and not affected by any stellar absorption, so it is highly likely that this source contains an AGN (see also Figure fig:whan ).

sources.summary: The paper treats eRO-QPE2 as a low-luminosity active galaxy with a 2.4-hour recurrence time and estimates its model remaining disruption time at about one year.

quotes.quote: black hole physics-- accretion-- accretion disk -- white dwarf -- gravitational wave Recently discovered regular X-ray bursts are reported as quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) in five galaxies, which recur from 2.4-hour (eRO-QPE2) to 18.5-hour (eRO-QPE1) timescale with duration varies from 0.5 hour (eRO-QPE2) to 7.6 hours (eRO-QPE1) Miniutti2019,Giustini2020,Arcodia2021,Joheen2021 .
The other QPE events eRO-QPE1/2 with a 18.5/2.4 hours recurrence time were discovered in two low-luminosity active galaxies by eROSITA Arcodia2021 .
For eRO-QPE2 with M_ BH 2.3 10^5 M_ and L 1.0 10^ 42 erg/s , the remaining time is 1 \ year .

gmw_00000889.name: XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244
gmw_00000889.overview: XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 is a star-forming or composite galaxy [arXiv:2110.10786] hosting a low-mass nuclear black hole [arXiv:2110.10786] and identified as a tidal disruption event candidate [arXiv:2110.10786, arXiv:2206.03092]. In 2006, the galaxy exhibited soft X-ray flares [arXiv:2110.10786, arXiv:2206.03092] and corresponding ultraviolet dips [arXiv:2110.10786], but this activity ceased after the source dimmed by over an order of magnitude [arXiv:2110.10786].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 as a star-forming/composite galaxy and TDE candidate with QPE-like soft X-ray flares, UV dips, strong dimming and no later QPE activity.

quotes.quote: We have conducted a blind algorithm-assisted search of the \ Source Catalog and found a fifth QPE candidate, XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244. This is a star-forming galaxy hosting a relatively low-mass nuclear black hole, and has previously been identified as a Tidal Disruption Event candidate. An \ pointed observation of the source in 2006 exhibited nearly two QPE-like flares in soft X-rays, and, unlike in other QPE sources, there are hints of corresponding dips in the UV light curves. Afterwards, a series of \ observations observed the rapid dimming of the source; thereafter, in August 2021, we triggered a second \ observation, which revealed that the source is detected, but the QPEs are no longer present.

During an 11.7 ks observation performed by \ in July 2006, 1.5 rapid symmetric QPE-like flares separated by 9 ks were observed from the star-forming/composite galaxy XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244. We call these flares QPE-like because they share with previously known QPEs the following set of distinctive properties: (I) they are confined to soft energy bands ( <2 keV); (II) they are higher-amplitude and shorter-lived in higher energy bands; (III) the high-flux states are associated with rapid transitions to harder spectra; and (IV) the quiescent intervals between flares are remarkably stable, thus indicating the flares are unrelated to other forms of traditional AGN variability. After the initial detection, the source dimmed considerably, by over an order of magnitude in the period from 2006 to 2021. Our follow-up 33.8 ks \ DDT observation in August 2021 found the source in a low-flux, non-variable state, indicating that QPE-like activity had ceased in J0249 altogether.

sources.summary: The paper discusses XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 as a QPE source following a flare, with observed fading and recurrence behavior used to motivate an eccentric AGN-disk collision model.

quotes.quote: Thereafter, the second and fifth QPE source, RX J1301.9+2747 Giustini2020 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 Chakraborty2021 , were discovered by XMM-Newton Jansen2001 , and the third and fourth, eRO-QPE1 and eRO-QPE2 Arcodia2021 , by the wide-field X-ray telescope eROSITA Predehl2021 . It is remarkable that the QPEs in GSN069 and XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 followed flares interpreted as tidal disruption events Miniutti2019,Chakraborty2021,Miniutti2022 .

Miniutti2022 and Chakraborty2021 , respectively, reported that the luminosity for the events GSN069 and XMMSL1 gradually decrease by an order of magnitude in 2 and 10 years. Recently, after the disappearance of QPEs in GSN069, their reappearance has been reported Miniutti2023_reQPE . Prior to this reappearance, the quiescent luminosity is again enhanced, possibly due to additional partial disruption or intense mass loss from a star. This suggests that QPEs occur when the accretion rate is lower than some critical value. In our scenario, this criterion can arise as follows. The gas additionally lost from the star is initially aligned with the stellar orbit. Due to the vigorous mass loss, a thicker disk forms as the disk thickness is proportional to the accretion rate ( H_ AGN L_ AGN / ). For a thicker disk, the alignment radius by the Bardeen-Petterson effect can become smaller than the distance of the star from the SMBH ( r_ w H_ AGN ^ 4/3 , Eq. eq:r_w ). Then, at the stellar position, the accretion disk is not fully aligned with the SMBH spin, and the QPE brightness is reduced due to the larger inclination angle. In our model, the change in the inclination angle of the accretion disk by 30^ can explain the inactive and active phases of GSN069, between which the luminosity of QPEs is changed by a factor of 10 . After the accretion rate decreases below the critical value, the alignment radius exceeds the stellar distance to the SMBH, and the QPEs become brighter again. Miniutti2022 also found that the recurrence time for GSN069 is longer when a stronger QPE is followed by a weaker QPE, and shorter vice versa. On the other hand, Chakraborty2021 found the opposite trend for XMMSL1. We propose that these modulation can be explained by a low orbital eccentricity. When the stellar orbit is eccentric, the recurrence time between two collisions around the pericentre is shorter than that around the apocentre. Also, emission from shocks around a star moving toward an observer is brighter than that moving away from the observer due to Doppler beaming effects. Hence, slightly eccentric orbits possibly explain the modulation of the recurrence time between QPEs found for GSN069 and XMMSL1.

sources.summary: The paper treats XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 as a likely QPE source, adopting a low black-hole mass and qualitatively fitting its light curve and spectral evolution.

quotes.quote: XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 This source is regarded as the most probable QPEs because it showed 1.5 QPE-like flares in 2006 and had a spectral evolution similar to that of GSN 069. Two black hole mass of XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 are given by the previous works 2016A&A...592A..74S,2019MNRAS.487.4136W , i.e., M_ BH 8.5 10^ 4 M_ and M_ BH 5 10^ 5 M_ . However, the black hole mass in this source should be much smaller than that of GSN 069 due to its higher temperature in low state. Here we adopt M_ BH =7 10^ 4 M_ .
Figure fig:XMM gives the comparison of our model with observations. We find that our model can qualitatively reproduce the light curve and spectral evolution as a whole.

sources.summary: The paper treats 2MASXJ0249 as the newest QPE candidate in a TDE-candidate host, reanalyzes its ESI spectrum, and notes classification discrepancies after remeasuring line fluxes.

quotes.quote: Finally, the newest candidate member of the QPE family is 2MASXJ0249, discovered in the host galaxy of a tidal disruption event (TDE) candidate Chakraborty21 .

We also reanalyse an archival Keck/Echelette Spectrograph and Imager (ESI, esi ) spectrum (for 2MASXJ0249, presented in Wevers19a ) and an archival Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum (for RXJ1301, first presented in Dewangan2000 ).

Remeasuring the line fluxes in the case of 2MASXJ0249 also leads to slightly different results compared to Wevers19a , who reported line measurements performed in iraf which likely have larger systematic errors than the method used in this work. We conclude from Figures fig:bpt and fig:whan that our optical spectroscopy provides strong evidence for an AGN in 4/5 sources. We remark that there remain some (well-known) discrepancies between the BPT and WHAN diagram classification for 2MASXJ0249.

sources.summary: The paper identifies XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 as a fifth QPE candidate with soft-X-ray flares, TDE evidence, and an estimated two-year remaining disruption time.

quotes.quote: In addition, the fifth QPE candidate XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 shows two QPE-like flares in soft X-rays in 2006 Joheen2021 . These QPEs have a coincidence with the galactic nuclei, where all host galaxies are found to be dwarf galaxies with total stellar mass around m_ 10^9M_ Arcodia2021 and black hole (BH) mass of 10^ 5-6 e.g., Wevers2022 .
Furthermore, our model is also supported by the observational evidence of tidal disruption events in XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 Joheen2021 .
For XMMSL1 J024916.6-041244 with M_ BH 8.5 10^4 M_ and L 3.4 10^ 41 erg/s , the remaining time is 2 years .

gmw_00000890.name: NGC 5248
gmw_00000890.overview: NGC 5248 is a barred grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:0710.4036, arXiv:2010.11815] with a regular rotating stellar field [arXiv:0705.4162] and a large nuclear disc [arXiv:2003.08946]. It features active circumnuclear star-forming rings [arXiv:1306.5758, arXiv:2010.11815], a nuclear dust spiral [arXiv:0710.4036], and strong star formation along its spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0606480, arXiv:2003.08946].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5248 as a barred grand-design spiral with nested star-formation rings, a nuclear dust spiral, regular stellar rotation, and no detected NIR nuclear emission lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 5248 The grand-design Sbc spiral NGC 5248 is a ``posterchild'' example for the impact of a stellar bar on the gas dynamics and circumnuclear star formation. have convincingly argued that the spiral structure is being driven by an extended, moderately strong stellar bar that has a deprojected ellipticity of 0.44 and a semimajor axis a_ bar of 95 ( 7.1 ). The inner kiloparsec of NGC 5248 has been well studied with ground-based images in emission lines ken89,lai01,kna06 as well as optical kna06 and NIR continuum elm97,per00 . These images have revealed a 750 diameter star formation ring which, when seen at HST resolution, is clearly resolved into numerous bright HII regions and young star clusters mao96,mao01 . Near-infrared color maps have also revealed a nuclear dust spiral lai99 that spans the radial range between 1 (75 ) and 4 (300 ) . This structure likely forms the inward continuation of the grand-design spiral arm system that is apparent in various tracers out to a radius of about 10 jog02a .

At its inside, the nuclear dust spiral appears to lead into a second bright ring that has a radius of only 1.25 \ (100 ) lai01,mao01 and thus forms one of the smallest such structures known. As with NGC 613, NGC 5248 is believed to harbor an AGN, and is classified as Sy 2. However, at the sensitivity limits of the SINFONI data, we find no evidence for emission lines in the NIR spectrum of the NGC 5248 nucleus.

sources.summary: NGC 5248 has a prominent large-scale bar and inner and outer spiral arms; its outer disk shows alternating flows, while its central torques are weak and atypical.

quotes.quote: NGC 5248 shows a prominent stellar large-scale bar Jog02a visible in the NIR image and in the gaseous distribution as well Haa08 . Further, two inner stellar spiral arms are present within the bar Jog02 and two outer ones continue from the ends of the bar to the outer disk at 15 kpc. The gravitational torque pattern seems to be a mix between the bar and spiral arm contributions. In particular, the outer disk exhibits an additional torque concentration at the end of the north-western spiral arm at a distance of 7 kpc from the center. For the outer disk we found outflow at distances from (11-15), (2-8) kpc and inflow between (11-8) kpc.

The gravitational torques in the center are very small (maximum of d L/L=0.04 ) and show no typical torque pattern for a bar or oval. Instead a bipolar pattern is present, but might be caused by a possible offset between the real center and our used values (see subsec:torque_error ) or is alternatively suggesting the presence of a lopsided disk. Interestingly, NGC 5248 is the only galaxy in our sample without AGN activity, which might be linked to the absence of gravitational torques and/or a possible lopsided disk in the center.

sources.summary: The paper uses H-alpha data and NII corrections for NGC 5248, then discusses its CO spiral arms, gas concentration, rotation speed, and estimated arm dwelling time.

quotes.quote: H continuum-subtracted images of NGC 5248 and NGC 5457 were obtained from . The NGC 4321, NGC 5055, NGC 5194 H images have a resolution of 0.305^ / pixel, whereas NGC 5248 and NGC 5457 images have a resolution of 0.241 ^ / pixel.

We have estimated the contribution from the neighboring NII lines using integrated spectrophotometry of the galaxies from in the case of NGC 4321 and NGC 5194, and an empirical scaling relation between the NII/H ratio and M _ B described in Appendix B of in the case of NGC 5055, NGC 5248 and NGC 5457. The resulting NII/H ratios are 0.430 (NGC 4321), 0.486 (NGC 5055), 0.590 (NGC 5194), 0.369 (NGC 5248) and 0.389 (NGC 5457).

For example, a CO map of the galaxy shown in Figure 4, NGC 5248 kuno07 , has two spiral arms midway out with 12 K km s ^ 1 integrated intensities that occupy about 20 25 of the azimuthal distance to the next arm, and it shows interarm regions with less than 3 K km s ^ 1 . Thus the arms contain about half of the gas at that radius ( (12/3)*0.25=0.5 ), and the arm dwelling time is half of the arm-to-arm time if most of the gas is molecular. This fraction would be lower if the X factor that converts CO to H _2 is lower in the arms than in the interarms because of greater CO temperatures in the arms. Using V_ rel 100 km s ^ 1 , which is half of the rotation speed of NGC 5248 nishiyama01 , assuming this position is half the distance to co-rotation, and assuming a galactocentric distance of 1 arcmin 4 kpc, the arm-to-arm time is 126 Myr and the arm dwelling time could be about 60-70 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5248 as a grand-design spiral with active circumnuclear rings, young stellar populations throughout the disc, high young-star fractions, and dusty star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 5248 is classified as an SAB(rs)bc galaxy in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ , and it is a prime example of a grand-design spiral. There is a very active star-forming circumnuclear ring of 650 pc radius 2010MNRAS.402.2462C populated by numerous young star clusters 1996AJ....111.2248M, 2001AJ....121.3048M .

In NGC 1097 and NGC 5248 we do not only find a well defined ring-like structure, but also relatively young stellar populations within them. This is especially noticeable in the ring of NGC 5248. In the two rings we estimate an average age below 4 Gyr ( 3.5 Gyr for NGC 1097 and 1.8 Gyr for NGC 5248). It is remarkable that in NGC 5248 we find a relatively small range of ages (from 0.5 Gyr to 5.0 Gyr) while in the ring of NGC 1097 it is much larger (from 0.8 Gyr to 10.7 Gyr), which clearly affects the obtained mean value.

NGC 5248 shows high fractions of young stars in all the regions. The ring is recognisable by a greater presence of bins with high fractions of young stars, resulting in a slightly higher mean value, 0.63 , when compared with the other two regions ( 0.53 and 0.55 for the disc and nucleus, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5248 as an inactive spiral control galaxy with circum-nuclear rings, a bar-driven inner molecular spiral, streaming motions, and a regular rotating stellar field.

quotes.quote: NGC 5248 is a nearby inactive spiral galaxy, control for both NGC 4051 and NGC 6951. It harbours two prominent circum-nuclear rings 5248_1 and an inner molecular spiral within 1.5 kpc driven by the large scale bar 5248_2 . The arms of the spiral connect with the inner circumnuclear ring. also mention the presence of streaming motions in this galaxy.

The stellar continuum shows an elongated structure along the global disk orientation at PA 150 (Fig.  pair3 ). The velocity field presents a regular rotation pattern, the major-axis of which is oriented at a PA of 115 , aligned with the photometric major-axis PA=105 5248_2 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5248, focusing on its claimed two circumnuclear star-forming rings, high-resolution CO observations, molecular gas distribution, and central gas dynamics.

quotes.quote: The nearby galaxy NGC 5248 is especially interesting in this sense since it is said to host 2 circumnuclear star forming rings at 100 pc and 370 pc from its quiescent nucleus. Here we present new subarcsecond PdBI+30m CO(2-1) emission line observations of the central region. For the first time the molecular gas distribution at the smallest stellar ring is resolved into a gas ring, consistent with the presence of a quiescent nucleus. However, the molecular gas shows no ring structure at the larger ring. We combine analyses of the gaseous and stellar content in the central kiloparsec of this galaxy to understand the gas distribution and dynamics of this star forming central region.

We discuss the probability of two scenarios leading to the current observations, given our full understanding of this system, and discuss whether there are really two circumnuclear star forming rings in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The review portrays NGC 5248 as a late-type Seyfert barred galaxy with nested spiral and ring structures, self-gravitating gas, and intermittent central inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 5248 (late-type Sy2, Jogee et al. 2002) follows the prototype of a barred galaxy, with low central mass concentration. The spiral structure extends from 10 kpc down to 100 pc. There are at least two embedded structures, since inside the starburst ring of super-star clusters at 375 pc, develops another spiral structure with a second H ring inside, and a dust spiral revealed by HST. The modelisation shows that the gas is self-gravitating, even if the bar is driving the spiral density wave. Although most of the central gas is confined to the nuclear ring, there is some gas in the very center, maybe infalling intermittently.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5248 as a barred grand-design spiral with barred radio emission, strong circumnuclear and arm star formation, and average RC-CO behavior.

quotes.quote: NGC 5248 . This is a nearby barred galaxy with a spectacular grand-design spiral structure. Our radio image shows the presence of a barred structure (Fig. figura1a ). The properties of the circum-nuclear region and the relation to the host galaxy have been discussed by .
